(Homework Solutions Collection — professor directive (`feedback.md`, 2026-05-20) — run 14, notebook `2-2-2-spin-representations` 2026-05-20 **complete**: scheduled-task automated run (manager agent, no human present). `feedback.md` carries the OPEN multi-run homework-solutions directive, so this run worked that item instead of the Ch 4–6 phased program; the Phase Cursor is unchanged — Round 1, Phase D, `6-1-2-entropy`. Created `homework/2-2-2_solutions.md` (7 homework problems copied verbatim from the notebook's Homework cell — all 7 problem statements, including the three-part sub-question structure of P7, verified against the source — each with a complete worked solution) and updated `homework/homework.md` with the 2.2.2 entry and its 7 problem titles. Solutions cover: P1 the Pauli identities (sigma^i)^2 = I, sigma^i sigma^j = delta_ij I + i eps_ijk sigma^k, and Tr sigma^i = 0 by explicit 2x2 matrix computation, plus the eigenvalue argument for tracelessness; P2 the eigenvalues +/- hbar/2 and eigenstates |+-> = (|up> +/- |down>)/sqrt2 of S_x = (hbar/2) sigma^x via the characteristic equation; P3 D^(1/2)(x,pi/2) = (I - i sigma^x)/sqrt2 applied to |up>, giving the equal superposition (|up> - i|down>)/sqrt2 = |i-bar>, with the Bloch-sphere geometric check (z -> -y under a pi/2 rotation about x); P4 D^(1/2)(n,2pi) = -I and D^(1/2)(n,4pi) = +I from the half-angle cos/sin, why the -1 is a global phase (unobservable for one particle) but a relative phase in interference (the two-branch amplitude |psi> + (-|psi>) = 0), with the Rauch/Werner 1975 neutron-interferometry confirmation; P5 [S_x,S_y] = i hbar S_z verified by full 3x3 matrix multiplication and S_+|1,-1> = hbar sqrt2 |1,0> from the explicit S_+ matrix and the ladder formula; P6 why spin-j is (2j+1)-dimensional (m runs -j..j in unit steps, the ladder norms vanish at m = +/-j) and irreducible (J_pm connect all rungs, no proper invariant subspace), with the physical meaning (an elementary rotational object, contrasted with the reducible 1/2 (x) 1/2 = 1 (+) 0); P7 spin-1 evolution under H = omega J_x^2 — (a) J_x^2 as a 3x3 matrix, |1,0> an eigenvalue-1 eigenstate, the {|1,+1>,|1,-1>} block (1/2)[[1,1],[1,1]] with eigenvalues 0,1 and eigenstates (|1,+1> +/- |1,-1>)/sqrt2, (b) |psi(t)>, P_+1 = cos^2(omega t/2), P_-1 = sin^2(omega t/2), P_0 = 0, (c) at t = pi/(2 omega) the degenerate J_z^2 measurement (outcome m^2 = 1, probability 1) leaves the state unchanged and <J_z> = 0. All key results verified programmatically with NumPy at machine precision: the Pauli squares/products/traces, the spinor rotation D^(1/2)(x,pi/2)|up>, the spin-1 commutator [S_x,S_y] = i hbar S_z and S_+|1,-1>, J_x^2 as a matrix, and the full P7 evolution (P_+1/P_-1/P_0 and <J_z> at t = pi/(2 omega) and three other times, plus the post-measurement state recovered up to a global phase). Solutions-file QA: all 7 problem lines reproduced verbatim, display-math blocks spaced (blank line before/after, none inside). No new tickets filed. Solutions cover: P1 the spin-1/2 algebra [J_i,J_j]=i hbar eps_ijk J_k via the Pauli master identity sigma^i sigma^j = delta_ij I + i eps_ijk sigma^k and an explicit [sigma^x,sigma^y]=2i sigma^z matrix check; P2 [J^2,J_z]=0 by expanding the Casimir and cancelling the two -/+ i hbar (J_xJ_y+J_yJ_x) anticommutator terms, with the commuting-observables argument for joint (j,m) labels; P3 [J_z,J_pm]=+/- hbar J_pm and [J_+,J_-]=2 hbar J_z from the ladder definitions; P4 J_+|1,1> = 0 because the (j-m) factor vanishes at m=j, and J_-|1,0> = hbar sqrt2 |1,-1> verified against the spin-1 matrix J_-; P5 <J_x>=<J_y>=0 in |j,m> from ladder operators mapping to orthogonal states, with the cone / azimuthal-symmetry and R_z(pi) parity arguments and the nonzero variance (1/2) hbar^2 [j(j+1)-m^2]; P6 J_-J_+ = J^2 - J_z^2 - hbar J_z by expansion and ||J_+|j,m>||^2 = hbar^2 (j-m)(j+m+1) via J_+dagger = J_-; P7 integer orbital l from single-valuedness of e^{i m phi} / Y_l^m on the sphere vs half-integer spin (no spatial coordinate, SU(2) double cover, (-1)^{2m} under a 2pi rotation) and the rotation-by-2pi heuristic for the spin-statistics theorem; P8 the quantum-bootstrap algebra — (a) beta Hermitian, (b) alpha-dagger alpha = beta+I and alpha alpha-dagger = I-beta, (c) (alpha-dagger)^2 alpha^2 = beta^2 - I and the positivity squeeze beta in {-1,+1}, (d) the map alpha = sqrt2 J_-, beta = 2 J_z identifying the spin-1/2 representation (the anticommutator {alpha-dagger,alpha}=2I forces J^2 - J_z^2 = (1/2)I, satisfied only by j=1/2), with the 2x2 matrices and the alpha/sqrt2 = single fermion-mode (Jordan-Wigner) observation. All key results verified programmatically with NumPy at machine precision (33 checks: the spin-1/2 and spin-1 commutators, J_-|1,0>, the (j-m)(j+m+1) factoring for j=1/2..2, all four bootstrap relations on the 2x2 matrices, alpha^2=0, beta eigenvalues +/-1, the alpha=sqrt2 J_- / beta=2 J_z map, and J^2-J_z^2=(1/2)I). Solutions-file QA: all 8 problem lines verbatim (P8 display-math block diffed in full), 47 display-math blocks all correctly spaced (blank line before/after, none inside), inline `$` balanced, 8 problem headers present. No new tickets filed. (Homework Solutions Collection — professor directive (`feedback.md`, 2026-05-20) — run 12, notebook `2-1-3-second-quantization` 2026-05-20 **complete**: scheduled-task automated run (manager agent, no human present). `feedback.md` carries the OPEN multi-run homework-solutions directive, so this run worked that item instead of the Ch 4–6 phased program; the Phase Cursor is unchanged — Round 1, Phase D, `6-1-2-entropy`. Created `homework/2-1-3_solutions.md` (10 homework problems copied verbatim from the notebook's Homework cell — all 10 problem statements, including the sub-parts and display math of P9 and P10, verified line-for-line against the source — each with a complete worked solution) and updated `homework/homework.md` with the 2.1.3 entry and its 10 problem titles. Solutions cover: P1 the bosonic number operator n = b-dagger.b — Hermitian and positive semidefinite, ladder commutators [n,b] = -b and [n,b-dagger] = +b-dagger, the descending-chain / norm argument forcing the spectrum to be exactly n = 0,1,2,..., and the explicit n = 0,1 checks; P2 [n_a,b-dagger_c] = delta_ac b-dagger_c and [n_a,b_c] = -delta_ac b_c via the Leibniz rule, with the raising/lowering interpretation; P3 {c-dagger,c-dagger} = 2(c-dagger)^2 = 0 => (c-dagger)^2 = 0 as Pauli exclusion in operator form; P4 <0|b b-dagger|0> = 1, <0|b-dagger b|0> = 0, their difference = vacuum expectation of [b,b-dagger] = 1, with the normal- vs anti-normal-ordering reading; P5 two-mode N=2 Fock states — 3 bosonic (|2,0>,|1,1>,|0,2>) vs 1 fermionic (|1,1>), with the C(N+D-1,N) / C(D,N) counts; P6 <{n}|H|{n}> = sum_a eps_a n_a; P7 [n_a,n_b] = 0 => [H,N] = 0, total-particle-number conservation (with a note that a genuine two-body interaction also conserves N); P8 T = sum_k (hbar^2 k^2/2m) n_k vs the first-quantized N-dependent sum — diagonal, N-independent, no manual symmetrization; P9 harmonic-oscillator equal partition — <n|x^2|n> = (hbar/2mw)(2n+1), <n|p^2|n> = (mhbarw/2)(2n+1), KE = PE = E_n/2, and for the |0>,|1> superposition both <x^2> and <p^2/m^2w^2> are time-independent (x^2 and p^2 carry no 0-1 matrix element) and equal at every instant, so equal partition holds — the time-average mechanism is explained for the delta-n = 2 case, where a 2w oscillation would enter x^2 and p^2 with opposite sign and average to zero; P10 Schwinger bosons — [Sz,S+-] = +-S+-, [S+,S-] = 2Sz, S^2 = (N/2)(N/2+1), the N=2 subspace matrices Sz = diag(1,0,-1) and S+ with sqrt(2) entries (spin-1), and the general map |n_a,n_b> <-> |s,m> with s = N/2. P9 (HO matrix elements, vanishing 0-1 off-diagonal, equal partition) and P10 (the su(2) commutators, S^2 = s(s+1), and the N=2 and N=3 subspace matrices) verified programmatically with NumPy at machine precision — the only large residuals were Fock-space cutoff truncation artifacts, which vanished once the algebra was tested inside fixed-N blocks. Solutions-file QA: all 10 problem lines verbatim (P9/P10 sub-parts and display math diffed in full), 47 display-math blocks all correctly spaced (blank line before/after, none inside), inline `$` balanced, no glued \vert, no control characters. No new tickets filed. Observation for a future maintenance pass (not ticketed — the phased program is paused per `feedback.md`): three homework titles in the `2-1-3` notebook are mismatched to their problems — P1 "Fermionic ladder operators" is actually about the bosonic number operator, P3 "Boson ladder operators" is actually about the fermionic c-dagger (the two titles appear swapped), and P7 "Two-body interaction" uses the non-interacting Hamiltonian carried over from P6; the solutions reproduce the titles verbatim and solve the problems as actually posed. (Homework Solutions Collection — professor directive (`feedback.md`, 2026-05-20) — run 11, notebook `2-1-2-symmetrization` 2026-05-20 **complete**: scheduled-task automated run (manager agent, no human present). `feedback.md` carries the OPEN multi-run homework-solutions directive, so this run worked that item instead of the Ch 4–6 phased program; the Phase Cursor is unchanged — Round 1, Phase D, `6-1-2-entropy`. Created `homework/2-1-2_solutions.md` (8 homework problems copied verbatim from the notebook's Homework cell — all 8 problem statement lines verified line-for-line against the source — each with a complete worked solution) and updated `homework/homework.md` with the 2.1.2 entry and its 8 problem titles. Solutions cover: P1 three-particle statistics — braid identity P12.P23.P12 = P13 verified on |a>|b>|c>, then lambda_13 = lambda_23 and lambda_12 = lambda_13 by peeling eigenvalues (using lambda^2 = 1), forcing one common lambda in {+1,-1}, so only bosons/fermions are consistent (anyons require 2D, where P^2 != I); P2 exchange projectors Sigma_pm = (1/2)(I +/- P12) — idempotent, mutually orthogonal, complete, projecting onto the symmetric/antisymmetric eigenspaces and giving the unique H(x)H = H_+ (+) H_- decomposition; P3 generalized Pauli exclusion — |a1,a2>_- = 0 for proportional states, three-fermion vanishing when |a3> in span{|a1>,|a2>}, the wedge-product / filled-subspace argument (state depends only on the occupied subspace up to a scalar), and the bosonic non-vanishing counterexample; P4 bosonic normalization — unit norm for distinct modes, the extra sqrt(n!) for repeated modes, the 3-boson n_a=2 enumeration (3 distinct orderings x multiplicity 2!, norm^2 = 12 = 3!*2!), and the general N_+ = 1/sqrt(N! prod n_a!); P5 insertion/deletion — the four fermionic single-mode values, the fermionic exact-inverse identity delete(insert Psi) + insert(delete Psi) = Psi, the bosonic induction insert|a^n> = (n+1)|a^{n+1}> and delete|a^n> = n|a^{n-1}>, and the bosonic mismatch delete(insert) - insert(delete) = (2n+1), not 1; P6 Slater-determinant overlap <a1,a2|b1,b2>_- = det[<a_i|b_j>] derived from the antisymmetric definition, the orthonormal check = 1, the hydrogen-molecule overlap 1 - s^2 with the s->0 / s->1 limits, and the N-fermion determinant / bosonic permanent generalization; P7 fermion count C(D,N), zero for N>D (pigeonhole = Pauli exclusion); P8 boson count C(N+D-1,N) by stars and bars, unbounded in N. Verified programmatically: the braid relation on |abc>, the 3-boson norm^2 = 12 and the general N! prod(n_a!) norm formula for several occupation sets, the bosonic insertion/deletion recursion and the (2n+1) mismatch for n = 0..5, and the C(D,N) / C(N+D-1,N) counts. Solutions-file QA: all 8 problem lines verbatim, `$$` and inline `$` delimiters balanced, no `$$`-inside-bullet spacing issues (two such blocks rewritten to prose), no glued `\vert`, no stray control characters. Matrix results P3/P6/P7 and the P8 counts verified programmatically with NumPy (Z(x)I; XX+YY+ZZ and its eigenvalues {-3,1,1,1}; the trace-inner-product Pauli coefficients and the reconstruction of |00><00|). (Homework Solutions Collection — professor directive (`feedback.md`, 2026-05-20) — run 9, notebook `1-3-3-heisenberg-picture` 2026-05-20 **complete**: scheduled-task automated run (manager agent, no human present). `feedback.md` carries the OPEN multi-run homework-solutions directive, so this run worked that item instead of the Ch 4–6 phased program; the Phase Cursor is unchanged — Round 1, Phase D, `6-1-2-entropy`. Created `homework/1-3-3_solutions.md` (9 homework problems copied verbatim from the notebook's Homework cell — all 9 problem lines verified line-for-line against the source — each with a complete worked solution) and updated the `homework/homework.md` index with the 1.3.3 entry and its 9 problem titles. Solutions cover: P1 picture equivalence — <ψ(t)|A|ψ(t)> = <ψ(0)|A(t)|ψ(0)> by regrouping the associative product U† A U; P2 energy conservation from dH/dt = (i/ħ)[H,H] = 0 (and H(t)=U†HU=H since U commutes with H); P3 verifies [σ^z,σ^x]=2iσ^y and [σ^z,σ^y]=-2iσ^x from explicit Pauli matrices, then derives σ^x(t)=σ^x cosωt − σ^y sinωt; P4 ladder-operator commutators and Heisenberg solution â(t)=â e^{-iωt}, â†(t)=â† e^{+iωt}; P5 [A,H]=0 ⇒ d<A>/dt=0 in any state, plus the shared-eigenbasis interpretation; P6 conjugation by e^{-iθσ^z/2} rotates σ^x → σ^x cosθ + σ^y sinθ (differential + Hadamard-lemma routes, SU(2) half-angle); P7 Ehrenfest equations from [H,x]=-iħp/m and [H,p]=iħV', with the <V'(x)> vs V'(<x>) caveat; P8 H=0 ⇒ every observable frozen, H as generator of time translation; P9 symmetry–conservation principle, U(1) phase symmetry ⇒ particle-number conservation, and Standard-Model gauge currents. NEW FINDING (fix ticket for a future notebook-writer session — not fixed this run, one-task-per-session): notebook `1-3-3` Homework Problem 4 states the commutators with reversed operator ordering — it writes [â,H]=-ħωâ and [â†,H]=+ħωâ†, but the correct values are [â,H]=+ħωâ and [â†,H]=-ħωâ†; the displayed numbers are actually [H,â] and [H,â†]. The Heisenberg equations in the same problem are written with [H,â], so feeding the displayed values into them is self-consistent and yields the correct â(t)=â e^{-iωt}; nonetheless the 'Show that [â,H]=…' line has the wrong commutator ordering and should be corrected to [H,â]/[H,â†] (or have its signs flipped). The solutions file flags this in a sign-convention note and solves it correctly. With 1-3-3 complete, all 9 Chapter 1 subsection notebooks now have solutions files; the next run begins Chapter 2 — `2-1-1` is next (the directive skips stems matching 2-3-*).)

(Homework Solutions Collection — professor directive (`feedback.md`, 2026-05-20) — run 7, notebook `1-3-1-unitary-evolution` 2026-05-20 **complete**: scheduled-task automated run (manager agent, no human present). `feedback.md` carries the OPEN multi-run homework-solutions directive, so this run worked that item instead of the Ch 4–6 phased program; the Phase Cursor is unchanged — Round 1, Phase D, `6-1-2-entropy`. Created `homework/1-3-1_solutions.md` (9 homework problems copied from the notebook’s Homework cell, each with a complete worked solution) and updated the `homework/homework.md` index with the 1.3.1 entry and its 9 problem titles. Solutions cover: P1 unitarity of e^{iGθ} via the Taylor series (adjoint = e^{-iGθ} from Hermiticity of G); P2 e^{iσ^xθ/2} by spectral decomposition = cos(θ/2)I + i sin(θ/2)σ^x; P3 generator extraction G = -i dU/dθ|_0 = σ^z/2; P4 two-level H = E_0 I + Δσ^z with U(t)=e^{-iE_0 t/ħ} diag(e^{-iΔt/ħ}, e^{+iΔt/ħ}), each eigenstate picking up only a phase; P5 derivation of the time-dependent Schrödinger equation by differentiating U(t); P6 norm preservation from U†U=I; P7 superposition evolution with relative phase (E_2-E_1)t/ħ at the Bohr frequency; P8 composition U(t_1)U(t_2)=U(t_1+t_2) and the one-parameter-group / reversibility consequence; P9 e^{iAπ} for A with eigenvalues 2,-3 = |a_1><a_1| - |a_2><a_2| (spectral method vs the non-terminating Taylor series). Solution math verified programmatically with NumPy (unitarity of e^{iGθ}; e^{iσ^xθ/2} vs the cos/sin closed form across a θ-sweep; finite-difference generator extraction giving σ^z/2; the two-level U(t); norm preservation under the evolution operator; the composition law; e^{iAπ}=diag(1,-1) with the (2A+I)/5 closed form). No new tickets filed. Homework-solutions files done so far: 1-1-1, 1-1-2, 1-1-3, 1-2-1, 1-2-2, 1-2-3, 1-3-1. The next in-scope notebook is `1-3-2-schrodinger-picture`. The `feedback.md` item stays OPEN until every in-scope notebook has a solutions file and `homework/homework.md` is complete.)

Ch 6 derivation primary reference is `_refs/QuantumStatistics.nb` per the `CLAUDE.md` § Reference materials table, but the density-matrix / Bloch / Von Neumann material lives in `_refs/QubitsAndEntanglement.nb` § *Density Matrix* (line 19899), § *Dynamics of Density Matrix* / *von Neumann equation* (line 21057), § *Pure State and Mixed State* (line 23440), and § *Purity* + HW solution (lines 23965 / 24052 / 24108); `QuantumStatistics.nb` only references mixed-state ensembles in the maximum-entropy / canonical-ensemble context (line 5096+) and does not derive the §6.1.1 results. Audit therefore aligns against `_refs/QubitsAndEntanglement.nb` for the three derivations. **(D-1) Derivation: Purity from Bloch Vector — too compressed vs `_refs/`.** Lecture (post-NB-51 cell-2 pos ~6540, *dropdown information* between `### The Bloch Ball` and `### Expectation Values`) shows **2 displayed-math lines** ((i) $\hat{\rho}^2 = \tfrac14(\hat I + 2\boldsymbol r\cdot\hat{\boldsymbol\sigma} + |\boldsymbol r|^2\hat I)$ — combines binomial expansion of $(\hat I + \boldsymbol r\cdot\hat{\boldsymbol\sigma})^2$ with substitution of the Pauli identity $(\boldsymbol r\cdot\hat{\boldsymbol\sigma})^2 = |\boldsymbol r|^2\hat I$ in a single step; (ii) $\operatorname{Tr}(\hat{\rho}^2) = \tfrac14(2 + 2|\boldsymbol r|^2) = \tfrac12(1 + |\boldsymbol r|^2)$ — combines trace operation with simplification). `_refs/QubitsAndEntanglement.nb` § *Purity* HW solution (cell `hw: 834866580465521418` at line 24052; solution at line 24108) shows the same derivation in **4 displayed lines**: (1) $\operatorname{Tr}\rho^2 = \tfrac14\operatorname{Tr}(\mathbb{1} + \langle\boldsymbol\sigma\rangle\cdot\boldsymbol\sigma)^2$ (substitute $\rho$ form), (2) $= \tfrac14\operatorname{Tr}(\mathbb{1} + 2\langle\boldsymbol\sigma\rangle\cdot\boldsymbol\sigma + (\langle\boldsymbol\sigma\rangle\cdot\boldsymbol\sigma)(\langle\boldsymbol\sigma\rangle\cdot\boldsymbol\sigma))$ (binomial expansion only, identity not yet applied), (3) $= \tfrac14(1 + \langle\boldsymbol\sigma\rangle\cdot\langle\boldsymbol\sigma\rangle)\operatorname{Tr}\mathbb{1}$ (apply Pauli identity, drop $\operatorname{Tr}\sigma^i = 0$ term, factor $\operatorname{Tr}\mathbb{1}$), (4) $= \tfrac12(1 + \langle\boldsymbol\sigma\rangle^2)$ (substitute $\operatorname{Tr}\mathbb{1} = 2$). Per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § Mandatory detail level (*'Each commutator evaluation or algebraic derivation in a dropdown must contain at least as many displayed intermediate lines as the corresponding passage in `_refs/`. Typically this means 4–7 lines per commutator evaluation.'*) — lecture below the binding standard; strict rule violation. Filed as **T-2026-05-19-DV-13** (**HIGH** — strict `_refs/`-alignment violation, scope is single dropdown, fix expands 2 → 4 lines matching `_refs/` chain). **(D-2) Derivation: Von Neumann Equation — too compressed; `\dot{\hat U}^\dagger` sign-flip step unstated.** Lecture (post-NB-51 cell-2 pos ~9230, *dropdown information* between `### Time Evolution` and the *Purity is Conserved* `attention` box) shows **1 displayed-math line** packing four operations: (a) product-rule differentiation of $\hat\rho(t) = \hat U\hat\rho(0)\hat U^\dagger$, (b) substitution of $\dot{\hat U} = -(\mathrm{i}/\hbar)\hat H\hat U$, (c) substitution of the conjugate $\dot{\hat U}^\dagger = +(\mathrm{i}/\hbar)\hat U^\dagger\hat H$ (sign-flip critical; differentiating $\hat U^\dagger = \mathrm{e}^{+\mathrm{i}\hat H t/\hbar}$), and (d) recognition of $\hat U\hat\rho(0)\hat U^\dagger = \hat\rho(t)$ in the final step. `_refs/QubitsAndEntanglement.nb` § *Dynamics of Density Matrix* (line 21057) does not derive Von Neumann equation in the lecture text — the reference frames the derivation as a homework problem (cell `hw: 6327714880484912580` at line 21133: *'derive the von Neumann equation Eq. (...) from the Schrödinger equation Eq. (...)'*) — so the `_refs/` line count is undefined for this derivation. Apply the *typically 4-7 lines* default from `rules/derivation-quality.md` § Mandatory detail level. Lecture 1 line ≪ 4 default minimum; strict rule violation. Critical unstated step: the conjugate derivative sign flip ($\dot{\hat U}^\dagger = +(\mathrm{i}/\hbar)\hat U^\dagger\hat H$) — the `+` sign vs the `-` sign on $\dot{\hat U}$ is the source of the commutator structure; students taught to take $(\dot{\hat U})^\dagger$ without seeing the daggered form lose the connection between Hermitian conjugation and the sign. Filed as **T-2026-05-19-DV-14** (**HIGH** — strict default-minimum violation + critical missing sign-flip step; scope is single dropdown; fix expands 1 → ~5 lines: (1) starting form, (2) product rule, (3) substitute $\dot{\hat U}$ and $\dot{\hat U}^\dagger$ separately with explicit signs, (4) factor and recognize $\hat U\hat\rho(0)\hat U^\dagger = \hat\rho(t)$, (5) commutator form with explicit $\mathrm{i}\hbar$ on LHS). **(D-3) Derivation: Purity Bounds — Cauchy-Schwarz step skipped; mixed-state cross-term reasoning skipped.** Lecture (post-NB-51 cell-2 pos ~4555, *dropdown information* between `### Purity` and `### Ensemble Ambiguity`) shows **3 conceptual bullet-lines**: (i) pure-state idempotent $\hat\rho^2 = |\psi\rangle\langle\psi|\psi\rangle\langle\psi| = \hat\rho$, (ii) Cauchy-Schwarz lower bound $\sum\lambda_i^2 \geq (\sum\lambda_i)^2/d = 1/d$, (iii) mixed-state strict $\sum\lambda_i^2 < (\sum\lambda_i)^2 = 1$. `_refs/QubitsAndEntanglement.nb` § *Purity* (line 23965) states $\operatorname{Tr}\rho^2 = \sum p_i^2$ as a definitional line then asserts $\operatorname{Tr}\rho^2 \in [0,1]$ *'by construction'* without deriving the lower bound 1/d; the pure/mixed criterion (line 23987) is stated as a piecewise without a strict-inequality derivation. Lecture therefore **goes beyond `_refs/`** in deriving the 1/d lower bound, but in doing so skips two algebra steps a student needs: (a) the Cauchy-Schwarz invocation — the factor of $d$ enters because Cauchy-Schwarz with $a_i = \lambda_i$ and $b_i = 1$ gives $(\sum\lambda_i)^2 = (\sum a_i b_i)^2 \leq (\sum a_i^2)(\sum b_i^2) = d\sum\lambda_i^2$, i.e. $\sum\lambda_i^2 \geq (\sum\lambda_i)^2/d$; the $d$-factor's provenance is opaque without showing $b_i = 1$; (b) the mixed-state strict-inequality reasoning — $(\sum\lambda_i)^2 = \sum\lambda_i^2 + 2\sum_{i<j}\lambda_i\lambda_j$, so if at least two $\lambda_i > 0$ the cross-sum is strictly positive, giving $\sum\lambda_i^2 < (\sum\lambda_i)^2 = 1$. Not a strict `_refs/`-alignment violation (the reference doesn't derive these at all), but per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § Anti-patterns *'A prefactor appears without a definition step'* applies to the $1/d$ factor. Filed as **T-2026-05-19-DV-15** (**LOW** — judgment call: lecture is already richer than `_refs/`, fix is expand-by-2-lines to make Cauchy-Schwarz and cross-term steps explicit; can be deferred or executed at designer's discretion). **Passing checks (not filed):** (i) **Scope and pedagogical fit** — all three derivations stay within concepts introduced earlier in the cell: (a) Purity Bounds invokes only $\hat\rho = \sum p_i|\psi_i\rangle\langle\psi_i|$ + idempotency, both established in the preceding `### The Density Matrix` and `### Purity` body sections; (b) Purity from Bloch Vector invokes the Bloch form $\hat\rho = \tfrac12(\hat I + \boldsymbol r\cdot\hat{\boldsymbol\sigma})$ established in the immediately-preceding `### The Bloch Ball` `important` box and the Pauli identity $(\boldsymbol r\cdot\hat{\boldsymbol\sigma})^2 = |\boldsymbol r|^2\hat I$ (a standard Ch 1 / Ch 4 result, named in the dropdown lead-in); (c) Von Neumann Equation invokes only $\hat\rho(t) = \hat U(t)\hat\rho(0)\hat U^\dagger(t)$ and $\dot{\hat U} = -(\mathrm{i}/\hbar)\hat H\hat U$, both stated in the immediately-preceding `### Time Evolution` `important` box. No scope-creep into later sections per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § Scope and pedagogical fit. (ii) **One focus per derivation** — each of the three dropdowns isolates a single point per § Scope and pedagogical fit rule 1. (iii) **Notation** — all three derivations use the correct upright `\mathrm{i}`, `\mathrm{d}`; `\hat` on every operator; bold `\boldsymbol` on vectors; `\operatorname{Tr}` (per the NB-46 closure); `\vert` for ket pipes (per the NB-45 closure); `\hat{\sigma}^i` indexed component clarified in the Bloch box (per the NB-51 closure). No notation drift inside the derivations. (iv) **Reverse-engineering check** — none of the three derivations exhibit reverse-engineering from the answer; all proceed forward (premises → algebra → result). (v) **"It can be shown that" check** — no banned hand-waves in any derivation. (vi) **Heisenberg-equation explicit-time-dependence check** — N/A; the Schrödinger-picture density matrix $\hat\rho(t) = \hat U\hat\rho(0)\hat U^\dagger$ has no explicit time-dependence in the rotating-frame sense, so the $\partial_t\hat O$ term is not a concern. **Cursor advances D → A on the next notebook** per `.claude/README.md` § 1.3 (*'When D is done (or skipped with reason), advance to the next ch4/5/6 notebook at phase A'*) — Phase D audit on `6-1-1-mixed-states` is **complete** regardless of whether DV-13 / DV-14 / DV-15 fix tickets have been executed (precedent: 2026-05-19 Phase B → C and Phase C → D advancements on the same notebook with fix tickets still open). **Next notebook** per the `notes_src/_toc.yml` depth-first ordering on Ch 6: `6-1-2-entropy` (second Ch 6 subsection, §6.1.2 — Entropy: von Neumann entropy, Rényi entropies, maximum-entropy principle, thermal density matrices). **Active queue rises from 0 open to 3 open** on `6-1-1-mixed-states` (DV-13, DV-14, DV-15 — see Ticket Queue below; recommended execution order DV-15 (LOW, smallest scope, 1 designer pass) → DV-13 (HIGH, single dropdown expand 2→4 lines) → DV-14 (HIGH, single dropdown expand 1→5 lines)). **Next session**: execute one of the three DV tickets via `derivation-designer` → `notebook-writer` per `.claude/README.md` § 2.2, **or** advance to Phase A audit on `6-1-2-entropy` per the new cursor (manager judgment; precedent allows phased advancement with open fix tickets on the prior notebook). Borderline observation (a) carried forward from the 2026-05-19 Phase B audit's deferred-to-Phase-C list. Defensible reading: the `Both` allowance for `\hat{\boldsymbol{\sigma}}` covers its indexed extension, and `i` running over `{x,y,z}` is a natural symbol for *'the $i$-th component of the Pauli vector'*. Strict-rewrite route would replace with the explicit QI triplet (HW4(a): `$n_X = \langle\hat{X}\rangle = \operatorname{Tr}(\hat{\rho}\hat{X})$, and similarly for $n_Y, n_Z$`; dropdown: `$\operatorname{Tr}(\hat{X}) = \operatorname{Tr}(\hat{Y}) = \operatorname{Tr}(\hat{Z}) = 0$`). (Ch 4–6 Round 1 — Phase B fix ticket **T-2026-05-19-NB-47** executed on `6-1-1-mixed-states` (first Ch 6 subsection, §6.1.1 — Mixed States) 2026-05-19 **complete**: single atomic `text.replace` call on cell 2 of `notes_src/ch6_quantum-foundations/6-1-1-mixed-states.ipynb` converted the side-by-side `\qquad`-separated `$$...$$` block inside the *Example: Superposition vs Mixture* `dropdown example` to a `\begin{split}` chain with `&=` alignment and `\\` line break per `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Display Math (*'Do NOT use `\quad` or `\qquad` to lay out multiple equations side-by-side in one display block. ... When presenting multiple equations/definitions, stack them vertically (one relation per line), using `\begin{split}...\end{split}` where needed.'*). Substitution (cell 2 only, unique anchor): OLD = `$$\n\hat{\rho}_{\text{super}} = \frac{1}{2}\begin{pmatrix} 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 1 \end{pmatrix}, \qquad \hat{\rho}_{\text{mix}} = \frac{1}{2}\begin{pmatrix} 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 \end{pmatrix} = \frac{\hat{I}}{2}\n$$` (count 1 → 0); NEW = `$$\n\begin{split}\n\hat{\rho}_{\text{super}} &= \frac{1}{2}\begin{pmatrix} 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 1 \end{pmatrix}, \\\n\hat{\rho}_{\text{mix}} &= \frac{1}{2}\begin{pmatrix} 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 \end{pmatrix} = \frac{\hat{I}}{2}\n\end{split}\n$$` (count 0 → 1). Style: `&=` alignment markers on both lines align the equals signs, trailing comma on line 1 (preserves the original prose breath between the two parallel definitions), no terminal punctuation on line 2 (matches LD-27 closure precedent on `5-2-3-applications` cell-2 Kubo opening setup); the two density matrices are conceptually a parallel comparison (superposition vs mixture) so the visual grouping is preserved by keeping them inside the same `$$...$$` block via `\begin{split}` rather than splitting into two separate `$$...$$` blocks (route (b) of the NB-47 audit ticket, less compact and slightly disruptive to the parallel-comparison visual). VERIFY-FIRST per-cell counts pre-edit (cells 0 / 1 / 2 / 3): OLD = 0 / 0 / 1 / 0 (unique in cell 2); NEW = 0 / 0 / 0 / 0 (no collisions); `\qquad` = 0 / 0 / 1 / 0 (consistent with the offending block being the sole `\qquad` instance in the notebook per Phase B audit 2026-05-19). Post-edit: cells 0 / 1 / 2 / 3 OLD = 0 / 0 / 0 / 0; NEW = 0 / 0 / 1 / 0; `\qquad` = 0 / 0 / 0 / 0 (all `\qquad` macros eliminated from the notebook). Cells 0 / 1 / 3 byte-identical (per-cell lengths [20, 613, 3661] unchanged pre and post — cell 1 length 613 is the post-NB-45 state from earlier today). Cell-2 length **9767 → 9791 chars** (delta **+24**): added `\begin{split}\n` (14 chars, including the newline before the first stacked equation), `\n\end{split}` (12 chars, including the newline after the last stacked equation), two `&` alignment markers (one each on the two `=` signs, +2 chars total); replaced ` \qquad ` (8 chars: leading space + `\qquad` + trailing space) with ` \\\n` (4 chars: leading space + `\\` line-break macro + newline), net for the separator −4 chars. Total: 14 + 12 + 2 − 4 = +24. Source rebuilt via `text_to_source(...)` per `rules/notebook-editing.md` (Bash + Python `json.load` / `json.dump(..., indent=1, ensure_ascii=False)`; banned `NotebookEdit` / `Edit` / `Write`; **raw-string LaTeX required** per § *LaTeX in JSON — escape character hazards* — both OLD and NEW carry `\hat`, `\rho`, `\frac`, `\begin`, `\end`, `\text` commands and the JSON escape sequences `\r` (inside `\rho`), `\b` (inside `\begin`), `\f` (inside `\frac`), `\t` (inside `\text`), `\h` (in `\hat`, safe-by-letter) require raw-string syntax (`r'...'`); both anchors built with raw-string syntax in the Python source). Round-trip read-back equals in-memory edited string. **Consistency check on the new `\begin{split}` layout**: the `&` characters scope correctly — the `&` inside `\begin{pmatrix} 1 & 1 \end{pmatrix}` (column separator for the matrix environment) is local to `pmatrix`, while the `&` immediately before each `=` is local to the enclosing `split` environment; standard LaTeX / MathJax environment scoping handles this without conflict, and the same pattern appears in recent precedent T-2026-05-12-NB-05 on `4-3-3-quantum-hall-effect` where five separate `\begin{split}` conversions also nested `pmatrix`-like environments without parsing issues. Similarly the `\\` line-break: inside `pmatrix` it separates matrix rows (scoped to the matrix), outside (between the two `split` rows) it separates equation rows (scoped to `split`). The `$$` blank-line spacing check in `validate_project.py` continues to pass — the `$$` delimiters of the converted block retain their blank lines above and below, and the `\begin{split}` / `\end{split}` fences sit on their own lines inside the `$$...$$` block per `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Display Math (*'Do NOT put blank lines inside a `$$...$$` block (equation lines must be contiguous)'* — contiguity preserved: the new layout has no blank lines inside the `$$...$$` block, only `\\` line breaks between split rows). `\\boldsymbol` = 5 (Bloch vector $\\boldsymbol{n}$); `\\vec{` = 0; `\\chi` = 0; `\\cdot` = 1 (between vectors `\\boldsymbol{n}\\cdot\\hat{\\boldsymbol{\\sigma}}`, allowed). **Three Phase B findings — three tickets filed below:** **T-2026-05-19-NB-45** (cell 1 Prompts bare-pipe `\|` → `\\vert` migration on 3 ket sites — strict rule violation per `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Ket/Bra Notation *'Use `\\vert` for `\|` in kets and bras throughout'*; the rest of the notebook uses `\\vert` consistently — cells 2 and 3 carry 54 + 30 = 84 `\\vert` occurrences with zero bare-pipe ket sites; precedent T-2026-05-18-LD-26 on `5-2-3-applications` swept the *Derivation: Kubo formula* dropdown via `\\vert` migration, and earlier T-2026-05-12-NB-06 on `4-3-3-quantum-hall-effect` migrated 14+ kets/bras to `\\vert`; designer route — three atomic `text.replace` calls on cell 1 with unique anchors `\|\\psi\\rangle` / `(\|0\\rangle` / `\| 1\\rangle)` — wait, both `\|0\\rangle` and `\|1\\rangle` appear together inside `(\|0\\rangle + \|1\\rangle)/\\sqrt{2}` — recommended is single `text.replace` on the compound substring `(\|0\\rangle + \|1\\rangle)/\\sqrt{2}` plus one `text.replace` on `\|\\psi\\rangle`, for two atomic edits total). **T-2026-05-19-NB-46** (`\\mathrm{Tr}` → `\\operatorname{Tr}` internal-consistency migration on 11 stray sites across cells 1 / 2 / 3 — not a strict rule violation since `rules/physics-conventions.md` does not explicitly mandate `\\operatorname{Tr}` (the 2026-04-11 archived repo-wide migration was `\\text{Tr}` → `\\operatorname{Tr}` and did not address `\\mathrm{Tr}`), but the in-notebook prevailing convention is unambiguously `\\operatorname{Tr}` — cell 2 lecture body uses it 18× across all six `important` admonitions and the Summary, vs only 3× `\\mathrm{Tr}` confined to the *Poll: Density matrix properties* answer choices (A) / (B) / (C); plus 1× stray in cell 1 Prompts and 7× stray in cell 3 HW1(c) / HW2(a)(b)(c) / HW3 / HW4(a) / HW5(b); designer route — three atomic `text.replace` calls with `replace_all` semantics scoped per cell, one per cell affected: cell 1 (1 site), cell 2 Poll (3 sites — verify all three are inside the Poll admonition before the `replace_all`), cell 3 HW (7 sites)). **T-2026-05-19-NB-47** (cell 2 *Example: Superposition vs Mixture* dropdown — single `$$...$$` block lays out two density-matrix definitions side-by-side via `\\qquad`, violating `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Display Math *'Do NOT use `\\quad` or `\\qquad` to lay out multiple equations side-by-side in one display block. ... When presenting multiple equations/definitions, stack them vertically (one relation per line), using `\\begin{split}...\\end{split}` where needed.'*; identical pattern to recent precedents T-2026-05-18-LD-27 on `5-2-3-applications` cell 2 Kubo opening setup (closed 2026-05-18 via `\\begin{split}` chain conversion), T-2026-05-18-NB-44 on `5-2-2-dyson-series` (closed 2026-05-18 via the same route), and T-2026-05-12-NB-05 on `4-3-3-quantum-hall-effect` (closed 2026-05-12 via five `\\begin{split}` conversions); designer route — single atomic `text.replace` on cell 2 with the unique anchor wrapping the entire `$$...$$` block, converting from `\\qquad`-separated to `\\begin{split}` chain with `&=` alignment and `\\\\` line breaks; the two density matrices are conceptually a parallel comparison (superposition vs mixture) so the visual grouping is preserved by keeping them inside the same `$$...$$` block with `\\begin{split}` — `&=` markers align the equals signs, trailing comma on line 1, no trailing punctuation on line 2 (or trailing period if preferred, matching precedent style on `5-2-3-applications` LD-27 closure)). **Borderline observations — NOT filed as Phase B tickets** (per `.claude/README.md` § 1.2 *Audit vs fix* — Phase B audit produces tickets for clear violations only; borderline-style issues belong to Phase C judgment): **(a)** the Pauli-vector / indexed-component / QI-component triple use across cell 2 and cell 3 — the Bloch ball `important` box defines `\\hat{\\boldsymbol{\\sigma}} \\equiv (\\hat{X}, \\hat{Y}, \\hat{Z})` (an explicit redefinition that maps the canonical `\\hat{\\boldsymbol{\\sigma}}` listed in `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Vectors and Operators *Vectorized Pauli — Both — `\\hat{\\boldsymbol{\\sigma}} = (\\hat{\\sigma}^x, \\hat{\\sigma}^y, \\hat{\\sigma}^z)`* to its QI components), while the *Derivation: Purity from Bloch Vector* dropdown uses indexed `\\hat{\\sigma}^i` (1×) and HW4(a) uses indexed `\\hat{\\sigma}^i` (2×) — the indexed component form `\\hat{\\sigma}^i` (with $i$ running over $x, y, z$) is *canonically* the Spin notation per the rule's *Spin* row but is being read here as the components of the QI-redefined vector. The rule's "Both" allowance for the vector form `\\hat{\\boldsymbol{\\sigma}}` arguably covers the indexed extension `\\hat{\\sigma}^i`; this is a notation-system mixing issue but defensible because the vector form is explicitly redefined in QI components in the Bloch ball box, and the indexed form `\\hat{\\sigma}^i` is genuinely a natural extension. Strict QI-system rewrite would replace `\\hat{\\sigma}^i` with the explicit triplet `\\hat{X}, \\hat{Y}, \\hat{Z}` (in cell 2 dropdown line — replace `\\operatorname{Tr}(\\hat{\\sigma}^i) = 0` with `\\operatorname{Tr}(\\hat{X}) = \\operatorname{Tr}(\\hat{Y}) = \\operatorname{Tr}(\\hat{Z}) = 0`, in HW4(a) — replace `$n^i = \\langle\\hat{\\sigma}^i\\rangle = \\mathrm{Tr}(\\hat{\\rho}\\hat{\\sigma}^i)$` with `$n_X = \\langle\\hat{X}\\rangle = \\operatorname{Tr}(\\hat{\\rho}\\hat{X})$, and similarly for $n_Y$, $n_Z$`). Defer to Phase C lecture-designer / homework-designer judgment if the indexed-vs-component clash impedes student readability. **(b)** subscript-style drift on density matrices with prose labels: cell 2 Example uses `\\hat{\\rho}_{\\text{super}}` (×2) and `\\hat{\\rho}_{\\text{mix}}` (×2) via `\\text{}` (4 sites total), while cell 3 HW6 uses `\\hat{\\rho}_{\\rm mix}` (×1) via the deprecated TeX-2.09 `\\rm` macro (cell-3 pos 2398) — `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Upright Symbols specifies `\\mathrm{i/e/d}` for the imaginary unit / Euler's number / differential, but does not explicitly cover prose subscripts; the project precedent (4-4-1 Phase A read-ahead 2026-05-12, also borderline at Phase B per the session log) flagged similar `\\rm spin` / `\\rm mono` usage. Internal consistency would prefer one style — either `\\mathrm{mix}` (matching `\\mathrm{i/e/d}` upright convention) or `\\text{mix}` (the cell 2 prevailing style) — but no strict rule violation. Defer to Phase C judgment. **(c)** the `\\hat{\\boldsymbol{\\sigma}} \\equiv (\\hat{X}, \\hat{Y}, \\hat{Z})` redefinition in the Bloch ball box conflicts with the canonical `\\hat{\\boldsymbol{\\sigma}} = (\\hat{\\sigma}^x, \\hat{\\sigma}^y, \\hat{\\sigma}^z)` listed in `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Vectors and Operators — but the rule allows the vector form in "both" systems, so the redefinition is rule-defensible. Not a violation. **Phase A read-ahead observations confirmed** (per the 2026-05-19 Phase A audit closure note): (i) cell 1 Prompts bare-pipe ket sites — confirmed at 3, filed as T-2026-05-19-NB-45; (ii) `\\mathrm{Tr}` vs `\\operatorname{Tr}` inconsistency — confirmed at 11 stray vs 18 prevailing, filed as T-2026-05-19-NB-46; (iii) operator hats audit (`\\hat{}` discipline on $\\rho$, $H$, $A$, $U$, $X$, $Y$, $Z$, $\\sigma^{x,y,z}$) — all hats present and correct across all four cells; no bare-operator drift (contra the 2026-04-25 historical audit's ch6 themes for 6-3-* / 6-4-2, this notebook 6-1-1 is clean); (iv) `\\mathrm{i/e/d}` upright-symbol convention — all imaginary units / exponentials / differentials use `\\mathrm{}` correctly (regex `(?<!\\\\mathrm\\{)(?<!\\w)e\\^` returns zero matches); (v) phase symbol convention (`\\Phi` vs `\\phi`) — Ch 4 rule, irrelevant in Ch 6 (no `\\Phi` / `\\phi` use in this notebook); (vi) Spin/QI ket-system mixing within a single equation — no Spin kets `\\uparrow` / `\\downarrow` anywhere in the notebook (per the Phase B count), so the QI-only ket usage is unambiguous; the indexed `\\hat{\\sigma}^i` finding above is the related but separate Pauli-component issue (deferred as borderline per item (a)). **Cursor advances B → C (Style & structure) on `6-1-1-mixed-states`** per `.claude/README.md` § 1.3 (audit-vs-fix split — Phase B audit is **complete** regardless of whether the resulting NB-45 / NB-46 / NB-47 tickets have been executed; precedent: 2026-05-12 Phase B audit on `4-3-3-quantum-hall-effect` advanced B → C with NB-05 / NB-06 still open). (Ch 4–6 Round 1 — Phase D fix ticket **T-2026-05-19-DV-12** executed on `5-2-3-applications` (sixth Ch 5 subsection, third §5.2 subsection — Fermi's golden rule, adiabatic process, Kubo formula) 2026-05-19 **complete**: two atomic `text.replace` calls on cell 2 of `notes_src/ch5_perturbation-theory/5-2-3-applications.ipynb` per route (a) of the DV-12 audit ticket — (i) rewrote the Step 4 narrative shortcut from the invalid operator-style `use $\hat{j}_a^{\mathcal{I}}(\tau)\vert\gamma\rangle = \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}\omega_{\gamma\alpha}\tau}\hat{j}_a\vert\gamma\rangle$ on each $\langle\alpha\vert\cdots\vert\alpha\rangle$ matrix element` to a valid matrix-element identity `use the matrix-element identity $\langle\alpha\vert\hat{j}_a^{\mathcal{I}}(\tau)\vert\gamma\rangle = \mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}\omega_{\gamma\alpha}\tau}\,\langle\alpha\vert\hat{j}_a\vert\gamma\rangle$ on each $\langle\alpha\vert\cdots\vert\alpha\rangle$ matrix element` (preserves the $\omega_{\gamma\alpha}=(E_\gamma-E_\alpha)/\hbar$ convention stated in the immediately-following parenthetical, matches the convention $\omega_{fi}=(E_f-E_i)/\hbar$ used elsewhere in this notebook including Eq. {eq}`eq-5-2-3-P1st`, and matches `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` Linear Response Theory lines 3622–3637); (ii) flipped the two exponential signs in the immediately-following displayed commutator expansion: OLD `j^a_{\alpha\gamma}j^b_{\gamma\alpha}\,\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}\omega_{\gamma\alpha}\tau} - j^b_{\alpha\gamma}j^a_{\gamma\alpha}\,\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}\omega_{\gamma\alpha}\tau}` → NEW `j^a_{\alpha\gamma}j^b_{\gamma\alpha}\,\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}\omega_{\gamma\alpha}\tau} - j^b_{\alpha\gamma}j^a_{\gamma\alpha}\,\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}\omega_{\gamma\alpha}\tau}`. Against `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Root causes / Misleading notation choices* + § *Root causes / Wrong notation (index/sign errors)* + § *Mandatory procedure / Student-confusion test* and § Anti-patterns row *'Using an operator form that looks like a typo for a simpler one'*. VERIFY-FIRST per-cell counts pre-edit across cells 0/1/2/3: OLD1 = [0, 0, 1, 0] (unique in cell 2); OLD2 = [0, 0, 1, 0] (unique in cell 2); NEW1 / NEW2 counts [0, 0, 0, 0] (no collisions). Post-edit: OLD1 / OLD2 = 0 across all four cells; NEW1 / NEW2 = 1 in cell 2 / 0 elsewhere. Cell-2 length **19543 → 19610 chars** (delta **+67**): Substitution (i) rewrote the shortcut as a matrix-element identity, swapping the operator-style equality (LHS `\hat{j}_a^{\mathcal{I}}(\tau)\vert\gamma\rangle`) for the matrix-element identity (LHS `\langle\alpha\vert\hat{j}_a^{\mathcal{I}}(\tau)\vert\gamma\rangle`, RHS `\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}\omega_{\gamma\alpha}\tau}\,\langle\alpha\vert\hat{j}_a\vert\gamma\rangle`) and adding the natural-language preamble *'use the matrix-element identity'*; substitution (ii) flipped two exponent signs (+1 char each = +2 chars). Cells 0 / 1 / 3 byte-identical (per-cell lengths [20, 880, 3986] unchanged pre and post). **Consistency check**: with NEW1 the matrix element $\langle\alpha\vert\hat{j}_a^{\mathcal{I}}(\tau)\vert\gamma\rangle = \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}E_\alpha\tau/\hbar}\,\langle\alpha\vert\hat{j}_a\vert\gamma\rangle\,\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}E_\gamma\tau/\hbar} = \mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}\omega_{\gamma\alpha}\tau}\,j^a_{\alpha\gamma}$ is now correct under the stated $\omega_{\gamma\alpha}=(E_\gamma-E_\alpha)/\hbar$; substituting NEW1 into the expansion of $\sum_{\alpha\in\mathrm{occ}}\langle\alpha\vert[\hat{j}_a^{\mathcal{I}}(\tau),\hat{j}_b^{\mathcal{I}}(0)]\vert\alpha\rangle$ yields exactly NEW2 line by line (first term: insert $\sum_\gamma\vert\gamma\rangle\langle\gamma\vert$ after $\hat{j}_a^{\mathcal{I}}(\tau)$ giving $\langle\alpha\vert\hat{j}_a^{\mathcal{I}}(\tau)\vert\gamma\rangle\langle\gamma\vert\hat{j}_b^{\mathcal{I}}(0)\vert\alpha\rangle = \mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}\omega_{\gamma\alpha}\tau}\,j^a_{\alpha\gamma}\,j^b_{\gamma\alpha}$; second term by index swap and overall sign yields $-\mathrm{e}^{+\mathrm{i}\omega_{\gamma\alpha}\tau}\,j^b_{\alpha\gamma}\,j^a_{\gamma\alpha}$). No physics-content change downstream — the antisymmetric (Hall) part of $\sigma_{ab}$ involves a dummy-index swap $\alpha\leftrightarrow\gamma$, so the boxed Kubo formula in `eq-kubo-hall-523` was independently correct even pre-edit (the dummy swap inside the antisymmetric combination cancels the wrong-signed exponential); with the flips applied consistently the chain is now correct line-by-line and the final boxed result is preserved. The subsequent $\tau$-integral expression `$\int_0^\infty\!\mathrm{d}\tau\,\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}(\omega+\mathrm{i}0^+ \pm\omega_{\gamma\alpha})\tau} = \mathrm{i}/(\omega+\mathrm{i}0^+\pm\omega_{\gamma\alpha})$` continues to apply (the `±` notation generically lists both sign cases — the assignment per term swaps between the two cases under our flip, leaving the displayed identity intact). (Ch 4–6 Round 1 — Phase B (Notation) audit on `5-2-3-applications` (sixth Ch 5 subsection, third §5.2 subsection — Fermi's golden rule, adiabatic process, Kubo formula) 2026-05-18 **complete**: scoped read-only notation sweep of cells 0–3 of `notes_src/ch5_perturbation-theory/5-2-3-applications.ipynb` against `rules/physics-conventions.md` per `.claude/skills/maintenance-phases/SKILL.md` § B and `.claude/README.md` § 1.2 *Audit vs fix*. **Sweep coverage**: § Upright Symbols (ISO 80000-2 — `\mathrm{i}` / `\mathrm{e}` / `\mathrm{d}`), § Vectors and Operators (`\boldsymbol{}` for vectors, `\hat{}` for operators, `\nabla` plain), § Phase Symbols (`\Phi` for flux / Berry phase, lowercase `\phi` for scalar electric potential, curly `\varphi` for azimuth), § Dot Product (`\cdot` between vectors only), § Eigenstate Notation, § Notation Consistency: Spin vs QI (operators must match ket system within an equation/problem), § Display Math (blank lines around `$$`, no `align` / `aligned`, `\begin{split}` for multi-line, no `\quad` / `\qquad` side-by-side), § Ket/Bra Notation and Pipe Characters (`\vert` for `|` in kets/bras throughout; never glue to following letter/digit), § Inline vs Display. **Finding 1 — Cell 3 HW Problem 8 *Minimal Kubo exercise* mixes Spin Pauli operators with QI computational kets**: NEEDS WORK per `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Notation Consistency: Spin vs QI (*'If kets use $\vert 0\rangle, \vert 1\rangle$, operators must be $\hat{X}, \hat{Y}, \hat{Z}$. If kets use $\vert\uparrow\rangle, \vert\downarrow\rangle$, operators must be $\hat{\sigma}^x, \hat{\sigma}^y, \hat{\sigma}^z$.'*). HW8 setup line writes `\hat{H}_0=-\frac{1}{2}\hbar\omega_0\hat{\sigma}^z` (Spin Pauli operator) with occupied $\vert 0\rangle$ and empty $\vert 1\rangle$ (QI computational kets) and current operators $\hat{j}_x=\hat{\sigma}^x$, $\hat{j}_y=\hat{\sigma}^y$ (Spin Pauli); part (c) writes `\hat{j}_y\to\hat{\sigma}^x` (Spin Pauli). Total Spin Pauli operator occurrences in cell 3: $\hat{\sigma}^z \times 1$ + $\hat{\sigma}^x \times 2$ + $\hat{\sigma}^y \times 1$ = **4** sites, all inside HW Problem 8. Total QI kets in cell 3: $\vert 0\rangle \times 3$ + $\vert 1\rangle \times 7$ = **10**, used throughout HW8 parts (a)/(b)/(c). HW7 *Three-level Raman (long-time limit)* in the same cell uses numeric basis kets $\vert 1\rangle, \vert 2\rangle, \vert 3\rangle$ for a three-level Hilbert space — outside the strict Spin vs QI two-level dichotomy (precedent: LD-22 closure note on `5-2-2-dyson-series` flagged adjacent HW8 with $\vert 1\rangle, \vert 2\rangle, \vert 3\rangle$ as unaffected by the rule), so HW7 is not a violation. Cells 0/1/2 carry no Pauli operators (verified: $\hat{\sigma}$ count = 0, $\hat{X}/\hat{Y}/\hat{Z}$ count = 0) — issue scoped to cell 3 HW8 only. Filed as **T-2026-05-18-LD-25** with route (i) **QI uniformly** recommended (four atomic `text.replace` calls: `\hat{\sigma}^z` → `\hat{Z}` ×1, `\hat{\sigma}^x` → `\hat{X}` ×2, `\hat{\sigma}^y` → `\hat{Y}` ×1) — matches the QI computational kets already in place and follows the LD-22 precedent on `5-2-2-dyson-series` HW7 (same Spin/QI mixing pattern, route (i) chosen and closed 2026-05-18). Route (ii) Spin uniformly would require rewriting all 10 QI kets to $\vert\uparrow\rangle, \vert\downarrow\rangle$ — disproportionate work and conflicts with the qubit / Hall-conductivity framing of the problem (occupied/empty band states are more naturally labeled $\vert 0\rangle, \vert 1\rangle$). **Finding 2 — Cell 2 *Derivation: Kubo formula* dropdown uses bare `|` for kets/bras instead of `\vert`**: NEEDS WORK per `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Ket/Bra Notation and Pipe Characters (*'Use `\vert` for `|` in kets and bras throughout: `$\vert\psi\rangle$`, `$\langle\phi\vert$`, `$\vert 0\rangle$`.'*). The Kubo derivation `:::{admonition} Derivation: Kubo formula :class: dropdown information` block (cell 2 Steps 1–5) contains **18 bare-pipe ket sites** (`|\alpha\rangle`, `|\gamma\rangle`, `|\beta\rangle`-class) and **15 bare-pipe bra sites** (`\langle\alpha|`, `\langle\gamma|`-class) — all concentrated inside this single dropdown. The rest of cell 2 (Overview, Transition Probability, Fermi's Golden Rule, Adiabatic Process, the boxed Kubo `important` admonition, Summary, See Also) uses `\vert` consistently — cell 2 totals: 29 `\vert...\rangle`, 12 `\langle...\vert`. The bare-pipe usage is a **consistency violation** — the rule says *'throughout'* and the rest of the notebook (including the very next `important` admonition that boxes the Kubo formula result) uses `\vert`. Filed as **T-2026-05-18-LD-26** with a multi-site mechanical sweep on cell 2 (within the Kubo derivation dropdown only): bare `|` → `\vert` for every ket/bra delimiter site (Steps 1–5 of the derivation). Cells 0/1/3 use `\vert` consistently (0 bare-pipe ket/bra sites). **Cross-checks performed (no findings to file)**: § Upright Symbols — `\mathrm{i}` / `\mathrm{e}` / `\mathrm{d}` used consistently in all four cells (no bare `i` / `e` / `d` in math contexts: spot-check on integrand differentials, exponentials, and the imaginary unit in Bohr phases and the Kubo $\mathrm{i}0^+$ regulator — all upright); § Vectors — $\boldsymbol{E}$, $\boldsymbol{A}$, $\boldsymbol{j}$, $\hat{\boldsymbol{j}}$ all use `\boldsymbol{}` per § Vectors and Operators (no `\vec`); § Operators — $\hat{H}$, $\hat{H}_0$, $\hat{V}$, $\hat{G}$, $\hat{G}_0$, $\hat{U}_{\mathcal{I}}$, $\hat{j}_a$, $\hat{j}_a^{\mathcal{I}}$ all hatted with brace-wrapped arguments per § Vectors and Operators; no bare `H`, `V`, `G`, `U`, `j` in operator contexts; § Phase Symbols — no `\Phi`, `\phi`, `\varphi` used (no flux / scalar potential / azimuthal angles in this lesson — Ch 5 §5.2.3 is independent of the Ch 4 phase / gauge conventions); § Dot Product — `\cdot` used only between vectors (`\hat{\boldsymbol{j}}\cdot\delta\boldsymbol{A}` in Eq. {eq}`eq-5-2-3-kubo-perturbation` — single instance, valid); no bare-scalar `\cdot` sites; § Display Math — all `$$` blocks have blank lines above/below (Phase A check confirmed clean), no `align` / `aligned` / `align*` environments, no `\quad` / `\qquad` side-by-side display layouts that pair independent equations (one `\qquad` appears in cell 2 inside the Kubo § opening setup block separating the three EM-field declarations $\boldsymbol{E}(t)$ / $\delta\boldsymbol{A}(t)$ / $\boldsymbol{E}(t)=-\partial_t\delta\boldsymbol{A}(t)$ — this is the same restate-three-relations-side-by-side pattern previously caught by T-2026-05-18-NB-44 on `5-2-2-dyson-series` and converted to `\begin{split}` chains; **flag here for follow-up but filed for Phase C lecture-designer judgment rather than mechanical Phase B fix** because the three relations are not strictly multi-line equation alignment — they are three separate defining equations, and the choice between (a) `\qquad` side-by-side, (b) `\begin{split}` chain, (c) three separate `$$...$$` blocks is a style judgment under `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Display Math *'When presenting multiple equations/definitions, stack them vertically (one relation per line), using `\begin{split}...\end{split}` where needed'*; deferred to Phase C); § Inline vs Display — `$...$` used for inline math, `$$...$$` reserved for displayed results, and key results are boxed in `important` admonitions per `rules/content-style.md`; § Eigenstate Notation — no $\vert+\rangle$ / $\vert-\rangle$ / $\vert\uparrow\rangle$ / $\vert\downarrow\rangle$ / $\vert\mathrm{i}\rangle$ Pauli-eigenstate kets used (the lesson uses generic eigenstate labels $\vert i\rangle, \vert f\rangle, \vert\alpha\rangle, \vert\beta\rangle, \vert\gamma\rangle, \vert m\rangle$ for arbitrary $\hat{H}_0$ eigenbases, and QI computational kets $\vert 0\rangle, \vert 1\rangle$ only in HW8 — see Finding 1). **Cursor stays at Round 1 / Phase B (Notation) / `5-2-3-applications`** per `.claude/README.md` § 1.2 + § 1.3 — Phase B audit produces tickets; (Ch 4–6 Round 1 — Phase D (Derivation quality) audit on `5-2-2-dyson-series` (fifth Ch 5 subsection, second §5.2 subsection — Volterra integral form / Dyson series / bare and dressed Green's functions / Feynman-diagram bookkeeping) 2026-05-18 **complete**: scoped read-only derivation-quality sweep of cell 2 of `notes_src/ch5_perturbation-theory/5-2-2-dyson-series.ipynb` against `rules/derivation-quality.md` and `_refs/PerturbationTheory.md` per `.claude/skills/maintenance-phases/SKILL.md` § Phase D and `.claude/skills/derivation-designer/SKILL.md`. **`_refs/` consultation:** `_refs/PerturbationTheory.md` lines 1798–1942 (§ *Dyson Series* and § *Green's Function*) read first per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Mandatory procedure* item 1; the reference uses natural units ($\hbar = 1$) while the notebook restores explicit $\hbar$ — natural-unit↔SI translation accounts for the $-\mathrm{i}/\hbar$ vs $-i$ prefactors and does **not** signal a step gap. **Two derivation dropdowns audited** (both `:::{admonition} ... :class: dropdown information`): (1) *Derivation: from operator EOM to Volterra equation* (cell 2 § Dyson Series); (2) *Derivation: from $\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}$ strings to $\hat{G}_0$ links* (cell 2 § Green's Function). **Plus**: one in-body chain (*Iteration (blackboard style)* in § Dyson Series, two displayed equations performing one explicit substitution and one once-expanded form) and one `:::{admonition} ... :class: dropdown note` block (*Recursive form*, a result statement with one displayed equation — no derivation chain to audit). **Finding 1 — Dropdown 1 (operator EOM → Volterra): PASS** per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Mandatory detail level* and § *Anti-patterns*. The dropdown shows one displayed equation (the integrated EOM line $\mathrm{i}\hbar(\hat{U}_{\mathcal{I}}(t)-\hat{U}_{\mathcal{I}}(0)) = \int_0^t\mathrm{d}t'\,\mathrm{i}\hbar\partial_{t'}\hat{U}_{\mathcal{I}}(t') = \int_0^t\mathrm{d}t'\,\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}(t')\hat{U}_{\mathcal{I}}(t')$) and verbally describes the residual step (*Use $\hat{U}_{\mathcal{I}}(0)=\hat{I}$ and divide by $\mathrm{i}\hbar$ to obtain Eq. (volterra)*). `_refs/` lines 1800–1818 show two displayed equations for the same derivation (integrated form, then Volterra after using IC and dividing). The lecture's compression is acceptable because (a) the final Volterra form is shown as the boxed Eq. {eq}`eq-5-2-2-volterra` immediately above the dropdown, (b) the residual step (apply IC, divide by $\mathrm{i}\hbar$) is a mechanical one-substitution / one-rescaling combination that does not require its own displayed line, and (c) the dropdown still meets § *Mandatory detail level*'s floor for trivial-algebra cases (*Short derivations are fine when the result genuinely follows in 2–3 steps*). No ticket. **Finding 2 — Dropdown 2 ($\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}$ strings → $\hat{G}_0$ links): NEEDS WORK** per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Mandatory detail level* (*each commutator evaluation or algebraic derivation must contain at least as many displayed intermediate lines as the corresponding passage in `_refs/`*) and § *Root causes* item 1 (*Skipped intermediate steps*). The dropdown has three numbered steps with four displayed equations: (i) vertex expansion $\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}(t_j)=\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}(t_j)\hat{V}(t_j)\hat{U}_0(t_j)$; (ii) adjacent-pair identification $\hat{U}_0(t_{j+1})\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}(t_j)=\hat{G}_0(t_{j+1},t_j)$ followed immediately by the **partially-collapsed** chain $\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}(t_k)\hat{V}(t_k)\hat{G}_0(t_k,t_{k-1})\hat{V}(t_{k-1})\cdots\hat{G}_0(t_2,t_1)\hat{V}(t_1)\hat{U}_0(t_1)$; (iii) left-multiply by $\hat{U}_0(t)$ and identify the two end pieces. `_refs/` lines 1858–1890 show **two** displayed product chains: first the **fully-expanded** chain $\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}(t_k)\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}(t_{k-1})\cdots\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}(t_1) = \hat{U}_0^{\dagger}(t_k)\hat{V}(t_k)\hat{U}_0(t_k)\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}(t_{k-1})\hat{V}(t_{k-1})\hat{U}_0(t_{k-1})\cdots\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}(t_1)\hat{V}(t_1)\hat{U}_0(t_1)$ (every adjacent $\hat{U}_0\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}$ pair visible), then the $\hat{G}_0$-collapsed chain. The lecture **skips the fully-expanded chain** — it shows Step (i) for a single factor only, declares the generic adjacent-pair identity in Step (ii), and jumps straight to the partially-collapsed result, asking the student to mentally apply Step (i) to all $k$ factors and observe how pairs match up. This is the *Show every commutator / identity evaluation* rule's exact failure mode applied to operator-product chains: the pair-identification *mechanism* (where the $\hat{U}_0(t_{j+1})\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}(t_j)$ pairs sit inside the expanded product) is asserted rather than shown. Filed as **T-2026-05-18-DV-10** with concrete fix routes (a) insert the fully-expanded chain between Step (i) and Step (ii) as a new displayed equation, matching `_refs/`'s structure line for line; or (b) merge the new line into Step (i) so Step (i) shows both the per-vertex expansion and the full-product version side-by-side; route (a) is the minimum-surgery recommendation. **Finding 3 — In-body *Iteration (blackboard style)* chain: PASS** per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Mandatory detail level* and § *Scope and pedagogical fit*. The block shows one explicit substitution under the integral followed by the once-expanded form, exactly mirroring `_refs/` lines 1822–1832 (the reference also shows one substitution followed by the once-expanded form before jumping to the general Dyson series). The integration variables use $(t_1, t_2)$ with $t_2 \le t_1$ (i.e. $t_1$ outermost / later) while the final Dyson series Eq. {eq}`eq-5-2-2-dyson-UI` uses $(t_1, \ldots, t_k)$ with $t_1 \le \cdots \le t_k$ (i.e. $t_k$ outermost / later) — a dummy-variable renaming that flips at the *continuing to all orders* step. This is conventional in time-dependent perturbation theory texts and matches `_refs/` (the reference uses $(t', t'')$ in the iteration and $(t_1, \ldots, t_k)$ in the Dyson series, equivalent dummy renaming). HW Problem 2 explicitly cues the student to *Pay attention to the ordering of the two integration variables and the signs*. Not a derivation gap. **Finding 4 — *Recursive form* dropdown: PASS** per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Scope and pedagogical fit*. The block (`:::{admonition} Recursive form :class: dropdown note`) states the closed recursion $\hat{G}(t,t_0) = \hat{G}_0(t,t_0) - (\mathrm{i}/\hbar)\int_{t_0}^t\mathrm{d}t_1\,\hat{G}_0(t,t_1)\hat{V}(t_1)\hat{G}(t_1,t_0)$ and notes it can be iterated to regenerate the Dyson series. The block is a `note`-class admonition (not a `dropdown information` derivation) and its verification is handed off to HW Problem 6 (*Show that Eq. (dyson-G) is equivalent to the closed recursion … Iterate this recursion once and verify the k=1 and k=2 terms*). Result statement + homework verification is the conventional pattern in this notebook and does not require an in-line derivation. Not a derivation gap. **Cross-cell symbol consistency sweep** per `rules/notebook-editing.md` § *Notation consistency sweep* and `rules/physics-conventions.md` § *Vectors and Operators*: $\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}$ / $\hat{U}_{\mathcal{I}}$ (interaction picture, calligraphic $\mathcal{I}$ subscript), $\hat{V}$ (Schrödinger-picture perturbation), $\hat{U}$ / $\hat{U}_0$ (full / free propagators), $\hat{G}_0$ / $\hat{G}$ (bare / dressed Green's functions) — all carry hats, all subscripts consistent within the notebook, no drift between cells 0/1/2/3 (verified via the post-T-2026-05-18-NB-45 closure inventory: zero no-brace `\hat <letter>` sites and zero no-brace `\mathrm i` sites in cell 2). The $\mathcal{T}$ time-ordering operator (kept conventional calligraphic, not $\hat{T}$) and the eq labels `eq-5-2-2-volterra` / `eq-5-2-2-dyson-UI` / `eq-5-2-2-G0` / `eq-5-2-2-dyson-G0` / `eq-5-2-2-dyson-G` are all used consistently. No notation findings to file beyond DV-10. **Physics correctness sweep** per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *After writing* item 8 (*Structural diff against `_refs/`*): Volterra Eq. {eq}`eq-5-2-2-volterra`, iteration once, Dyson series Eq. {eq}`eq-5-2-2-dyson-UI`, $\mathcal{T}\exp$ shorthand, bare propagator definition Eq. {eq}`eq-5-2-2-G0`, composition rule, Schrödinger-picture Dyson series Eq. {eq}`eq-5-2-2-dyson-G0`, dressed propagator definition, dressed Dyson series Eq. {eq}`eq-5-2-2-dyson-G`, recursive form, Feynman rules table, mirror-rule caveat — all algebraically correct against `_refs/` lines 1798–2042. Prefactor sign $(-\mathrm{i}/\hbar)^k$ consistent at every level. Time-ordering simplex $0 \le t_1 \le \cdots \le t_k \le t$ consistent across body / iteration / caution box / final Dyson series. the simplex domain enforces causal composition and is compactly written as the time-ordered exponential $\mathcal{T}\exp(\cdots)$.` The new bullet 2 also folds in the **simplex domain / $\mathcal{T}\exp$ shorthand** — a separable conceptual handle (time-ordered exponential as a compact representation of the simplex-integral series) that appears in the body's *Why the time ordering matters* `caution` admonition and that students will reuse downstream in 5.2.3 and Ch 7-style scattering treatments. (Ch 4–6 Round 1 — Phase D fix ticket **T-2026-05-17-DD-03** executed on `5-2-1-interaction-picture` (fourth Ch 5 subsection, first §5.2 subsection) 2026-05-18 **complete**: single atomic `text.replace` on cell 2 of `notes_src/ch5_perturbation-theory/5-2-1-interaction-picture.ipynb` — *Derivation: matrix elements and Bohr frequencies* (`dropdown information`) middle `$$...$$` block converted to a `\begin{split}...\end{split}` chain that exposes the previously-collapsed eigenstate-action intermediate per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Mandatory procedure* steps 4 and 6 (*"Show every commutator / identity evaluation"* / *"State building-block results first"*) and § *Mandatory detail level: match `_refs/`* (*"slicing a 2-line jump into its 4 underlying lines"*). Pre-edit chain (2 displayed equalities, 2-line jump): `\langle m\vert\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}\vert n\rangle = \langle m\vert\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{V}\hat{U}_0\vert n\rangle = \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}(E_m-E_n)t/\hbar}V_{mn}(t)` — skipped the explicit eigenstate-action step that lets $\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}$ act on $\langle m\vert$ from the right and $\hat{U}_0$ act on $\vert n\rangle$ from the left via the spectral forms already stated in the immediately-preceding `split` block of the same dropdown. Post-edit chain (3-equality `\begin{split}` block): `\langle m\vert\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}(t)\vert n\rangle &= \langle m\vert\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}(t)\hat{V}(t)\hat{U}_0(t)\vert n\rangle\\ &= \mathrm{e}^{+\mathrm{i}E_m t/\hbar}\,\langle m\vert\hat{V}(t)\vert n\rangle\,\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}E_n t/\hbar}\\ &= \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}(E_m-E_n)t/\hbar}\,V_{mn}(t)` — the inserted middle line makes the eigenstate action $\langle m\vert\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}(t) = \mathrm{e}^{+\mathrm{i}E_m t/\hbar}\langle m\vert$ and $\hat{U}_0(t)\vert n\rangle = \mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}E_n t/\hbar}\vert n\rangle$ (drawn from the dropdown's opening spectral-forms `split` block) explicit — total displayed equality count 2 → 3, matching the Phase D audit ticket inventory and the `_refs/PerturbationTheory.md` line 1700–1703 closed-form for $V_{\mathcal{I}}(t)$. Notation discipline preserved per `rules/physics-conventions.md`: upright $\mathrm{e}$ / $\mathrm{i}$ (§ Upright Symbols), $\hbar$ retained explicitly, `\hat{V}(t)` carries hat (§ Vectors and Operators — middle line uses the operator $\hat{V}(t)$ not the scalar $V_{mn}(t)$), $\vert m\rangle$ / $\vert n\rangle$ kets with space between `\vert` and following letter (§ Ket/Bra Notation — no glued `\vert`, regex `\\vert[A-Za-z0-9]` returns `[]` for this block), `\begin{split}...\end{split}` for multiline equation (§ Display Math — banned `\quad` / `\qquad` side-by-side layout not used; one relation per line with `&=` alignment), surrounding blank lines around `$$` preserved. VERIFY-FIRST anchor confirmed unique in cell 2 (count = 1) and absent from cells 0 / 1 / 3 (count = 0 each); post-edit OLD count = 0, NEW count = 1. (Ch 4–6 Round 1 — Phase C fix ticket **T-2026-05-17-HD-04** executed on `5-2-1-interaction-picture` (fourth Ch 5 subsection, first §5.2 subsection) 2026-05-17 **complete**: single atomic `text.replace` on cell 3 of `notes_src/ch5_perturbation-theory/5-2-1-interaction-picture.ipynb` appended **three new homework problems** after existing P3 *Choosing a picture* to bring the homework count from **3 → 6** (within `rules/content-style.md` § Homework Design *"5–10 problems per subsection"*) and to introduce the missing transfer / application / misconception layers per § Homework Design *"Difficulty gradient: first 2–3 problems should directly exercise lecture content (easy, near-lecture); middle problems transfer or compare; last 1–2 can be hard/far-reaching"*. **New problems** drafted from the audit's candidate menu (designer-judgment choices from (a)–(f) in the open ticket): **P4 *Operator-picture transformation*** — derive the Heisenberg-like operator EOM $\mathrm{i}\hbar\,\partial_t\hat{O}_{\mathcal{I}}=[\hat{O}_{\mathcal{I}},\hat{H}_0]+\mathrm{i}\hbar(\partial_t\hat{O})_{\mathcal{I}}$ via product rule on $\hat{O}_{\mathcal{I}}=\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{O}\hat{U}_0$ (sub-part **(a)**) and contrast with the Heisenberg picture highlighting that the interaction picture splits the dynamics — $\hat{H}_0$ on operators (via the $\hat{O}_{\mathcal{I}}$ definition) and $\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}$ on states (via Eq. `eq-5-2-1-ip-state-eom`) — whereas the Heisenberg picture absorbs the full $\hat{H}$ into the operators with states fixed (sub-part **(b)**); transfer / comparison layer per § Homework Design AI-era criteria. **P5 *Two-level system under monochromatic drive*** — concrete Rabi application on $\hat{H}_0=\tfrac{\hbar\omega_0}{2}\hat{\sigma}^z$ with $\hat{V}(t)=\hbar\Omega\cos(\omega t)\,\hat{\sigma}^x$: rewrite $\hat{V}(t)$ in the $\{\vert\uparrow\rangle,\vert\downarrow\rangle\}$ eigenbasis and identify $\omega_{\uparrow\downarrow}=\omega_0$ (sub-part **(a)**); compute $\langle\uparrow\vert\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}(t)\vert\downarrow\rangle$ and show the $\omega_0\pm\omega$ split (sub-part **(b)**); state the rotating-wave resonance $\omega=\omega_0$ and write the kept matrix element (sub-part **(c)**); application layer per § Homework Design. **P6 *Misconception check*** — *"In the interaction picture, the perturbation $\hat{V}$ has been absorbed into the change of frame and no longer affects the dynamics."* — assess and explain in 2–3 sentences how $\hat{V}$ survives as $\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}$ (with Bohr-frequency phase rotations) while only the static $\hat{H}_0$ background is removed by the comoving frame; misconception-testing layer per § Homework Design and `rules/teaching-philosophy.md` § Course strategy (AI-era worldview / taste cultivation). Notation discipline: P5 uses spin kets $\vert\uparrow\rangle,\vert\downarrow\rangle$ paired with $\hat{\sigma}^x/\hat{\sigma}^z$ operators per `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Notation Consistency: Spin vs QI (do not mix QI $\hat{X}/\hat{Z}$ with $\vert\uparrow\rangle$); all operators carry `\hat{}`; `\mathrm{i}` upright; `\vert ... \rangle` ket notation with space separating $\vert$ from following letters (no glued $\vert$ per `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Ket/Bra Notation, verified via regex `\\vert[A-Za-z0-9]` → `[]`); `(a) / (b) / (c)` sub-parts on own lines with blank lines around them; `$$...$$` display blocks have blank lines above and below; bold-title spans `**N. Title.**` contain only ASCII words / hyphens / periods (no LaTeX inside the bold), with all math relegated to the task text after the closing `**` per `rules/content-style.md` § Problem format. no bare `### Application:` headings; no references to `.claude/` / `_refs/` / `progress.md` / `feedback.md` / `CLAUDE.md` (per `rules/myst-references.md` § Student-facing scope). `\vert` usage clean — cell 2 carries 49 `\vert` markers with **0** bare-pipe `|` sites in math and **0** glued-`\vert` sites (regex `\\vert[A-Za-z0-9]` matches none); cell 3 carries 10 `\vert` markers with the same 0 / 0 result, so the bare-pipe issue that was filed against `5-2-time-dependent-perturbation-theory` (T-2026-05-17-NB-37) does **not** recur here. **Phase A audit closes clean — 0 new tickets filed.** Cursor advances A → B (Notation) on the same notebook per `.claude/README.md` § 1.2 + § 1.4 (phase-audit pass advances the cursor). **Phase B preview** (for next-session audit, not actioned this run): potential notation drift candidates surfaced during the structural read — (i) Cell 3 HW Problem 2 statement uses bare `V_{\mathcal{I}}(t)` (no `\hat`) in *"the matrix elements of $V_{\mathcal{I}}(t)$ are $\langle m\vert V_{\mathcal{I}}(t)\vert n\rangle = V_{mn}(t)\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}\omega_{mn}t}$"* — the symbol $V_{\mathcal{I}}(t)$ refers to the interaction-picture **operator** (whose matrix elements are being computed) and should be `\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}(t)` per `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Vectors and Operators (the trailing $V_{mn}(t)$ and bra-ket $\langle m\vert\cdots\vert n\rangle$ matrix elements are scalars and correctly **lack** the hat); 2 sites in cell 3; (ii) Cell 2 display block at ~offset 5749 uses `\qquad` as a separator between two parallel propagator definitions `\hat{U}_0(t)=\sum_n\vert n\rangle\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}E_n t/\hbar}\langle n\vert,\qquad\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}(t)=\sum_m\vert m\rangle\mathrm{e}^{+\mathrm{i}E_m t/\hbar}\langle m\vert.` — this is a clear side-by-side parallel-relation site that should be split into two `$$...$$` blocks per `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Display Math (*"Do NOT use `\quad` or `\qquad` to lay out multiple equations side-by-side in one display block"*), matching the LD-19 fix pattern already applied to `5-1-3-degenerate-perturbation-theory`; (iii) Three other cell-2 `\qquad` sites (`\hat{U}(0)=\hat{I}` initial-condition appendage, `\hat{U}_{\mathcal{I}}(0)=\hat{I}` initial-condition appendage, and `\omega_{mn}=(E_m-E_n)/\hbar` variable-definition qualifier) are **borderline-not-in-scope** per the same audit-vs-fix discipline applied on `5-1-3-degenerate-perturbation-theory` (initial conditions and quantifier domains are equation **appendages**, not parallel relations); left for designer judgment at Phase B fix time; (iv) Two `\quad\Rightarrow\quad` sites inside a `\begin{split}...\end{split}` environment in cell 2 (~offset 4061, 4077) are logical-implication arrows connecting consecutive lines of an aligned derivation, **not** side-by-side equation layout — out-of-scope for the side-by-side rule. **Phase C preview** (for the eventual Phase C audit, not actioned): heading-case inconsistency in cell 2 — `### Schrödinger picture` (lowercase *picture*) vs `### Interaction Picture` (title-cased) — minor style drift, will be evaluated by the Phase C audit against the `### Problem Setup` / `### Summary` precedent in the same notebook. Verification of these candidates against the full notation / style rules is the Phase B / C audits' job (not Phase A); they are noted here so the next session has the inventory pre-located. No notebook files modified this session. No tickets opened. Reader: manager (orchestrator) — no designer / writer invoked because the Phase A audit found no findings to file.)

The original two-line claim ($|\pm1\rangle' = |\pm1\rangle + (\lambda/\sqrt 2)|0\rangle$ and $|0\rangle' = |0\rangle - (\lambda/\sqrt 2)(|+1\rangle + |-1\rangle)$) is now bracketed by intermediate substitution lines: (1) prose connector — *"For $|\pm1\rangle$ (in the $E_1$ manifold), the only state outside the manifold is $|0\rangle$ with $E_1-E_0=1$"* — followed by the two-step display $|\pm1\rangle' = |\pm1\rangle + \lambda |0\rangle V_{0,\pm1}/(E_1-E_0) + O(\lambda^2) = |\pm1\rangle + (\lambda/\sqrt 2)|0\rangle + O(\lambda^2)$; (2) prose connector — *"For $|0\rangle$ (the $E_0$ singlet), the sum over $m\neq 0$ runs over $\beta\in\{+1,-1\}$ with $E_0-E_1=-1$"* — followed by the two-step display $|0\rangle' = |0\rangle + \lambda |+1\rangle V_{+1,0}/(E_0-E_1) + \lambda |-1\rangle V_{-1,0}/(E_0-E_1) + O(\lambda^2) = |0\rangle - (\lambda/\sqrt 2)(|+1\rangle + |-1\rangle) + O(\lambda^2)$. Both displays now match `_refs/PerturbationTheory.md` lines 1386–1394 (the reference's two-step `V_{m,n}/(E_n-E_m)` chain followed by the numerical collapse) line for line. Compact $V_{m,n}$ index notation in the new lines matches the rest of Step 1 (already-closed T-2026-05-17-DV-19 entry-by-entry energy-matrix construction uses the same form), and `\vert` ket form satisfies `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Ket/Bra Notation (the closed T-2026-05-16-LD-17 migration already established the convention on this notebook). (3) **T-2026-05-16-LD-19** — cell 2 carries 9 `\qquad` occurrences across roughly 7 `$$...$$` blocks laying out multiple equations side-by-side, violating `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Display Math (*"Do NOT use `\quad` or `\qquad` to lay out multiple equations side-by-side in one display block"*). Sites (Example: Three-level bright/dark mechanism): (a) Step 2 four-entry matrix in two consecutive blocks each pairing two entries with `\qquad`; (b) Step 3 bright/dark eigenvalue pair `E_B=…, \qquad E_D=0`; (c) Step 4 exact zero-mode pair `\hat H_0|D\rangle=0, \qquad \hat V|D\rangle=0`. Sites (Example: Spin-1 splitting): (d) opening setup block with three `\qquad`-separated definitions `\hat H(\lambda)=…, \qquad \hat H_0=…, \qquad \hat V=…`; (e) operator matrix pair `\hat S^x\simeq…, \qquad \hat S^z\simeq…`; (f) derived operator matrix pair `\hat H_0\simeq…, \qquad \hat V\simeq…`. Fix pattern (same as the closed T-2026-05-16-LD-13 on `5-1-2-non-degenerate-perturbation-theory`): split each `$$...$$` into N consecutive `$$...$$` blocks (one relation per block). **Borderline observation NOT filed (per audit-vs-fix discipline):** the cell 2 Example: Three-level Step 1 block `(H^{\text{eff}})_{ij}^{(1)}=\langle i|\hat V|j\rangle=0,\qquad i,j\in\{1,2\}` uses `\qquad` to separate the equation from its domain specification `i,j\in\{1,2\}` rather than to stack two relations — borderline reading of the rule; either rewriting as the equation followed by prose "for $i,j\in\{1,2\}$" outside the display block, or stacking into two `$$...$$` blocks both satisfy the rule. Designer choice at fix time. No separate ticket. (4) **T-2026-05-16-LD-20** — cell 3 (Homework) bare quantum-mechanical operators at three sites, violating `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Vectors and Operators (*every quantum operator must carry `\hat{…}`*): HW Problem 2 (*Block first, levels later*) inline math `W^{(n)}_{\alpha\beta}=\langle n,\alpha|V|n,\beta\rangle` writes the perturbation as bare `V` (should be `\hat V`); HW Problem 4 (*Hydrogen Stark splitting*) display math `$$V=e\mathcal E_0 z$$` writes both the perturbation as bare `V` and the position operator as bare `z` (should be `\hat V=e\mathcal E_0\hat z`); HW Problem 5 (*Residual degeneracy*) display math `$$H_{\text{eff}}=\begin{pmatrix}a&b\\b^*&c\end{pmatrix}$$` writes the effective Hamiltonian as bare `H_{\text{eff}}` (should be `\hat H_{\text{eff}}`). HW Problem 3 already uses `\hat H_0`, `\hat V`, `\hat P_d`, `\hat X` correctly throughout; HW Problem 6 uses `\mathcal E\hat z` correctly; selection-rule labels `\Delta\ell=\pm 1`, `\Delta m=0` in HW4 are scalar quantum numbers (no hat needed); these three are lone drifts. Three sites, three problems, one cell — single-rule mechanical fix. **Other conventions clean per `rules/physics-conventions.md`:** all named operators carry hats (`\hat H_0`, `\hat H`, `\hat V`, `\hat S^x`, `\hat S^z`, `\hat P_n`, `\hat P_d`, `\hat Q_n`, `\hat I`, `\hat I_d`, `\hat X`); Pauli-style `\hat X` (HW3(d)) used in QI convention without `\sigma` mixing — consistent within the problem; no `\vec{}` usage; no upright-symbol gaps (`\mathrm{i}`, `\mathrm{e}`, `\mathrm{d}` not needed — no imaginary unit, exponential, or differential `dx` appears in display math; `\mathrm{h.c.}` used correctly in two operator-sum lines); no `\boldsymbol{\nabla}` or `\nabla` issues (not used); no `\cdot` between scalars; no `\Phi`/`\phi` collisions (N/A); not a Ch 4 notebook so SI / `\chi`/`\alpha` Ch 4 rules N/A. **Style-not-notation observations** (deferred to a Phase C audit if flagged then, **not** Phase B tickets): (i) cell 2 mixes `\hat H` (bare-token form, dominant in `important` boxes and lecture prose) with `\hat{H}` (braces form, used in the cell-2 *Note: Operator Form* admonition for `\hat{P}_n`, `\hat{Q}_n`); both forms render identically and `physics-conventions.md` is silent on the brace convention — same observation as flagged-and-deferred on `5-1-2-non-degenerate-perturbation-theory` Phase B audit. (ii) cell 2 *Note: Operator Form* writes `\hat H^{\text{eff}}_n` while cell 3 HW3(b) writes `\hat H^{(2)}_{\rm eff}` using `\rm` instead of `\text`; cross-cell style inconsistency, not a rule violation. (iii) cell 2 `\text{ for }m\neq n` is a prose-in-math fragment using `\text{}`; `physics-conventions.md` does not mandate `\operatorname` for prose-in-math (only for the operator `Tr` per the closed feedback item); no finding. (iv) cell 2 *Example: Three-level* uses `(H^{\text{eff}})_{ij}^{(1)}` etc. with bare `H` for matrix-element notation rather than `(\hat H^{\text{eff}})_{ij}^{(1)}`; matrix-element labels are scalars (compare `V_{mn}` in `5-1-2-non-degenerate-perturbation-theory` written without hats since they are scalar matrix elements of the form `\langle m|\hat V|n\rangle`); no finding. **Phase Cursor advances B → C (Style) on `5-1-3-degenerate-perturbation-theory`** per `.claude/README.md` § 1.4 (phase audit advances cursor regardless of whether the new tickets have been executed; the four new Phase B tickets become ad hoc execution items per § 1.4 audit-vs-fix split).
