(Ch 4–6 Round 1 — Phase D (Derivation quality) audit on `5-1-2-non-degenerate-perturbation-theory` 2026-05-16 **complete**: read-only chain-quality sweep of the four `:::{admonition} Derivation: ... :class: dropdown information` blocks in cell 2 of `notes_src/ch5_perturbation-theory/5-1-2-non-degenerate-perturbation-theory.ipynb` plus the cell-2 `dropdown example` *Example: check against the qubit toy model* worked-chain block, structurally diffed against `_refs/PerturbationTheory.md` § Non-Degenerate Perturbation Theory (sub-sections *Hellmann-Feynman Theorems* lines 232–321, *Energy Corrections* lines 323–436, *State Corrections* lines 438–589, *Application: the Qubit Model* lines 769–803) per the Phase D checklist in `skills/maintenance-phases/SKILL.md` and the mandatory procedure in `rules/derivation-quality.md` (§ *Scope of `_refs/` alignment*, § *Mandatory detail level: match `_refs/`*, § *Root causes*, § *Anti-patterns*). Designer role — audit pass, no edits, no save, no validator CLI run; `*-designer` skills do not invoke validator commands per `.claude/README.md` § 2.1. **Per-derivation diff** (5 chains in cell 2): (1) *Derivation: Hellmann-Feynman identities* — 7 displayed equation lines (start from eigenvalue equation; differentiate; left-multiply by $\langle m\vert$; substitute $\langle m\vert\hat H=E_m\langle m\vert$, orthonormality $\langle m\vert n\rangle=\delta_{mn}$, and $\partial_\lambda\hat H=\hat V$ in one consolidated step to land $V_{mn}=\partial_\lambda E_n\delta_{mn}+(E_n-E_m)\langle m\vert\partial_\lambda n\rangle$; split $m=n$ and $m\neq n$ cases) — reference splits the consolidated substitution into two sub-steps (apply $\langle m\vert\hat H=E_m\langle m\vert$ → rearrange to expose $E_n-E_m$, then substitute $\hat V$ and $\delta_{mn}$) for 7 lines total. Notebook compression is borderline (all three substitutions are elementary and explicitly named in the prose lead-in); chain is sound, no skipped algebra, line count matches `_refs/`. **PASS** (no ticket; borderline observation logged below). (2) *Derivation: first-order energy derivative* — 1 displayed line citing the $m=n$ case of the first Hellmann-Feynman identity established in (1). Matches `_refs/` § Energy Corrections lines 345–351 exactly. **PASS**. (3) *Derivation: second-order energy derivative* — 6 displayed lines (start from $\partial_\lambda E_n=\langle n\vert\hat V\vert n\rangle$; product-rule differentiate to land 2 terms; insert resolution of identity $\sum_m\vert m\rangle\langle m\vert=\mathbf{1}$; separate $m\neq n$ and $m=n$ contributions; invoke 2nd Hellmann-Feynman identity for $m\neq n$; close the $m=n$ piece by $\partial_\lambda\langle n\vert n\rangle=\partial_\lambda 1=0$; arrive at $\partial_\lambda^2 E_n=2\sum_{m\neq n}V_{nm}V_{mn}/(E_n-E_m)$). Reference (lines 353–429) starts the differentiation step from $\partial_\lambda E_n=\langle n\vert\partial_\lambda\hat H\vert n\rangle$, writes the **full three-term** product rule including $\langle n\vert\partial_\lambda^2\hat H\vert n\rangle$, then explicitly drops that middle term invoking $\partial_\lambda^2\hat H=0$ from the linear ansatz $\hat H(\lambda)=\hat H_0+\lambda\hat V$. The notebook compresses this by starting from $\langle n\vert\hat V\vert n\rangle$ (with $\hat V$ manifestly $\lambda$-independent), bypassing the need to discuss $\partial_\lambda^2\hat H=0$ — but the compression leaves the *reason* the product rule gives only 2 terms (rather than 3) implicit. Per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § Root causes #4 (*Missing motivation for 'why this form'*) and § Mandatory detail level (line-count match with `_refs/`), one explanatory sentence ($\partial_\lambda\hat V=0$ from the linear ansatz, so only the bra and ket are differentiated) would close the gap with a single-line addition. **NEEDS WORK** → ticket **T-2026-05-16-DV-16** filed below. (4) *Derivation: first-order state derivative* — 5 displayed lines (expand $\vert\partial_\lambda n\rangle$ in the unperturbed basis with diagonal/off-diagonal split; apply 2nd Hellmann-Feynman identity for $m\neq n$; close the $m=n$ piece via normalization $\partial_\lambda\langle n\vert n\rangle=0\Rightarrow\langle n\vert\partial_\lambda n\rangle$ pure imaginary; choose phase convention (parallel-transport gauge) $\langle n\vert\partial_\lambda n\rangle=0$; arrive at $\vert\partial_\lambda n\rangle=\sum_{m\neq n}\vert m\rangle V_{mn}/(E_n-E_m)$). Reference (lines 466–473) is significantly *less* detailed — it simply drops the $m=n$ contribution without justification, implicitly using the parallel-transport gauge. Notebook is **strictly better** than reference here (explicit gauge discussion is the right pedagogy and is a valid back-reference to §4.2.1 Berry connection, which precedes §5.1.2 in the TOC). **PASS**. (5) *Example: check against the qubit toy model* — cell-2 `dropdown example` worked chain (Hamiltonian $\hat H_0=\hat Z$, $\hat V=\hat X$ in the $\{\vert 0\rangle,\vert 1\rangle\}$ basis; matrix elements $V_{00}=V_{11}=0$, $V_{10}=V_{01}=1$; first-order energy corrections $E_0^{(1)}=E_1^{(1)}=0$; second-order energy corrections $E_0^{(2)}=+1/2$, $E_1^{(2)}=-1/2$; first-order state derivatives $\vert\partial_\lambda 0\rangle=(1/2)\vert 1\rangle$, $\vert\partial_\lambda 1\rangle=-(1/2)\vert 0\rangle$; cross-check against exact spectrum $E_\pm(\lambda)=\pm\sqrt{1+\lambda^2}$ expanded to $O(\lambda^2)$). Reference *Application: the Qubit Model* lines 769–803 covers the same content with the same arithmetic; notebook is arguably *more careful* (explicit basis statement, separate `$$` blocks for $\hat H_0\vert 0\rangle$ and $\hat H_0\vert 1\rangle$ eigenvalue equations; both branches and the state-derivative check are spelled out symmetrically). All numerical answers match `_refs/` exactly. **PASS**. **Borderline observations NOT filed** (per audit-vs-fix discipline): (a) HF derivation (chain 1) compresses three elementary substitutions ($\langle m\vert\hat H=E_m\langle m\vert$ + orthonormality + $\partial_\lambda\hat H=\hat V$) into a single step where `_refs/` splits them into two; the prose lead-in *names* all three substitutions explicitly, the simplifications are mechanical, and the line-count (7 lines) matches `_refs/` total. Defensible compression; no ticket. (b) Cell-2 prose between the *Hellmann-Feynman Identities* `important` box and the derivation dropdown — "Differentiating the eigenvalue equation with respect to $\lambda$ gives the two Hellmann-Feynman identities below." — pre-stages the derivation but does not preempt; consistent with `rules/derivation-quality.md` § Scope and pedagogical fit (*Stay in context with the surrounding lecture*). No finding. (c) Cell-2 *State Corrections* derivation references "parallel-transport gauge" — this is a back-reference to §4.2.1 (already in the TOC); per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § Scope and pedagogical fit (*A derivation may only invoke concepts ... that the surrounding lecture body has *already* introduced*), the back-reference is valid (Chapter 4 precedes Chapter 5). No finding. (d) Worked example uses QI notation ($\hat Z$, $\hat X$, $\vert 0\rangle$, $\vert 1\rangle$) consistently; matches the cell-2 mixed-system convention established in 5-1-1 toy model and bridged in 5-1-2 via *Notation Clarification* `dropdown note`; no notation mixing per `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Notation Consistency: Spin vs QI. **Phase Cursor advances D → A (Validate) on the next Ch 4–6 target notebook `5-1-3-degenerate-perturbation-theory`** per `.claude/README.md` § 1.3 (audit-vs-fix split — Phase D audit is **done** regardless of whether the new T-2026-05-16-DV-16 ticket has been executed; tickets are opened by the Phase D audit pass, not Phase A on the next notebook). Active ticket queue grows from **0 open** to **1 open** (DV-16). (Ch 4–6 Round 1 — Phase C (Style & structure) audit on `5-1-2-non-degenerate-perturbation-theory` (second Ch 5 subsection) 2026-05-16 **complete**: scoped read-only style/structure sweep of cells 0–3 of `notes_src/ch5_perturbation-theory/5-1-2-non-degenerate-perturbation-theory.ipynb` against `rules/content-style.md`, `rules/notebook-architecture.md`, `rules/prompt-preview.md`, and `rules/maintenance-scope.md` per the Phase C checklist in `skills/maintenance-phases/SKILL.md` (designer role — audit pass, no edits, no save, no validator CLI run; `*-designer` skills do not invoke validator commands per `.claude/README.md` § 2.1). **One Phase C finding filed**: **T-2026-05-16-LD-15** — notation drift between the lecture body and the Summary/Homework. The body proves first- and second-order corrections using the $\partial_\lambda$ shorthand introduced in the *Notation Clarification* `dropdown note` ($\vert\partial_\lambda n\rangle$, $\partial_\lambda E_n$, $\partial_\lambda^2 E_n$), but the `### Summary` bullets and every homework problem 1–8 pivot to Rayleigh–Schrödinger order-superscript notation ($\vert n^{(1)}\rangle$, $E_n^{(1)}$, $E_n^{(2)}$, $\langle n^{(0)}\vert n^{(1)}\rangle$) without an explicit bridge defining $\vert n^{(k)}\rangle \equiv (1/k!)\partial_\lambda^k\vert n(\lambda)\rangle\big|_{\lambda=0}$ and $E_n^{(k)} \equiv (1/k!)\partial_\lambda^k E_n(\lambda)\big|_{\lambda=0}$. HW Problem 2(b) mixes both notations in a single sub-part ("compute $\langle 2\vert\partial_\lambda 1\rangle$ and $\langle 3\vert\partial_\lambda 1\rangle$ at $\lambda=0$. Assemble $\vert 1^{(1)}\rangle$."). Violates `rules/content-style.md` § Content Philosophy (*"Each `###` section is a self-contained conceptual unit"*) and `rules/maintenance-scope.md` § Prefer (*"One clear thread per lesson — each section advances the same main question; cut tangents that do not serve it"*). **Cell-arc / Overview→Summary structure is otherwise clean** per `rules/notebook-architecture.md` and `rules/content-style.md`: subsection x.y.z 4-cell layout — cell 0 `# 5.1.2 Non-Degenerate Perturbation Theory` (dotted title); cell 1 `:::{admonition} Prompts :class: tip` with 4 LLM-preview bullets covering algorithmic build-up of first/second-order corrections, projection logic ($m=n$ vs $m\neq n$), coupling-over-gap controls, and the breakdown signal pointing to degenerate perturbation theory — all 4 prompts satisfy `rules/prompt-preview.md` criteria 1–9 (specific, math-as-naming, one main thread per bullet, tracks cell 2, covers key ideas, varied prompt types, impersonal, subsection-level, self-contained); cell 2 `## Lecture Notes` → `### Overview` immediately with high-level motivation only, no display equations, no preface prose — content-arc satisfied → `### Problem Setup` (`important` *Non-Degenerate Perturbation Problem* box + `dropdown note` *Notation Clarification*) → `### Hellmann-Feynman Identities` (`important` results box + `dropdown information` *Derivation: Hellmann-Feynman identities*) → `### Energy Corrections` (`important` *Energy Expansion* Taylor box + two `dropdown information` derivations [first-order energy derivative, second-order energy derivative] + `dropdown poll` *Poll: sign of ground-state shift*) → `### State Corrections` (`important` *State expansion and first-order correction* box + `dropdown information` *Derivation: first-order state derivative*) → `### Physical Intuition and Validity` (4 bullets + forward pointer to Sec. 5.1.3 + `dropdown example` *Example: check against the qubit toy model* worked check + `dropdown tip` *Discussion: what does divergence mean?*) → `### Summary` (5 outcome bullets within the typical 5–7 range — borderline density of inline display formulas in bullets 2/3/4 but not flagged: see below) → `:::{admonition} See Also :class: seealso` (2 bullets prev/next per `notes_src/_toc.yml` depth-first order: `[5.1.1 Toy Model](5-1-1-toy-model)` and `[5.1.3 Degenerate Perturbation Theory](5-1-3-degenerate-perturbation-theory)` — kebab-case stems, gloss after colon, no bold-wrapped link tokens — compliant with § Summary and See Also); cell 3 `## Homework` with 8 problems all in `**N. Title.** Task...` format with sub-parts on own lines and impersonal voice (no "a student"/"the student"/"you"); ASCII-only titles; all titles capitalized; difficulty gradient direct (HW1–2) → transfer/comparison (HW3–5) → broader/applied (HW6–7) → meta-method (HW8) per `rules/content-style.md` § Homework Design. Admonition class table (cell 2): `important` ×4 (Problem Setup, Hellmann-Feynman identities, Energy Expansion, State expansion); `dropdown note` ×1 (Notation Clarification); `dropdown information` ×4 (HF derivation, 1st-order energy derivative, 2nd-order energy derivative, 1st-order state derivative); `dropdown poll` ×1 (Sign of ground-state shift, 4 options A/B/C/D — borderline see below); `dropdown example` ×1 (qubit toy-model check); `dropdown tip` ×1 (Discussion); `seealso` ×1 (See Also) — all match `rules/content-style.md` § Admonition Usage. Polls inventory: 1 poll — within the cap of 2 per lecture. Banned patterns (`---` horizontal rules, nested `:::`, `plt.show()`, bare `### Application:` headings, agent-file paths) all absent. **Borderline observations NOT filed** (per audit-vs-fix discipline): (a) Summary bullets 2/3/4 each carry an inline display formula ($E_n^{(1)}=V_{nn}$; $\vert n^{(1)}\rangle = \sum_{m\neq n}\vert m\rangle V_{mn}/(E_n-E_m)$; $E_n^{(2)} = \sum_{m\neq n}\vert V_{mn}\vert^2/(E_n-E_m)$) — `rules/content-style.md` § Summary and See Also cautions against `{eq}`/equation-label spam but does not forbid inline formulas; bullets remain outcome-forward, and the formulas are the lesson's deliverable. Borderline density only, no ticket; if T-2026-05-16-LD-15 — the body/Summary/HW notation bridge — is implemented via route (a), the inline-formula density in Summary is preserved and well-defined against the new bridge. (b) Poll *Sign of ground-state shift* option (B) is labeled "always negative" while HW Problem 4(a) carefully proves the bound as $E_0^{(2)} \le 0$ (i.e. non-positive, zero only if all couplings to other non-degenerate levels vanish); the poll's intended answer is (B), and "always negative" is acceptable shorthand for the generic-coupling case (a trivial-coupling reading makes the question vacuous), but a strict reading creates a minor body/poll tension. No ticket. (c) Cell 1 Prompts uses `\hat{H}_0` braces-form while cell 2 / cell 3 use the bare-token `\hat H_0` form throughout — already flagged in the Phase B audit (2026-05-16) as style-not-notation; both forms render identically and neither `rules/physics-conventions.md` nor `rules/content-style.md` mandates a brace convention. No Phase C ticket. (d) Cell 2 *Physical Intuition and Validity* ends with the prose "Then we must switch to **degenerate perturbation theory** (Sec. 5.1.3)." — uses `§5.1.3` numbering inline; acceptable per `rules/myst-references.md` § Cross-reference conventions (section refs in prose use `§x.y.z`). No ticket. (e) Cell 2 *Hellmann-Feynman identities (non-degenerate)* `important` box renders the 2nd identity's qualifier as `\text{ for }m\neq n` — `rules/physics-conventions.md` doesn't mandate `\operatorname` for prose-in-math fragments. No finding. **Phase Cursor advances C → D (Derivation quality) on `5-1-2-non-degenerate-perturbation-theory`** per `.claude/README.md` § 1.3 (audit-vs-fix split — Phase C audit is **done** regardless of whether the new T-2026-05-16-LD-15 ticket has been executed; tickets are opened by the Phase C audit pass, not Phase D). The exact eigenenergies `E_\pm(\lambda)=\pm\sqrt{1+\lambda^2}` (labeled `eq-5-1-1-exact-energies`) and exact eigenstates `\vert\psi_\pm(\lambda)\rangle` are stated as benchmarks without derivation; the Taylor-expanded forms are stated as results of applying the generic Taylor formula, not derived step by step. **Structural diff vs `_refs/`**: the reference's parallel section likewise *states* the exact `E_\pm(\lambda)=\pm\sqrt{1+\lambda^2}`, the exact eigenstates, the generic Taylor formula, and the $E_\pm$ + state Taylor expansions as benchmarks for the order-by-order algorithm developed in the next subsection (*Non-Degenerate Perturbation Theory*) — no derivation chains shown for either the exact solutions or the Taylor coefficients. The lecture's narrative (state exact solutions → recall Taylor formula → state Taylor coefficients → flag practical convergence) **matches the reference's narrative one-for-one**; no missing derivation steps to surface as a Phase D ticket per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § Scope of `_refs/` alignment (math/physics argument layer is empty here — there is no chain of commutator evaluations / identity substitutions to diff against). **Per `skills/maintenance-phases/SKILL.md` § D ("Only if the lesson has derivations or worked chains that should match `_refs/`"), Phase D effectively skips on `5-1-1-toy-model` with no findings and no tickets filed** — the previous two sessions' read-aheads already anticipated this outcome, and the formal Phase D audit cycle now closes that loop. **One borderline observation NOT filed** (deferred per audit-vs-fix discipline and `rules/maintenance-scope.md` § Prefer tightening over adding): the reference's state Taylor series expands $\vert\psi_\pm(\lambda)\rangle$ to order $\lambda^4$ (with coefficients $3\lambda^3/16$ on the off-component and $11\lambda^4/128$ on the diagonal component), while the lecture truncates at $O(\lambda^2)$ with $O(\lambda^3)$ remainder; this is a *result-listing depth* mismatch (not a derivation-chain depth mismatch) and `rules/derivation-quality.md` § Mandatory detail level binds derivation-step counts, not result-listing depth; the lecture's introductory-subsection framing justifies the minimal benchmark (the order-by-order algorithm in `5-1-2-non-degenerate-perturbation-theory` is where deeper state coefficients are derived and benchmarked against the exact series). Observation only, no ticket. **Closing Phase D completes the full A → B → C → (D-clean) cycle on `5-1-1-toy-model`** per `.claude/README.md` § 1.2; **Phase Cursor advances to `5-1-2-non-degenerate-perturbation-theory` at Phase A (Validate)** per `.claude/README.md` § 1.3 and depth-first `_toc.yml` order (`5-1-1-toy-model` → `5-1-2-non-degenerate-perturbation-theory` → `5-1-3-degenerate-perturbation-theory` → §5.2 parent → `5-2-1` …). (Ch 4–6 Round 1 — Phase D audit on `4-3-1-cyclotron-motion` 2026-05-11 **complete**: read-only chain-quality sweep of the two `:::{dropdown information}` derivation blocks in cell 2 of `notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-3-1-cyclotron-motion.ipynb` per `rules/derivation-quality.md` (§ *Root causes of quality gaps*, § *Mandatory procedure*, § *Minimum derivation depth*, § *Anti-patterns*) and per the `phys130b-derivation-designer` skill. `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` § *Cyclotron Motion* (lines ~1595–1764) and § *Hall Effect* (lines ~1766–1893) confirmed to carry parallel material; structural-diff procedure applied. **Finding 1 — *Derivation: Cyclotron Frequency and Radius*: PASS.** Forward chain (Lorentz EOM in components → reduce to SHO equation → quote general solution with phase offset $\varphi_0$ → integrate to circular trajectory → define $r_c$), depth roughly matches `_refs/` line counts (component EOM 3 lines, SHO + general solution 2+2 lines, trajectory 2 lines, definitions 1–2 lines), no skipped commutators, single focus, no scope creep into guiding-center-operator or Landau-level content. One borderline observation: notebook quotes both $v_x(t)$ and $v_y(t)$ together as the SHO general solution, whereas `_refs/` (lines 1675–1684) derives $v_y$ from $v_x$ via the first-order relation $\dot{y} = (1/\omega_c)\,\mathrm{d}\dot{x}/\mathrm{d}t$ — the pedagogically tighter route makes clear that $v_y$ is **forced** to be $-v_\perp\sin(\omega_c t + \varphi_0)$ (not an independent SHO mode that could also be $\cos$). Not a separate ticket; bundled as an optional companion in the Phase D ticket below per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Mandatory detail level*. **Finding 2 — *Derivation: from force balance to $\rho_{xy}^{\mathrm{cl}}(B)$*: NEEDS WORK.** **Sign bug.** The displayed force-balance equation `qE_y + qv_xB = 0` implies $E_y = -v_xB$, but the Lorentz cross-product $\boldsymbol{v}\times\boldsymbol{B}$ for $\boldsymbol{v} = v_x\hat{x}$, $\boldsymbol{B} = B\hat{z}$ is $-v_xB\hat{y}$ (since $\hat{x}\times\hat{z} = -\hat{y}$), so the correct force balance is `qE_y - qv_xB = 0` giving $E_y = +v_xB$. **The notebook's own *Cyclotron Frequency and Radius* derivation has the same cross-product correctly** (`m\dot{v}_y = -qBv_x`), so this is internally inconsistent. The downstream paper-over `\rho_{xy}^{\mathrm{cl}} \equiv |E_y|/j_x = B/(nq)` masks the sign error but introduces a second issue: for electrons $q = -e < 0$, the signed $B/(nq) < 0$ while $|E_y|/j_x \geq 0$ — contradiction. The body's *Classical Hall response* `important` box writes $\rho_{xy}^{\mathrm{cl}}(B) = B/(nq)$ (signed, no absolute value), so the cleanest fix is route (a): correct the sign in the force-balance display, drop the absolute value, write $\rho_{xy}^{\mathrm{cl}} = E_y/j_x = B/(nq)$ directly. Filed as **T-2026-05-11-DV-04** below. Per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Root causes* (#3 *Wrong notation* and #5 *Reverse-engineering from the answer* — the `|·|` paper-over is a smoking gun for sign-handling inconsistency) and § *Anti-patterns*. **Other potential Phase D items confirmed NOT ticket-worthy**: (i) body's *Why low-$T$, high-$B$ changes the game* `attention` block with $\Delta E = \hbar\omega_c = \hbar qB/m$ — not a derivation chain (regime argument prose), and the unsigned-$q$ reading is consistent with the body's generic-$q$ convention; (ii) integer-quantum-Hall plateau formula $\rho_{xy} = h/(\nu e^2)$ in `### Experimental fact: Hall plateaus` is **stated as an experimental fact**, not derived in this lesson (the actual derivation is §4.3.3) — no chain to audit here; (iii) homework derivations (HW1(a), HW3(a–c)) are exercises for students, outside Phase D scope (any depth issues are `homework-designer` territory); (iv) the missing guiding-center-operator reconstruction $\boldsymbol{\xi} = \boldsymbol{x} - \hat{z}\times\boldsymbol{\pi}/(qB)$ that `_refs/` shows after the cyclotron derivation — the notebook deliberately stays classical and pre-quantum here, and the reconstruction is a Landau-level tool (§4.3.2), so its absence is **correct scope discipline** per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Scope and pedagogical fit*. **Cursor stays at D / `4-3-1-cyclotron-motion`** (1 open D ticket: T-2026-05-11-DV-04); will advance to next notebook `4-3-2-landau-quantization` at Phase A only after T-DV-04 closes, per `.claude/README.md` § 1.3 and the precedent set by `4-2-3-flux-ring` Phase D 2026-05-11. No notebook files modified this session. Designer: `phys130b-derivation-designer` (audit pass — no edits, no validator run).)

Executed via `phys130b-derivation-designer` → `phys130b-notebook-writer` handoff (`.claude/README.md` § 2.3); designer-recommended route (a) chosen (correct sign + drop `|·|`). **(a) Required Hall derivation sign fix** in `:::{admonition} Derivation: from force balance to $\rho_{xy}^{\mathrm{cl}}(B)$ :class: dropdown information` (§ *Classical Hall effect and its expectation*): made the cross-product explicit ($\boldsymbol{v} = v_x\hat{x}$, $\boldsymbol{B} = B\hat{z}$, $(\boldsymbol{v}\times\boldsymbol{B})_y = v_xB(\hat{x}\times\hat{z})_y = -v_xB$ since $\hat{x}\times\hat{z} = -\hat{y}$); corrected the force-balance `\begin{split}` block to `qE_y + q(\boldsymbol{v}\times\boldsymbol{B})_y = qE_y - qv_xB = 0 \Rightarrow E_y = v_xB` (previously $qE_y + qv_xB = 0 \Rightarrow E_y = -v_xB$, wrong sign); propagated to the downstream line $E_y = (B/(nq))j_x$ (dropped the leading minus); dropped the `|E_y|` paper-over so the closing definition is now $\rho_{xy}^{\mathrm{cl}} \equiv E_y/j_x = B/(nq)$ — matches the body's *Classical Hall response* `important` box ($\rho_{xy}^{\mathrm{cl}}(B) = B/(nq)$) verbatim and is internally consistent with the same notebook's *Derivation: Cyclotron Frequency and Radius* cross-product ($m\dot{v}_y = -qBv_x$). Closing sentence updated: removed "What matters for comparison with experiment is the magnitude" (no longer relevant since signed form is now used); kept the carrier-type sentence and "linear in $B$ with no plateaus" clause. **(b) Optional cyclotron companion** (bundled, as the ticket noted *bundle if convenient*): in `:::{admonition} Derivation: Cyclotron Frequency and Radius` the prior single `\begin{split}` listing $v_x$ and $v_y$ together as "the general solution" was split — $v_x(t) = v_\perp\cos(\omega_c t + \varphi_0)$ is presented as the SHO general solution, then $v_y$ is identified as *forced* by the first-order relation $v_y = \dot{v}_x/\omega_c$ (with explicit annotation *(not an independent SHO mode)*), yielding $v_y(t) = (1/\omega_c)\dot{v}_x(t) = -v_\perp\sin(\omega_c t + \varphi_0)$. Matches the pedagogical structure of `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` lines 1675–1684. **Scope discipline** per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Scope and pedagogical fit* and `rules/maintenance-scope.md`: edits stayed strictly inside the two existing `:::{dropdown information}` derivation blocks; the body's three `important` boxes (*Cyclotron frequency and radius*, *Classical Hall response*, *Integer quantum Hall observation*) were not touched; no Landau-level / guiding-center-operator / kinetic-momentum / §4.3.2 / §4.3.3 content imported; the cyclotron lesson remains classical and pre-quantum. Filed as **T-2026-05-11-NB-02** (direct parallel to the `T-2026-05-11-NB-01` pattern on `4-3-1-cyclotron-motion`: one rule, one scope, mechanical layout fix via `\begin{split}` stacking; math content unchanged). **(ii) Ket/Bra Notation (§ Ket/Bra).** Eight inline-math instances of bare `|n,m\rangle` / `|0,0\rangle` spread across cell 1 bullet 4 *Eigenstates are labeled $|n,m\rangle$* and cell 2 § *Eigenstates and Their Physical Meaning* (eigenvalue display, joint-vacuum prose, ladder-construction display, post-display *all $|n,m\rangle$ at fixed $n$* prose). The rule mandates `\vert` throughout, and the rest of the notebook (cell 3 HW1 `\mathrm{e}^{-\vert z\vert^2/(4\ell_B^2)}` and HW2(b) `\vert\langle n+1\vert\hat{V}\vert n\rangle\vert^2`) already uses `\vert` consistently — this is internal-consistency drift in the lecture-cell + Prompts only. Filed as **T-2026-05-11-NB-03**. **(iii) Bundled micro-cleanups (§ Phase Symbols + LaTeX hygiene).** Two single-token substitutions in different cells: (α) cell 2 *Landau-level degeneracy* `important` box — `\Phi_\text{total}` → `\Phi_{\mathrm{total}}` (compound `\Phi` subscripts use `\mathrm{...}` per the rule's established examples `\Phi_{\mathrm{path}}` / `\Phi_{\mathrm{Berry}}` / `\Phi_{\mathrm{AB}}`, and the prior `\text{Tr}` → `\operatorname{Tr}` migration direction discourages `\text{}` in math constructions); (β) cell 3 HW6 *Landau levels in graphene* — `E_n = \mathrm{sgn}(n)\,v_F\sqrt{2qℏ\vert n\vert B}` uses Unicode `ℏ` (U+210F PLANCK CONSTANT OVER TWO PI) instead of `\hbar`, breaking uniform `\hbar` convention (HW6 part (a), elsewhere in HW6, and the whole rest of the notebook use `\hbar` correctly). Filed as **T-2026-05-11-NB-04** (bundled because each fix is too small for a separate ticket; both fall under `rules/physics-conventions.md`). **Other conventions clean** (no findings): SI EM units throughout (symmetric gauge `\boldsymbol{A}=(B/2)(-y,x,0)`, kinetic momentum `\hat{\boldsymbol{\pi}}=\hat{\boldsymbol{p}}-q\boldsymbol{A}`, no `(q/c)\boldsymbol{A}`, no Gaussian markers), magnetic length `\ell_B=\sqrt{\hbar/(qB)}` and cyclotron frequency `\omega_c=qB/m` in SI form; `\boldsymbol` vectors (no `\vec`); `\hat{}` on every operator (`\hat{H},\hat{a},\hat{b},\hat{X},\hat{Y},\hat{\pi}_x,\hat{\pi}_y,\hat{L}_z,\hat{\boldsymbol{r}},\hat{V}`); `\mathrm{i/e/d}` upright operators where they appear; no `\chi` anywhere in the notebook (Ch 4 gauge-function rule); no `\boldsymbol{\nabla}`; no `\cdot` between scalars; no `align`/`aligned` environments; no banned MyST patterns (`---`, nested `:::`); flux quantum `\Phi_0` and Landau-level flux count `N_\Phi` already conform to § Phase Symbols. **Three read-ahead observations retained for later phases** (not filed as Phase B tickets): **(R1)** HW2(a) *Express $\hat{x}$ and $\hat{y}$ in terms of the cyclotron ladder operators $\hat{a}, \hat{a}^\dagger$* — physically incomplete, since the lecture explicitly defines `\hat{X}=\hat{x}+\hat{\pi}_y/(qB)` (so `\hat{x}=\hat{X}-\hat{\pi}_y/(qB)` mixes guiding-center and cyclotron ladders); request should be either *in terms of the cyclotron and guiding-center ladders $\hat{a},\hat{a}^\dagger,\hat{b},\hat{b}^\dagger$* or, narrower, *show $\hat{V}(t)$ couples only to $n\pm 1$ irrespective of $\hat{b}$*. Phase D (`derivation-designer`) territory or Phase C (`homework-designer` problem-wording) — **not** Phase B. **(R2)** HW2 uses `-e\boldsymbol{E}(t)\cdot\hat{\boldsymbol{r}}` and HW6 uses bare `e` in `4e^2/h`, specializing to electron carriers, while the body lecture writes generic charge `q` throughout (`\omega_c=qB/m`, `\ell_B=\sqrt{\hbar/(qB)}`, `\boldsymbol{A}=(B/2)(-y,x,0)` with the stated *(with $B>0$, $q>0$)*) — direct parallel to the prior `T-2026-05-11-HD-01` body→HW notation drift on `4-3-1-cyclotron-motion` HW3. Phase C (`homework-designer`) territory — **not** Phase B. **(R3)** HW1(a) writes `\hat{a} = (\hat{\pi}_x + \mathrm{i}\hat{\pi}_y)/\sqrt{2q\hbar B}` while the lecture defines `\hat{a} = (\ell_B/(\sqrt{2}\,\hbar))(\hat{\pi}_x + \mathrm{i}\hat{\pi}_y)` — mathematically equivalent (since `\hbar^2/\ell_B^2 = q\hbar B`), but the HW form re-derives the normalization in raw EM constants where the lecture has just defined `\ell_B` as a shorter symbol. Tightening to a single canonical form is style discipline (Phase C / `homework-designer`), **not** Phase B. **Cursor advances B → C (Style) on `4-3-2-landau-quantization`** per `.claude/README.md` § 1.3 (audit-vs-fix split — Phase B audit work is complete regardless of whether the resulting NB-02 / NB-03 / NB-04 tickets have been executed). The rule's prohibition on `\quad`/`\qquad` is specifically about *side-by-side equation layout in one display block*; the `\quad (i=x,y)` here is an index-range qualifier attached to a single equality chain, not a parallel equation being placed beside another. Stacking the index range on its own `\begin{split}` line reads more clumsily than the inline parenthetical, so the **looser reading applies** and (g) stays as-is. **Out of scope** per the ticket: the bare-`|` kets in (f) (those are `T-2026-05-11-NB-03`), the `\Phi_\text{total}` and Unicode `ℏ` cleanups (those are `T-2026-05-11-NB-04`), and the read-ahead observations R1/R2/R3 from the Phase B audit (Phase C / `homework-designer` or Phase D / `derivation-designer` territory). the downstream claim that $\hat{V}(t)$ couples $n$ only to $n\pm 1$ is correct since the $\hat{b}, \hat{b}^\dagger$ pieces commute with $\hat{a}^\dagger\hat{a}$ and change only $m$, but the operator expansion request needs the broader form; Executed via `phys130b-homework-designer` → `phys130b-notebook-writer` handoff (`.claude/README.md` § 2.3). **Substitution.** HW Problem 2 *Cyclotron resonance* sub-part (a) rewritten: prior wording "Express $\hat{x}$ and $\hat{y}$ in terms of the cyclotron ladder operators $\hat{a}, \hat{a}^\dagger$. Show that $\hat{V}(t)$ couples Landau level $n$ only to $n\pm 1$." → new wording "Express $\hat{x}$ and $\hat{y}$ in terms of the cyclotron and guiding-center ladder operators $\hat{a}, \hat{a}^\dagger, \hat{b}, \hat{b}^\dagger$. Show that the $\hat{b}, \hat{b}^\dagger$ pieces leave the Landau-level index $n$ unchanged, so $\hat{V}(t)$ couples $n$ only to $n\pm 1$." **Rationale**: matches the lecture-body two-ladder decomposition $\hat{x} = \hat{X} - \hat{\pi}_y/(qB) = (\ell_B/\sqrt{2})[(\hat{b}+\hat{b}^\dagger) + \mathrm{i}(\hat{a}-\hat{a}^\dagger)]$ (and the analogous $\hat{y}$), which involves **both** ladder sets — guiding-center $\hat{X}$ in $\hat{b}, \hat{b}^\dagger$ via $\hat{X} = (\ell_B/\sqrt{2})(\hat{b}+\hat{b}^\dagger)$, plus cyclotron $\hat{\pi}_y$ in $\hat{a}, \hat{a}^\dagger$ via $\hat{\pi}_y = -\mathrm{i}(\hbar/(\sqrt{2}\ell_B))(\hat{a}-\hat{a}^\dagger)$. The new wording makes the $\hat{b}$-piece (which commutes with $\hat{a}^\dagger\hat{a}$ and changes only $m$) explicit, so the student sees why only the $\hat{a}, \hat{a}^\dagger$ piece can shift $n$ to $n\pm 1$. This sub-part now supports HW2(b)'s matrix element $\vert\langle n+1\vert\hat{V}\vert n\rangle\vert^2$ computation, which needs the full $\hat{x}, \hat{y}$ expression. **Scope discipline** per the ticket's *Out of scope* clause: HW2(b) and HW2(c) wording not touched (they read coherently with the broadened (a)); HW Problem 2 preamble's $-e\boldsymbol{E}(t)\cdot\hat{\boldsymbol{r}}$ vs body-$q$ charge-convention drift **not addressed** (that is T-2026-05-11-HD-03, separate ticket); HW1/3/4/5/6, cell 1 Prompts, cell 2 lecture body all untouched. (Ch 4–6 Round 1 — Phase D audit on `4-3-2-landau-quantization` 2026-05-12 **complete**: read-only chain-quality sweep of the single `:::{dropdown information}` *Derivation: Commutators* block in cell 2 of `notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-3-2-landau-quantization.ipynb` per `rules/derivation-quality.md` (§ *Root causes of quality gaps*, § *Mandatory procedure*, § *Minimum derivation depth*, § *Anti-patterns*) and per the `phys130b-derivation-designer` skill. `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` § *Landau Level Quantization* (lines ~1942–2650) confirmed to carry parallel material with substantially deeper algebraic chains in $\hbar=q=1$ units; structural-diff procedure applied (line counts mapped to SI). **Finding 1 — *Guiding center* sub-block $[\hat{X},\hat{Y}]$: WRONG ALGEBRA PATH (paper-over).** The displayed three-term expansion writes signs $-/-/-$ on the surviving terms: $[\hat{X},\hat{Y}] = -[\hat{x},\hat{\pi}_x]/(qB) - [\hat{\pi}_y,\hat{y}]/(qB) - [\hat{\pi}_y,\hat{\pi}_x]/(qB)^2$. The correct bilinear expansion of $[\hat{x}+\hat{\pi}_y/(qB),\,\hat{y}-\hat{\pi}_x/(qB)]$ is signs $-/+/-$ on the surviving terms (i.e. the second term carries $+[\hat{\pi}_y,\hat{y}]/(qB)$, not $-$). The notebook then **papers over** the sign error in the next substitution line by appending an ad-hoc `\cdot (-1)` factor to the third substituted term — `-(-\mathrm{i}q\hbar B)/(qB)^2 \cdot (-1)` — which has no algebraic justification (the $[\hat{\pi}_y,\hat{\pi}_x]=-[\hat{\pi}_x,\hat{\pi}_y]$ sign flip is already in the displayed value `-\mathrm{i}q\hbar B`). The final numerical answer $-\mathrm{i}\hbar/(qB) = -\mathrm{i}\ell_B^2$ comes out right by accident because the `\cdot(-1)` exactly compensates the upstream second-term sign error, but the algebra path that gets there is broken. Per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Root causes* #3 (*Wrong notation*) and #5 (*Reverse-engineering from the answer*); § *Anti-patterns* (paper-over via spurious sign factors). **Finding 2 — *Kinetic momenta* sub-block $[\hat{\pi}_x,\hat{\pi}_y]$: depth gap.** The notebook compresses to 4 displayed lines, jumping from $[\hat{p}_x-qA_x,\hat{p}_y-qA_y]$ directly to $-q[\hat{p}_x,A_y]-q[A_x,\hat{p}_y]$ without showing the 4-term expansion that demonstrates why $[\hat{p}_x,\hat{p}_y]=0$ and $[A_x,A_y]=0$ drop out. `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` lines 2101–2109 shows 5 lines including this 4-term layout. Per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Mandatory detail level* (≥4–7 lines per commutator evaluation). **Finding 3 — *Guiding center* sub-block depth gap (coupled to Finding 1).** Beyond the sign bug, the 4-term bilinear expansion of $[\hat{X},\hat{Y}]$ is itself not displayed — the notebook says "expand and use the four sub-commutators" but jumps straight to a 3-term sum (with one wrong sign). `_refs/` lines 2206–2220 shows 5 lines for the same identity (in $\hbar=q=1$ units). **Finding 4 — *Cross-commutators* sub-block: shows only 1 of 4 cases.** The dropdown writes $[\hat{X},\hat{\pi}_x]=0$ in one line and says "similarly for the other three combinations." `_refs/` lines 2229–2274 derives all four cases. Borderline scope discipline — one-line compression is permissible at the lecture level, but the rule prefers structural parity with `_refs/`. **Findings 1+2+3 (required) and 4 (optional companion) bundled** as **T-2026-05-12-DV-05** below (single ticket, single dropdown — *one focus per derivation* per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Scope and pedagogical fit*; designer chooses whether to expand the cross-commutators in the same edit). **Other potential Phase D items confirmed NOT ticket-worthy this session** (per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Scope and pedagogical fit* — *Adding detail* must slice existing steps finer, not import new building-block results): (i) the *Key result: Hamiltonian uses only cyclotron ladder* `important` box $\hat{H} = \hbar\omega_c(\hat{a}^\dagger\hat{a}+1/2)$ is stated without a derivation dropdown — `_refs/` lines 2299–2334 derives $\hat{a}^\dagger\hat{a}+1/2=\hat{\boldsymbol{\pi}}^2/(2B)$ in 6 lines; adding this derivation would be **scope expansion** (a new dropdown for a stated result), not a fix to an existing chain — separate future ticket if warranted; (ii) the *Cyclotron and guiding-center ladders* `important` box states $[\hat{a},\hat{a}^\dagger]=1$ and $[\hat{b},\hat{b}^\dagger]=1$ without derivation — `_refs/` lines 2405–2431 derives both in ~10 lines; same scope-expansion judgment as (i); (iii) the § *Macroscopic Degeneracy* body-prose identity $\hat{R}^2=\hat{X}^2+\hat{Y}^2=\ell_B^2(2\hat{b}^\dagger\hat{b}+1)$ is stated inline without showing the ~3-line manipulation $(\hat{X}+\mathrm{i}\hat{Y})(\hat{X}-\mathrm{i}\hat{Y})=\hat{X}^2+\hat{Y}^2-\mathrm{i}[\hat{X},\hat{Y}]$ — body prose, not in a dropdown; adding a derivation dropdown there is also scope expansion. These three are flagged in this audit as scope-expansion candidates and intentionally **not bundled** into T-DV-05 — future Phase D rounds may pick them up if the maintenance program judges them warranted. **Cursor stays at D / `4-3-2-landau-quantization`** with 1 open D ticket (T-2026-05-12-DV-05) per `.claude/README.md` § 1.3 and § 1.4 — advances to next notebook `4-3-3-quantum-hall-effect` at Phase A only after T-DV-05 closes, following the precedent set by `4-3-1-cyclotron-motion` Phase D 2026-05-11. No notebook files modified this session. Designer: `phys130b-derivation-designer` (audit pass — no edits, no validator run).)

(β) `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Ket/Bra Notation states *Use `\vert` for `|` in kets and bras throughout*; required in markdown tables but recommended elsewhere for project-wide consistency. Migrating 4-3-3 to `\vert` brings it into consistency with the recently-completed 4-3-2 sibling. **Other conventions are clean** in the notebook: SI EM units throughout (no Gaussian markers, no `(q/c)\boldsymbol{A}`); `\boldsymbol{}` vectors (`\boldsymbol{E}`, `\boldsymbol{j}`, `\boldsymbol{\pi}`, `\hat{\boldsymbol{\pi}}`); hatted operators (`\hat{H}`, `\hat{H}_0`, `\hat{j}_x`, `\hat{j}_y`, `\hat{\pi}_x`, `\hat{\pi}_y`, `\hat{a}`, `\hat{a}^\dagger`, `\hat{b}`, `\hat{b}^\dagger`); `\mathrm{i}\hbar` upright imaginary unit; `\mathrm{occ}`/`\mathrm{empty}`/`\mathrm{encl}`/`\mathrm{cl}` upright subscript labels per `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Upright Symbols; uppercase `\Phi` and `\Phi_0` for magnetic flux and flux quantum, `N_\Phi` for flux-count degeneracy per `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Phase Symbols; no `\chi` (Ch 4 gauge-function rule); no `\vec` or `\boldsymbol{\nabla}`; no `\cdot` between scalars; cell 3 (Homework) clean — no `\quad`/`\qquad`, no bare `|` (no kets in HW), uses `e` consistently as the positive elementary charge across all six problems. **Filed as two single-rule tickets:** **T-2026-05-12-NB-05** in the queue above (display-math `\qquad` layout fix, parallel to T-2026-05-11-NB-01 on `4-3-1` and T-2026-05-11-NB-02 on `4-3-2`) and **T-2026-05-12-NB-06** (bare `|` → `\vert` migration, for consistency with the recently-completed 4-3-2 sibling). (Ch 4–6 Round 1 — Phase D audit on `4-3-3-quantum-hall-effect` 2026-05-12 **complete**: read-only chain-quality sweep of the two derivation chains in cell 2 of `notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-3-3-quantum-hall-effect.ipynb` per `rules/derivation-quality.md` (§ *Root causes of quality gaps*, § *Mandatory procedure*, § *Minimum derivation depth*, § *Anti-patterns*) and per the `phys130b-derivation-designer` skill. `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` § *Quantum Hall Effect* (lines ~3030–3828) confirmed to carry parallel material for both chains — § *Charge Pumping* (lines ~3115–3220) parallels *Argument 1: Laughlin's Charge Pumping* (body prose, Steps 1–4), and § *Linear Response Theory* (lines ~3222–3828) parallels the *Derivation: Kubo evaluation on Landau-level eigenstates* `:::{dropdown information}` block. Structural-diff procedure applied. **Finding 1 — *Argument 1: Laughlin's Charge Pumping* (body prose, Steps 1–4): PASS.** Forward chain (single-valuedness → $e\Phi_{\mathrm{encl}}/\hbar = 2\pi m$ → enclosed-flux decomposition $\Phi_{\mathrm{encl}} = \pi r_m^2 B + \Phi(t)$ → solve for $r_m^2(t) = (\Phi_0/(\pi B))(m - t/T)$ → radii shrink by one slot per flux quantum → one electron pumped outer→inner per filled level → $j_r = e/(2\pi r T)$ and $E_\theta = \Phi_0/(2\pi r T)$ → $\sigma_H = j_r/E_\theta = e/\Phi_0 = e^2/h$ per filled level → sum to $\nu e^2/h$), single focus, no skipped commutators, signs consistent, no scope creep into Kubo-derivation territory. One borderline observation: Step 1 algebraic solution for $r_m^2(t)$ is a single-line jump from the two preceding equations, whereas `_refs/` lines 3174–3180 shows three intermediate substitutions ($r_m^2 = (1/(\pi B))(2\pi\hbar m/e - \Phi(t)) = (1/(\pi B))(hm/e - \Phi_0 t/T) = (\Phi_0/(\pi B))(m - t/T)$ using $h/e = \Phi_0$ and $\Phi(t) = \Phi_0 t/T$). Borderline by `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Mandatory detail level*; since this is a 2-substitution algebraic simplification (not a commutator evaluation), the one-line jump is defensible. **Not** a separate Phase D ticket; bundled as an optional companion in the Phase D ticket below. **Finding 2 — *Derivation: Kubo evaluation on Landau-level eigenstates* (`:::{dropdown information}` block): NEEDS WORK.** Step 5 (*Simplify the prefactor*) has an algebra error in the first displayed equation. Current display: $(q/m)^2 q\hbar B / (\hbar\omega_c)^2 = q/\hbar$. With $\omega_c = qB/m$, $(\hbar\omega_c)^2 = \hbar^2 q^2 B^2/m^2$ and $(q/m)^2 q\hbar B = q^3\hbar B/m^2$, so the correct ratio is $q^3\hbar B/(\hbar^2 q^2 B^2) = q/(\hbar B)$ — the lecture's $q/\hbar$ is missing a factor of $1/B$. The downstream conclusion $|\sigma_{xy}| = (q^2/h)(N_{\mathrm{occ}}/N_\Phi)$ is nevertheless correct (verified directly: $\sigma_{xy} = (i\hbar/A) \cdot i \cdot (q/(\hbar B)) \cdot N_{\mathrm{occ}} = -qN_{\mathrm{occ}}/(AB) = -q^2 N_{\mathrm{occ}}/(hN_\Phi)$ using $N_\Phi = qBA/h$), but the published Step 5 algebra does not actually compose to it — combining the wrong $q/\hbar$ with the (correct) $1/A = qB/(hN_\Phi)$ leaves a stray factor of $B$ in the final expression. Classic *reverse-engineering from the answer* pattern per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Root causes* #5 — the author knew the final answer, decomposed the prefactor into two pieces, and bridged them with a wrong intermediate that "looked right" but doesn't compose. The `$\mathrm{i}^2 = -1$ absorbs into the carrier-sign convention` sentence is a secondary paper-over of the sign question (`rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Anti-patterns*). Filed as **T-2026-05-12-DV-06** below; minimal fix is `q/\hbar` → `q/(\hbar B)` with light prose adjustment so the algebra composes cleanly to the (already-correct) final formula. **Other items in the Kubo derivation confirmed NOT separately ticket-worthy** (one ticket, one focus per `rules/maintenance-scope.md`): (i) Step 4's *"blocked pairs cancel pairwise"* argument is correct (verified explicitly: for $\nu$ filled Landau levels, unconstrained sum equals $N_{\mathrm{occ}}$, and each pair of adjacent occupied $(n, m)$ and $(n+1, m)$ contributes $+(n+1)$ and $-(n+1)$ via the $\delta_{n', n+1}$ and $-n_\alpha\delta_{n', n_\alpha - 1}$ terms, both removed in the constrained sum and canceling) but pedagogically dense — `_refs/` lines 3793–3818 takes a more direct route, but the lecture's compression is acceptable scope discipline; (ii) Step 2's antisymmetric current product is correct including the *"Hermiticity of $\hat\pi_x, \hat\pi_y$"* justification for *"swap = c.c."*, with only a minor one-line gap on cross-terms — not separately ticket-worthy; (iii) Step 1's matrix elements (ladder evaluation) match `_refs/` lines 3522–3534 in $\hbar=q=1$ units, no issue. **Scope-expansion candidates intentionally NOT bundled into T-DV-06** (per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Scope and pedagogical fit* — *Adding detail* must slice existing steps finer, not import new building-block results): (a) the *Kubo formula* `important` box states the formula without proof — its derivation lives in §5.2.3 per the lecture's explicit `{eq}` ref, so the absence here is correct scope discipline; (b) Argument 1 Step 1 single-valuedness $e\Phi_{\mathrm{encl}}/\hbar = 2\pi m$ is asserted without a chain back to the symmetric-gauge wavefunction $\psi_m \propto \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}m\varphi}$ — heuristic / semi-classical, consistent with the body framing; deepening would import §4.3.2 LLL wavefunction content (scope expansion); (c) the Argument 1 → Argument 2 transition is interpretive prose, not a derivation chain. **Cursor stays at D / `4-3-3-quantum-hall-effect`** with 1 open D ticket (T-2026-05-12-DV-06) per `.claude/README.md` § 1.3 and § 1.4 — advances to the next Ch 4 subsection at Phase A only after T-DV-06 closes, following the precedent set by `4-3-2-landau-quantization` Phase D 2026-05-12. Next session: execute T-2026-05-12-DV-06 via `phys130b-derivation-designer` → `phys130b-notebook-writer` handoff per `.claude/README.md` § 2.3 (one-ticket-per-session per § 1.4). No notebook files modified this session. Designer: `phys130b-derivation-designer` (audit pass — no edits, no validator run).)

Executed via `phys130b-derivation-designer` → `phys130b-notebook-writer` handoff (`.claude/README.md` § 2.3); designer-recommended **route (a) minimal typo fix** chosen over route (b) (full Step 5 rewrite) because the existing presentation structure is otherwise clean and a single-character algebraic correction preserves student-facing readability with minimal disturbance to the surrounding `:::{admonition} Derivation: Kubo evaluation on Landau-level eigenstates :class: dropdown information :::` block. **Edit applied**: Step 5 first split line `\frac{(q/m)^2\,q\hbar B}{(\hbar\omega_c)^2} &= \frac{q}{\hbar}, \\` → `\frac{(q/m)^2\,q\hbar B}{(\hbar\omega_c)^2} &= \frac{q}{\hbar B}, \\` — the Step 5 LHS now correctly evaluates to $q/(\hbar B)$ (with $\omega_c = qB/m$: $(\hbar\omega_c)^2 = \hbar^2 q^2 B^2/m^2$ and $(q/m)^2 q\hbar B = q^3\hbar B/m^2$, so the ratio is $q^3\hbar B/(\hbar^2 q^2 B^2) = q/(\hbar B)$, not $q/\hbar$), and the downstream composition now closes cleanly to $|\sigma_{xy}| = (q^2/h)(N_{\mathrm{occ}}/N_\Phi)$ via $N_\Phi = qBA/h$ (verified symbolically with sympy: Step 5 LHS = $q/(\hbar B)$ and $\sigma_{xy} = (\mathrm{i}\hbar/A)\cdot\mathrm{i}\cdot(q/(\hbar B))\cdot N_{\mathrm{occ}} = -q^2 N_{\mathrm{occ}}/(h N_\Phi)$ — magnitude matches the existing closing line). The second split line $1/A = qB/(h N_\Phi)$ stays unchanged (it was already correct); the trailing prose (*The two $\mathrm{i}$ factors out front give $\mathrm{i}^2 = -1$, which absorbs into the carrier-sign convention*) is preserved verbatim, as the carrier-sign treatment is explicitly **out of scope** of T-DV-06 per the ticket's *Out of scope* clause (separate sign-discipline issue requiring broader convention reconciliation across both arguments). **Optional companion** (expansion of *Argument 1: Laughlin's Charge Pumping* Step 1's single-line algebraic solution for $r_m^2(t)$ into the 3-line chain shown in `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` lines 3174–3180) **NOT taken** this session — the audit explicitly marked it *borderline rather than required* (a 2-substitution algebraic simplification can defensibly be a one-line jump per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Mandatory detail level*), and `rules/maintenance-scope.md` § *Prefer*: single-scope tightening rules against bundling an optional companion fix with a required typo correction when the optional fix is not strictly required by either the ticket or the audit. Should a future round identify the compressed step as a barrier in practice, it can be filed as its own Phase D ticket. **Scope discipline** per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Scope and pedagogical fit* and `rules/maintenance-scope.md`: edit stayed strictly inside the existing `:::{dropdown information}` Kubo derivation block; the *Kubo formula* `important` box was not touched (its derivation lives in §5.2.3 per the lecture's explicit `{eq}` ref); no §4.3.2 Landau-level wavefunction content imported; no Argument 1 single-valuedness chain back to $\psi_m \propto \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}m\varphi}$ added; the *Robustness from Disorder* section and all `important` / `dropdown poll` / `seealso` blocks are untouched. Cell-2 length **15989 → 15991 chars** (delta **+2**) via a single raw-string `r'...'` `text.replace` call with `assert text.count(old_block) == 1` and `assert old_block in text` guards before replacement and `assert new_block in text` plus `r'\frac{q}{\hbar},' not in text` (the wrong-sign trailing-comma form) after replacement (per `rules/notebook-editing.md` § *LaTeX in JSON* — the target string contains `\h`, `\f`, `\o`, `\m` near `\hbar`, `\frac`, `\omega_c`, `\mathrm`, so raw-string is required to avoid JSON control-escape corruption); JSON written with `json.dump(indent=1, ensure_ascii=False)`. Symbolic algebra spot-check (sympy `simplify`) confirmed both the Step 5 LHS = $q/(\hbar B)$ and the downstream identity $|\sigma_{xy}| = q^2 N_{\mathrm{occ}}/(h N_\Phi)$. **Validators clean** — `python3 .claude/skills/notebook-writer/scripts/safe_edit.py validate notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-3-3-quantum-hall-effect.ipynb` → *OK: 1 notebook(s) validated, no issues found* and `python3 .claude/scripts/validate_project.py --scope 4-3-3-quantum-hall-effect` → *Notebooks checked: 1 / Rule docs checked: .claude/rules/physics-conventions.md / All validations passed* per `rules/validation.md`. **Phase cursor advances to `4-4-1-classical-spin` at Phase A (Validate)** per `.claude/README.md` § 1.3 — the notebook `4-3-3-quantum-hall-effect` has now cycled through Phases A → B → C → D this round (alongside 4-1-1 / 4-1-2 / 4-1-3 / 4-2-1 / 4-2-2 / 4-2-3 / 4-2-3-flux-ring / 4-3-1 / 4-3-2 — ten Ch 4 subsections complete for Round 1). cell 3 HW Problem 4 likewise pairs `A_\theta=0,\qquad A_\varphi=…` — all forbidden by `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Display Math (*Do NOT use `\quad` or `\qquad` to lay out multiple equations side-by-side in one display block.* … *stack them vertically, using `\begin{split}...\end{split}` where needed*); (ii) cell 2 uses `\begin{aligned}\ldots\end{aligned}` inside `\boxed{…}` for the two boxed equations *Equation of Motion for the Spin Axis* (`eq-spin-axis-dynamics`) and the analog *Spin as a Charged Particle on a Monopole Sphere* — forbidden by `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Display Math (*Do NOT use `align` or `aligned` environments in notebooks*; use `\begin{split}` instead); (iii) lowercase `\rm` (in `s_{\rm spin}`, `s_{\rm mono}`, `\Phi_{\mathrm{Berry}}` is correct already — `\mathrm` per ISO80000-2 convention — but `s_{\rm spin}` and `s_{\rm mono}` use `\rm` not `\mathrm`); borderline notation drift, will assess at Phase B against `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Upright Symbols. **Other conventions are clean** in this notebook: SI EM units (no `(q/c)\boldsymbol A`, no Gaussian markers); `\boldsymbol` vectors throughout (no `\vec`, no `\boldsymbol{\nabla}`); `\mathrm{i/e/d}` upright differentials where they appear (e.g. `\mathrm d\boldsymbol l`); no `\cdot` between scalars; no `\chi` (Ch 4 gauge-function rule); ket/bra not used here; no `---` horizontal rules; no nested `:::` admonitions; no `plt.show()`; display-math `$$` blank-line spacing clean (validator-verified). **Cursor advances A → B (Notation) on `4-4-1-classical-spin`** per `.claude/README.md` § 1.3 (audit-vs-fix split — Phase A audit is complete regardless of whether the read-ahead Phase B issues have produced tickets yet; (Ch 4–6 Round 1 — Phase B audit on `4-4-1-classical-spin` 2026-05-12 **complete**: read-only notation sweep of cells 2 and 3 of `notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-4-1-classical-spin.ipynb` against `rules/physics-conventions.md` per the Phase B checklist in `skills/maintenance-phases/SKILL.md` (designer: `phys130b-notebook-writer` for an audit pass — no edits, no save, no validator run; designers do not run validator CLIs per `.claude/README.md` § 2.1). **Findings cluster on two display-math layout rules plus one upright-symbol drift.** **(α) `\qquad` / `\quad` side-by-side equations in one display block** — forbidden by `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Display Math (*"Do NOT use `\quad` or `\qquad` to lay out multiple equations side-by-side in one display block. When presenting multiple equations/definitions, stack them vertically (one relation per line), using `\begin{split}...\end{split}` where needed."*). **5 violations**: cell 2 § *Spin Axis and Angular Momentum* opening (`\boldsymbol n(t),\qquad \boldsymbol n^2=1.` — `eq-spin-axis-unit`); cell 2 § *Spin as a Charged Particle on a Monopole Sphere* dimensionless-form line (`s_{\rm spin}=\frac{S}{\hbar}\quad\leftrightarrow\quad |s_{\rm mono}|=\left|\frac{qg}{4\pi\hbar}\right|.` — `eq-spin-monopole-qnum-preview`); cell 2 § *Quantization from Berry Phase* full-sphere condition (`\frac{4\pi S}{\hbar}=2\pi n, \qquad n\in\mathbb Z.`); cell 2 *Spin quantization as monopole flux quantization* `important` box (`S=\hbar\frac{n}{2}, \qquad s\equiv\frac{S}{\hbar}=\frac{n}{2}.` — `eq-spin-quantization`); cell 3 HW Problem 4 *Curl of the monopole potential* setup (`A_\theta=0, \qquad A_\varphi=S\frac{\cos\theta-1}{\sin\theta}.`). Confirms and extends Phase A read-ahead observation (i). Parallel to T-2026-05-11-NB-01 on `4-3-1`, NB-02 on `4-3-2`, NB-05 on `4-3-3` — same `\qquad` layout rule on each Ch 4 subsection so far. **(β) `\begin{aligned}` inside `\boxed{...}`** — forbidden by `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Display Math (*"Do NOT use `align` or `aligned` environments in notebooks (unsafe parsing around leading commutators like `[A,B]`)"*; canonical multiline form is `\begin{split}...\end{split}`). **2 violations**, both in cell 2: § *Equation of Motion for the Spin Axis* boxed `eq-spin-axis-dynamics` (`\boxed{\begin{aligned} I\left.\ddot{\boldsymbol n}\right|_\perp &= \boldsymbol\tau\times\boldsymbol n - \dot{\boldsymbol n}\times\boldsymbol S \end{aligned}}`) and § *Spin as a Charged Particle on a Monopole Sphere* analog Lorentz-force box (`\boxed{\begin{aligned} m\left.\ddot{\boldsymbol x}\right|_\perp &= \boldsymbol E_\perp + q\dot{\boldsymbol x}\times\boldsymbol B \end{aligned}}`). Each box contains a single `&=` line, so the `\begin{aligned}` wrapper is unnecessary; cleanest fix is to drop the wrapper. Confirms Phase A read-ahead observation (ii). New violation pattern not seen on `4-3-1` / `4-3-2` / `4-3-3` (all of which used `\begin{split}` inside `$$`); the `\boxed{\begin{aligned}}` construction appears unique to this notebook so far in Ch 4. **(γ) `\rm` vs `\mathrm` notation drift** — `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Upright Symbols prescribes `\mathrm{...}` for upright text (table lists `\mathrm{i}`, `\mathrm{e}`, `\mathrm{d}`; project-wide pattern extends to subscript labels — `\Phi_{\mathrm{Berry}}`, `\Phi_{\mathrm{path}}`, `\Phi_{\mathrm{AB}}`, `\rho_{\mathrm{th}}`, etc.). **2 occurrences** in cell 2's `eq-spin-monopole-qnum-preview` line: `s_{\rm spin}` and `s_{\rm mono}`. Inconsistent with `\Phi_{\mathrm{Berry}}` used elsewhere in the same notebook (cell 2 § *Quantization from Berry Phase* and `eq-spin-berry-phase`) and the project-wide convention. Confirms Phase A read-ahead observation (iii). **Other conventions are clean** in the notebook: SI EM units throughout (no Gaussian markers, no `(q/c)\boldsymbol A`); `\boldsymbol` vectors throughout — `\boldsymbol n`, `\boldsymbol S`, `\boldsymbol\tau`, `\boldsymbol\Omega`, `\boldsymbol L`, `\boldsymbol B`, `\boldsymbol A`, `\boldsymbol e_\varphi`, `\boldsymbol x`, `\boldsymbol E_\perp` (no `\vec`); `\mathrm{i/e/d}` upright differentials and Euler/imaginary throughout (`\mathrm d\boldsymbol l`, `\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}S\Omega(\theta)/\hbar}`, `\mathrm{e}^{-4\pi\mathrm{i}}`, `\mathrm{e}^{-2\pi\mathrm{i}}`, `\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}\pi}`); `\nabla_\perp` plain (no `\boldsymbol{\nabla}`); no `\cdot` between scalars; no `\chi` (Ch 4 gauge-function rule); ket/bra not used here (no kets in this lesson — `\vert` rule N/A); no `---` horizontal rules; no nested `:::` admonitions; no `plt.show()`; uppercase `\Phi_{\mathrm{Berry}}` per § Phase Symbols (Berry-phase symbol convention satisfied); `\varphi` reserved for azimuthal angle in `\boldsymbol e_\varphi`, `A_\varphi`, `(\theta,\varphi)`; display-math `$$` blank-line spacing clean (Phase A validator-verified). **One borderline observation NOT filed** (intentional scope discipline): the abstract spin-sphere "monopole field" `\boldsymbol B(\boldsymbol n)=g/(4\pi)\boldsymbol n` (in cell 2 § *Spin as a Charged Particle on a Monopole Sphere*) omits the `\mu_0` factor that `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Chapter 4 Electromagnetic Units prescribes for real-space point-monopole fields (`\boldsymbol B=\mu_0 g\,\hat{\boldsymbol r}/(4\pi r^2)`). However, this is an **abstract analogy on the unit sphere of spin orientations**, not a real magnetic field in 3D space — the lecture's dictionary identification `qg/(4\pi) \leftrightarrow -S` gives `g` units of (angular momentum / charge), not SI magnetic-monopole charge. The Berry-phase formula `\Phi_{\mathrm{Berry}}=\hbar^{-1}\oint\boldsymbol A\cdot\mathrm d\boldsymbol l` likewise omits the AB-phase `q` factor (per § Chapter 4 EM Units, `q\oint\boldsymbol A\cdot\mathrm d\boldsymbol l/\hbar`) because the abstract `\boldsymbol A` already absorbs the charge into its scale. Treating this as intentional scope discipline (abstract spin-sphere structure preparing §4.4.2–§4.4.3, not a real-EM monopole problem), no ticket. Should the convention in `4-4-2-dirac-monopole.ipynb` use `\mu_0 g` for the real EM monopole, a future Phase B / consistency audit can revisit whether the spin-sphere `g` should be relabeled (e.g. `g_{\mathrm{spin}}`) to avoid conflation, but that is out of scope for a single-notebook Phase B pass. **Filed as three single-rule tickets** in the Ticket Queue below: **T-2026-05-12-NB-07** (`\qquad`/`\quad` → `\begin{split}` stacking, 4 sites in cell 2 + 1 site in cell 3 HW4), **T-2026-05-12-NB-08** (`\begin{aligned}` inside `\boxed{...}` → drop wrapper or use `\begin{split}`, 2 sites in cell 2), **T-2026-05-12-NB-09** (`\rm` → `\mathrm` migration, 2 occurrences on one line in cell 2's `eq-spin-monopole-qnum-preview`). All three routed to `phys130b-notebook-writer` direct per `.claude/README.md` § 2.2 (mechanical layout / notation fixes; math content unchanged; single rule per ticket; one-ticket-per-session per `.claude/README.md` § 1.4). the time-dependence declaration `\boldsymbol n = \boldsymbol n(t)` is mathematically equivalent to the original notational label and reads more explicitly with the alignment. **(β)** cell 2 § *Spin as a Charged Particle on a Monopole Sphere* dimensionless-form line (`eq-spin-monopole-qnum-preview`) — `$$s_{\rm spin}=\frac{S}{\hbar}\quad\leftrightarrow\quad |s_{\rm mono}|=\left|\frac{qg}{4\pi\hbar}\right|.$$` stacked per designer route (iii) as `s_{\rm spin} &= \frac{S}{\hbar}, \\ |s_{\rm mono}| &= \left|\frac{qg}{4\pi\hbar}\right|.` — the `\quad\leftrightarrow\quad` connector dropped; the parallel-definition identification is conveyed by the existing lead-in prose *In dimensionless form,* together with the existing follow-up sentence *This is the bridge to the monopole quantum number used in §4.4.3.* The lead-in/follow-up prose was deliberately left **untouched** — both already convey the correspondence semantically, so no prose adjustment was needed (route iii of the ticket allows the writer to use surrounding prose to carry the ↔ identification, and the existing surrounding prose already does so). **(γ)** cell 2 § *Quantization from Berry Phase* full-sphere quantization — `$$\frac{4\pi S}{\hbar}=2\pi n, \qquad n\in\mathbb Z.$$` stacked per designer route (i) as `\frac{4\pi S}{\hbar} &= 2\pi n, \\ n &\in \mathbb Z.` (route ii would have been equally valid by absorbing *for $n\in\mathbb Z$* into surrounding prose, but route i keeps the display self-contained and matches the precedent set by T-2026-05-11-NB-01 / NB-02 / T-2026-05-12-NB-05 on the prior three Ch 4 subsections). **(δ)** cell 2 *Spin quantization as monopole flux quantization* `important` box (`eq-spin-quantization`) — `$$S=\hbar\frac{n}{2}, \qquad s\equiv\frac{S}{\hbar}=\frac{n}{2}.$$` stacked as `S &= \hbar\frac{n}{2}, \\ s &\equiv \frac{S}{\hbar} = \frac{n}{2}.` — the `\equiv` symbol on the second line aligns at the `&` with the `=` symbol on the first line, which is the conventional split-block layout for parallel definitions. **(ε)** cell 3 HW Problem 4 *Curl of the monopole potential* setup — `$$A_\theta=0, \qquad A_\varphi=S\frac{\cos\theta-1}{\sin\theta}.$$` stacked as `A_\theta &= 0, \\ A_\varphi &= S\frac{\cos\theta-1}{\sin\theta}.` with `&=` alignment. **Out of scope** per the ticket: T-2026-05-12-NB-09 (`\rm` → `\mathrm` migration, still open — site (β) still contains `s_{\rm spin}` / `s_{\rm mono}` in the new `\begin{split}` block, awaiting NB-09 execution; NB-09's old/new strings will need to be updated to target the new split form rather than the original `\quad\leftrightarrow\quad` single-line form, as the NB-09 ticket explicitly anticipated under its *Coordination with T-NB-07* clause); (Ch 4–6 Round 1 — Phase D audit on `4-4-1-classical-spin` 2026-05-12 **complete**: read-only chain-quality sweep of the three `:::{dropdown information}` derivation blocks in cell 2 of `notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-4-1-classical-spin.ipynb` per `rules/derivation-quality.md` (§ *Root causes of quality gaps*, § *Mandatory procedure*, § *Minimum derivation depth*, § *Mandatory detail level*, § *Anti-patterns*, § *Scope and pedagogical fit*) and per the `phys130b-derivation-designer` skill. `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` § *Classical Spin* (lines ~3829–4068) and § *Magnetic Monopole* (lines ~4598–4793) confirmed to carry parallel material; structural-diff procedure applied. **Finding 1 — *Derivation: kinematics of the spin axis*: NEEDS WORK (depth gap, missing algebra).** The dropdown title promises a derivation but the block asserts the key inversion $\boldsymbol\Omega=\boldsymbol n\times\dot{\boldsymbol n}$ directly after the prose "With this transverse choice," without showing the algebraic step that gets there. `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` lines 3961–3972 derive the inversion in ~4 displayed lines: cross with $\boldsymbol n$ from the left on $\dot{\boldsymbol n}=\boldsymbol\Omega\times\boldsymbol n$, apply the BAC-CAB identity $\boldsymbol n\times(\boldsymbol\Omega\times\boldsymbol n)=\boldsymbol\Omega(\boldsymbol n\cdot\boldsymbol n)-\boldsymbol n(\boldsymbol n\cdot\boldsymbol\Omega)$, then use $\boldsymbol n\cdot\boldsymbol n=1$ and the transverse condition $\boldsymbol n\cdot\boldsymbol\Omega=0$ to collapse to $\boldsymbol\Omega$. Per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Mandatory detail level* ("4–7 lines per commutator/identity evaluation; a 2-line derivation that jumps from the definition to the answer is a rule violation unless the result genuinely follows in one step of trivial algebra"). The inversion here is **not** one step of trivial algebra — it is a BAC-CAB invocation that solves the cross-product equation, and it is exactly the chain that HW Problem 1(b) asks students to do independently ("Assuming $\boldsymbol\Omega$ is tangent to the sphere, show that $\boldsymbol\Omega=\boldsymbol n\times\dot{\boldsymbol n}$"). The lecture dropdown should either show the algebra explicitly (3–4 added displayed lines) or, less preferably, add an explicit *(see HW 1(b) for the derivation)* annotation; the dropdown's *Derivation:* title makes the algebra-showing route strongly preferred (per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Mandatory procedure* item 4 *Show every commutator/identity evaluation* and the principle that a derivation dropdown is "the reference-grade proof that a student (or AI) can follow step by step"). Filed as **T-2026-05-12-DV-07** in the queue below; designer-preferred **route (a)** is to show the algebra inline. **Finding 2 — *Derivation: spin-axis equation*: PASS.** Forward chain — differentiate $\boldsymbol L=S\boldsymbol n+I\boldsymbol n\times\dot{\boldsymbol n}$ to get $\boldsymbol\tau=S\dot{\boldsymbol n}+I\boldsymbol n\times\ddot{\boldsymbol n}$ (with $\dot{\boldsymbol n}\times\dot{\boldsymbol n}=0$ noted); cross with $\boldsymbol n$ from the right; apply BAC-CAB to $(\boldsymbol n\times\ddot{\boldsymbol n})\times\boldsymbol n$ to get the tangent acceleration $\ddot{\boldsymbol n}|_\perp=\ddot{\boldsymbol n}-\boldsymbol n(\boldsymbol n\cdot\ddot{\boldsymbol n})$; identify $\boldsymbol S=S\boldsymbol n$ to write the gyroscopic term as $-\dot{\boldsymbol n}\times\boldsymbol S$. Four displayed equations match the level of detail expected for the spherically-symmetric single-$I$ simplification of `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` lines 3993–4068, which uses both $I_a$ and $I_t$ and is therefore one substitution step longer. No skipped commutators, no paper-overs, no wrong signs; the lecture's single-$I$ simplification is a deliberate pedagogical choice consistent with the lesson's opening "Assume the spinning object is spherically symmetric, so a single moment of inertia $I$ describes rotations." The final identification $S\dot{\boldsymbol n}\times\boldsymbol n=-\dot{\boldsymbol n}\times(S\boldsymbol n)=-\dot{\boldsymbol n}\times\boldsymbol S$ (a sign-flip rearrangement using the antisymmetry of $\times$) is compressed into "Using $\boldsymbol S=S\boldsymbol n$ then gives {eq}`eq-spin-axis-dynamics`" — borderline but defensible since it is one elementary step of trivial algebra. **Finding 3 — *Check: curl of the spin-sphere potential*: PASS, optional companion candidate.** Forward chain — state surface-curl formula for $\varphi$-only $\boldsymbol A$ on the unit sphere → substitute $A_\varphi=S(\cos\theta-1)/\sin\theta$ → evaluate $(1/\sin\theta)\partial_\theta[S(\cos\theta-1)]=-S$ → conclude $\nabla_\perp\times\boldsymbol A=-S\boldsymbol n$. Two displayed equations vs `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` lines 4666–4673 which shows 5. The compression skips the explicit $A_\varphi\sin\theta=S(\cos\theta-1)$ substitution as a separate displayed step. Both algebraic moves are individually trivial (one substitution + one elementary derivative $\partial_\theta(\cos\theta-1)=-\sin\theta$), so the rule's "trivial algebra" exception applies; HW Problem 4 also walks students through this computation as an exercise. Not separately ticket-worthy this round; flagged as an **optional companion** that the designer may bundle into T-2026-05-12-DV-07's implementation if convenient (route: split the second displayed equation into 3 lines — $A_\varphi\sin\theta=S(\cos\theta-1)$ → $\partial_\theta[S(\cos\theta-1)]=-S\sin\theta$ → $(1/\sin\theta)(-S\sin\theta)=-S$ — matching `_refs/` line by line). **Other potential Phase D items confirmed NOT ticket-worthy** (per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Scope and pedagogical fit* — *Adding detail* must slice existing steps finer, not import new building-block results, and one focus per derivation): (i) the boxed *Equation of Motion for the Spin Axis* `eq-spin-axis-dynamics` and the analog Lorentz-force `\boxed{...}` block in § *Spin as a Charged Particle on a Monopole Sphere* are **statements** of results, not derivation chains — the chain is in the *spin-axis equation* dropdown (Finding 2); (ii) the *Spin-monopole dictionary* `important` box (5-row `\begin{array}{ccl}` correspondence) is a parallel-definition table, not a derivation; (iii) the boxed *Spin quantization as monopole flux quantization* `important` box ($S=\hbar n/2$, `eq-spin-quantization`) is the **conclusion** of the Berry-phase quantization argument which itself is body prose, not a dropdown — the argument from $\Phi_{\mathrm{Berry}}=-S\Omega/\hbar$ for a closed loop to $4\pi S/\hbar=2\pi n$ for the full sphere is essentially one identification step (set $\Omega=4\pi$ on the full sphere, demand single-valuedness modulo $2\pi$), which is exhibited in the body; HW Problem 5 (latitude Berry phase) and HW Problem 6 (single-valuedness across spin values) walk students through the per-cap Berry-phase computation and the half-integer ladder explicitly — these belong to homework-designer territory, not derivation-designer Phase D scope; (iv) the **Berry-connection formula** $\Phi_{\mathrm{Berry}}[\mathcal C]=(1/\hbar)\oint\boldsymbol A\cdot\mathrm d\boldsymbol l$ in § *Quantization from Berry Phase* is **stated** as a definition, not derived in this lesson — the actual derivation lives in §4.2.1 (Berry Phase, see See Also link), so no scope expansion; (v) the abstract spin-sphere $\boldsymbol B(\boldsymbol n)=(g/(4\pi))\boldsymbol n$ convention (no $\mu_0$ factor) is intentional scope discipline per the Phase B audit on this notebook — it is an abstract analogy on the unit sphere of spin orientations, not a real EM monopole field; no action. **Cursor stays at D / `4-4-1-classical-spin`** with 1 open D ticket (T-2026-05-12-DV-07) per `.claude/README.md` § 1.3 and § 1.4 — advances to next notebook `4-4-2-dirac-monopole` at Phase A only after T-DV-07 closes, following the precedent set by `4-3-1-cyclotron-motion` Phase D 2026-05-11 (cursor stayed at D until T-DV-04 closed before advancing to `4-3-2`), `4-3-2-landau-quantization` Phase D 2026-05-12 (cursor stayed at D until T-DV-05 closed before advancing to `4-3-3`), and `4-3-3-quantum-hall-effect` Phase D 2026-05-12 (cursor stayed at D until T-DV-06 closed before advancing to `4-4-1`). No notebook files modified this session. Designer: `phys130b-derivation-designer` (audit pass — no edits, no validator run; designers do not run validator CLIs per `.claude/README.md` § 2.1).)

(Ch 4–6 Round 1 — Phase B audit on `4-4-2-dirac-monopole` 2026-05-13 **complete**: read-only notation sweep of cells 1, 2, and 3 against `rules/physics-conventions.md` per the Phase B checklist in `skills/maintenance-phases/SKILL.md` (designer: `phys130b-notebook-writer` for an audit pass — no edits, no save). **Findings — two single-rule notation tickets + three read-ahead observations.** **(i) Ch 4 monopole SI convention drift (§ Chapter 4 Electromagnetic Units).** The rule mandates point-monopole field $\boldsymbol{B} = \mu_0 g\,\hat{\boldsymbol{r}}/(4\pi r^2)$, divergence $\nabla\cdot\boldsymbol{B} = \mu_0\rho_m$, and total monopole flux $\mu_0 g$. The notebook explicitly states in cell 2 § *The Concept of a Magnetic Monopole* *\"For the monopole discussion we use the standard Dirac-monopole convention where magnetic charge is defined directly by flux\"* — i.e., $g$ absorbs $\mu_0$ — and threads that convention through every monopole equation in cells 2 and 3 (~10 sites). Affected sites: cell 2 § *The Concept of a Magnetic Monopole* (Maxwell with monopole source line $\nabla\cdot\boldsymbol{B} = \rho_m$, point-monopole field), § *The Problem: Vector Potential Singularity* ($\nabla\cdot\boldsymbol{B} = g\delta^3(\boldsymbol{r})$), § *The Dirac String Solution* ($\boldsymbol{A}^{(N)}$ curl form and prefactor $g/(4\pi)$), § *Aharonov-Bohm Phase and Quantization* (closed-surface flux integral $\oint\boldsymbol{B}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{S} = g$ and downstream $qg = 2\pi n\hbar$), § *Charge Quantization* ($eg = 2\pi n_e\hbar$), § *Two-Patch Gauge Formulation* ($\boldsymbol{A}^{(N)}/\boldsymbol{A}^{(S)}$ forms, gauge function $\alpha(\varphi) = g\varphi/(2\pi)$, single-valuedness $qg/\hbar = 2\pi n$); cell 3 HW1 ($\boldsymbol{B} = g\hat{\boldsymbol{r}}/(4\pi r^2)$ explicitly labeled *\"the SI field\"*), HW2 ($\boldsymbol{A}^{(N)}$ and curl), HW3 ($g_0 = h/e$ in this convention), HW4, HW5 ($U_{NS}(\varphi) = \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}n\varphi}$ with $n = qg/h$), HW7 ($s = qg/(4\pi\hbar)$); cell 1 Prompts contains no equations directly affected. Filed as **T-2026-05-13-NB-10** in the queue below. Designer routes split between (a) bring notebook into rule compliance by inserting $\mu_0$ throughout (heavy edit, preserves rule as canonical SI source) and (b) update `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Chapter 4 EM Units to allow a *\"magnetic charge $\equiv$ flux\"* convention as an explicit exception for monopole derivations (precedent: 2026-05-06 feedback allowing $c=1$ in spacetime-unified 4-vector/tensor blocks, declared explicitly at the start of any block where it is used). The notebook's prose already declares the convention, which favors route (b) on minimal-disruption grounds; designer evaluates which the professor prefers. **(ii) Display Math `\quad` side-by-side layout (§ Display Math).** Cell 2 § *The Concept of a Magnetic Monopole* Maxwell-equations display lays out two relations per row using `& \quad`: `\nabla\cdot\boldsymbol{E} = \rho_e/\epsilon_0,& \quad \nabla\times\boldsymbol{E} = -\partial_t\boldsymbol{B}, \\ \nabla\cdot\boldsymbol{B} = 0,& \quad \nabla\times\boldsymbol{B} = \mu_0(\boldsymbol{j}_e + \epsilon_0\partial_t\boldsymbol{E})`. Violates the rule *\"Do NOT use `\quad` or `\qquad` to lay out multiple equations side-by-side in one display block. When presenting multiple equations/definitions, stack them vertically (one relation per line), using `\begin{split}...\end{split}` where needed.\"* Plus one borderline: § *The Dirac String Solution* `\nabla\times\boldsymbol{A}^{(N)} = (g/(4\pi))\hat{\boldsymbol{r}}/r^2 \quad \text{for } \theta < \pi` — condition tag, not a parallel equation; the rule's prohibition targets *equations* side-by-side, not annotation tags. Filed as **T-2026-05-13-NB-11** — direct parallel to T-2026-05-11-NB-01 (4-3-1, 6 sites), T-2026-05-11-NB-02 (4-3-2, 6 sites), T-2026-05-12-NB-05 (4-3-3, 5 sites), T-2026-05-12-NB-07 (4-4-1, 5 sites). **Other conventions clean** (no findings): gauge function symbol `\alpha` (correct per § Chapter 4 EM Units — no `\chi`, no `\Lambda`); $\Phi_{\mathrm{AB}}$ for AB phase with correct `\mathrm{}` subscript per § Phase Symbols; `\varphi` for azimuth and `\hat{\boldsymbol{\varphi}}` for the corresponding unit vector (correct); `\mathrm{i/e/d}` upright operators throughout (no Unicode `ℏ`, no `\rm`); `\boldsymbol{}` vectors (no `\vec`, no `\boldsymbol{\nabla}`); `\hat{}` on operators ($\hat{\boldsymbol{r}}$, $\hat{\boldsymbol{p}}$, $\hat{\boldsymbol{J}}$, $\hat{\boldsymbol{L}}$ in HW7); no `\cdot` between scalars; no `align`/`aligned` environments anywhere in the notebook; AB-phase line-integral form $\frac{q}{\hbar}\oint\boldsymbol{A}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l}$ matches the rule's SI form (no $1/c$ factors); no kets/bras in this lesson, so the `\vert` rule is inert; equation label `(eq-dirac-quantization)` clean per `myst-references.md`. **Three read-ahead observations retained for later phases** (not filed as Phase B tickets): **(R1)** cell 3 HW3(a) *\"For an electron ($q = -e$)\"* — uses $-e$ for electron carrier while cell 2 § *Charge Quantization from Monopoles* writes $eg = 2\pi n_e\hbar$ with $e$ implicitly positive. Direct parallel to T-2026-05-11-HD-01 (4-3-1 HW3) and T-2026-05-11-HD-03 (4-3-2 HW2/HW6) body→HW carrier-convention drift. Phase C (`homework-designer`) territory — **not** Phase B. **(R2)** Cell 2 § *The Problem: Vector Potential Singularity* prose `$\nabla\cdot\boldsymbol{B} = g\,\delta^3(\boldsymbol{r}) \neq 0$ everywhere on the sphere far from the origin` — the $\delta^3$ is supported only at the origin, so the *\"everywhere on the sphere far from the origin\"* clause is logically inconsistent with the $\delta^3$ form. Likely intended: $\oint\boldsymbol{B}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{S} = g$ over any sphere enclosing the origin. Phase C/D (`lecture-designer` or `derivation-designer`) prose tightening — **not** Phase B. **(R3)** Maxwell-equations display uses `\frac{\partial \boldsymbol{B}}{\partial t}` form, while the rule's canonical SI examples use the shorter operator form `\partial_t\boldsymbol{B}`. Project usage is mixed; the rule lists `\partial_t` as the canonical example but does not strictly forbid `\frac{\partial}{\partial t}`. Borderline style discipline — **not** Phase B; bundle into NB-11 only if the writer wants to tighten both during the same `\begin{split}` rewrite, otherwise leave as-is. **Cursor advances B → C (Style) on `4-4-2-dirac-monopole`** per `.claude/README.md` § 1.3 (audit-vs-fix split — Phase B audit work is complete regardless of whether the resulting NB-10 / NB-11 tickets have been executed). Next session: either execute one of T-2026-05-13-NB-10 / NB-11 via the matching designer → `phys130b-notebook-writer` handoff (one-ticket-per-session per `.claude/README.md` § 1.4), or, if the queue is empty when that session starts, proceed to the Phase C (Style) audit on `4-4-2-dirac-monopole` per `skills/maintenance-phases/SKILL.md` Phase C checklist via `lecture-designer` / `prompt-designer` / `homework-designer` / `summary-designer` as needed. No notebook files were modified this session.)
