(Ch 4–6 Round 1 — Phase D audit on `4-4-2-dirac-monopole` 2026-05-13 **complete**: read-only chain-quality sweep of the two derivation chains in cell 2 of `notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-4-2-dirac-monopole.ipynb` per `rules/derivation-quality.md` (§ *Root causes of quality gaps*, § *Mandatory procedure*, § *Minimum derivation depth*, § *Anti-patterns*) and per the `phys130b-derivation-designer` skill. **Chains assessed**: **(1)** body-prose derivation in § *Aharonov-Bohm Phase and Quantization* — define AB phase $\Phi_{\mathrm{AB}} = (q/\hbar)\oint\boldsymbol{A}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l}$ → state two-patch gauge $\boldsymbol{A}^{(N)} - \boldsymbol{A}^{(S)} = \nabla\alpha$ on equatorial overlap → wavefunction transition $\psi^{(N)} = \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}q\alpha/\hbar}\psi^{(S)}$ → single-valuedness $(q/\hbar)[\alpha(2\pi)-\alpha(0)] = 2\pi n$ → winding-equals-flux $\alpha(2\pi)-\alpha(0) = \oint\boldsymbol{B}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{S} = g$ → result $qg = 2\pi n\hbar$ `(eq-dirac-quantization)`; **(2)** the `:::{admonition} Derivation: same condition from the single-patch loop integral :class: dropdown information` block — compute $\oint_{\mathcal{C}}\boldsymbol{A}^{(N)}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l}$ on equator $\theta=\pi/2$ → naive half-flux $g/2$ and naive phase $\Phi_{\mathrm{AB}}^{(N)} = qg/(2\hbar)$ → repeat with $\boldsymbol{A}^{(S)}$ → opposite sign $\Phi_{\mathrm{AB}}^{(S)} = -qg/(2\hbar)$ → difference $\Phi^{(N)}-\Phi^{(S)} = qg/\hbar = 2\pi n$ recovers the body's quantization condition. **`_refs/` consultation**: `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` § *Magnetic Monopole* lines 4070–4793 carries parallel content but in a **different framing** — the abstract *spin-axis on a unit sphere* metaphor with $S = \hbar s$ playing the role of monopole charge ($A_\varphi = S(\cos\theta-1)/\sin\theta$, gauge function $\chi(\varphi) = -2(S/\hbar)\varphi$, quantization $2S/\hbar \in \mathbb{Z}$). The notebook's framing is the **concrete EM Dirac monopole in $\mathbb{R}^3$** (with $g$ as magnetic charge); the two presentations are unitarily equivalent but structurally distinct, so the structural-diff procedure of `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Mandatory procedure* item 8 applies only partially. The rule's other criteria (forward chain, no skipped steps, no reverse-engineering from answer, motivation for each chosen form, single-focus per derivation, symbol consistency, student-confusion test) are fully applicable and were used for the audit. **Finding 1 — Body derivation in § *Aharonov-Bohm Phase and Quantization*: PARTIAL.** Chain steps (i) → (vi) are well-laid-out, but step (v) — *winding equals total flux* — is a single displayed equation `\alpha(2\pi)-\alpha(0) = \oint\boldsymbol{B}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{S} = \int(g/4\pi r^2)r^2\sin\theta\,\mathrm{d}\theta\,\mathrm{d}\varphi = g` that asserts the first equality (gauge-function winding = surface flux of $\boldsymbol{B}$) **without intermediate derivation**. The actual bridge requires Stokes' theorem applied separately to the **upper hemisphere** (where $\boldsymbol{A}^{(N)}$ is smooth, so $\oint_{eq}\boldsymbol{A}^{(N)}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l} = \int_{\mathrm{upper}}\boldsymbol{B}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{S} = +g/2$) and the **lower hemisphere** (where $\boldsymbol{A}^{(S)}$ is smooth and the equator's orientation is opposite to the bounded-region normal, so $\oint_{eq}\boldsymbol{A}^{(S)}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l} = -\int_{\mathrm{lower}}\boldsymbol{B}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{S} = -g/2$), then subtraction using $\boldsymbol{A}^{(N)}-\boldsymbol{A}^{(S)} = \nabla\alpha$: $\alpha(2\pi)-\alpha(0) = \oint_{eq}\nabla\alpha\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l} = +g/2 - (-g/2) = g = \oint_{S^2}\boldsymbol{B}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{S}$. The notebook compresses three logical steps (apply Stokes on each patch + subtract + identify total flux) into a single equality. Per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Root causes* #1 (*Skipped intermediate steps* — *the derivation jumps from premises to conclusions without showing the evaluations that connect them*) and § *Mandatory procedure* item 4 (*Show every commutator / identity evaluation* — *''It can be shown that'' is banned in derivation dropdowns*) and item 5 (*State building-block results first*). Filed as **T-2026-05-13-DV-08** in the queue below; designer recommendation: insert a 3–4 displayed-line Stokes-bridge between the *''The winding... equals the total magnetic flux''* sentence and the displayed equation (route (a) inline; route (b) small `dropdown information` block). **Finding 2 — *Derivation: same condition from the single-patch loop integral* `dropdown information`: PASS.** Forward chain (compute equator line integral → half-flux $g/2$ from upper-hemisphere Stokes → naive phase $qg/(2\hbar)$ → repeat with south patch → opposite sign $-qg/(2\hbar)$ → difference $qg/\hbar = 2\pi n$ matches body result); algebra correct ($(1-\cos(\pi/2))/\sin(\pi/2) \cdot \sin(\pi/2) = 1$, $\int_0^{2\pi}(g/4\pi)\,\mathrm{d}\varphi = g/2$), signs consistent with the body's single-valuedness argument, no reverse-engineering pattern, single-focus on the alternative path. **One borderline algebraic compression**: the integrand display $\frac{g}{4\pi}\frac{(1-\cos(\pi/2))}{\sin(\pi/2)}\sin(\pi/2)\,\mathrm{d}\varphi$ presents both a $\sin(\pi/2)$ factor (entering from $\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l} = r\sin\theta\,\mathrm{d}\varphi\,\hat{\boldsymbol{\varphi}}$ on the equatorial circle of radius $r\sin\theta$) and a $1/\sin(\pi/2)$ factor (from $\boldsymbol{A}^{(N)} = (g/4\pi r)(1-\cos\theta)/\sin\theta\,\hat{\boldsymbol{\varphi}}$) as if they had appeared independently — they cancel exactly at $\theta = \pi/2$. A student unfamiliar with the parametrization might read this as algebraic coincidence rather than parameter-canceling structure. Bundled into T-DV-08 as an **optional companion** (per `rules/derivation-quality.md` precedents T-DV-04 on `4-3-1-cyclotron-motion` and T-DV-07 on `4-4-1-classical-spin`, both of which bundled optional companions into the main Phase D fix). **Other potential Phase D items confirmed NOT ticket-worthy**: (i) the dropdown's framing *''Both phases are valid in their respective patches; their **difference** on the overlap is what must be a multiple of $2\pi$''* — this restates the body's single-valuedness conclusion two paragraphs earlier, so it is technically grounded in the body derivation; minor presentation tightness, not a derivation gap; (ii) HW Problem 2(a) *Verify that $\nabla\times\boldsymbol{A}^{(N)} = (g/4\pi r^2)\hat{\boldsymbol{r}}$ for $\theta\neq\pi$* — homework exercise (the student is supposed to *do* the verification), not a lecture derivation; (iii) HW Problem 5 winding-number argument and HW Problem 7 angular momentum in monopole field — exercises for students, outside Phase D scope (any depth issues would be `homework-designer` territory); (iv) the *Topological Origin of Quantization* `important` box stating the $\pi_1(U(1)) = \mathbb{Z}$ classification — a *result statement* (admonition with no derivation chain), not a derivation; (v) the *Charge Quantization from Monopoles* algebra ($eg = 2\pi n_e\hbar$, $qg = 2\pi n_q\hbar$, $q/e = n_q/n_e$) — forward chain, trivial division, no missing steps. **Scope discipline** per `rules/maintenance-scope.md` and `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Scope and pedagogical fit*: the proposed Stokes-bridge addition stays inside the body derivation's existing scope — no scope creep into Wu-Yang formalism, monopole harmonics, or fiber-bundle classification that lives in §4.4.3 or §4.4-parent; the optional companion stays inside the existing `:::{dropdown information}` block. **Cursor stays at D (Derivation quality) / `4-4-2-dirac-monopole`** until T-DV-08 closes (audit-vs-fix split per `.claude/README.md` § 1.4); upon close, the notebook will have cycled through Phases A → B → C → D this round (alongside 4-1-1 / 4-1-2 / 4-1-3 / 4-2-1 / 4-2-2 / 4-2-3 / 4-2-3-flux-ring / 4-3-1 / 4-3-2 / 4-3-3 / 4-4-1 — twelve Ch 4 subsections complete for Round 1), and the cursor will advance to `4-4-3-monopole-harmonics` at Phase A per the depth-first `notes_src/_toc.yml` order. No notebook files were modified this session. Designer: `phys130b-derivation-designer` (audit pass — no edits, no validator run). - ~~**T-2026-05-20-NB-64**~~ **DONE 2026-05-21** (Homework content, dimensionally-inconsistent formula, notebook-writer direct, **LOW**) — `notes_src/ch3_path-integral/3-3-3-bohr-sommerfeld-quantization.ipynb` cell 3 Homework **Problem 3(a)**: the problem asks to compute $\int_{0}^{x_a}\sqrt{2m(E - Fx)}\,\mathrm{d}x = \tfrac{2}{3F}(2mE)^{3/2}$, but the printed target $\tfrac{2}{3F}(2mE)^{3/2}$ is dimensionally inconsistent — it is larger than the true value by a factor $2m$. The correct value is $\dfrac{(2mE)^{3/2}}{3mF} = \dfrac{2\sqrt{2m}}{3F}E^{3/2}$ (verified analytically by the substitution $u = E - Fx$ and numerically with SciPy). Fix: change the printed target to $\dfrac{(2mE)^{3/2}}{3mF}$ (or the equivalent $\tfrac{2\sqrt{2m}}{3F}E^{3/2}$). Downstream parts 3(b)/3(c) build on the full orbit integral and are unaffected by the typo. Discovered while building `homework/3-3-3_solutions.md`; the solutions file already uses the corrected value and flags the discrepancy in a parenthetical note. Out of the Ch 4–6 phased-maintenance scope (Ch 3) — opportunistic. **Resolved 2026-05-21** (scheduled-task automated run, no human present): single atomic `text.replace` on cell 3 changed the printed target `\tfrac{2}{3F}(2mE)^{3/2}` → `\tfrac{(2mE)^{3/2}}{3mF}` — the true value of the integral; inline-sized `\tfrac` chosen over the ticket's `\dfrac` for consistency with the surrounding inline math (mathematically identical). Re-verified independently before editing: SymPy symbolic integration and a numeric trapezoid check both give the corrected value; the old form was larger by exactly $2m$. `safe_edit.py validate` and `validate_project.py --scope 3-3-3-bohr-sommerfeld-quantization` both clean. Follow-up filed as **T-2026-05-21-NB-68** (solutions-file sync).

- ~~**T-2026-05-20-NB-65**~~ **DONE 2026-05-20** (Phase D, derivation depth / skipped step, derivation-designer -> notebook-writer, **MEDIUM**) - `notes_src/ch6_quantum-foundations/6-1-2-entropy.ipynb` cell 2 `:::{admonition} Derivation: Maximum Entropy and Thermal State` (`dropdown information`): the variation step "Varying $S(\hat\rho)=-\operatorname{Tr}(\hat\rho\ln\hat\rho)$ ... yields $\delta S/\delta\hat\rho = -(\ln\hat\rho+\hat I)$" asserts the functional derivative with no intermediate lines - the origin of the "$+\hat I$" term is unjustified ("it can be shown that" gap, `derivation-quality.md` S Minimum derivation depth). The professor reference `_refs/QuantumStatistics.nb` (Principle of Maximum Entropy) shows the full variational gradient explicitly: $\delta\mathcal{L}/\delta p_k = -\ln p_k-\alpha-\beta E_k-1$, exposing the analogous "$-1$". Fix (one focus, scoped to this dropdown): add the missing intermediate lines - either (a) the operator variation $\delta\operatorname{Tr}(\hat\rho\ln\hat\rho)=\operatorname{Tr}[(\delta\hat\rho)\ln\hat\rho]+\operatorname{Tr}[\hat\rho\,\delta\ln\hat\rho]$ with the lemma $\operatorname{Tr}[\hat\rho\,\delta\ln\hat\rho]=\operatorname{Tr}[\delta\hat\rho]$, giving $\operatorname{Tr}[(\ln\hat\rho+\hat I)\delta\hat\rho]$; or (b, `_refs`-aligned) recast the variation in the energy eigenbasis via the already-present spectral form $S=-\sum_i\lambda_i\ln\lambda_i$, where the "$+1$" follows transparently from $\mathrm{d}(\lambda\ln\lambda)/\mathrm{d}\lambda=\ln\lambda+1$. derivation-designer audits the chain -> notebook-writer applies and validates. Discovered: Phase D audit 2026-05-20. **Resolved 2026-05-20** (scheduled-task automated run, manager agent, no human present): the one-line assertion $\delta S/\delta\hat\rho = -(\ln\hat\rho+\hat I)$ in the *Derivation: Maximum Entropy and Thermal State* dropdown (cell 2) was replaced by a five-equation chain via fix option (b) (`_refs`-aligned) - recast $\operatorname{Tr}(\hat\rho\ln\hat\rho)$ in the $\hat\rho$-eigenbasis using `eq-entropy-spectral`, take $\mathrm{d}(\lambda\ln\lambda)/\mathrm{d}\lambda = \ln\lambda+1$ (the origin of the formerly-unjustified "$+\hat I$"), apply the first-order eigenvalue shift $\delta\lambda_i = \langle\psi_i\vert\delta\hat\rho\vert\psi_i\rangle$, reassemble into $\operatorname{Tr}[(\ln\hat\rho+\hat I)\delta\hat\rho]$, read off the functional derivative. Cross-checked against `_refs/QuantumStatistics.nb` (scalar gradient $\delta\mathcal{L}/\delta p_k = -\ln p_k-\alpha-\beta E_k-1$). Scope held to the variation step; cell 2 11393 -> 12925 chars. Validators clean: `safe_edit.py validate` no issues, `validate_project.py --scope 6-1-2-entropy` all validations passed.

- **T-2026-05-20-NB-66** **OPEN** 2026-05-20 (Phase D, entropy-convention inconsistency + skipped steps, derivation-designer -> notebook-writer, **MEDIUM**) - `notes_src/ch6_quantum-foundations/6-1-2-entropy.ipynb` cell 2 section *Partition Function and Thermodynamics*: equation `eq-entropy-from-partition` writes $S(\hat\rho_{\rm th})=k_B[\beta\langle E\rangle+\ln Z]$, but the notebook defines von Neumann entropy as the dimensionless $S(\hat\rho)=-\operatorname{Tr}(\hat\rho\ln\hat\rho)$ (`eq-entropy-definition`; bounds $0\le S\le\ln d$, `eq-entropy-spectral`). The von Neumann entropy of the thermal state is $S(\hat\rho_{\rm th})=\beta\langle E\rangle+\ln Z$ (no $k_B$); the $k_B[\dots]$ form is the thermodynamic entropy $S_{\rm thermo}=k_B S_{\rm vN}$ - an unflagged convention switch (the section also uses $F=\langle E\rangle-TS$ and $S=-(\partial F/\partial T)|_V$, and the `### Summary` repeats $S=k_B[\beta\langle E\rangle+\ln Z]$). Separately, $S(\hat\rho_{\rm th})$ and $\langle E\rangle=-\mathrm{d}\ln Z/\mathrm{d}\beta$ are stated with zero intermediate lines. Fix: pick one entropy convention and apply it consistently - recommended is to keep $S$ dimensionless throughout (matches `eq-entropy-definition` and `_refs/QuantumStatistics.nb` $S[p]=-\sum_k p_k\ln p_k$): write $S(\hat\rho_{\rm th})=\beta\langle E\rangle+\ln Z$, and either name the thermodynamic entropy separately as $S_{\rm thermo}=k_B S$ or use the dimensionless-consistent relation $F=\langle E\rangle-k_B T S$; add a short `dropdown information` deriving $S(\hat\rho_{\rm th})$ and $\langle E\rangle$ from $\ln\hat\rho_{\rm th}=-\beta\hat H-(\ln Z)\hat I$. The convention is a pedagogical choice and the fix ripples into HW 5/6/9 wording - recommend professor sign-off before editing. derivation-designer audits -> notebook-writer applies and validates. Discovered: Phase D audit 2026-05-20.

- ~~**T-2026-05-20-NB-58**~~ **DONE 2026-05-20** (Phase C, Poll design correctness, lecture-designer → notebook-writer, **HIGH**) — `notes_src/ch6_quantum-foundations/6-1-2-entropy.ipynb` cell 2 `:::{admonition} Poll: Entropy and mixedness :class: dropdown poll` — two coupled defects vs `rules/content-style.md` § Poll Design. (1) The stem writes $S(\hat{\rho}) = -\operatorname{Tr}(\hat{\rho}\log_2\hat{\rho})$ but the entire lecture body uses natural log ($S=-\operatorname{Tr}(\hat{\rho}\ln\hat{\rho})$, bound $S\le\ln d$, $S=\ln 2$) — notation drift (NB-55's Phase B close explicitly left this `\log_2` as out-of-Phase-B scope). (2) "One definite correct answer / all distractors plausible" is violated: intended answer (A) is correct, but choice (D) "$S$ can exceed 1 for systems with more than two dimensions" is **also true** — a maximally mixed 3-level state has $S=\ln 3\approx1.10$ nats (or $\log_2 3\approx1.58$ bits), both $>1$ — so the poll has two correct choices. Fix: redesign the poll block as a unit — adopt $\ln$ in the stem and reword choice (D) (or the choice set) so exactly one option is correct and every distractor is genuinely wrong. Single scope = the Poll admonition. Skill: lecture-designer → notebook-writer. **Fix applied 2026-05-20:** two atomic `text.replace` ops on cell 2. (1) Poll stem `\log_2` → `\ln`, matching the body's natural-log convention ($S=-\operatorname{Tr}(\hat{\rho}\ln\hat{\rho})$ throughout). (2) Choice (D) reworded from the *true* statement "$S$ can exceed 1 for systems with more than two dimensions" (a genuine second correct answer — a maximally mixed 3-level state has $S=\ln3\approx1.10>1$) to the false-but-plausible distractor "$S(\hat{\rho})$ depends on the basis in which $\hat{\rho}$ is measured" — wrong because von Neumann entropy is basis-independent / unitary-invariant (only the Shannon entropy of measurement outcomes is basis-dependent; the notebook's *Connection to Information Theory* section makes this point explicitly). Result: exactly one correct choice (A); distractors map to distinct misconceptions — (B) "entropy strictly positive / level-counting", (C) the "1 bit per qubit" base-2 confusion, (D) basis-dependence. No inline answer block added (repo polls carry none; nested `:::` banned by `rules/myst-references.md`). Cell-2 length 10331 → 10344 chars. Validators clean: `safe_edit.py validate` no issues; `validate_project.py --scope 6-1-2-entropy` all validations passed.

- ~~**T-2026-05-20-NB-59**~~ **DONE 2026-05-20** (Phase C, Summary + See Also closing block, summary-designer → notebook-writer, **MEDIUM**) — `notes_src/ch6_quantum-foundations/6-1-2-entropy.ipynb` cell 2 `### Summary` + the following `See Also`. Summary defects vs `rules/content-style.md` § Summary and See Also: (a) only **3 bullets** (rule: typically 5–7; 4 only for an intentionally short lesson — this lesson has 5 substantial body sections and is not short); (b) **phantom bullet** — bullet 2 "Purity and mixedness" recaps $\operatorname{Tr}(\hat{\rho}^2)$ / purity, which is **not developed anywhere in this notebook's body** (purity is the topic of §6.1.1); (c) the lesson's conceptual core — maximum-entropy principle, thermal state, partition function, free energy, the QM/thermodynamics bridge — has no dedicated summary bullet; (d) bullet 1's "measures classical ignorance (diagonal terms)" framing sits awkwardly against the body's explicit point that von Neumann entropy is basis-independent. Also **See Also bullet 2**: the gloss "[6.1.3 Quantum Statistics]: Thermal states from entropy maximization—entropy as a variational principle" describes §6.1.2's own content, not §6.1.3 (which derives the Bose-Einstein / Fermi-Dirac distributions). Fix: rework the Summary to 5–7 grounded bullets (drop the phantom, cover the max-entropy / partition-function core) and reword the See Also 6.1.3 gloss to that notebook's actual content. Skill: summary-designer → notebook-writer.

Single atomic `text.replace` on cell 2 of `notes_src/ch6_quantum-foundations/6-1-1-mixed-states.ipynb` replaced the *Derivation: Purity from Bloch Vector* dropdown body with a 4-line `\begin{split}` chain matching the `_refs/QubitsAndEntanglement.nb` § *Purity* HW solution (line 24108): (line 1) $\operatorname{Tr}(\hat\rho^2) = \tfrac14\operatorname{Tr}(\hat I + \boldsymbol r\cdot\hat{\boldsymbol\sigma})^2$ — square $\hat\rho$, factor $1/4$, trace; (line 2) $= \tfrac14\operatorname{Tr}(\hat I + 2\boldsymbol r\cdot\hat{\boldsymbol\sigma} + (\boldsymbol r\cdot\hat{\boldsymbol\sigma})^2)$ — binomial expansion of $(\hat I + \boldsymbol r\cdot\hat{\boldsymbol\sigma})^2$, identity not yet applied; (line 3) $= \tfrac14\operatorname{Tr}(\hat I + 2\boldsymbol r\cdot\hat{\boldsymbol\sigma} + \vert\boldsymbol r\vert^2 \hat I)$ — apply Pauli identity $(\boldsymbol r\cdot\hat{\boldsymbol\sigma})^2 = \vert\boldsymbol r\vert^2 \hat I$ as its own step; (line 4) $= \tfrac14(2 + 0 + 2\vert\boldsymbol r\vert^2) = \tfrac12(1 + \vert\boldsymbol r\vert^2)$ — use $\operatorname{Tr}\hat I = 2$ and $\operatorname{Tr}\hat\sigma^i = 0$ explicitly, then simplify. - ~~**T-2026-05-19-DV-14**~~ **DONE 2026-05-19** (Phase D, derivation chain detail, **DONE**, **HIGH**, derivation-designer → notebook-writer) — `notes_src/ch6_quantum-foundations/6-1-1-mixed-states.ipynb` cell 2 `:::{admonition} Derivation: Von Neumann Equation :class: dropdown information` (post-NB-51 cell-2 pos ~9230): lecture shows **1 displayed-math line** packing four operations: (a) product-rule differentiation of $\hat\rho(t) = \hat U\hat\rho(0)\hat U^\dagger$, (b) substitution of $\dot{\hat U} = -(\mathrm{i}/\hbar)\hat H\hat U$, (c) substitution of the conjugate $\dot{\hat U}^\dagger = +(\mathrm{i}/\hbar)\hat U^\dagger\hat H$ (sign-flip critical), (d) recognition of $\hat U\hat\rho(0)\hat U^\dagger = \hat\rho(t)$ on the way to the commutator form. `_refs/QubitsAndEntanglement.nb` § *Dynamics of Density Matrix* (line 21057) does not derive Von Neumann equation in lecture text — frames it as homework (cell `hw: 6327714880484912580` at line 21133) — so the `_refs/` line count is undefined and the default *typically 4–7 lines per commutator evaluation* from `rules/derivation-quality.md` § Mandatory detail level applies; lecture's 1 line ≪ 4 default minimum. Critical missing step: the conjugate derivative sign flip $\dot{\hat U}^\dagger = +(\mathrm{i}/\hbar)\hat U^\dagger\hat H$ (differentiating $\hat U^\dagger = \mathrm{e}^{+\mathrm{i}\hat H t/\hbar}$) — the `+` sign vs the `-` sign on $\dot{\hat U}$ is the source of the commutator structure; the daggered form should be stated explicitly so students see Hermitian conjugation as the source of the sign. Strict rule violation per § Mandatory detail level + anti-pattern *'It can be shown that'* (implicit product rule + implicit conjugate). **Spec for derivation-designer (single dropdown, single `text.replace` on cell 2 after design):** expand to ~5-line chain: (line 1) start from $\hat\rho(t) = \hat U(t)\hat\rho(0)\hat U^\dagger(t)$; (line 2) product rule: $\tfrac{\mathrm{d}\hat\rho}{\mathrm{d}t} = \dot{\hat U}\hat\rho(0)\hat U^\dagger + \hat U\hat\rho(0)\dot{\hat U}^\dagger$ (state both derivatives separately, no substitution yet); (line 3) substitute $\dot{\hat U} = -(\mathrm{i}/\hbar)\hat H\hat U$ and $\dot{\hat U}^\dagger = +(\mathrm{i}/\hbar)\hat U^\dagger\hat H$ (state the conjugate derivative explicitly in the lead-in prose, noting the sign flip from differentiating $\mathrm{e}^{+\mathrm{i}\hat H t/\hbar}$): $\tfrac{\mathrm{d}\hat\rho}{\mathrm{d}t} = -\tfrac{\mathrm{i}}{\hbar}\hat H\hat U\hat\rho(0)\hat U^\dagger + \tfrac{\mathrm{i}}{\hbar}\hat U\hat\rho(0)\hat U^\dagger\hat H$; (line 4) factor and recognize $\hat U\hat\rho(0)\hat U^\dagger = \hat\rho(t)$: $\tfrac{\mathrm{d}\hat\rho}{\mathrm{d}t} = -\tfrac{\mathrm{i}}{\hbar}(\hat H\hat\rho - \hat\rho\hat H) = -\tfrac{\mathrm{i}}{\hbar}[\hat H, \hat\rho]$; (line 5) multiply by $\mathrm{i}\hbar$ to match the form quoted in the `important` box: $\mathrm{i}\hbar\tfrac{\mathrm{d}\hat\rho}{\mathrm{d}t} = [\hat H, \hat\rho]$ — matches `eq-mixed-von-neumann`. Lead-in prose should explicitly state the conjugate derivative $\dot{\hat U}^\dagger$ with the `+` sign before the substitution step. Option to use `\begin{split}` with `&=` alignment per `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Display Math. **Implementer:** `derivation-designer` outlines the 5-line chain per `rules/derivation-quality.md` mandatory procedure (no direct `_refs/` line count to match since reference treats as HW; default 4–7 lines applies), then hands single `text.replace` spec on cell 2 to `notebook-writer`. **Validators:** scoped `validate_project.py --scope 6-1-1-mixed-states` + `safe_edit.py validate` per `rules/validation.md`. **CLOSURE 2026-05-19** (scheduled-task automated run, manager agent): single atomic `text.replace` on cell 2 expanded the *Derivation: Von Neumann Equation* `dropdown information` from 1 displayed-math line to a 5-line `\begin{split}` chain (product rule with both propagator derivatives stated separately → substitute $\mathrm{d}\hat U/\mathrm{d}t$ and the conjugate $\mathrm{d}\hat U^\dagger/\mathrm{d}t=+(\mathrm{i}/\hbar)\hat U^\dagger\hat H$ → regroup $\hat U\hat\rho(0)\hat U^\dagger=\hat\rho(t)$ → factor → commutator); lead-in prose now states the conjugate-derivative sign flip explicitly (differentiating $\mathrm{e}^{+\mathrm{i}\hat Ht/\hbar}$) and closing prose multiplies by $\mathrm{i}\hbar$ to recover `eq-mixed-von-neumann`. `_refs/QubitsAndEntanglement.nb` § Dynamics of Density Matrix confirmed to frame Von Neumann as homework (line 21133) — default 4–7-line minimum per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § Mandatory detail level satisfied. Cell-2 11215 → 12292 chars (+1077). `safe_edit.py validate` + `validate_project.py --scope 6-1-1-mixed-states` both clean. Cursor unchanged per `.claude/README.md` § 1.4. See Session Log.

- ~~**T-2026-05-19-DV-15**~~ **DONE 2026-05-19** (Phase D, derivation chain detail, **DONE**, **LOW**, notebook-writer direct from pre-specified audit ticket) — `notes_src/ch6_quantum-foundations/6-1-1-mixed-states.ipynb` cell 2 `:::{admonition} Derivation: Purity Bounds :class: dropdown information` (post-NB-51 cell-2 pos ~4555): lecture shows **3 conceptual bullet-lines**: (i) pure-state idempotent $\hat\rho^2 = |\psi\rangle\langle\psi|\psi\rangle\langle\psi| = \hat\rho \Rightarrow \operatorname{Tr}\hat\rho^2 = 1$; (ii) Cauchy-Schwarz lower bound $\sum_i\lambda_i^2 \geq (\sum_i\lambda_i)^2/d = 1/d$; (iii) mixed-state strict $\sum\lambda_i^2 < (\sum\lambda_i)^2 = 1$. `_refs/QubitsAndEntanglement.nb` § *Purity* (line 23965) states $\operatorname{Tr}\rho^2 = \sum p_i^2$ as definitional and asserts $\operatorname{Tr}\rho^2 \in [0,1]$ *'by construction'* without deriving the lower bound 1/d; pure/mixed criterion stated as piecewise at line 23987 without strict-inequality derivation. Lecture therefore goes **beyond `_refs/`** (which is fine), but in doing so skips two algebra steps a student needs: (a) Cauchy-Schwarz invocation: the factor of $d$ enters because Cauchy-Schwarz with $a_i = \lambda_i$ and $b_i = 1$ gives $(\sum\lambda_i)^2 = (\sum a_ib_i)^2 \leq (\sum a_i^2)(\sum b_i^2) = d\sum\lambda_i^2$, i.e. $\sum\lambda_i^2 \geq (\sum\lambda_i)^2/d$ — the $d$ factor's provenance is opaque without showing $b_i = 1$; (b) mixed-state strict-inequality reasoning: $(\sum\lambda_i)^2 = \sum\lambda_i^2 + 2\sum_{i<j}\lambda_i\lambda_j$, so if at least two $\lambda_i > 0$ the cross-sum is strictly positive, giving $\sum\lambda_i^2 < (\sum\lambda_i)^2 = 1$. Not a strict `_refs/`-alignment violation (reference doesn't derive these), but `rules/derivation-quality.md` § Anti-patterns *'A prefactor appears without a definition step'* applies to $1/d$; judgment call whether to expand. **Spec for derivation-designer (single dropdown, single `text.replace` on cell 2 after design, lighter than DV-13 / DV-14):** for the **Lower bound** bullet, add 1-line intermediate: *'Cauchy-Schwarz with $a_i = \lambda_i$, $b_i = 1$: $(\sum_i\lambda_i)^2 \leq (\sum_i\lambda_i^2)(\sum_i 1) = d\sum_i\lambda_i^2$, so $\sum_i\lambda_i^2 \geq (\sum_i\lambda_i)^2/d = 1/d$, with equality iff all $\lambda_i = 1/d$.'* For the **Mixed state** bullet, add 1-line cross-term reasoning: *'$(\sum_i\lambda_i)^2 = \sum_i\lambda_i^2 + 2\sum_{i<j}\lambda_i\lambda_j$; if $\geq 2$ eigenvalues are positive the cross-sum is strictly positive, so $\sum_i\lambda_i^2 < (\sum_i\lambda_i)^2 = 1$.'* Both expansions keep the existing three-bullet structure intact, just deeper math. **Implementer:** `derivation-designer` drafts the two 1-line additions per `rules/derivation-quality.md` mandatory procedure (read `_refs/` for context — none for these specific bounds — then outline and write), then hands single `text.replace` spec on cell 2 to `notebook-writer`. Alternative route (defer): leave as-is since lecture is already richer than `_refs/`; document the judgment in the ticket closure note. **Validators:** scoped `validate_project.py --scope 6-1-1-mixed-states` + `safe_edit.py validate` per `rules/validation.md`. **CLOSURE 2026-05-19** (scheduled-task automated run, manager agent, no human present): routed `notebook-writer` direct from the pre-specified audit ticket — the ticket gave both 1-line expansions concretely, so no separate `derivation-designer` drafting session was needed (precedent: DV-13 closure on this notebook). Reference checked first per `rules/derivation-quality.md` mandatory procedure: `_refs/QubitsAndEntanglement.nb` § *Purity* (line 23965) gives $\operatorname{Tr}\rho^2=\sum_i p_i^2$ as definitional and only asserts $\operatorname{Tr}\rho^2\in[0,1]$ *'by construction'* — it does not derive the $1/d$ lower bound, so the lecture exceeds `_refs/` (no reference line count to match); the expansion is justified by § Anti-patterns *'a prefactor appears without a definition step'* applied to $1/d$. Single atomic `text.replace` on cell 2 replaced the two-paragraph **Lower bound** + **Mixed state** block of the *Derivation: Purity Bounds* `dropdown information` admonition. **Lower bound** now states Cauchy-Schwarz $(\sum_i a_ib_i)^2\le(\sum_i a_i^2)(\sum_i b_i^2)$, applies it with $a_i=\lambda_i$, $b_i=1$ (so $\sum_i b_i^2=d$ — the $1/d$ provenance is now explicit), and rearranges with $\sum_i\lambda_i=1$ to $\sum_i\lambda_i^2\ge1/d$ (equality-iff-$\lambda_i=1/d$ remark retained). **Mixed state** now shows the cross-term expansion $(\sum_i\lambda_i)^2=\sum_i\lambda_i^2+2\sum_{i<j}\lambda_i\lambda_j$ and argues the strictly-positive cross-term gives $\sum_i\lambda_i^2<1$ when $\ge2$ eigenvalues are positive. **Pure state** paragraph and the three-bold-paragraph dropdown structure unchanged; both additions are inline `$...$` math only (no `$$` blocks). `$d$` confirmed defined in the `important` *Purity* box directly above (*'Maximally mixed ($d$-dimensional)'*) — additions stay in-context per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § Scope. **Counts**: cell-2 OLD 1 → 0, NEW 0 → 1 (unique anchor verified pre-edit); cells 0/1/3 byte-identical at [20, 905, 3706] pre and post; cell-2 **12292 → 12731 chars** (delta **+439**). Edited via Bash + Python `json.load` / `json.dump(..., indent=1, ensure_ascii=False)` with raw-string LaTeX and `text_to_source(...)` rebuild per `rules/notebook-editing.md`; round-trip read-back equals in-memory string. **Prior queue** (kept for reference, all closed 2026-05-19): Active queue **was** 0 open tickets after DV-11 and DV-12 closed 2026-05-19 — original "0 open tickets" header text: — DV-11 prior text (now CLOSED): T-2026-05-19-DV-11 — Phase D, derivation quality, **HIGH**, derivation-designer → notebook-writer: in cell 2 § Kubo Formula the opening `\begin{split}` block before the *Kubo formula* derivation states three relations $\boldsymbol{E}(t)=\boldsymbol{E}\,\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}(\omega+\mathrm{i}0^{+})t}$, $\delta\boldsymbol{A}(t)=\frac{\mathrm{i}\boldsymbol{E}}{\omega+\mathrm{i}0^{+}}\,\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}(\omega+\mathrm{i}0^{+})t}$, and $\boldsymbol{E}(t)=-\partial_t\delta\boldsymbol{A}(t)$ that are **internally inconsistent**: computing $-\partial_t\delta\boldsymbol{A}$ with the stated $\delta\boldsymbol{A}$ gives $-\boldsymbol{E}\,\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}\omega t}$, not $+\boldsymbol{E}\,\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}\omega t}$, so the three lines together contradict each other. Against `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Root causes / Wrong notation* and § *Student-confusion test*. Reference: `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` Linear Response Theory lines 3289–3305 carry the same setup but use the (unusual but self-consistent) convention $\delta\boldsymbol{A}(t)=\int_{-\infty}^{t}\!\mathrm{d}t'\,\boldsymbol{E}(t')$ which corresponds to $\boldsymbol{E}=+\partial_t\delta\boldsymbol{A}$. **Fix routes:** (a) flip the sign of $\delta\boldsymbol{A}$ to $-\mathrm{i}\boldsymbol{E}/(\omega+\mathrm{i}0^{+})\,\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}\omega t}$ (matches the SI convention $\boldsymbol{E}=-\partial_t\boldsymbol{A}$ stated in `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Chapter 4 EM Units and on the third line of the lecture's own split block); *or* (b) flip the sign in the third line to $\boldsymbol{E}(t)=+\partial_t\delta\boldsymbol{A}(t)$ (matches the `_refs/` convention). Route (a) is recommended — it preserves the standard SI relation and matches Chapter 4 throughout; the wrongly-signed $\delta\boldsymbol{A}$ is then substituted into Step 3 of the dropdown (`$\delta A_b(t') = (\mathrm{i}E_b/(\omega+\mathrm{i}0^+))\,\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}(\omega+\mathrm{i}0^+)t'}$`), which must be flipped to $-\mathrm{i}E_b/(\omega+\mathrm{i}0^+)$ to stay consistent. Audit note: the two sign errors cancel inside the dropdown's final boxed $\sigma_{xy}$, so the **boxed Kubo formula result is correct**; this is a chain-consistency fix, not a result fix. **Out of scope:** the four other display equations in the dropdown (Step 4 commutator-expansion sign — see DV-12), the boxed *Kubo formula (zero temperature)* result, the integer Hall coda $\sigma_{xy}=\nu e^2/h$, cell 1 Prompts, cell 3 Homework, and the first three derivation dropdowns (phase cancellation / exponential-to-sinc / Lorentzian-from-ramp — all checked PASS this audit). Surgical sites: 2 atomic `text.replace` calls on cell 2 (one in the `\begin{split}` opener; one in Step 3 of the dropdown). T-2026-05-19-DV-12 — Phase D, derivation quality, **HIGH**, derivation-designer → notebook-writer: in cell 2 § Kubo Formula dropdown *Derivation: Kubo formula* Step 4, the asserted matrix-element shortcut *"use $\hat{j}_a^{\mathcal{I}}(\tau)\vert\gamma\rangle = \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}\omega_{\gamma\alpha}\tau}\hat{j}_a\vert\gamma\rangle$ on each $\langle\alpha\vert\cdots\vert\alpha\rangle$ matrix element (with $\omega_{\gamma\alpha}=(E_\gamma-E_\alpha)/\hbar$)"* is **doubly problematic**: (i) it is **not a valid operator equation** because the exponential's frequency $\omega_{\gamma\alpha}$ depends on $\alpha$ — the *other* state in the sandwich — so the action of $\hat{j}_a^{\mathcal{I}}(\tau)$ on $|\gamma\rangle$ cannot be a state-independent operator; (ii) even read as a *matrix-element* shortcut, the sign of the exponent is **wrong**: $\langle\alpha|\hat{j}_a^{\mathcal{I}}(\tau)|\gamma\rangle = \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}E_\alpha\tau/\hbar}\,j^a_{\alpha\gamma}\,\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}E_\gamma\tau/\hbar} = \mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}\omega_{\gamma\alpha}\tau}\,j^a_{\alpha\gamma}$, not $\mathrm{e}^{+\mathrm{i}\omega_{\gamma\alpha}\tau}\,j^a_{\alpha\gamma}$. This sign error propagates to the displayed commutator expansion immediately below, which currently reads $\sum_{\alpha\in\mathrm{occ}}\sum_\gamma(j^a_{\alpha\gamma}j^b_{\gamma\alpha}\,\mathrm{e}^{+\mathrm{i}\omega_{\gamma\alpha}\tau} - j^b_{\alpha\gamma}j^a_{\gamma\alpha}\,\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}\omega_{\gamma\alpha}\tau})$ but should read $\sum_{\alpha\in\mathrm{occ}}\sum_\gamma(j^a_{\alpha\gamma}j^b_{\gamma\alpha}\,\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}\omega_{\gamma\alpha}\tau} - j^b_{\alpha\gamma}j^a_{\gamma\alpha}\,\mathrm{e}^{+\mathrm{i}\omega_{\gamma\alpha}\tau})$ with $\omega_{\gamma\alpha}=(E_\gamma-E_\alpha)/\hbar$ as stated. Against `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Root causes / Misleading notation choices* + § *Root causes / Wrong notation (index/sign errors)* + § *Mandatory procedure / Student-confusion test* and § Anti-patterns row *"Using an operator form that looks like a typo for a simpler one"*. Reference: `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` Linear Response Theory lines 3622–3637 carry the structurally identical step (with $\hat{\pi}_j$ in place of $\hat{j}_a$ and $|n,m\rangle$ in place of $|\alpha\rangle$) and writes the matrix element expansion with the **correct** signs $\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}(E_n-E_{n'})t}$ (= $\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}\omega_{n',n}t}$ under the same convention). **Fix routes:** (a) rewrite the shortcut as a matrix-element identity *"$\langle\alpha|\hat{j}_a^{\mathcal{I}}(\tau)|\gamma\rangle = \mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}\omega_{\gamma\alpha}\tau}\,\langle\alpha|\hat{j}_a|\gamma\rangle$"* (preserves $\omega_{\gamma\alpha}=(E_\gamma-E_\alpha)/\hbar$ as defined) and flip the two exponential signs in the displayed commutator expansion accordingly; *or* (b) keep the operator-style shortcut but redefine $\omega_{\gamma\alpha}\equiv(E_\alpha-E_\gamma)/\hbar$ to absorb the sign — **not recommended** because it conflicts with the standard $\omega_{fi}=(E_f-E_i)/\hbar$ convention already used elsewhere in this notebook (Eq. {eq}`eq-5-2-3-P1st`). Route (a) recommended. Audit note: because the antisymmetric (Hall) part of $\sigma_{ab}$ involves a dummy-index swap $\alpha\leftrightarrow\gamma$, the **boxed Kubo formula** in `eq-kubo-hall-523` is **independently correct** even with the current Step 4 sign error — the $\gamma\leftrightarrow\alpha$ swap inside the antisymmetric combination cancels the wrong-signed exponential. This is a derivation-chain quality fix per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Mandatory detail level* ("add the missing lines"), not a result fix. **Out of scope:** the EM-field/vector-potential sign issue (see DV-11), the final boxed $\sigma_{xy}$ result, the Step 5 DC-limit expansion (which is correct), Step 1's glossed first-order expansion (separate issue if needed — left for a future audit), and cell 3 Homework. Surgical sites: 2 atomic `text.replace` calls on cell 2 — one rewriting the shortcut text, one flipping the two exponential signs in the immediately-following display equation. - **T-2026-05-18-DV-10** (Phase D, derivation chain detail, **CLOSED 2026-05-18** via route (a) — single atomic `text.replace` on cell 2 inserted the fully-expanded $\hat{V}_{\mathcal I}$ product chain between Step 1 and Step 2; validators clean; cell 2 12532 → 12860 chars; designer-writer) — `notes_src/ch5_perturbation-theory/5-2-2-dyson-series.ipynb` cell 2 `:::{admonition} Derivation: from $\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}$ strings to $\hat{G}_0$ links :class: dropdown information` block skips the **fully-expanded $\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}$ product chain** between Step (i) (per-vertex expansion $\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}(t_j) = \hat{U}_0^{\dagger}(t_j)\hat{V}(t_j)\hat{U}_0(t_j)$) and Step (ii) (adjacent-pair identification $\hat{U}_0(t_{j+1})\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}(t_j) = \hat{G}_0(t_{j+1}, t_j)$ followed by the partially-collapsed chain $\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}(t_k)\hat{V}(t_k)\hat{G}_0(t_k,t_{k-1})\hat{V}(t_{k-1})\cdots\hat{G}_0(t_2,t_1)\hat{V}(t_1)\hat{U}_0(t_1)$), violating `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Mandatory detail level: match `_refs/`* (*each commutator evaluation or algebraic derivation must contain at least as many displayed intermediate lines as the corresponding passage in `_refs/`*) and § *Root causes* item 1 (*Skipped intermediate steps* — *The derivation jumps from premises to conclusions without showing the evaluations that connect them*). `_refs/PerturbationTheory.md` lines 1858–1890 (§ *Green's Function*) carry **two** displayed product chains: first the **fully-expanded** product $\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}(t_k)\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}(t_{k-1})\cdots\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}(t_1) = \hat{U}_0^{\dagger}(t_k)\hat{V}(t_k)\hat{U}_0(t_k)\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}(t_{k-1})\hat{V}(t_{k-1})\hat{U}_0(t_{k-1})\cdots\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}(t_1)\hat{V}(t_1)\hat{U}_0(t_1)$ (with every adjacent $\hat{U}_0\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}$ pair visible), then the $\hat{G}_0$-collapsed chain. The lecture jumps straight from Step (i)'s per-vertex expansion (shown for a single factor only) to Step (ii)'s partially-collapsed result, asking the student to mentally apply Step (i) to all $k$ factors and observe how adjacent pairs match into $\hat{G}_0$. **Fix routes** (small designer judgment before writer applies): **(a) Insert the fully-expanded chain as a new displayed equation between Step (i) and Step (ii)** — minimal surgery, mirrors `_refs/` line for line, total derivation grows from four to five displayed equations (still well within the 4–7 typical range per `derivation-quality.md` § *Mandatory detail level*). Proposed insertion (after Step (i)'s vertex-expansion equation, before Step (ii)'s *In a product, every adjacent pair produces a free propagator:* prose): a one-sentence connector (*Applying this to every factor in the time-ordered product gives* or *Substituting into the ordered product makes the structure explicit:*) followed by `$$ \hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}(t_k)\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}(t_{k-1})\cdots\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}(t_1) = \hat{U}_0^{\dagger}(t_k)\hat{V}(t_k)\hat{U}_0(t_k)\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}(t_{k-1})\hat{V}(t_{k-1})\hat{U}_0(t_{k-1})\cdots\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}(t_1)\hat{V}(t_1)\hat{U}_0(t_1). $$`. The existing prose *In a product, every adjacent pair produces a free propagator: $\hat{U}_0(t_{j+1})\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}(t_j)=\hat{G}_0(t_{j+1},t_j).$* then naturally lands as the pair-identification rule applied to the now-visible adjacent pairs, and the existing *So the ordered string becomes* line + the partially-collapsed chain remains as Step (ii)'s conclusion — unchanged. **(b) Merge into Step (i)** so Step (i) shows both the per-vertex expansion and the full-product version side-by-side — slightly larger change to Step (i) prose and slightly less natural as the build-up to Step (ii)'s pair-identification move; route (a) recommended. **Scope discipline** per `rules/maintenance-scope.md`: do **not** touch the per-vertex expansion equation in Step (i) (algebraically correct, unchanged); do **not** touch Step (iii) (the end-piece $\hat{U}_0(t)\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}(t_k) = \hat{G}_0(t,t_k)$ and $\hat{U}_0(t_1) = \hat{G}_0(t_1, 0)$ identifications are clean — lecture uses a different but equivalent end-piece handling from `_refs/`'s $\mathbf{1} = \hat{U}_0(t)\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}(t)$ left-insertion trick, and the lecture's approach is cleaner and should be preserved); do **not** touch the *Derivation: from operator EOM to Volterra equation* dropdown above (Phase D audit Finding 1 — PASS, no ticket); do **not** touch the *Iteration (blackboard style)* in-body block (Finding 3 — PASS); do **not** touch the *Recursive form* `dropdown note` block (Finding 4 — PASS, result statement + HW6 verification); do **not** touch cell 1 Prompts, cell 3 Homework, the *Summary* bullets, or the See Also admonition. **VERIFY-FIRST anchor** (for writer): under route (a), the OLD anchor is the prose line *In a product, every adjacent pair produces a free propagator:* immediately following Step (i)'s `$$` block close. Original ticket text retained: **T-2026-05-18-LD-22** (Phase B, notation, mechanical, **OPEN**, notebook-writer direct after route pick) — `notes_src/ch5_perturbation-theory/5-2-2-dyson-series.ipynb` cell 3 HW Problem 7 *Two-level Feynman diagrams* mixes Spin-system Pauli operators with QI-system computational kets within the same problem, violating `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Notation Consistency: Spin vs QI (*"If kets use $\vert 0\rangle, \vert 1\rangle$, operators must be $\hat{X}, \hat{Y}, \hat{Z}$. If kets use $\vert\uparrow\rangle, \vert\downarrow\rangle$, operators must be $\hat{\sigma}^x, \hat{\sigma}^y, \hat{\sigma}^z$."*). VERIFY-FIRST sites in HW7: (i) setup line `\hat{H}_0=\frac{\hbar\omega_0}{2}\hat{\sigma}^z, \qquad \hat{V}=\hbar\Omega\,\hat{\sigma}^x` (Spin Pauli operators); (ii) part (a) `\langle 1\vert\hat{U}(t)\vert 0\rangle` (QI kets `\vert 0\rangle, \vert 1\rangle`); (iii) part (c) `\vert 0\rangle\to\vert 1\rangle` (QI kets). Fix routes — (i) **QI uniformly** (recommended): rewrite operators as `\hat{H}_0=\frac{\hbar\omega_0}{2}\hat{Z}` and `\hat{V}=\hbar\Omega\,\hat{X}` to match the existing `\vert 0\rangle, \vert 1\rangle` kets and HW7(c) qubit-transition framing; (ii) **Spin uniformly**: convert kets to `\vert\uparrow\rangle, \vert\downarrow\rangle` and matrix elements in (a) accordingly — less convenient because HW7(c) names the transition as `\vert 0\rangle\to\vert 1\rangle` and adjacent HW Problem 8 already uses numeric-label kets `\vert 1\rangle, \vert 2\rangle, \vert 3\rangle` (three-level Hilbert-space basis, outside the strict spin/qubit dichotomy and unaffected by the rule). Owner: notebook-writer direct via two atomic `text.replace` calls on cell 3 (setup line then `\hat{\sigma}^z` → `\hat{Z}`, `\hat{\sigma}^x` → `\hat{X}`) under route (i), with pre/post count guards per `rules/notebook-editing.md` § Post-edit checklist. Out of scope of this ticket: the `\qquad` in the HW7 setup line (covered by T-2026-05-18-NB-44 below — sequence the two edits to avoid stomping each other).

- **T-2026-05-18-NB-44** (Phase B, notation, mechanical, **OPEN**, notebook-writer direct) — `notes_src/ch5_perturbation-theory/5-2-2-dyson-series.ipynb` uses `\qquad` to lay out two equations side-by-side within a single `$$...$$` block at three sites, violating `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Display Math (*"Do NOT use `\quad` or `\qquad` to lay out multiple equations side-by-side in one display block. When presenting multiple equations/definitions, stack them vertically (one relation per line), using `\begin{split}...\end{split}` where needed."*). - **T-2026-05-17-DD-01** (Phase D, derivation depth vs `_refs/`, **OPEN**, derivation-designer → notebook-writer) — `notes_src/ch5_perturbation-theory/5-2-1-interaction-picture.ipynb` cell 2 `:::{admonition} Derivation: state equation in the interaction picture :class: dropdown information` is compressed below the reference detail level mandated by `rules/derivation-quality.md` § Mandatory detail level: match `_refs/` (*\"Each commutator evaluation or algebraic derivation in a dropdown must contain at least as many displayed intermediate lines as the corresponding passage in `_refs/`\"*). VERIFY-FIRST: lecture dropdown contains **4 displayed `$$...$$` blocks** (define $\vert\psi\rangle_{\mathcal{S}}=\hat{U}_0\vert\psi\rangle_{\mathcal{I}}$; product-rule differentiation; collapsed prose-with-equation step \"Use $\mathrm{i}\hbar\partial_t\hat{U}_0=\hat{H}_0\hat{U}_0$ and Eq. ..., the $\hat{H}_0$ terms cancel, giving\" + one displayed line; multiply by $\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}$ + identify $\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}$ in one line). Reference `_refs/PerturbationTheory.md` lines 1717-1732 shows the analogous derivation as **7 distinct displayed `$$...$$` blocks**: (i) $\mathrm{i}\partial_t\vert\psi\rangle_{\mathcal{I}}=\mathrm{i}\partial_t(\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}\hat{H}_0 t}\vert\psi\rangle_{\mathcal{S}})$; (ii) product rule splitting into $\mathrm{i}\partial_t(\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}\hat{H}_0 t})\vert\psi\rangle_{\mathcal{S}} + \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}\hat{H}_0 t}\,\mathrm{i}\partial_t\vert\psi\rangle_{\mathcal{S}}$; (iii) substitute $-\hat{H}_0$ from $\mathrm{i}\partial_t \hat{U}_0^{\dagger}$ and $(\hat{H}_0+\hat{V})$ from Schrödinger equation, two $\hat{H}_0$ terms visible; (iv) cancellation leaves $\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}\hat{H}_0 t}\hat{V}\vert\psi\rangle_{\mathcal{S}}$; (v) rewrite $\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}\hat{H}_0 t}=\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}$ giving $\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{V}\vert\psi\rangle_{\mathcal{S}}$; (vi) insert identity $\hat{U}_0\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}=\hat{I}$ → $\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{V}\hat{U}_0\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\vert\psi\rangle_{\mathcal{S}}$; (vii) identify $\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}\vert\psi\rangle_{\mathcal{I}}$. Lecture takes a slightly different starting path (substitute $\vert\psi\rangle_{\mathcal{S}}=\hat{U}_0\vert\psi\rangle_{\mathcal{I}}$ first rather than $\vert\psi\rangle_{\mathcal{I}}=\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\vert\psi\rangle_{\mathcal{S}}$ — both paths are valid; the issue is **step compression**, not path choice). Specifically, the lecture's third step (\"Use $\mathrm{i}\hbar\partial_t\hat{U}_0=\hat{H}_0\hat{U}_0$ and Eq. {eq}`eq-5-2-1-schrodinger-state`; the $\hat{H}_0$ terms cancel, giving\" $\Rightarrow$ $\mathrm{i}\hbar\hat{U}_0\partial_t\vert\psi\rangle_{\mathcal{I}}=\hat{V}\hat{U}_0\vert\psi\rangle_{\mathcal{I}}$) hides three reference sub-steps: (a) multiplying through by $\mathrm{i}\hbar$, (b) substituting Schrödinger equations on both sides ($\mathrm{i}\hbar\partial_t\vert\psi\rangle_{\mathcal{S}}=(\hat{H}_0+\hat{V})\vert\psi\rangle_{\mathcal{S}}=(\hat{H}_0+\hat{V})\hat{U}_0\vert\psi\rangle_{\mathcal{I}}$ and $\mathrm{i}\hbar(\partial_t\hat{U}_0)\vert\psi\rangle_{\mathcal{I}}=\hat{H}_0\hat{U}_0\vert\psi\rangle_{\mathcal{I}}$), (c) explicit cancellation of the two $\hat{H}_0\hat{U}_0\vert\psi\rangle_{\mathcal{I}}$ terms. Per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § Root causes #1 (Skipped intermediate steps), § Anti-patterns table (*\"It can be shown that $[A,B]=C$\"* → *\"Show the evaluation, or cite the equation where it was shown\"*), and § Mandatory detail level (*\"4–7 lines per commutator evaluation; AI authors consistently underestimate how many steps students need\"*), the missing intermediate lines are required. **Fix**: `phys130b-derivation-designer` expands the dropdown's step 3 from one prose-with-jump-equation into 3 separate displayed blocks mirroring `_refs/` lines 1722-1726, then `phys130b-notebook-writer` applies via single atomic `text.replace` on cell 2 (unique anchor: the existing 3-line block beginning *\"Use $\\mathrm{i}\\hbar\\partial_t\\hat{U}_0=\\hat{H}_0\\hat{U}_0$ and Eq. {eq}`eq-5-2-1-schrodinger-state`; the $\\hat{H}_0$ terms cancel, giving\"* through the closing `$$` of the $\\mathrm{i}\\hbar\\hat{U}_0\\partial_t$ display) per `rules/notebook-editing.md` § Required method (Bash + Python `json.load` / `json.dump(indent=1, ensure_ascii=False)`; raw-string LaTeX required — the expansion carries `\\hbar`, `\\hat{}`, `\\boldsymbol{}` and the JSON escape sequences `\\h` / `\\b` would otherwise silently corrupt them per § *LaTeX in JSON — escape character hazards*); post-save validators per `rules/validation.md`. **Out of scope:** the dropdown's existing steps 1 (define) / 2 (product rule) / 4 (multiply by $\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}$) — well-presented, no rewrites; the other two derivation dropdowns (D1 *matrix elements*, D3 *operator equation* — separate tickets DD-03 / DD-02); cell-2 lecture body outside the three dropdowns; cells 0 / 1 / 3.

- **T-2026-05-17-DD-02** (Phase D, derivation depth, **OPEN**, derivation-designer → notebook-writer) — `notes_src/ch5_perturbation-theory/5-2-1-interaction-picture.ipynb` cell 2 `:::{admonition} Derivation: operator equation for $\hat{U}_{\mathcal{I}}$ :class: dropdown information` skips two implicit identity-evaluation steps that `rules/derivation-quality.md` § Mandatory procedure step 4 (*\"Show every commutator / identity evaluation\"*) and § Anti-patterns table (*\"By the Heisenberg equation ... for an operator with explicit $t$-dependence\"* → *\"State the full ... equation including ... and explain why ... term is present\"*) require explicit. VERIFY-FIRST: lecture dropdown contains **2 displayed `$$...$$` blocks** plus prose: (i) product-rule differentiation $\partial_t\hat{U}_{\mathcal{I}}=(\partial_t\hat{U}_0^{\dagger})\hat{U}+\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}(\partial_t\hat{U})$; (ii) prose \"Using $\mathrm{i}\hbar\partial_t\hat{U}_0=\hat{H}_0\hat{U}_0$ and $\mathrm{i}\hbar\partial_t\hat{U}=(\hat{H}_0+\hat{V})\hat{U}$:\" followed by chain $\mathrm{i}\hbar\partial_t\hat{U}_{\mathcal{I}}=-\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{H}_0\hat{U}+\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}(\hat{H}_0+\hat{V})\hat{U}=\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{V}\hat{U}=\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}\hat{U}_{\mathcal{I}}$; (iii) closing prose for initial condition $\hat{U}_{\mathcal{I}}(0)=\hat{I}$. Two implicit steps in chain (ii): (1) **Hermitian-conjugate substitution.** Going from $\mathrm{i}\hbar\partial_t\hat{U}_0=\hat{H}_0\hat{U}_0$ to the appearance of $-\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{H}_0\hat{U}$ in the chain requires taking the dagger of the $\hat{U}_0$ Schrödinger equation to get $-\mathrm{i}\hbar\partial_t\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}=\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{H}_0$ (equivalently $\mathrm{i}\hbar\partial_t\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}=-\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{H}_0$); this dagger evaluation is silent — the lecture cites only the un-daggered $\hat{U}_0$ equation. (2) **Identity insertion.** Going from $\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{V}\hat{U}$ directly to $\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}\hat{U}_{\mathcal{I}}$ skips the identity insertion $\hat{U}=\hat{U}_0\hat{U}_{\mathcal{I}}$ (or equivalently $\hat{U}_0\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}=\hat{I}$ inserted in the middle): $\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{V}\hat{U}=\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{V}\hat{U}_0\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{U}=(\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{V}\hat{U}_0)(\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{U})=\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}\hat{U}_{\mathcal{I}}$. The lecture writes \"$=\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{V}\hat{U}=\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}\hat{U}_{\mathcal{I}}$\" with no visible identity-insertion intermediate. `_refs/PerturbationTheory.md` lines 1772-1783 does not derive this operator equation explicitly (defers to *\"Following the similar derivation in Eq. (XXX)\"* — i.e., the state-equation chain that is the target of DD-01); however, `rules/derivation-quality.md` § Anti-patterns table (*\"A prefactor / definition step missing\"* → *\"Define both ... before the derivation\"*) and § Mandatory procedure step 6 (*\"Explain where each term comes from. When a term appears in an intermediate expression, the reader must be able to trace it to a specific step\"*) flag the implicit dagger and the implicit identity-insertion independently of any `_refs/` line-count comparison. **Fix**: `phys130b-derivation-designer` adds two intermediate lines — (a) explicit Hermitian-conjugate of the $\hat{U}_0$ Schrödinger equation immediately before the chain, framed as *\"Taking the Hermitian conjugate of $\mathrm{i}\hbar\partial_t\hat{U}_0=\hat{H}_0\hat{U}_0$ gives $\mathrm{i}\hbar\partial_t\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}=-\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{H}_0$,\"* (since $\hat{H}_0$ is Hermitian); (b) explicit identity-insertion step in the chain — replace \"$=\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{V}\hat{U}=\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}\hat{U}_{\mathcal{I}}$\" with \"$=\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{V}\hat{U}_0\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{U}=(\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{V}\hat{U}_0)(\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{U})=\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}\hat{U}_{\mathcal{I}}$\" (using the previously-defined $\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}=\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{V}\hat{U}_0$ from `eq-5-2-1-def-VI` and $\hat{U}_{\mathcal{I}}=\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{U}$ from `eq-5-2-1-def-UI`). Then `phys130b-notebook-writer` applies via single atomic `text.replace` on cell 2 per `rules/notebook-editing.md` (raw-string LaTeX required for `\\hbar` / `\\hat{}` / `\\dagger`). Post-save validators per `rules/validation.md`. **Out of scope:** the dropdown's existing product-rule line and initial-condition prose; the two other derivation dropdowns (D1 / D2 — separate tickets DD-03 / DD-01); cell-2 body outside the three dropdowns; cells 0 / 1 / 3.

- **T-2026-05-17-DD-03** (Phase D, derivation depth, **OPEN**, derivation-designer → notebook-writer) — `notes_src/ch5_perturbation-theory/5-2-1-interaction-picture.ipynb` cell 2 `:::{admonition} Derivation: matrix elements and Bohr frequencies :class: dropdown information` middle displayed block collapses the eigenstate-action step that produces the Bohr-frequency phases — the conceptual core of the derivation. VERIFY-FIRST: lecture dropdown contains **3 displayed `$$...$$` blocks**: (i) spectral expansions of $\hat{U}_0$ and $\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}$ in a `\begin{split}` pair; (ii) **the offending middle block** — `\langle m\vert\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}(t)\vert n\rangle = \langle m\vert\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}(t)\hat{V}(t)\hat{U}_0(t)\vert n\rangle = \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}(E_m-E_n)t/\hbar}\,V_{mn}(t)` (two equalities, one displayed jump); (iii) final boxed result with $\omega_{mn}$ identification. The middle block's 2-line jump skips the natural intermediate $\mathrm{e}^{+\mathrm{i}E_m t/\hbar}\langle m\vert\hat{V}(t)\vert n\rangle\,\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}E_n t/\hbar}$ that exhibits the eigenstate-action explicitly: $\langle m\vert\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}(t) = \langle m\vert\,\mathrm{e}^{+\mathrm{i}E_m t/\hbar}$ on the left (from the spectral expansion just stated, applying $\hat{H}_0\vert m\rangle = E_m\vert m\rangle$ in the bra) and $\hat{U}_0(t)\vert n\rangle = \mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}E_n t/\hbar}\vert n\rangle$ on the right. `_refs/PerturbationTheory.md` lines 1700-1702 does not show this matrix-element derivation explicitly (just states the result $V_{\mathcal{I}}(t)=\sum_{m,n}\vert m\rangle V_{mn}(t)\,\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}(E_m-E_n)t}\langle n\vert$) — so the lecture is more detailed than the reference at the global derivation level (`_refs/` gives 0 derivation lines; lecture gives 3 displayed blocks). However, within the lecture's own middle block the 2-line jump still violates `rules/derivation-quality.md` § Mandatory procedure step 4 (*\"Show every commutator / identity evaluation\"*) and step 6 (*\"Explain where each term comes from\"*) — the eigenstate-action is the conceptual mechanism by which Bohr frequencies emerge, and skipping it loses the pedagogical payoff of having stated the spectral expansions in block (i). **Fix**: `phys130b-derivation-designer` inserts one intermediate equality in the middle block, expanding from `\langle m\vert\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}(t)\vert n\rangle = \langle m\vert\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}(t)\hat{V}(t)\hat{U}_0(t)\vert n\rangle = \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}(E_m-E_n)t/\hbar}\,V_{mn}(t)` to a 3-equality chain: `\langle m\vert\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}(t)\vert n\rangle = \langle m\vert\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}(t)\hat{V}(t)\hat{U}_0(t)\vert n\rangle = \mathrm{e}^{+\mathrm{i}E_m t/\hbar}\,\langle m\vert\hat{V}(t)\vert n\rangle\,\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}E_n t/\hbar} = \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}(E_m-E_n)t/\hbar}\,V_{mn}(t)` (using `\begin{split}` to stack across multiple lines if width is an issue, or keeping the chain inline). The new middle equality makes the eigenstate-action explicit and connects the dropdown's block (i) spectral expansion to block (iii) Bohr-frequency identification without a conceptual gap. Then `phys130b-notebook-writer` applies via single atomic `text.replace` on cell 2 per `rules/notebook-editing.md` (raw-string LaTeX required for `\\langle`, `\\hat{}`, `\\mathrm{}`, `\\vert`). Post-save validators per `rules/validation.md`. **Out of scope:** the dropdown's existing block (i) spectral-expansion pair (well-presented, no rewrites) and block (iii) boxed result (well-presented); the two other derivation dropdowns (D2 / D3 — separate tickets DD-01 / DD-02); cell-2 body outside the three dropdowns; cells 0 / 1 / 3. **Severity**: lowest of the three Phase D findings (single 1-line addition vs DD-01's 3-line expansion vs DD-02's 2-line addition) — file separately per the audit's single-scope ticket discipline (precedent: 2026-05-17 Phase C audit on the same notebook filed 4 single-scope tickets NB-40 / NB-41 / SD-06 / HD-04 of varying severity).

Fix pattern: two atomic `text.replace` calls on cell 2 — `iteraction` → `interaction` (expected count 2 → 0; new count 0 → 2) and `evoluation` → `evolution` (expected count 2 → 0; new count 0 → 2) with pre/post count guards per `rules/notebook-editing.md` § Required method (Bash + Python `json.load` / `json.dump(indent=1, ensure_ascii=False)`; banned `NotebookEdit` / `Edit` / `Write`; raw-string LaTeX not required for this fix since the target strings are pure ASCII English). All 3 are near-lecture conceptual checks; per § Homework Design *"Difficulty gradient: first 2-3 problems should directly exercise lecture content (easy, near-lecture); middle problems transfer or compare; last 1-2 can be hard/far-reaching"*, the set lacks any transfer / inversion / edge-case / comparison / misconception problem. Candidate problem topics (draft-time menu — designer chooses 2-4): (a) **Operator-picture transformation** — derive $\hat{O}_{\mathcal{I}}(t)=\hat{U}_0^\dagger(t)\hat{O}\hat{U}_0(t)$ and show $\mathrm{i}\hbar\,\partial_t\hat{O}_{\mathcal{I}} = [\hat{O}_{\mathcal{I}},\hat{H}_0] + (\partial_t\hat{O})_{\mathcal{I}}$ (Heisenberg-like equation in the interaction picture, transfer to operator language); (b) **Time-independent perturbation** — if $\hat{V}$ has no explicit time dependence, show that the interaction picture coincides with the Heisenberg picture for $\hat{H}_0$ (comparison / edge case); (c) **Two-level Rabi setup** — for $\hat{V}(t)=\hbar\Omega\cos(\omega t)\hat{\sigma}^x$ on a $\hat{H}_0=\frac{\hbar\omega_0}{2}\hat{\sigma}^z$ baseline, compute the matrix elements $\langle\pm|\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}(t)|\mp\rangle$ in the rotating frame and identify the resonance condition (concrete application); (d) **Unitarity check** — verify directly that $\hat{U}=\hat{U}_0\hat{U}_{\mathcal{I}}$ with $\hat{U}_{\mathcal{I}}$ governed by $\mathrm{i}\hbar\,\partial_t\hat{U}_{\mathcal{I}}=\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}\hat{U}_{\mathcal{I}}$ remains unitary at all $t$ (consistency / derivation); (e) **Misconception** — *"In the interaction picture, $\hat{V}$ is removed from the dynamics."* — assess this claim and explain in 2-3 sentences how the perturbation survives as $\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}$ with phase-rotated matrix elements (misconception testing); (f) **Free particle in a brief impulse** — for $\hat{H}_0=\hat{p}^2/2m$ and $\hat{V}(t)=F_0\,\delta(t)\,\hat{x}$, compute $\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}(t)$ and the impulse-induced wavefunction shift (concrete application, ties to cell-1 prompt 5 and P3 scenario C). After designer drafts 2-4 chosen problems with sub-parts where appropriate, `phys130b-notebook-writer` applies a single atomic cell-3 `text.replace` that appends new `**N. Title.** Task...` blocks after the existing P3, with `**N.**` numbering picking up at 4-7 per the chosen count and `\n\n` separation between problems per `rules/content-style.md` § Common formatting errors #3 (sub-parts on own lines, blank lines around). **Out of scope:** P1 / P2 / P3 existing problems (well-formed, no rewrites); cell 1 Prompts (already covered by closed-clean Phase C audit); cell 2 lecture body (already covered by NB-40 / NB-41 / SD-06).

Per `rules/physics-conventions.md` § *Upright Symbols* the project convention is `\mathrm{…}`, not the deprecated short `\rm` (the same migration closed **T-2026-05-12-NB-09** on `4-4-1-classical-spin`; see also **T-2026-05-13-NB-13** on `4-4-3-monopole-harmonics`). **Fix:** single atomic `text.replace` in cell 3 changing `\hat H^{(2)}_{\rm eff}` → `\hat H^{(2)}_{\mathrm{eff}}`. **Acceptance:** post-fix grep of cells 0–3 shows zero `\\rm\b` occurrences inside math mode. Routed directly to `phys130b-notebook-writer` (single rule, single site).

(4) Same example — *true* multi-equation stack with `&=` column alignment of two state structures `|\pm 1\rangle'&=|\pm 1\rangle+\frac{\lambda}{\sqrt 2}|0\rangle+O(\lambda^2),\\ |0\rangle'&=|0\rangle-\frac{\lambda}{\sqrt 2}(|+1\rangle+|-1\rangle)+O(\lambda^2)` → split into two separate `$$...$$` blocks. Designer: `phys130b-notebook-writer` direct (sites (1)/(2) are local in-place substitution `\begin{aligned}` → `\begin{split}` or removal; sites (3)/(4) restructure the source by replacing one `$$\begin{aligned}…\end{aligned}$$` block with N consecutive `$$...$$` blocks, one per equation). - ~~**T-2026-05-16-LD-19**~~ **DONE 2026-05-16** (Phase B, notation, mechanical) — `notes_src/ch5_perturbation-theory/5-1-3-degenerate-perturbation-theory.ipynb` cell 2 carries 9 `\qquad` occurrences across roughly 7 `$$...$$` blocks laying out multiple equations side-by-side, violating `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Display Math (*"Do NOT use `\quad` or `\qquad` to lay out multiple equations side-by-side in one display block. When presenting multiple equations/definitions, stack them vertically (one relation per line)"*). Sites in *Example: Three-level bright/dark mechanism*: (a) Step 2 second-order block — two consecutive `$$...$$` blocks each pairing two matrix entries with `\qquad`: `(H^{\text{eff}})_{11}^{(2)}=-|\lambda_1|^2/\Delta,\qquad (H^{\text{eff}})_{22}^{(2)}=-|\lambda_2|^2/\Delta` and `(H^{\text{eff}})_{12}^{(2)}=-\lambda_1\lambda_2^*/\Delta,\qquad (H^{\text{eff}})_{21}^{(2)}=-\lambda_2\lambda_1^*/\Delta` (2 blocks × 2 entries = 4 entries; split each into separate `$$...$$` blocks, or fold all four into one 4-line `\begin{split}` block with a single `&` for alignment); (b) Step 3 eigenvalue pair `E_B=-(|\lambda_1|^2+|\lambda_2|^2)/\Delta,\qquad E_D=0`; (c) Step 4 exact zero-mode pair `\hat H_0|D\rangle=0,\qquad \hat V|D\rangle=0`. Sites in *Example: Spin-1 splitting from a degenerate manifold*: (d) opening Hamiltonian setup block with three `\qquad`-separated definitions `\hat H(\lambda)=\hat H_0+\lambda\hat V,\qquad \hat H_0=(\hat S^z)^2,\qquad \hat V=\hat S^x+\hat S^z` (3 relations in one block); (e) operator matrix pair `\hat S^x\simeq\frac{1}{\sqrt 2}\begin{pmatrix}…\end{pmatrix},\qquad \hat S^z\simeq\begin{pmatrix}…\end{pmatrix}`; (f) derived operator matrix pair `\hat H_0\simeq\begin{pmatrix}…\end{pmatrix},\qquad \hat V\simeq\begin{pmatrix}…\end{pmatrix}`. Fix pattern (same as the closed T-2026-05-16-LD-13 on `5-1-2-non-degenerate-perturbation-theory`): split each `$$...$$` block into N consecutive `$$...$$` blocks (one relation per block), matching the dominant pattern used elsewhere in the lecture (e.g. cell 2 *Problem Setup* `\hat H_0=…` and `\hat V=…` already use two separate `$$...$$` blocks for parallel operator definitions). **Borderline case NOT in scope for this ticket:** cell 2 Example: Three-level Step 1 block `(H^{\text{eff}})_{ij}^{(1)}=\langle i|\hat V|j\rangle=0,\qquad i,j\in\{1,2\}` uses `\qquad` to separate the equation from its domain specification `i,j\in\{1,2\}` rather than to stack two relations — borderline reading of the rule. Designer choice at fix time. Single rule, ~7 sites in one cell. Designer: `phys130b-notebook-writer` direct. Post-edit: validate per `rules/validation.md`.

Sites: (1) HW Problem 2 (*Block first, levels later*) inline math `W^{(n)}_{\alpha\beta}=\langle n,\alpha|V|n,\beta\rangle` — bare `V` should be `\hat V`; (2) HW Problem 4 (*Hydrogen Stark splitting*) display math `$$V=e\mathcal E_0 z$$` — bare `V` and bare `z` should be `\hat V` and `\hat z`; (3) HW Problem 5 (*Residual degeneracy*) display math `$$H_{\text{eff}}=\begin{pmatrix}a&b\\b^*&c\end{pmatrix}$$` — bare `H_{\text{eff}}` should be `\hat H_{\text{eff}}`. HW Problem 3 already uses `\hat H_0`, `\hat V`, `\hat P_d`, `\hat X` correctly throughout; HW Problem 6 uses `\mathcal E\hat z` correctly; HW4 selection-rule labels `\Delta\ell=\pm 1`, `\Delta m=0` are scalar quantum numbers (no hat needed); these three are lone drifts. Suggested mechanical fixes (atomic per site; verify uniqueness first): site 1 — `\langle n,\alpha|V|n,\beta\rangle` → `\langle n,\alpha|\hat V|n,\beta\rangle` (after LD-17 migration this becomes `\langle n,\alpha\vert\hat V\vert n,\beta\rangle`); site 2 — `V=e\mathcal E_0 z` → `\hat V=e\mathcal E_0\hat z`; site 3 — `H_{\text{eff}}=\begin{pmatrix}` → `\hat H_{\text{eff}}=\begin{pmatrix}`. LD-17 and LD-20 touch different characters and can be sequenced in either order. Single rule, three sites, single cell. Designer: `phys130b-notebook-writer` direct. Post-edit: validate per `rules/validation.md`.

- **T-2026-05-16-DV-16** (Phase D, derivation quality, **DONE 2026-05-16**) — `notes_src/ch5_perturbation-theory/5-1-2-non-degenerate-perturbation-theory.ipynb` cell 2, `:::{admonition} Derivation: second-order energy derivative :class: dropdown information`. The step from $\partial_\lambda E_n=\langle n\vert\hat V\vert n\rangle$ to $\partial_\lambda^2 E_n=\langle\partial_\lambda n\vert\hat V\vert n\rangle+\langle n\vert\hat V\vert\partial_\lambda n\rangle$ implicitly uses $\partial_\lambda\hat V=0$, which follows from the linear ansatz $\hat H(\lambda)=\hat H_0+\lambda\hat V$ stated in the cell-2 *Non-Degenerate Perturbation Problem* `important` box. The reference `_refs/PerturbationTheory.md` § Energy Corrections (lines 353–375) starts the same derivation from $\partial_\lambda E_n=\langle n\vert\partial_\lambda\hat H\vert n\rangle$, writes the full three-term product rule including $\langle n\vert\partial_\lambda^2\hat H\vert n\rangle$, then explicitly drops that middle term invoking $\partial_\lambda^2\hat H=0$ from the linear ansatz. Per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § Mandatory detail level (*"Each commutator evaluation or algebraic derivation in a dropdown must contain at least as many displayed intermediate lines as the corresponding passage in `_refs/`"*) and § Root causes #4 (*"Missing motivation for 'why this form'"*), insert a single-sentence aside before the product-rule step explaining that $\hat V$ has no $\lambda$ dependence (so $\partial_\lambda\hat V=0$ and only the bra and ket are differentiated). Suggested wording (designer to refine): replace the prose connector "Differentiate once more:" with "Differentiate once more; since $\hat V$ has no $\lambda$ dependence in the linear ansatz $\hat H(\lambda)=\hat H_0+\lambda\hat V$ (so $\partial_\lambda\hat V=0$), only the bra and ket are differentiated:" (one short sentence; pre/post line count delta ≈ +1; no other content change). Cell-2 length delta expected ≈ +130 chars. Designer: `phys130b-derivation-designer` (proposes exact replacement string) → `phys130b-notebook-writer` (applies single atomic `text.replace` against the connector anchor + validates per `rules/validation.md`).

- **T-2026-05-16-LD-15** (Phase C, style/structure, **OPEN**) — `notes_src/ch5_perturbation-theory/5-1-2-non-degenerate-perturbation-theory.ipynb`: the lecture body (cell 2) proves first- and second-order corrections using the $\partial_\lambda$ shorthand introduced in the *Notation Clarification* `dropdown note` ($\vert\partial_\lambda n\rangle$, $\partial_\lambda E_n$, $\partial_\lambda^2 E_n$), while the `### Summary` bullets (cell 2) and every Homework problem 1–8 (cell 3) pivot to Rayleigh-Schrödinger order-superscript notation ($\vert n^{(1)}\rangle$, $E_n^{(1)}$, $E_n^{(2)}$, $\langle n^{(0)}\vert n^{(1)}\rangle$) without an explicit bridge defining $\vert n^{(k)}\rangle \equiv (1/k!)\partial_\lambda^k\vert n(\lambda)\rangle\big|_{\lambda=0}$ and $E_n^{(k)} \equiv (1/k!)\partial_\lambda^k E_n(\lambda)\big|_{\lambda=0}$. HW Problem 2 (b) mixes both notations in a single sub-part: "compute $\langle 2\vert\partial_\lambda 1\rangle$ and $\langle 3\vert\partial_\lambda 1\rangle$ at $\lambda=0$. Assemble $\vert 1^{(1)}\rangle$." Site inventory (cell 2 Summary): bullet 2 ($E_n^{(1)}=V_{nn}$), bullet 3 ($\vert n^{(1)}\rangle = \sum_{m\neq n}\vert m\rangle V_{mn}/(E_n-E_m)$), bullet 4 ($E_n^{(2)} = \sum_{m\neq n}\vert V_{mn}\vert^2/(E_n-E_m)$). Site inventory (cell 3 Homework): HW1 ($\langle n^{(0)}\vert n^{(1)}\rangle = 0$); HW2(b) ($\vert 1^{(1)}\rangle$ in the assembly step, alongside $\langle 2\vert\partial_\lambda 1\rangle$ / $\langle 3\vert\partial_\lambda 1\rangle$ in the same sub-part) + HW2(c) ($E_1^{(1)}$); HW3(a) ($\vert 1^{(1)}\rangle = \sum_{m\neq 1}\vert m\rangle V_{m1}/(E_1-E_m)$) + HW3(b) ($E_1^{(2)}$); HW4 preamble ($E_n^{(2)}$) + HW4(a) ($E_0^{(2)}$); HW5(a) ($E_\pm^{(1)}$, $E_\pm^{(2)}$) + HW5(b) ($\vert\pm^{(1)}\rangle$); HW7(a) ($E_n^{(1)}$) + HW7(b) ($E_n^{(2)}$); HW8(a) ($E_1^{(2)}$). Violates `rules/content-style.md` § Content Philosophy (*"Each `###` section is a self-contained conceptual unit"*) and `rules/maintenance-scope.md` § Prefer (*"One clear thread per lesson — each section advances the same main question; cut tangents that do not serve it"*). Two designer routes for resolution: **(a) (recommended) extend the body's *Notation Clarification* `dropdown note`** (cell 2 § Problem Setup) with two additional display blocks defining the Taylor-coefficient form of the Rayleigh-Schrödinger orders — $\vert n^{(k)}\rangle \equiv (1/k!)\,\partial_\lambda^k\vert n(\lambda)\rangle\big|_{\lambda=0}$ (equivalently, the $\lambda^k$ coefficient in $\vert n(\lambda)\rangle = \sum_k \lambda^k \vert n^{(k)}\rangle$) and $E_n^{(k)} \equiv (1/k!)\,\partial_\lambda^k E_n(\lambda)\big|_{\lambda=0}$. With this bridge, the Summary's $E_n^{(1)}=V_{nn}$, $\vert n^{(1)}\rangle = \sum_{m\neq n}\vert m\rangle V_{mn}/(E_n-E_m)$, and $E_n^{(2)} = \sum_{m\neq n}\vert V_{mn}\vert^2/(E_n-E_m)$ become well-defined consequences of the body's $\partial_\lambda$ derivations (multiply by $1/k!$ where needed; for $k=1$ the factor is trivially 1, so $\vert n^{(1)}\rangle = \vert\partial_\lambda n\rangle$ and $E_n^{(1)} = \partial_\lambda E_n = V_{nn}$; for $k=2$, $E_n^{(2)} = (1/2)\partial_\lambda^2 E_n = \sum_{m\neq n}\vert V_{mn}\vert^2/(E_n-E_m)$ matches the *Energy Expansion* Taylor box). HW1–8 then read against this same definition without ambiguity. Minimal-edit option: one `text.replace` in cell 2 to extend the existing *Notation Clarification* `dropdown note` with the two new display blocks (preserve the existing four blocks untouched). Preserves the body's $\partial_\lambda$ pedagogy and the HW's textbook Rayleigh-Schrödinger notation; reads as "the standard textbook order labels are defined this way," matching the convention any AI tutor or undergraduate textbook (Tong Ch. 6, Sakurai Ch. 5) will use naturally. **Recommended.** **(b) convert Summary and HW to $\partial_\lambda$ notation throughout** — drop order-superscripts everywhere, write $\vert\partial_\lambda n\rangle$, $\partial_\lambda E_n$, $(1/2)\partial_\lambda^2 E_n$ in the Summary and across all 8 HW problems. Cleaner internally but breaks alignment with the textbook standard and the conventions students will see in any QM reference; much larger edit (~15–20 sites across cells 2 and 3). **Not recommended.** Out of scope: cell 2 derivation chains (those stay in the $\partial_\lambda$ shorthand they already use — the Hellmann-Feynman derivation explicitly works in that idiom); the qubit *Example: check against the qubit toy model* `dropdown example` (uses ad hoc per-level superscripts like $E_0^{(1)}$, $E_0^{(2)}$ for the worked check — these are consequences of the bridge added in route (a) and need no edit); cell 1 Prompts (no order-superscript notation present). Designer: `phys130b-lecture-designer` (chooses between routes (a) and (b) and writes the spec); implementer: `phys130b-notebook-writer` (applies the edit, runs validators per `rules/validation.md`). Route (a) recommended — single-cell single-block edit (extend *Notation Clarification* box in cell 2; the box currently sits at cell 2 lines ~28–58 in the file, immediately after the *Non-Degenerate Perturbation Problem* `important` box). Post-edit verification: cell 2 *Notation Clarification* box gains 2 new display blocks for $\vert n^{(k)}\rangle$ and $E_n^{(k)}$ with explicit $1/k!$ Taylor-coefficient convention; Summary bullets and HW problems remain unchanged in route (a); cell-2 byte delta positive (estimate ~+150–250 chars for the two new display blocks plus a one-sentence intro). Original ticket text retained: **T-2026-05-16-LD-12** (Phase B, notation, mechanical, **OPEN**) — `notes_src/ch5_perturbation-theory/5-1-2-non-degenerate-perturbation-theory.ipynb` cells 1, 2, 3 carry 127 bare-pipe `|` ket/bra delimiters in math mode and **zero** existing `\vert` usages, violating `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Ket/Bra Notation and Pipe Characters (*"Use `\vert` for `|` in kets and bras throughout"*). Site counts (regex audit): cell 1 (Prompts) = 1 ket `|n\rangle` + 1 bra `\langle m|` inside the inline math `$V_{mn}=\langle m|\hat V|n\rangle$`; cell 2 (lecture) = 81 kets + 32 bras across the Problem Setup admonition, the Hellmann-Feynman identities + their derivations, the Energy / State Corrections derivations, the Physical Intuition bullets, the qubit-example dropdown, the Discussion dropdown, and the Summary; cell 3 (Homework) = 8 kets + 4 bras across Problems 1, 2, 3, 4. Migration: replace each ket-opening `|` → `\vert ` (mind the mandatory space — `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Ket/Bra Notation: *"never glue `\vert` to a following letter or digit"*) and each bra-closing `|` → `\vert` (no trailing space needed before `\hat`/`)` etc., but a space is required before letters/digits to avoid `\vertV` parsing failures). Closing `\rangle` already correct at every site. Take care with the inline-math expression in cell 1 which contains both an opening `|` (after `\langle m`) and a closing `|` (before `n\rangle`) — they need different mappings: `\langle m|\hat V|n\rangle` → `\langle m\vert\hat V\vert n\rangle`. Recommended implementation: per-cell `re.sub(r"(?<!\\vert)\|(?=[A-Za-z0-9_\\])", r"\\vert ", src)` for ket openings + similar for bra closings — but every replacement must be cross-checked because some `|` characters in display math may legitimately be absolute-value bars (e.g. `|V_{mn}|^2` in cell 2 Energy Expansion and second-order derivation, and in cell 3 Problems 3, 4, 8) which should be migrated to `\vert V_{mn}\vert^2` per the same rule but are semantically *not* kets. Pre-edit per-cell scan: cell 2 has at least 5 `|V_{mn}|^2`-style absolute-value sites that also need migration to `\vert V_{mn}\vert^2`; cell 3 has at least 3 such sites. Designer: `phys130b-notebook-writer` direct (single rule, multiple sites — apply per-cell with VERIFY-FIRST counts and per-cell length-delta assertions; consider splitting into three sub-tickets `LD-12a` (cell 1, 2 sites), `LD-12b` (cell 2, ~113 sites + ~5 abs-value sites), `LD-12c` (cell 3, 12 sites + ~3 abs-value sites) if the writer prefers atomic per-cell sessions). Post-edit: `python3 .claude/skills/notebook-writer/scripts/safe_edit.py validate notes_src/ch5_perturbation-theory/5-1-2-non-degenerate-perturbation-theory.ipynb` and `python3 .claude/scripts/validate_project.py --scope 5-1-2-non-degenerate-perturbation-theory` per `rules/validation.md` (`validate_project.py` already flags glued `\vert` per the rule).

Site 1 — Problem 2 (*Hellmann-Feynman in practice*): `$$ \hat H_0=\begin{pmatrix}1&0&0\\0&2&0\\0&0&4\end{pmatrix}, \quad \hat V=\begin{pmatrix}1&1&1\\1&0&1\\1&1&-1\end{pmatrix}, $$`. Site 2 — Problem 6 (*Harmonic oscillator with linear perturbation*): `$$ \hat V=\lambda \hat x, \qquad \hat x=\sqrt{\frac{\hbar}{2m\omega}}(\hat a+\hat a^\dagger). $$`. Two stacking options per the rule: (a) split each into two separate `$$...$$` blocks (the same pattern Problem 6 already uses for `\hat H_0=...` vs `\hat V=...` — see the two consecutive `$$` blocks at positions 2361 and 2432 in cell 3) — recommended for both sites because it matches the dominant pattern in this cell and across Ch 5; (b) keep one `$$...$$` block and use `\begin{split}...\end{split}` with `\\` line break — workable but adds non-trivial structure (matrix-in-split). Recommend option (a) for both sites. Verify-first: pre-edit cell-3 count of `\quad` = 1 and `\qquad` = 1 (both should map to 0 post-edit if option (a) is taken; remaining `\quad`/`\qquad` in the notebook are all in cell 2 implication-arrow connectors and are out of scope for this ticket — see the borderline observation in the Phase Cursor narrative). Designer: `phys130b-notebook-writer` direct (single rule, two sites, one cell, mechanical layout fix). Post-edit: validate per `rules/validation.md`.
