- ~~**T-2026-05-14-DV-10**~~ — **DONE 2026-05-14** (Phase D, derivation quality, `notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-4-3-monopole-harmonics.ipynb`): single atomic `text.replace` on cell 2 rewrote the `:::{admonition} Derivation: $[\hat{J}_i,\hat{J}_j]=\mathrm{i}\hbar\epsilon_{ijk}\hat{J}_k$` `dropdown information` block, restructured per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *While writing* step 5 (building-block identities → bilinear assembly → explicit cancellation). **(a)** Replaced the banned "on the sphere this gives an extra … term" hand-wave: ingredient 2 is now stated as an imported result — the kinetic-momentum commutator $[\hat{\pi}_i,\hat{\pi}_j]=\mathrm{i}\hbar q\epsilon_{ijk}B_k$ (§4.1.2, cited inline; §4.1.2 has no eq-label for it) with the explicit substitution $qB_k\to(qg/4\pi)\hat{r}_k=\hbar s\,\hat{r}_k$ (monopole field §4.4.2, $s$ via {eq}`eq-monopole-qnum`) — the ticket's sanctioned fallback (full $\epsilon\epsilon$ component derivation declined: expands scope past the dropdown's single focus and surfaces a sign-convention question, see read-ahead below). **(b)** Inserted the 4-term bilinear expansion $[\hat{\Lambda}_i,\hat{\Lambda}_j]+s[\hat{\Lambda}_i,\hat{r}_j]+s[\hat{r}_i,\hat{\Lambda}_j]+s^2[\hat{r}_i,\hat{r}_j]$ as a `\begin{split}` block, each term evaluated, plus an explicit **Cancellation** paragraph showing $-1+1+1=+1$ on the $\epsilon_{ijk}\hat{r}_k$ terms reassembling $\mathrm{i}\hbar\epsilon_{ijk}\hat{J}_k$; dropped the redundant prose paragraph that merely re-stated ingredient 2. **(c)** Bundled notation: all `\hat{\boldsymbol r}_{i,j,k}` (vector carrying a component index) → `\hat{r}_{i,j,k}` per `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Vectors and Operators (0 residual). Cell-2 length 11305 → 13558 chars (delta +2253). `safe_edit.py validate` *OK*; `validate_project.py --scope 4-4-3-monopole-harmonics` *all passed*. Designer: `phys130b-derivation-designer` → `phys130b-notebook-writer` (same session).
  - **T-2026-05-14-DV-10 (original ticket text, retained):** **T-2026-05-14-DV-10** (Phase D, derivation quality, NEEDS WORK) — `notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-4-3-monopole-harmonics.ipynb` cell 2, the `:::{admonition} Derivation: $[\hat{J}_i, \hat{J}_j] = \mathrm{i}\hbar\epsilon_{ijk}\hat{J}_k$ :class: dropdown information` block. Two required gaps + one bundled notation cleanup. **(a)** The anomalous kinetic-angular-momentum commutator $[\hat{\Lambda}_i,\hat{\Lambda}_j] = \mathrm{i}\hbar\epsilon_{ijk}(\hat{\Lambda}_k - s\hat{r}_k)$ — listed as "ingredient 2" and the crux of the whole result — is asserted with the banned "on the sphere this gives an extra … term" hand-wave (`rules/derivation-quality.md` § Anti-patterns "It can be shown that"; § Root causes #1 skipped intermediate steps). Fill the gap: derive it from the kinetic-momentum commutator $[\hat{\pi}_i,\hat{\pi}_j] = \mathrm{i}\hbar q\epsilon_{ijk}B_k$ (established in §4.1.2 — cite by equation label) together with the monopole field $\boldsymbol{B} = g\hat{\boldsymbol{r}}/(4\pi r^2)$ and $s = qg/(4\pi\hbar)$, so the reader sees the $-\mathrm{i}\hbar s\,\epsilon_{ijk}\hat{r}_k$ term emerge from $qB_k \to qg\,\hat{r}_k/(4\pi r^2)$ on the unit sphere. If a full component derivation expands scope too far, at minimum state ingredient 2 explicitly as a result imported from §4.1.2 / §4.4.2 with the substitution shown — not "on the sphere this gives." **(b)** The final boxed line jumps from $[\hat{\Lambda}_i + s\hat{r}_i,\hat{\Lambda}_j + s\hat{r}_j]$ directly to $\mathrm{i}\hbar\epsilon_{ijk}(\hat{\Lambda}_k + s\hat{r}_k)$ in one step (`rules/derivation-quality.md` § Minimum derivation depth "no 'it follows that' gaps"; § Mandatory detail level 4–7 lines per commutator evaluation). Insert the 4-term bilinear expansion $[\hat{\Lambda}_i,\hat{\Lambda}_j] + s[\hat{\Lambda}_i,\hat{r}_j] + s[\hat{r}_i,\hat{\Lambda}_j] + s^2[\hat{r}_i,\hat{r}_j]$; evaluate each using ingredient 1 ($[\hat{\Lambda}_i,\hat{r}_j]=\mathrm{i}\hbar\epsilon_{ijk}\hat{r}_k$), ingredient 2, and $[\hat{r}_i,\hat{r}_j]=0$; and show the cancellation explicitly — the $-\mathrm{i}\hbar s\,\epsilon_{ijk}\hat{r}_k$ anomaly term plus the two $+\mathrm{i}\hbar s\,\epsilon_{ijk}\hat{r}_k$ cross terms net to $+\mathrm{i}\hbar s\,\epsilon_{ijk}\hat{r}_k$, reassembling $\mathrm{i}\hbar\epsilon_{ijk}\hat{J}_k$. Currently "exactly cancels the extra term" is asserted but the cancellation arithmetic is invisible. Recommended structure (per § While writing step 5): (i) building-block identities 1 & 2 with derivation/citation, (ii) bilinear assembly, (iii) explicit cancellation — and drop the redundant prose paragraph that merely re-states ingredient 2. **(c)** Bundled notation: inside the dropdown, Cartesian components of the position operator are written `\hat{\boldsymbol r}_j` / `\hat{\boldsymbol r}_k` / `\hat{\boldsymbol r}_i` (a `\boldsymbol` vector carrying a component index); a component is a scalar operator and should be `\hat{r}_j` etc. per `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Vectors and Operators. (Same drift in HW3(a) — homework-designer / Phase C territory, retained as a read-ahead observation, not bundled here.) **Note**: T-2026-05-14-DV-09 (DONE 2026-05-14) already cleaned a `/r^2 \cdot r^2` artifact and a spurious `\hbar` from ingredient-line 2 of this same dropdown; DV-10 addresses the remaining, deeper gaps DV-09 did not touch — the *justification* of ingredient 2 (still a banned hand-wave), the invisible bilinear-assembly arithmetic in the final boxed line, and the `\boldsymbol`-component notation. **Scope discipline**: edit stays strictly inside the existing `:::{dropdown information}` block; do **not** add new dropdowns for the §4.4.2 Berry-curvature or Chern-number results (those remain stated `important`-box results — adding derivations would be scope expansion per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § Scope and pedagogical fit). Designer: `phys130b-derivation-designer` → `phys130b-notebook-writer`.
  - **Read-ahead observation (DV-10 work, 2026-05-14 — not a ticket; folds into DV-11's VERIFY-FIRST scope):** while filling ingredient 2, an honest component derivation of $[\hat{\Lambda}_i,\hat{\Lambda}_j]$ from $[\hat{\pi}_i,\hat{\pi}_j]=\mathrm{i}\hbar q\epsilon_{ijk}B_k$ with $s=qg/(4\pi\hbar)>0$ yields the anomaly with the **opposite** sign to the notebook's ingredient 2 — i.e. the standard charge-monopole convention has $\hat{\boldsymbol J}=\hat{\boldsymbol\Lambda}-s\hat{\boldsymbol r}$ (field angular momentum $-s\hat{\boldsymbol r}$), whereas this notebook uses $\hat{\boldsymbol J}=\hat{\boldsymbol\Lambda}+s\hat{\boldsymbol r}$ with ingredient 2 $=\mathrm{i}\hbar\epsilon_{ijk}(\hat{\Lambda}_k-s\hat{r}_k)$. The notebook is **internally self-consistent** (the final result $[\hat J_i,\hat J_j]=\mathrm{i}\hbar\epsilon_{ijk}\hat J_k$ closes, verified) and physics ($j_{\min}=\vert s\vert$, Chern $2s$) is sign-independent — so DV-10 left the notebook's sign convention untouched and presented ingredient 2 as imported. But the $\hat{\boldsymbol J}=+s\hat{\boldsymbol r}$ vs standard $-s\hat{\boldsymbol r}$ choice is the **same convention family** as the DV-11 $\Phi_{\mathrm{Berry}}$ sign question; the designer verifying DV-11 against §4.2.1 / §4.4.2 should reconcile the $\hat{\boldsymbol J}$ sign at the same time.

  - **T-2026-05-13-NB-14 (original ticket text, retained):** (Phase B, notation, `notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-4-3-monopole-harmonics.ipynb`): cell 2 has **six** `$$...$$` display-math blocks that lay out two or more equations side-by-side using `\quad` / `\qquad`, violating `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Display Math (*"Do NOT use `\quad` or `\qquad` to lay out multiple equations side-by-side in one display block"*; *"When presenting multiple equations/definitions, stack them vertically (one relation per line), using `\begin{split}...\end{split}` where needed"*). Sites (cell 2 line numbers approximate): (α) § *Convention: magnetic charge vs. monopole quantum number* attention block — `$$\Phi_{\mathrm{Berry}} = s\Omega,\qquad \Omega=4\pi \Rightarrow \Phi_{\mathrm{Berry}}=4\pi s=2\pi n.$$` (compound: side-by-side definition + implication chain — split into two relations); (β) § *Total Angular Momentum in a Monopole Field* important block — `$$\hat{J}^2 \vert s, j, m\rangle = \hbar^2 j(j+1) \vert s, j, m\rangle, \quad \hat{J}_z \vert s, j, m\rangle = \hbar m \vert s, j, m\rangle$$` (clear side-by-side, stack vertically); (γ) same block — `$$j = |s|,\; |s|+1,\; |s|+2,\; \ldots \quad (\text{not } j = 0, 1, 2, \ldots)$$` (borderline — single allowed-values expression + parenthetical aside; designer choice: stack with `\begin{split}` or move parenthetical to prose); (δ) § *Monopole Harmonics* important block — `$$\hat{J}^2 Y^s_{j,m} = \hbar^2 j(j+1) Y^s_{j,m}, \quad \hat{J}_z Y^s_{j,m} = \hbar m Y^s_{j,m}$$` (clear side-by-side); (ε) § *Spin-1/2 Emerges from $s = 1/2$* important block — `$$\vert \tfrac{1}{2}, +\tfrac{1}{2}\rangle \quad \text{and} \quad \vert \tfrac{1}{2}, -\tfrac{1}{2}\rangle$$` (two ket states separated by `\quad \text{and} \quad` — stack vertically or write as a set `\{\vert\cdot\rangle,\,\vert\cdot\rangle\}` inline); (ζ) `:::{admonition} Derivation: [J_i, J_j] = ...` :class: dropdown information block — `$$[J_i, J_j] = [\Lambda_i + s\hat{\boldsymbol r}_i, \Lambda_j + s\hat{\boldsymbol r}_j] = \mathrm{i}\hbar\epsilon_{ijk}(\Lambda_k + s\hat{\boldsymbol r}_k) = \mathrm{i}\hbar\epsilon_{ijk}J_k \quad \checkmark$$` (borderline — trailing `\quad \checkmark` is decorative, drop or move outside the display). **Designer:** `notebook-writer` for stacking; designer-preferred routes per `skills/notebook-writer/SKILL.md` (route (a) = `\begin{split}...\end{split}` matching the recent T-2026-05-11-NB-01 / T-2026-05-11-NB-02 / T-2026-05-12-NB-05 / T-2026-05-12-NB-07 / T-2026-05-13-NB-11 precedents). **Note (cross-ticket coordination):** the (ζ) derivation block's display will also be touched by T-2026-05-13-NB-16 (operator-hat sweep) — these two tickets share the same lines; serialize so the second edit reads the first edit's saved state.

- ~~**T-2026-05-13-DV-08**~~ — **DONE 2026-05-13** (Phase D, derivation quality, `notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-4-2-dirac-monopole.ipynb`; designer-preferred **route (a)** chosen — single `\begin{split}` block merges the Stokes-theorem bridge with the existing total-flux equation; **optional companion bundled** — single-patch loop integral rewritten with `\underbrace` labels on $A^{(N)}_\varphi$ and $|\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l}|$ so the parameter-canceling $r$ / $\sin\theta$ structure is visible. **Edit (a) — Stokes bridge** (body, § *Aharonov-Bohm Phase and Quantization*): the existing one-line *''$\alpha(2\pi)-\alpha(0) = \oint\boldsymbol{B}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{S} = \int(g/4\pi r^{2})\,r^{2}\sin\theta\,\mathrm{d}\theta\,\mathrm{d}\varphi = g$'' assertion was rewritten as a 3-line `\begin{split}` block making every step explicit — line 1 substitutes $\boldsymbol{A}^{(N)} - \boldsymbol{A}^{(S)} = \nabla\alpha$ into the equator integral; line 2 applies Stokes' theorem to each patch (upper hemisphere → $+\hat{\boldsymbol{\varphi}}$ induced orientation, lower hemisphere → $-\hat{\boldsymbol{\varphi}}$, with the minus sign on $\oint_{\mathrm{eq}}\boldsymbol{A}^{(S)}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l}$ flipping into $+\int_{\mathrm{lower}}\boldsymbol{B}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{S}$); line 3 reassembles into the closed-surface flux $\oint_{S^{2}}\boldsymbol{B}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{S} = g$. Preface prose names the orientation-induction rule on each patch; closing sentence states each hemisphere contributes $g/2$. **Edit (b) — optional companion** in `:::{admonition} Derivation: same condition from the single-patch loop integral :class: dropdown information`: the previous integrand $\frac{g}{4\pi}\frac{(1-\cos(\pi/2))}{\sin(\pi/2)}\sin(\pi/2)\,\mathrm{d}\varphi$ (which presented the cancellation as algebraic coincidence) was replaced with a `\begin{split}` block: line 1 shows the general-$\theta$ integrand with `\underbrace` labels naming the two factors ($A^{(N)}_\varphi = (g/4\pi r)(1-\cos\theta)/\sin\theta$ and $|\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l}| = r\sin\theta\,\mathrm{d}\varphi$ on the latitude circle), with the evaluation marker $\bigl|_{\theta=\pi/2}$ at the end; line 2 carries out the cancellation ($r$ vs $1/r$, $\sin\theta$ vs $1/\sin\theta$) explicitly to $\int_{0}^{2\pi}(g/4\pi)\bigl(1-\cos(\pi/2)\bigr)\,\mathrm{d}\varphi = g/2$. No `_refs/` structural-diff applied verbatim because `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` § *Quantization of Spin (or Monopole)* lines 4675–4793 uses a spin-axis-on-unit-sphere framing (loop integral on a latitude circle bounding a spherical cap, $\Theta = (1/\hbar)\oint\boldsymbol{A}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l} = -4\pi S/\hbar$); the EM-monopole formulation here is a parallel structure rather than a verbatim translation, so the rule's other criteria (forward chain, no skipped commutator/Stokes steps, single focus, no scope creep) governed the edit. **Cell-2 length 12769 → 14204 chars (delta +1435: ~+700 for Edit (a), ~+735 for Edit (b))** via two atomic `text.replace` calls on raw-string LaTeX targets (`r'...'` per `rules/notebook-editing.md` § *LaTeX in JSON* — both old and new contain `\b` near `\boldsymbol`, `\h` near `\hat`, `\p` near `\pi`, `\m` near `\mathrm`, `\v` near `\varphi`/`\vert`, `\a` near `\alpha`, `\n` near `\nabla`, `\t` near `\theta` — all Python/JSON control-escape hazards safely handled by raw-string syntax); Original ticket text retained for reference:
  - **T-2026-05-13-DV-08 (original ticket text, retained):** **T-2026-05-13-DV-08** (Phase D, derivation quality, `notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-4-2-dirac-monopole.ipynb`): body derivation in cell 2 § *Aharonov-Bohm Phase and Quantization* skips the bridge step linking the gauge-function winding $\alpha(2\pi)-\alpha(0)$ to the total magnetic flux $g$ of the monopole. The current display reads `$$\alpha(2\pi)-\alpha(0) = \oint\boldsymbol{B}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{S} = \int\frac{g}{4\pi r^{2}}\,r^{2}\sin\theta\,\mathrm{d}\theta\,\mathrm{d}\varphi = g.$$` asserting the first equality (winding = surface flux of $\boldsymbol{B}$) **without derivation**. Per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Root causes* #1 (*Skipped intermediate steps*) and § *Mandatory procedure* item 4 (*Show every commutator/identity evaluation* — *''It can be shown that'' is banned in derivation dropdowns*), the bridge from *''winding of α''* to *''total flux $g$''* needs to be visible: apply Stokes' theorem to the two hemispheres separately. On the equator, with $\boldsymbol{A}^{(N)}$ smooth on the upper hemisphere, $\oint_{eq}\boldsymbol{A}^{(N)}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l} = \int_{\mathrm{upper}}\boldsymbol{B}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{S} = g/2$; similarly with $\boldsymbol{A}^{(S)}$ smooth on the lower hemisphere and the equator's orientation opposite to the bounded-region normal, $\oint_{eq}\boldsymbol{A}^{(S)}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l} = -\int_{\mathrm{lower}}\boldsymbol{B}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{S} = -g/2$. Subtracting and using $\boldsymbol{A}^{(N)} - \boldsymbol{A}^{(S)} = \nabla\alpha$: $\alpha(2\pi)-\alpha(0) = \oint_{eq}\nabla\alpha\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l} = +g/2 - (-g/2) = g$, which equals the total flux $\oint_{S^2}\boldsymbol{B}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{S} = g$ through any closed surface enclosing the monopole. **Designer routes**: **(a)** preferred — insert a 3–4 displayed-line Stokes-theorem bridge directly in the body prose between the current *''The winding $\alpha(2\pi)-\alpha(0)$ equals the **total magnetic flux** of the monopole through any closed surface enclosing it''* sentence and the existing $\alpha(2\pi)-\alpha(0) = \oint\boldsymbol{B}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{S} = g$ displayed equation; keep the existing displayed equation as the closing line of the bridge or merge into a single `\begin{split}` block; **(b)** alternative — push the bridge into a small `:::{admonition} Derivation: Why winding equals total flux :class: dropdown information` block immediately after the *''The winding...''* sentence, leaving the body prose-driven; the existing displayed equation can be replaced by an in-prose statement *''one finds $\alpha(2\pi)-\alpha(0) = g$ (see derivation)''*. Route (a) preserves narrative flow; route (b) keeps the body prose tight. **Optional companion** (bundle if convenient, per `rules/derivation-quality.md` precedent T-DV-04 / DV-07 — *''bundle if convenient''*): in the existing `:::{admonition} Derivation: same condition from the single-patch loop integral :class: dropdown information`, the integrand expression $\frac{g}{4\pi}\frac{(1-\cos(\pi/2))}{\sin(\pi/2)}\sin(\pi/2)\,\mathrm{d}\varphi$ presents the factor $\sin(\pi/2)$ (coming from $\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l} = r\sin\theta\,\mathrm{d}\varphi\,\hat{\boldsymbol{\varphi}}$ on the equatorial circle of radius $r\sin\theta$) and the $1/\sin(\pi/2)$ factor (from $\boldsymbol{A}^{(N)} = (g/4\pi r)(1-\cos\theta)/\sin\theta\,\hat{\boldsymbol{\varphi}}$) as if they had appeared independently — they cancel exactly at $\theta = \pi/2$. Add a brief inline parenthetical naming the $\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l} = r\sin\theta\,\mathrm{d}\varphi\,\hat{\boldsymbol{\varphi}}$ factor (or split the integrand into the $\boldsymbol{A}^{(N)}\cdot\hat{\boldsymbol{\varphi}}$ and $|\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l}|$ pieces explicitly before substituting $\theta = \pi/2$) so the student sees the parameter-canceling structure rather than treating it as algebraic coincidence. **Out of scope**: dropdown's closing framing *''Both phases are valid in their respective patches; their **difference** on the overlap is what must be a multiple of $2\pi$''* — this restates the body's single-valuedness conclusion two paragraphs earlier (which is already established as the principle for the body derivation); minor presentation concern, not a derivation gap; HW Problem 2(a) *Verify $\nabla\times\boldsymbol{A}^{(N)} = (g/4\pi r^2)\hat{\boldsymbol{r}}$ for $\theta\neq\pi$* and HW Problem 5 winding-number argument and HW Problem 7 angular momentum in monopole field — homework exercises, outside Phase D scope; cell 1 Prompts, cell 2 Overview, Summary, See Also, the `Poll: Monopole charge quantization` (closed T-LD-04), the `Discussion: Monopole Charge Quantization` (closed T-LD-05), the *Topological Origin of Quantization* `important` box, the *Dirac quantization condition* `important` box with `(eq-dirac-quantization)`, and all other admonitions in cell 2 — not derivations; the *Charge Quantization from Monopoles* algebra ($eg = 2\pi n_e\hbar$, $qg = 2\pi n_q\hbar$, $q/e = n_q/n_e$) — forward chain with trivial division, no missing steps. **Designer**: `phys130b-derivation-designer` (read `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` § *Magnetic Monopole* lines 4070–4793 to ground the Stokes-bridge structure in the rule-mandated reference reading per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Mandatory procedure* item 1; note that `_refs/` uses a different framing (spin-axis on unit sphere with $S = \hbar s$ instead of EM monopole with charge $g$), so the structural-diff is partial — the rule's other criteria still apply; propose replacement text per route (a) or (b); confirm the upper/lower-hemisphere Stokes integrals evaluate to $+g/2$ and $-g/2$ respectively via the orientation convention $\hat{\boldsymbol{r}}$ outward from the closed sphere) → `phys130b-notebook-writer` (apply via Bash + Python `text.replace` per `rules/notebook-editing.md` § *LaTeX in JSON* using raw-string `r'...'` LaTeX targets — both old and new contain `\b` near `\boldsymbol`, `\h` near `\hat`, `\p` near `\pi`, `\m` near `\mathrm`, `\v` near `\varphi`/`\vert`, all JSON control-escape hazards; `assert text.count(old) == 1` guard before replacement; `text_to_source` helper per § *Source array format* to preserve `\n` terminators on non-final source lines; post-save run `python3 .claude/skills/notebook-writer/scripts/safe_edit.py validate notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-4-2-dirac-monopole.ipynb` and `python3 .claude/scripts/validate_project.py --scope 4-4-2-dirac-monopole` per `rules/validation.md`). **Coordination**: T-NB-10 closed prior session 2026-05-13 via route (b) (rule update only — flux-defined Dirac-monopole convention exception in `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Chapter 4 EM Units), so the existing convention declaration in cell 2 § *The Concept of a Magnetic Monopole* covers the Stokes-bridge's $\boldsymbol{B} = g\hat{\boldsymbol{r}}/(4\pi r^2)$ and the half-flux $g/2$ result without additional $\mu_0$ insertions; Borderline — strict reading of the new rule requires per-block declaration; minimal-disruption reading treats the whole notebook as one declared scope (the spirit of route (b) chosen in T-NB-10).   - **T-2026-05-13-LD-04 (original ticket text, retained):** (Phase C, style, `notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-4-2-dirac-monopole.ipynb`): fix the *Poll: Monopole charge quantization* block in cell 2 § *Two-Patch Gauge Formulation* per `rules/content-style.md` § Poll Design and `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Vectors and Operators. **Two issues**: **(i)** option **(D)** *"All of the above"* violates *"One definite correct answer"* — the lesson treats option (A) *gauge invariance of the quantum mechanical phase around a closed loop* and option (C) *single-valuedness of the electron wavefunction* as two phrasings of the same mechanism, while option (B) *"The discreteness of the vector potential circulation $\oint \mathbf{A}\cdot\mathrm{d}\ell = \pi\hbar/q$"* is wrong (the rule's AB form `\Phi_{\mathrm{AB}} = q\oint\boldsymbol{A}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l}/\hbar` becomes a multiple of $2\pi$, i.e. *the phase* is quantized, not the circulation to $\pi\hbar/q$); if (D) were correct it would require (B) to be correct, which it isn't, so a student with the right (C) intuition who also picks up the (A) framing will land on (D) as a second locally-consistent answer — exactly the failure mode T-2026-05-12-LD-03 fixed on `4-3-3-quantum-hall-effect`. **(ii)** Option (B) writes `\mathbf{A}` instead of `\boldsymbol{A}` — Ch 4 notebook-wide convention per `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Vectors and Operators is `\boldsymbol{...}` (not `\vec`, not `\mathbf`). **Designer routes** (writer picks): **(a) preferred** — drop option (D); tighten or rephrase option (A) so it's distinct enough from (C) that exactly one is *the* answer (e.g. make (A) a slightly wrong "gauge invariance fixes the phase to be globally well-defined" framing that doesn't fully capture the topology, while (C) remains the precise single-valuedness statement), or alternatively flip the question to "Which formulation **most precisely** captures the underlying principle?" so (C) is unambiguously preferred; fix `\mathbf{A}` → `\boldsymbol{A}` in (B). **(b) alternative** — keep all four options but explicitly fold (A) and (C) into a single combined option, and replace (D) with a fourth genuinely-wrong distractor (e.g. "The discrete spectrum of the angular-momentum operator $\hat{L}_z$ on a closed loop"). The notebook explicitly states in cell 2 § *The Concept of a Magnetic Monopole* *\"For the monopole discussion we use the standard Dirac-monopole convention where magnetic charge is defined directly by flux\"* — i.e., $g$ absorbs $\mu_0$ — and threads that convention through every monopole equation in cells 2 and 3 (~10 sites; full inventory in the 2026-05-13 Phase B audit log above). **Two designer routes** (designer picks one in concert with the professor): **(a) bring notebook into rule compliance** — insert `\mu_0` factors throughout cell 2 (Maxwell with monopole source, point-monopole field, single-patch curl, total-flux integral on the enclosing sphere, gauge-transition winding $\alpha(2\pi) - \alpha(0) = \mu_0 g$, and downstream quantization restated as $qg\mu_0 = 2\pi n\hbar$) and cell 3 HW1 / HW2 / HW3 / HW4 / HW5 / HW7; recompute concrete numerical values where they appear (HW3(a) $g_0 = h/e$ would update to $g_0 = h/(\mu_0 e)$ in the natural-SI form; HW8(a) $\Phi_0' = h/(2e)$ is the standard SC flux quantum and unchanged). Heavy edit (~10 LaTeX displays + downstream prose); preserves the existing rule as the canonical SI source. **(b) update `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Chapter 4 EM Units** to allow a *\"magnetic charge $\equiv$ flux\"* convention as an explicit exception for monopole derivations, with the convention declared explicitly at the start of any block where it is used (precedent: 2026-05-06 feedback allowing $c = 1$ in spacetime-unified 4-vector/tensor blocks; same wording template: *\"Outside such ... blocks, SI units ... remain mandatory.\"*). Light edit (~2 rule paragraphs + tighten the in-cell convention declaration on this notebook's Overview / § *The Concept of a Magnetic Monopole*). The notebook's prose already declares the convention, which favors route (b) on minimal-disruption grounds; route (a) keeps the rule as the single canonical SI source. **Designer**: `phys130b-derivation-designer` for route (a) (deep math-content review across all ~10 sites with `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` consultation per `rules/derivation-quality.md`) or `phys130b-lecture-designer` for route (b) (rule edit + brief notebook touch-up). **Out of scope**: AB-phase line-integral form `q\oint\boldsymbol{A}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l}/\hbar` already matches the rule, only the closed-surface flux equality $\oint\boldsymbol{B}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{S} = g$ vs $\mu_0 g$ is affected; the `\alpha` gauge-function symbol choice (already correct per the 2026-05-08 rule update); cell 1 Prompts (no equations affected); the read-ahead observations R1/R2/R3 from the Phase B audit (separate Phase C/D tickets if pursued); the AB-quantization derivation logical chain (Phase D territory if route (a) is taken and depth issues surface during the rewrite). **Implementation** (once route is chosen): hand off to chosen designer; on writer execution, use Bash + Python `text.replace` per `rules/notebook-editing.md`; raw-string `r'...'` for all LaTeX blocks per `notebook-editing.md` § *LaTeX in JSON*; `assert text.count(old) == 1` guard per site; post-save run both `python3 .claude/skills/notebook-writer/scripts/safe_edit.py validate notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-4-2-dirac-monopole.ipynb` and `python3 .claude/scripts/validate_project.py --scope 4-4-2-dirac-monopole` per `rules/validation.md`. **Cursor advance**: this notebook moves to next-notebook Phase A only after both NB-10 and NB-11 close (or are explicitly closed as out-of-scope) and Phase C / Phase D audits run.

Stack as four sequential `\begin{split}` lines (one Maxwell equation per line) with `&=` alignment per the rule. **(β) Borderline** — cell 2 § *The Dirac String Solution*: `\nabla \times \boldsymbol{A}^{(N)} = \frac{g}{4\pi}\,\frac{\hat{\boldsymbol{r}}}{r^2} \quad \text{for } \theta < \pi` — condition tag, not a parallel equation; the rule's prohibition targets *equations side-by-side*, not annotation tags. Acceptable to leave inline; designer may move *\"for $\theta < \pi$\"* into surrounding prose if desired. **Designer route**: `phys130b-notebook-writer` direct per `.claude/README.md` § 2.2 (mechanical layout fix; math content unchanged; single rule, single-scope) — parallel precedent T-2026-05-11-NB-01 (4-3-1, 6 sites), T-2026-05-11-NB-02 (4-3-2, 6 sites), T-2026-05-12-NB-05 (4-3-3, 5 sites), T-2026-05-12-NB-07 (4-4-1, 5 sites). **Coordination with T-NB-10**: both tickets touch the Maxwell-equations display; if NB-10 is taken first via route (a), the line `\nabla\cdot\boldsymbol{B} = 0` becomes `\nabla\cdot\boldsymbol{B} = \mu_0\rho_m` and the NB-11 split-block layout should use the updated form. The two tickets are otherwise independent. **Out of scope**: T-NB-10 (Ch 4 monopole SI-convention drift — separate ticket); the R3 read-ahead `\frac{\partial}{\partial t}` → `\partial_t` style choice (writer may bundle into the same `\begin{split}` rewrite for compactness, since the rule lists `\partial_t` as the canonical example, or leave as separate Phase C polish); cell 1 Prompts; cell 3 Homework; the `\boxed{...}` admonitions; the surrounding prose; all equation labels (preserve `(eq-dirac-quantization)` verbatim). **Implementation**: Bash + Python `json.load` / `json.dump(indent=1, ensure_ascii=False)` per `rules/notebook-editing.md`; one atomic `text.replace` on the (α) site (raw-string `r'...'` per `notebook-editing.md` § *LaTeX in JSON* — the target contains `\n`, `\b`, `\f`, `\m`, `\r`, all JSON control-escape characters requiring raw-string); `assert text.count(old) == 1` guard; post-write regex sweep `re.findall(r'\\quad\s+\\(nabla|partial|frac|boldsymbol|hat|sqrt|mathrm|begin|end)', text)` returns `[]` or only the borderline (β); post-save run `python3 .claude/skills/notebook-writer/scripts/safe_edit.py validate notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-4-2-dirac-monopole.ipynb` and `python3 .claude/scripts/validate_project.py --scope 4-4-2-dirac-monopole` per `rules/validation.md`.

- ~~**T-2026-05-12-DV-07**~~ — DONE 2026-05-12: see Session Log entry for `4-4-1-classical-spin` (Phase D, two atomic `text.replace` operations on cell 2 — designer-preferred **route (a)** (show the BAC-CAB algebra inline as a 3-equality `\begin{split}` block with surrounding prose naming the identity, citing `{eq}`eq-axis-kinematics`` and the scalar conditions $\boldsymbol n\cdot\boldsymbol n=1$ and $\boldsymbol n\cdot\boldsymbol\Omega=0$, then restating the boxed result $\boldsymbol\Omega=\boldsymbol n\times\dot{\boldsymbol n}$ with `(eq-Omega-from-n)` preserved verbatim) **plus optional companion** (split the *Check: curl of the spin-sphere potential* 2-line compression into the 3-line form $A_\varphi\sin\theta=S(\cos\theta-1)$ → $\partial_\theta[S(\cos\theta-1)]=-S\sin\theta$ → $(1/\sin\theta)\partial_\theta(A_\varphi\sin\theta)=-S$ matching `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` lines 4666–4673); cell-2 length 9796 → 10558 chars (delta +762 = +618 for the kinematics chain + +144 for the curl-check split); validators clean — `safe_edit.py validate` *OK: 1 notebook(s) validated, no issues found* and `validate_project.py --scope 4-4-1-classical-spin` *Notebooks checked: 1 / Rule docs checked: .claude/rules/physics-conventions.md / All validations passed* per `rules/validation.md`). Original ticket text retained for archival reference: (Phase D, derivation quality, `notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-4-1-classical-spin.ipynb` cell 2, inside the `:::{admonition} Derivation: kinematics of the spin axis :class: dropdown information :::` block): depth gap — the dropdown asserts $\boldsymbol\Omega=\boldsymbol n\times\dot{\boldsymbol n}$ from the "transverse choice" without showing the algebraic inversion of $\dot{\boldsymbol n}=\boldsymbol\Omega\times\boldsymbol n$. Per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Mandatory detail level* (4–7 displayed lines per derivation; "a 2-line derivation that skips from definition to answer is a rule violation unless the result genuinely follows in one step of trivial algebra") and § *Mandatory procedure* item 4 ("Show every commutator/identity evaluation"). Reference: `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` lines ~3961–3972 derives the inversion in ~4 displayed lines via BAC-CAB. **Current dropdown body** (verbatim): "If an angular velocity $\boldsymbol\Omega$ rotates the axis, then $\dot{\boldsymbol n}=\boldsymbol\Omega\times\boldsymbol n.$ (eq-axis-kinematics). Because $\boldsymbol n^2=1$, differentiating gives $\boldsymbol n\cdot\dot{\boldsymbol n}=0$: the velocity of $\boldsymbol n$ is tangent to the sphere. We choose $\boldsymbol\Omega$ to be the transverse angular velocity that changes the axis direction; any component parallel to $\boldsymbol n$ would describe additional rotation about the axis and is already contained in $S\boldsymbol n$. With this transverse choice, $\boldsymbol\Omega=\boldsymbol n\times\dot{\boldsymbol n}.$ (eq-Omega-from-n)". **Designer routes** (writer picks): **(a) default — show the algebra inline** (preferred, matches the *Derivation:* dropdown title and `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Mandatory procedure*): replace the closing prose+display with "With this transverse choice, cross {eq}`eq-axis-kinematics` with $\boldsymbol n$ from the left: $\boldsymbol n\times\dot{\boldsymbol n}=\boldsymbol n\times(\boldsymbol\Omega\times\boldsymbol n)=\boldsymbol\Omega(\boldsymbol n\cdot\boldsymbol n)-\boldsymbol n(\boldsymbol n\cdot\boldsymbol\Omega)=\boldsymbol\Omega$, using $\boldsymbol n\cdot\boldsymbol n=1$ and $\boldsymbol n\cdot\boldsymbol\Omega=0$. Hence $\boldsymbol\Omega=\boldsymbol n\times\dot{\boldsymbol n}.$ (eq-Omega-from-n)" — either as a single `\begin{split}` block (4 `&=` lines: BAC-CAB expansion in 2 lines, then collapse using the two scalar conditions in 2 lines), or as two short displays linked by prose justifying the collapse. Both forms are acceptable per `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Display Math (use `\begin{split}` for multiline; do not use `align`/`aligned`). Preserve the equation label `(eq-Omega-from-n)` immediately after the closing `$$`. **(b) alternative — point to HW 1(b)**: keep the assertion but add a parenthetical "(derivation: HW 1(b))" or "(see HW 1(b) for the explicit BAC-CAB step)" immediately after `eq-Omega-from-n`. Acceptable as a stopgap but weaker per the rule, since the dropdown's *Derivation:* title is then misleading — the dropdown does not actually derive its main result. Route (a) is strongly preferred. **Optional companion** (bundle into the same atomic edit if convenient, per `rules/maintenance-scope.md` single-scope discipline and the precedent set by T-2026-05-11-DV-04 on `4-3-1-cyclotron-motion`): § *Check: curl of the spin-sphere potential* dropdown — split the 2-line compression "Using $A_\varphi=S(\cos\theta-1)/\sin\theta$, $(1/\sin\theta)\partial_\theta[S(\cos\theta-1)]=-S$" into the 3-line form $A_\varphi\sin\theta=S(\cos\theta-1)$ → $\partial_\theta[S(\cos\theta-1)]=-S\sin\theta$ → $(1/\sin\theta)(-S\sin\theta)=-S$. The current compression is technically defensible (both algebraic moves are individually trivial), but slicing into 3 lines matches `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` lines 4666–4673 more closely; designer chooses whether to take this companion or leave it as-is. **Out of scope**: the *Derivation: spin-axis equation* dropdown (passes the chain-quality check at the level of detail expected for the single-`I` simplification — Finding 2 of the Phase D audit); the boxed equations `eq-spin-axis-dynamics` and `eq-spin-quantization` (statements, not chains); the *Spin-monopole dictionary* `important` box (parallel-definition table, not a derivation); cell 1 Prompts; cell 3 Homework (HW 1(b) and HW 4 already exercise the same derivations students will do independently); body prose outside the two target dropdowns; the boxed *Spin quantization as monopole flux quantization* `important` box and its body-prose single-valuedness argument (one-step identification of $\Omega=4\pi$ in $\Phi_{\mathrm{Berry}}=-S\Omega/\hbar$, not a dropdown); the abstract spin-sphere $\boldsymbol B(\boldsymbol n)=(g/(4\pi))\boldsymbol n$ convention question (intentional scope discipline per Phase B audit, no action); the $\Phi_{\mathrm{Berry}}$ formula stated in § *Quantization from Berry Phase* (definition citation, derivation in §4.2.1 per the See Also link). **Implementation**: `phys130b-derivation-designer` → `phys130b-notebook-writer` handoff per `.claude/README.md` § 2.3. **Designer** reads `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` § *Classical Spin* lines 3946–3972 for the BAC-CAB inversion; produces the replacement text (route a's `\begin{split}` block or paired displays); checks the BAC-CAB sign convention $\boldsymbol n\times(\boldsymbol\Omega\times\boldsymbol n)=\boldsymbol\Omega(\boldsymbol n\cdot\boldsymbol n)-\boldsymbol n(\boldsymbol n\cdot\boldsymbol\Omega)$ (matches the `_refs/` derivation and the standard BAC-CAB identity); confirms the final result $\boldsymbol\Omega=\boldsymbol n\times\dot{\boldsymbol n}$ matches `(eq-Omega-from-n)` verbatim. **Writer** applies via Bash + Python `text.replace` on raw-string `r'...'` LaTeX blocks per `rules/notebook-editing.md` § *LaTeX in JSON* (the replacement contains `\b` from `\boldsymbol`, `\O` and `\d` from operators/differentials — raw-string required); `assert text.count(old) == 1` guard before replacement; JSON written with `json.dump(indent=1, ensure_ascii=False)`. Post-save run `python3 .claude/skills/notebook-writer/scripts/safe_edit.py validate notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-4-1-classical-spin.ipynb` and `python3 .claude/scripts/validate_project.py --scope 4-4-1-classical-spin` per `rules/validation.md`. **Parallel precedent**: T-2026-05-11-DV-04 (`4-3-1-cyclotron-motion` Phase D, single-dropdown sign+algebra fix with optional companion bundled into the same atomic edit), T-2026-05-12-DV-05 (`4-3-2-landau-quantization` Phase D, single-dropdown depth gap with optional companion), T-2026-05-12-DV-06 (`4-3-3-quantum-hall-effect` Phase D, single-dropdown algebra-line fix without optional companion taken). **Cursor advances** to `4-4-2-dirac-monopole` at Phase A only after this ticket closes per `.claude/README.md` § 1.3 and the precedent chain `4-2-3` → `4-3-1` → `4-3-2` → `4-3-3` → `4-4-1`.

- ~~**T-2026-05-12-DV-06**~~ — DONE 2026-05-12: see Session Log entry for `4-3-3-quantum-hall-effect` (Phase D execution; designer-preferred route (a) — single atomic `text.replace` on the Step 5 first split line of the *Derivation: Kubo evaluation on Landau-level eigenstates* `:::{dropdown information}` block: `\frac{q}{\hbar}` → `\frac{q}{\hbar B}`; cell-2 length 15989 → 15991 chars (delta +2); downstream composition now closes cleanly to $|\sigma_{xy}| = (q^2/h)(N_{\mathrm{occ}}/N_\Phi)$ with no stray factor of $B$; symbolic-algebra spot-check (sympy) confirmed both the Step 5 LHS = $q/(\hbar B)$ and the downstream identity $|\sigma_{xy}| = q^2 N_{\mathrm{occ}}/(h N_\Phi)$ via $N_\Phi = qBA/h$; validators clean — `safe_edit.py validate` *OK: 1 notebook(s) validated, no issues found* and `validate_project.py --scope 4-3-3-quantum-hall-effect` *All validations passed*; **optional companion** ($r_m^2(t)$ chain expansion in *Argument 1* Step 1) **not taken** this session per `rules/maintenance-scope.md` single-scope discipline — the borderline algebraic-step compression remains as-is, consistent with the audit's *not separately ticket-worthy* judgement). Original ticket text: (Phase D, derivation quality, `notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-3-3-quantum-hall-effect.ipynb` cell 2, inside the `:::{admonition} Derivation: Kubo evaluation on Landau-level eigenstates :class: dropdown information :::` block, Step 5 *Simplify the prefactor*): algebra error in the Step 5 first displayed equation per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Root causes* #3 (*Wrong notation*) and especially #5 (*Reverse-engineering from the answer*), and § *Anti-patterns* (paper-over via an intermediate that does not compose). **Current display** (verbatim):

  $$\begin{split}\frac{(q/m)^2\,q\hbar B}{(\hbar\omega_c)^2} &= \frac{q}{\hbar}, \\ \frac{1}{A} &= \frac{qB}{h\,N_\Phi}.\end{split}$$

  **Issue.** With $\omega_c = qB/m$: $(\hbar\omega_c)^2 = \hbar^2 q^2 B^2/m^2$ and $(q/m)^2 q\hbar B = q^3\hbar B/m^2$, so the correct ratio is $q^3\hbar B/(\hbar^2 q^2 B^2) = q/(\hbar B)$ — **not** $q/\hbar$. The published Step 5 first equation is wrong by a factor of $1/B$. The downstream conclusion $|\sigma_{xy}| = (q^2/h)(N_{\mathrm{occ}}/N_\Phi)$ is nevertheless correct — direct algebra gives $\sigma_{xy} = (\mathrm{i}\hbar/A) \cdot \mathrm{i} \cdot (q/(\hbar B)) \cdot N_{\mathrm{occ}} = -q N_{\mathrm{occ}}/(AB) = -q^2 N_{\mathrm{occ}}/(h N_\Phi)$ using $N_\Phi = qBA/h$ — but composing the wrong $q/\hbar$ with the (correct) $1/A = qB/(h N_\Phi)$ leaves a stray factor of $B$ in the final expression. This is the textbook *reverse-engineering from the answer* pattern: the prefactor was decomposed into two pieces to "match" the known final result $(q^2/h)(N_{\mathrm{occ}}/N_\Phi)$, with the wrong intermediate bridging them. The `$\mathrm{i}^2 = -1$ absorbs into the carrier-sign convention` sentence is a secondary paper-over of the sign question — out of scope for this ticket. **Designer routes** (designer picks one): **(a) minimal typo fix** — replace `\frac{q}{\hbar}` with `\frac{q}{\hbar B}` in the first split line; downstream prose stays as-is. Cleanest single-character edit, preserves the existing presentation structure. **(b) Cleaner Step 5 rewrite** — replace the entire Step 5 with a forward composition: (i) state $\frac{(q/m)^2 q\hbar B}{(\hbar\omega_c)^2} = \frac{q}{\hbar B}$ using $\omega_c = qB/m$; (ii) combine with $1/A$ via $1/(AB) = q/(h N_\Phi)$ from $N_\Phi = qBA/h$; (iii) the two $\mathrm{i}$ factors give $\mathrm{i}^2 = -1$ so $\sigma_{xy} = -q^2 N_{\mathrm{occ}}/(h N_\Phi) = -\nu q^2/h$; (iv) magnitude with $|q| = e$ gives $|\sigma_{xy}| = \nu e^2/h$ (the sign is determined by carrier and orientation conventions — keep the existing prose treatment if desired). Designer should default to **route (a)** unless the prose readability suffers; **route (b)** is acceptable if the designer judges that showing the forward composition adds pedagogical value. **Optional companion** (bundle if convenient per ticket scope, per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Mandatory detail level*): expand *Argument 1: Laughlin's Charge Pumping* Step 1's single-line algebraic solution for $r_m^2(t)$ into the 3-line chain shown in `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` lines 3174–3180 ($r_m^2 = (1/(\pi B))(2\pi\hbar m/e - \Phi(t)) = (1/(\pi B))(hm/e - \Phi_0 t/T) = (\Phi_0/(\pi B))(m - t/T)$ using $h/e = \Phi_0$ and $\Phi(t) = \Phi_0 t/T$). Borderline rather than required — a 2-substitution algebraic simplification can defensibly be a one-line jump — so designer's call. **Out of scope** per `rules/maintenance-scope.md` and `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Scope and pedagogical fit*: the *Kubo formula* `important` box (its derivation lives in §5.2.3 per explicit `{eq}` ref); the Argument 1 Step 1 single-valuedness condition (heuristic / semi-classical, consistent with body framing); the `$\mathrm{i}^2 = -1$ absorbs into the carrier-sign convention` sentence in Step 5 (separate sign-discipline issue — would require broader convention reconciliation across both arguments); Step 4's *"blocked pairs cancel pairwise"* argument (correct, just dense); Step 2's *"swap = c.c."* and "cross-terms vanish" (correct, minor compression); Steps 1, 2, 3 matrix elements and ladder identities (clean); cell 1 Prompts; cell 3 Homework; all other admonitions (3 `important` other than the Kubo box, `note`, `dropdown poll`, `seealso`); Summary; See Also. **Designer**: `phys130b-derivation-designer` produces concrete MyST replacement (route a or route b, with optional companion fix to Argument 1 Step 1). Load `rules/derivation-quality.md`, `rules/physics-conventions.md` (Ch 4 SI EM units — keep $\hbar$, $q$, $B$ explicit; no Gaussian markers; per § Phase Symbols, keep `\Phi`, `\Phi_0`, `N_\Phi` uppercase), `rules/myst-references.md` § Display Math, `rules/notebook-architecture.md`, and `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` § *Linear Response Theory* (lines 3742–3819) for `_refs/` structural diff. Verify the proposed replacement: (a) Step 5 first equation reads $\frac{(q/m)^2 q\hbar B}{(\hbar\omega_c)^2} = \frac{q}{\hbar B}$; (b) downstream prose, if touched, composes cleanly to $|\sigma_{xy}| = (q^2/h)(N_{\mathrm{occ}}/N_\Phi)$ without dimensional mismatch; (c) stays in SI throughout; (d) preserves the existing $\mathrm{i}^2 = -1$ sign treatment (out of scope). **Implementation**: `phys130b-notebook-writer` (Bash + Python `json.load`/`json.dump(indent=1, ensure_ascii=False)` per `rules/notebook-editing.md`; raw-string `r'...'` for LaTeX-bearing replacements per `notebook-editing.md` § *LaTeX in JSON* — the target contains `\h`, `\f`, `\o`, `\m` near `\hbar`, `\frac`, `\omega_c`, `\mathrm`, so raw-string is required to avoid JSON control-escape corruption; single atomic `text.replace` on the Step 5 `$$\begin{split}...\end{split}$$` block (route a) or on a slightly larger surrounding region (route b); verify `text.count(old) == 1` before replacement; post-save run both `python3 .claude/skills/notebook-writer/scripts/safe_edit.py validate notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-3-3-quantum-hall-effect.ipynb` and `python3 .claude/scripts/validate_project.py --scope 4-3-3-quantum-hall-effect` per `rules/validation.md`).

Original ticket text retained for archival reference: **T-2026-05-12-LD-03** — Phase C (Style), `notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-3-3-quantum-hall-effect.ipynb`, cell 2 `:::{admonition} Poll: Hall resistivity quantization :class: dropdown poll`: fix poll options (A) and (D) per `rules/content-style.md` § Poll Design ("**One definite correct answer.** Every poll has exactly one correct choice. The question must not be open-ended or ambiguous." / "**All distractors plausible.** Every wrong choice should be wrong for a specific, instructive reason — a particular misconception, reasoning error, or conceptual trap."). **Current options** (intended answer is (C)): (A) *"All electrons contribute equally to transport"*; (B) [a real misconception, intentionally kept]; (C) [the correct answer about extended vs localized states]; (D) *"Both (A) and (C)"*. **Issue — two coupled problems**: (a) Option (A) is factually wrong and the lecture's § *Robustness from Disorder* explicitly refutes it (only extended states at level centers carry current; localized states do not contribute to transport, which is *why* plateaus are robust). Having a factually-wrong option is acceptable per the rule ("Every wrong choice should be wrong for a specific, instructive reason"), but the rule also requires that the misconception each distractor encodes be *specific* and *instructive* — "all electrons contribute equally" is too vague to map to a single named misconception. (b) Option (D) *"Both (A) and (C)"* creates a second locally-consistent answer for a student who has the right intuition for (C) but doesn't catch (A) as wrong — violating the *"One definite correct answer"* clause. **Designer routes** (designer picks one): (a) **drop option (D) entirely and re-letter to A/B/C** — leaves three plausible distractors, restores single-correct-answer discipline, minimal edit; **also tighten** option (A) wording to encode a specific named misconception (e.g., "All electrons participate equally regardless of where they sit in the Landau-level density of states" — names the density-of-states confusion explicitly); (b) **replace (D) with a new distractor tied to a specific misconception**, e.g. "The plateaus arise because the magnetic field $B$ is itself quantized in superconducting samples used for QHE metrology" (plausible-sounding error that pinpoints a particular confusion about the role of the magnet vs the 2DEG) — preserves the 4-option structure but eliminates the double-consistent-answer issue; also consider tightening (A) per the same rationale as route (a). Designer should prefer route (a) for minimal blast radius; route (b) is acceptable if a strong named-misconception distractor is available. **Out of scope**: the question stem itself (well-framed, no edit needed); option (B) (intentionally encodes a separate named misconception, no edit needed); the answer reveal (`(C)` stays the correct answer in both routes; if route (a) is chosen, the lettering shifts so the answer becomes the new `(C)` after re-lettering). **Designer**: `phys130b-lecture-designer` (poll content tied directly to lecture material; load `rules/content-style.md` § Poll Design and the lecture's § *Robustness from Disorder*) → `phys130b-notebook-writer` per `.claude/README.md` § 2.3. **Implementation**: Bash + Python `json.load` / `json.dump(indent=1, ensure_ascii=False)`; one or two atomic `text.replace` calls (option-line edits + answer-block re-letter if route (a)), each wrapped in `assert text.count(old) == 1` guard; raw-string `r'...'` per `notebook-editing.md` § *LaTeX in JSON* if the new option text contains backslashes; preserve the `:::{admonition} ... :::` fence and `:class: dropdown poll` directive verbatim; post-save run validators per `rules/validation.md`.

(β) § *The Quantized Hall Conductance* (cell-2 char ≈ 2267): `$$\sigma_{xy} = \nu\,\frac{e^2}{h}, \qquad \rho_{xy} = \frac{1}{\sigma_{xy}} = \frac{h}{\nu e^2}.$$` — two genuinely independent relations side-by-side; stack as `\begin{split} \sigma_{xy} &= \nu\,\frac{e^2}{h}, \\ \rho_{xy} &= \frac{1}{\sigma_{xy}} = \frac{h}{\nu e^2}. \end{split}`. (δ) *Derivation: Kubo evaluation* Step 1 (cell-2 char ≈ 8381): `$$\hat{\pi}_x = \sqrt{q\hbar B/2}\,(\hat{a}^\dagger + \hat{a}), \qquad \hat{\pi}_y = \mathrm{i}\sqrt{q\hbar B/2}\,(\hat{a}^\dagger - \hat{a}).$$` — kinetic-momentum ladder form, two definitions; stack as `\begin{split} \hat{\pi}_x &= \sqrt{q\hbar B/2}\,(\hat{a}^\dagger + \hat{a}), \\ \hat{\pi}_y &= \mathrm{i}\sqrt{q\hbar B/2}\,(\hat{a}^\dagger - \hat{a}). \end{split}`. - ~~**T-2026-05-12-DV-05**~~ — DONE 2026-05-12: see Session Log entry for `4-3-2-landau-quantization` (sign bug + 4-term expansions in *Derivation: Commutators*). Original ticket text retained for archival reference: (Phase D, derivation quality, `notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-3-2-landau-quantization.ipynb` cell 2, `:::{admonition} Derivation: Commutators :class: dropdown information` block): **wrong algebra path** in the *Guiding center* sub-block — the displayed expansion of $[\hat{X},\hat{Y}]$ writes the second nonzero term with the wrong sign and then papers over the error with an ad-hoc `\cdot (-1)` factor on a downstream term. The numerical answer $-\mathrm{i}\ell_B^2$ comes out right by accident, but the algebra path is broken. Plus two depth gaps (kinetic-momentum 4-term expansion not shown; guiding-center 4-term bilinear expansion not shown), per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Root causes* #3 (*Wrong notation*) and #5 (*Reverse-engineering from the answer*); § *Anti-patterns* (paper-over via spurious sign factors); § *Mandatory detail level* (≥4–7 lines per commutator evaluation; cf. `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` lines 2101–2109 and 2206–2220). **Issue 1 — Sign bug in *Guiding center* expansion (required fix).** Current displayed equation (verbatim): `[\hat{X},\hat{Y}] = -\frac{[\hat{x},\hat{\pi}_x]}{qB} - \frac{[\hat{\pi}_y,\hat{y}]}{qB} - \frac{[\hat{\pi}_y,\hat{\pi}_x]}{(qB)^2} = -\frac{\mathrm{i}\hbar}{qB} - \frac{-\mathrm{i}\hbar}{qB} - \frac{-\mathrm{i}q\hbar B}{(qB)^2} \cdot (-1)`. The correct bilinear expansion of $[\hat{x}+\hat{\pi}_y/(qB),\,\hat{y}-\hat{\pi}_x/(qB)]$ is **$0 - [\hat{x},\hat{\pi}_x]/(qB) + [\hat{\pi}_y,\hat{y}]/(qB) - [\hat{\pi}_y,\hat{\pi}_x]/(qB)^2$** (signs $-/+/-$ on the three nonzero terms). Substituting $[\hat{x},\hat{\pi}_x]=\mathrm{i}\hbar$, $[\hat{\pi}_y,\hat{y}]=-\mathrm{i}\hbar$, $[\hat{\pi}_y,\hat{\pi}_x]=-\mathrm{i}q\hbar B$ into the correct expansion gives $-\mathrm{i}\hbar/(qB)+(-\mathrm{i}\hbar)/(qB)-(-\mathrm{i}q\hbar B)/(qB)^2 = -\mathrm{i}\hbar/(qB)-\mathrm{i}\hbar/(qB)+\mathrm{i}\hbar/(qB) = -\mathrm{i}\hbar/(qB) = -\mathrm{i}\ell_B^2$. The notebook's `\cdot (-1)` factor is unmotivated — it exists only to compensate for the upstream sign error. **Suggested replacement (designer to refine, must satisfy `rules/derivation-quality.md`):** a `\begin{split}` block showing the full 4-term bilinear expansion with consistent signs and direct substitution, e.g. `[\hat{X},\hat{Y}] = [\hat{x},\hat{y}] - [\hat{x},\hat{\pi}_x]/(qB) + [\hat{\pi}_y,\hat{y}]/(qB) - [\hat{\pi}_y,\hat{\pi}_x]/(qB)^2 = 0 - \mathrm{i}\hbar/(qB) + (-\mathrm{i}\hbar)/(qB) - (-\mathrm{i}q\hbar B)/(qB)^2 = -\mathrm{i}\hbar/(qB) - \mathrm{i}\hbar/(qB) + \mathrm{i}\hbar/(qB) = -\mathrm{i}\hbar/(qB) = -\mathrm{i}\ell_B^2`. Cite `(eq-magnetic-length)` inline for the final equality. **Issue 2 — Depth gap in *Kinetic momenta* sub-block (required fix, bundled).** Current 4-line chain skips the explicit 4-term expansion of the commutator. `_refs/` lines 2101–2109 shows 5 lines including the layout. **Suggested replacement (designer to refine):** stack 5–6 lines via `\begin{split}` showing (i) the definition $[\hat{p}_x-qA_x,\,\hat{p}_y-qA_y]$, (ii) the 4-term bilinear expansion $[\hat{p}_x,\hat{p}_y]-q[\hat{p}_x,A_y]-q[A_x,\hat{p}_y]+q^2[A_x,A_y]$, (iii) drop $[\hat{p}_x,\hat{p}_y]=0$ and $[A_x,A_y]=0$ → $-q[\hat{p}_x,A_y]-q[A_x,\hat{p}_y]$, (iv) substitute the building-block $[\hat{p}_i,f]=-\mathrm{i}\hbar\partial_i f$ (note $[A_x,\hat{p}_y]=-[\hat{p}_y,A_x]=+\mathrm{i}\hbar\partial_y A_x$) → $\mathrm{i}q\hbar\partial_x A_y - \mathrm{i}q\hbar\partial_y A_x$, (v) factor → $\mathrm{i}q\hbar(\partial_x A_y - \partial_y A_x)$, (vi) substitute $\boldsymbol{B}=\nabla\times\boldsymbol{A}$ (z-component) → $\mathrm{i}q\hbar B$. **Issue 3 — Optional companion: expand *Cross-commutators* sub-block (designer judgement).** Current one-line statement plus "similarly for the other three combinations" is borderline scope-discipline. `_refs/` lines 2229–2274 shows all four cases. **Designer chooses** between (i) keeping the one-line statement (lecture-level compression is acceptable when the pattern is transparent — current behavior; permissible per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Scope and pedagogical fit* if Issues 1+2 are repaired); or (ii) expanding to all four cases for structural parity with `_refs/`. Default to route (i) unless the designer judges the extra pedagogy warrants the line cost. **Out of scope (Phase D scope discipline, per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Scope and pedagogical fit* and `rules/maintenance-scope.md`):** stay strictly inside the *Derivation: Commutators* dropdown. Do **not** add new derivation dropdowns for (a) $\hat{H}=\hbar\omega_c(\hat{a}^\dagger\hat{a}+1/2)$ (the *Key result* `important` box is stated without proof — adding the `_refs/` lines 2299–2334 derivation would be scope expansion; separate future ticket if warranted), (b) $[\hat{a},\hat{a}^\dagger]=1$, $[\hat{b},\hat{b}^\dagger]=1$ (the *Cyclotron and guiding-center ladders* `important` box states both without proof — `_refs/` lines 2405–2431 derives both; same scope-expansion judgment), (c) the inline body identity $\hat{R}^2=\hat{X}^2+\hat{Y}^2=\ell_B^2(2\hat{b}^\dagger\hat{b}+1)$ in § *Macroscopic Degeneracy* (body prose, not in a dropdown; ~3-line manipulation $(\hat{X}+\mathrm{i}\hat{Y})(\hat{X}-\mathrm{i}\hat{Y})=\hat{X}^2+\hat{Y}^2-\mathrm{i}[\hat{X},\hat{Y}]$ not shown — adding a dropdown here is also scope expansion). Do **not** touch: cell 1 Prompts, cell 3 Homework, the `important` boxes (*Canonical commutators in Landau problem*, *Magnetic length*, *Cyclotron and guiding-center ladders*, *Key result: Hamiltonian uses only cyclotron ladder*, *Landau-level degeneracy*), the *Discussion*/*Poll* admonitions, the *Eigenstates* section, the *Macroscopic Degeneracy* section, the Summary, or the See Also. **Designer**: `phys130b-derivation-designer` produces concrete MyST replacement for the *Derivation: Commutators* block (Issues 1 + 2 required; Issue 3 optional per designer judgement). Load `rules/derivation-quality.md`, `rules/physics-conventions.md` (Ch 4 SI EM units — keep $\hbar$, $q$, $B$ explicit; no Gaussian markers), `rules/myst-references.md` § Display Math (single $\begin{split}$ blocks per `physics-conventions.md`), `rules/notebook-architecture.md`, and `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` lines 2098–2274 for `_refs/` structural diff. Verify the proposed replacement: (a) shows correct algebra paths with no `\cdot (-1)` paper-overs; (b) matches `_refs/` line counts (≥4–7 lines per commutator evaluation per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Mandatory detail level*); (c) stays in SI units throughout; (d) uses the lecture's $\ell_B$ idiom for the final equality (cite `(eq-magnetic-length)`). **Implementation**: `phys130b-notebook-writer` (Bash + Python `json.load`/`json.dump(indent=1, ensure_ascii=False)` per `rules/notebook-editing.md`; raw-string `r'...'` for LaTeX-bearing replacements per `notebook-editing.md` § *LaTeX in JSON*; single atomic `text.replace` on the *Derivation: Commutators* dropdown's contents; verify `text.count(old) == 1` before replacement; post-save run both `python3 .claude/skills/notebook-writer/scripts/safe_edit.py validate notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-3-2-landau-quantization.ipynb` and `python3 .claude/scripts/validate_project.py --scope 4-3-2-landau-quantization` per `rules/validation.md`).


- ~~**T-2026-05-11-HD-02**~~ — DONE 2026-05-11: see Session Log entry for `4-3-2-landau-quantization` (Phase C, single-sub-part rewrite — HW Problem 2(a) *Cyclotron resonance* broadened from cyclotron-only ladders to both cyclotron and guiding-center ladders per designer-preferred route (i); cell-3 length 4405→4522 chars, delta +117; validators clean). Original ticket text: (Phase C, style & structure, `notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-3-2-landau-quantization.ipynb` cell 3 (Homework), Problem 2 (a) *Cyclotron resonance*): rephrase HW2(a) to align the operator-expansion request with the actual lecture-body decomposition. **Issue.** HW2 sets up the dipole coupling $\hat{V}(t) = -e\boldsymbol{E}(t)\cdot\hat{\boldsymbol{r}}$ and asks (a) "Express $\hat{x}$ and $\hat{y}$ in terms of the cyclotron ladder operators $\hat{a}, \hat{a}^\dagger$." But the lecture body explicitly defines $\hat{X} = \hat{x} + \hat{\pi}_y/(qB)$ and $\hat{Y} = \hat{y} - \hat{\pi}_x/(qB)$, so $\hat{x} = \hat{X} - \hat{\pi}_y/(qB)$ — which has a guiding-center piece $\hat{X}$ (expressed in $\hat{b}, \hat{b}^\dagger$ via $\hat{X} = (\ell_B/\sqrt{2})(\hat{b} + \hat{b}^\dagger)$) **and** a cyclotron piece $\hat{\pi}_y$ (expressed in $\hat{a}, \hat{a}^\dagger$ via $\hat{\pi}_y = -\mathrm{i}(\hbar/\sqrt{2}\ell_B)(\hat{a} - \hat{a}^\dagger)$). Concretely, $\hat{x} = (\ell_B/\sqrt{2})[(\hat{b} + \hat{b}^\dagger) + \mathrm{i}(\hat{a} - \hat{a}^\dagger)]$ (and similarly for $\hat{y}$). The current HW phrasing implies a wrong simplification — $\hat{x}, \hat{y}$ are **not** expressible in $\hat{a}, \hat{a}^\dagger$ alone. The downstream claim "show that $\hat{V}(t)$ couples Landau level $n$ only to $n\pm 1$" is correct as a final result — the $\hat{b}, \hat{b}^\dagger$ pieces commute with $\hat{a}^\dagger\hat{a}$ and only change the guiding-center index $m$, leaving $n \to n\pm 1$ as the only $n$-coupling channel — but the pedagogical step that gets the student there requires both ladder sets. **Two acceptable routes (designer chooses one).** (i) **Broaden the operator request**: "Express $\hat{x}$ and $\hat{y}$ in terms of the cyclotron **and** guiding-center ladder operators $\hat{a}, \hat{a}^\dagger, \hat{b}, \hat{b}^\dagger$. Show that the $\hat{b}, \hat{b}^\dagger$ pieces leave the Landau-level index $n$ unchanged, so $\hat{V}(t)$ couples $n$ only to $n \pm 1$." (ii) **Narrow the kinematic claim**: "Show that, expanded in the cyclotron ladders $\hat{a}, \hat{a}^\dagger$, the $n$-dependence of $\hat{V}(t)$ matrix elements couples Landau level $n$ only to $n \pm 1$ irrespective of the guiding-center quantum number $m$." Route (i) is preferred because it gives the student a complete expression for $\hat{x}, \hat{y}$ — useful for (b) which asks for the matrix element $|\langle n+1\vert\hat{V}\vert n\rangle|^2$ — and matches the lecture body's two-ladder structure. **Out of scope**: HW2(b) and HW2(c) wording (the resonance matrix-element scaling and the cyclotron-resonance experimental tie-in are independent), problems HW1/3/4/5/6, the cell 1 Prompts, and the cell 2 lecture body. **Designer**: `phys130b-homework-designer` (homework problem-wording fix; load `rules/content-style.md` § Homework Design and `skills/homework-designer/TEMPLATES.md`). **Implementation**: `phys130b-notebook-writer` (Bash + Python `json.load`/`json.dump(indent=1, ensure_ascii=False)` per `rules/notebook-editing.md`; raw-string `r'...'` for LaTeX-bearing replacements per `notebook-editing.md` § *LaTeX in JSON*; post-save run both `python3 .claude/skills/notebook-writer/scripts/safe_edit.py validate notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-3-2-landau-quantization.ipynb` and `python3 .claude/scripts/validate_project.py --scope 4-3-2-landau-quantization` per `rules/validation.md`).

- ~~**T-2026-05-11-HD-03**~~ — DONE 2026-05-12: see Session Log entry for `4-3-2-landau-quantization` (Phase C, two atomic `text.replace` operations on cell 3 — HW2 preamble switched from `-e\boldsymbol{E}` electron-specific form to generic `q\boldsymbol{E}` (with "Landau-quantized electron" → "Landau-quantized particle of charge $q$"), and HW6 preamble gained a one-sentence specialization clause "Specialize to electron carriers ($q = -e$ with $e > 0$) throughout this problem." so the $4e^2/h$ Hall-conductance formula in HW6(b) reads consistently with the surrounding generic-$q$ convention; designer-recommended route (i) chosen per precedent set by T-2026-05-11-HD-01 on 4-3-1; cell-3 length 4522 → 4616 chars (delta +94); validators clean — `safe_edit.py validate` *OK: 1 notebook(s) validated, no issues found* and `validate_project.py --scope 4-3-2-landau-quantization` *All validations passed*. Original ticket text: (Phase C, style & structure, `notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-3-2-landau-quantization.ipynb` cell 3 (Homework), Problem 2 *Cyclotron resonance* preamble + Problem 6 (b) *Landau levels in graphene*): align HW2's charge convention with the body lecture's generic-$q$ convention, and resolve HW6's internal $q$ vs $e$ mix. Direct parallel to **T-2026-05-11-HD-01** on `4-3-1-cyclotron-motion` HW3 (which switched to generic $q$). **Issue (HW2).** HW2 preamble silently specializes to electron carriers via $-e$: "$\hat{V}(t) = -e\boldsymbol{E}(t)\cdot\hat{\boldsymbol{r}}$". The body lecture, by contrast, uses generic charge $q$ throughout — symmetric-gauge vector potential `\boldsymbol{A} = (B/2)(-y, x, 0)` with the stated *(with $B > 0$, $q > 0$)*, kinetic-momentum components $\hat{\pi}_x = \hat{p}_x + (qB/2)\hat{y}$, magnetic length $\ell_B = \sqrt{\hbar/(qB)}$, cyclotron frequency $\omega_c = qB/m$. The HW switch from $q$ to $-e$ is the same silent body→HW notation drift that motivated **T-2026-05-11-HD-01** on `4-3-1` (resolved by switching HW3 to generic $q$). **Issue (HW6, mild).** HW6's preamble Landau-level formula uses generic $q$ ("$E_n = \mathrm{sgn}(n)\,v_F\sqrt{2q\hbar\vert n\vert B}$"), but HW6(b) writes the universal Hall conductance quantum as "$\sigma_{xy} = (n + 1/2)\cdot 4e^2/h$" — internally inconsistent, though $4e^2/h$ is the conventional way to write the von Klitzing constant. **Two acceptable routes (designer chooses one).** (i) **Use generic $q$ in HW2; specialize HW6 explicitly**: rewrite the HW2 preamble as "$\hat{V}(t) = q\boldsymbol{E}(t)\cdot\hat{\boldsymbol{r}}$" and rephrase any later reference to electron charge accordingly (HW2(b)/(c) carry no further $-e$ usage). For HW6: add a one-sentence preamble specializing to electron carriers ("Specialize to electron carriers, $q = -e$, with $e > 0$.") so the $4e^2/h$ formula reads consistently. Preferred — matches the precedent set by **T-2026-05-11-HD-01** on `4-3-1` (which chose generic $q$). (ii) **Specialize HW2 explicitly; leave HW6 alone**: add a one-sentence preamble to HW2 ("Specialize to electron carriers (charge $-e$, $e > 0$).") and keep $-e$ throughout the sub-parts. HW6 can then stay as-is (already in the same electron-specialized convention). Slightly more local but creates inconsistent conventions across the homework set. **Out of scope**: HW2(a) ladder expansion wording (T-2026-05-11-HD-02), HW1/3/4/5 problem text, HW6 part (a)/(c) wording, the cell 1 Prompts, and the cell 2 lecture body. **Designer**: `phys130b-homework-designer` (homework problem-wording / convention alignment; load `rules/content-style.md` § Homework Design and `skills/homework-designer/TEMPLATES.md`). **Implementation**: `phys130b-notebook-writer` (Bash + Python `json.load`/`json.dump(indent=1, ensure_ascii=False)` per `rules/notebook-editing.md`; post-save run both `safe_edit.py validate notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-3-2-landau-quantization.ipynb` and `validate_project.py --scope 4-3-2-landau-quantization` per `rules/validation.md`).

- **T-2026-05-11-HD-04 (original ticket text, retained):** (Phase C, style & structure, `notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-3-2-landau-quantization.ipynb` cell 3 (Homework), Problem 1 (a) *Lowest Landau level wavefunctions*): align HW1(a)'s cyclotron-ladder definition with the lecture's $\ell_B$-based form. **Issue.** HW1(a) writes $\hat{a} = (\hat{\pi}_x + \mathrm{i}\hat{\pi}_y)/\sqrt{2q\hbar B}$ — re-deriving the ladder normalization in raw EM constants. The body lecture's *Cyclotron and guiding-center ladders* `important` box defines $\hat{a} = (\ell_B/(\sqrt{2}\,\hbar))(\hat{\pi}_x + \mathrm{i}\hat{\pi}_y)$. These are **mathematically equivalent** (since $\ell_B^2 = \hbar/(qB)$ gives $\ell_B/(\sqrt{2}\,\hbar) = 1/\sqrt{2q\hbar B}$), but the HW form bypasses the lecture's $\ell_B$ abbreviation — defeating the pedagogical purpose of having just defined $\ell_B$ in the *Magnetic length* `important` box `(eq-magnetic-length)`. Per `rules/maintenance-scope.md` § *Prefer* ("tighter prose; merge redundant explanations") and `rules/content-style.md` § Homework Design ("Problems appear on the final exam — they must test understanding, not procedure"), the HW should reuse the lecture's symbol rather than re-introduce raw constants. **Two acceptable routes (designer chooses one).** (i) **Verbatim ladder restatement**: rewrite the HW1(a) ladder definition as "$\hat{a} = (\ell_B/(\sqrt{2}\,\hbar))(\hat{\pi}_x + \mathrm{i}\hat{\pi}_y)$" (matching the body verbatim). Preferred — preserves the explicit operator action the verification step needs while staying in the lecture's $\ell_B$ idiom. (ii) **Reference-only**: rewrite as "Verify by direct computation that $\hat{a}\,\psi_m = 0$, where $\hat{a}$ is the cyclotron lowering operator defined in the lecture." Briefer, but requires the student to fetch the definition. **Out of scope**: HW1(b) ($\langle\hat{r}^2\rangle$ computation) and HW1(c) ($\hat{L}_z$ eigenvalue check) wording — these sub-parts already use $\ell_B$ consistently with the lecture and need no change; problems HW2/3/4/5/6, the cell 1 Prompts, and the cell 2 lecture body. **Designer**: `phys130b-homework-designer` (homework notation alignment; load `rules/content-style.md` § Homework Design and `skills/homework-designer/TEMPLATES.md`). **Implementation**: `phys130b-notebook-writer` (Bash + Python `json.load`/`json.dump(indent=1, ensure_ascii=False)` per `rules/notebook-editing.md`; raw-string `r'...'` for LaTeX-bearing replacements per `notebook-editing.md` § *LaTeX in JSON*; post-save run both `safe_edit.py validate notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-3-2-landau-quantization.ipynb` and `validate_project.py --scope 4-3-2-landau-quantization` per `rules/validation.md`).

- ~~**T-2026-05-11-DV-04**~~ — DONE 2026-05-11: see Session Log entry for `4-3-1-cyclotron-motion` (Phase D, derivation quality — required (a) Hall derivation sign fix corrected the cross-product in the *Derivation: from force balance to $\rho_{xy}^{\mathrm{cl}}(B)$* dropdown ($qE_y + q v_x B = 0 \Rightarrow E_y = -v_xB$ replaced by $qE_y + q(\boldsymbol{v}\times\boldsymbol{B})_y = qE_y - qv_xB = 0 \Rightarrow E_y = v_xB$, dropped `|E_y|` paper-over so closing line is $\rho_{xy}^{\mathrm{cl}} \equiv E_y/j_x = B/(nq)$ — matches body's `important` box verbatim); optional (b) cyclotron-derivation companion bundled — split prior $v_x$+$v_y$ `\begin{split}` block into $v_x = v_\perp\cos$ as SHO general solution and $v_y = \dot{v}_x/\omega_c = -v_\perp\sin$ as *forced* by the first-order relation, matching `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` lines 1675–1684. Cell-2 length 9262 → 9604 chars (delta +342); validators clean (`safe_edit.py validate` *OK* and `validate_project.py --scope 4-3-1-cyclotron-motion` *All validations passed*). Original ticket text: `notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-3-1-cyclotron-motion.ipynb` cell 2 § *Classical Hall effect and its expectation*, `:::{admonition} Derivation: from force balance to $\rho_{xy}^{\mathrm{cl}}(B)$` `:class: dropdown information` block. **Phase D (Derivation quality)**, **sign bug** in the Lorentz cross-product term plus sign-convention cleanup, with an optional cyclotron-derivation companion. Per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Root causes of quality gaps* #3 (*Wrong notation*) and #5 (*Reverse-engineering from the answer*) and § *Anti-patterns* (paper-over via `|·|` to absorb a sign without motivation). **Current text** (cell 2, *Derivation: from force balance...* dropdown opening `\begin{split}` block, verbatim): `qE_y + q v_x B = 0` ⇒ `E_y = -v_x B`. Later in the same dropdown: `\rho_{xy}^{\mathrm{cl}} \equiv |E_y|/j_x = B/(nq)`. **Issue.** With $\boldsymbol{v} = v_x\hat{x}$ and $\boldsymbol{B} = B\hat{z}$, the cross product is $(\boldsymbol{v}\times\boldsymbol{B})_y = v_xB(\hat{x}\times\hat{z})_y = -v_xB$ (since $\hat{x}\times\hat{z} = -\hat{y}$). The $y$-component of the Lorentz force is therefore $q(\boldsymbol{v}\times\boldsymbol{B})_y = -qv_xB$ (not $+qv_xB$), and the steady-state $y$-force balance is $qE_y + (-qv_xB) = 0 \Rightarrow E_y = +v_xB$. The notebook gets the sign wrong. Crucially, **the same notebook's *Derivation: Cyclotron Frequency and Radius*** (component EOM in the dropdown above, $m\dot{v}_y = -qBv_x$) **has the cross-product correctly**, so the Hall derivation is internally inconsistent with its own cyclotron derivation. The error then gets masked by writing $\rho_{xy}^{\mathrm{cl}} \equiv |E_y|/j_x = B/(nq)$ at the end, but the absolute value mixed with signed $B/(nq)$ is only consistent for $q > 0$ — for electrons $q = -e < 0$, $|E_y|/j_x \geq 0$ while $B/(nq) < 0$, contradiction. The body's *Classical Hall response* `important` box ($\rho_{xy}^{\mathrm{cl}}(B) = B/(nq)$) is **signed**, not absolute-valued, so the derivation should match. **Fix — route (a), preferred.** Correct the sign in the displayed force-balance equation, then drop the `|E_y|` and write $\rho_{xy}^{\mathrm{cl}} = E_y/j_x = B/(nq)$ (signed). Concretely: replace the current `\begin{split}\nqE_y + q v_x B &= 0\\\n\Rightarrow\quad E_y &= -v_x B.\n\end{split}` with `\begin{split}\nqE_y + q(\boldsymbol{v}\times\boldsymbol{B})_y &= qE_y - qv_xB = 0\\\n\Rightarrow E_y &= v_xB.\n\end{split}` Then the elimination of $v_x$ via $j_x = nqv_x$ gives $E_y = (B/(nq))j_x$ directly, and the closing line becomes $\rho_{xy}^{\mathrm{cl}} \equiv E_y/j_x = B/(nq)$ (matches the body's `important` box verbatim, no absolute value). The follow-up sentence *“The sign of $E_y/j_x$ flips with the sign of $q$, so Hall measurements diagnose carrier type”* remains correct (for $q > 0$: $E_y/j_x > 0$; for electrons $q = -e$: $E_y/j_x < 0$). **Fix — route (b), not preferred.** Keep the current $E_y = -v_xB$ sign by declaring a non-standard orientation for $E_y$ (e.g. measured opposite to the Hall accumulation direction) and absorb the sign via $\rho_{xy} \equiv -E_y/j_x = B/(nq)$. This fights the body's `important` box convention and requires an extra explanatory sentence; route (a) is cleaner. **Optional companion** (bundle if convenient; do **not** file as a separate ticket): in the *Derivation: Cyclotron Frequency and Radius* dropdown immediately above, the general SHO solution currently quotes both $v_x(t) = v_\perp\cos(\omega_c t + \varphi_0)$ and $v_y(t) = -v_\perp\sin(\omega_c t + \varphi_0)$ together as the “general solution.” `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` (lines 1675–1684) instead derives $v_y$ from $v_x$ via the first-order relation $\dot{y} = (m/qB)\,\mathrm{d}\dot{x}/\mathrm{d}t = (1/\omega_c)\,\mathrm{d}\dot{x}/\mathrm{d}t = -v_\perp\sin(\omega_c t + \varphi_0)$. The pedagogical point is that $v_y$ is **forced** to be $-v_\perp\sin$ by the first-order relation — it is **not** an independent SHO mode that could also be $\cos(\omega_c t + \varphi_0')$ with a free phase. Recommend (if the designer judges it tightens the chain without scope expansion) inserting one line between the SHO equation and the integrated trajectory: $v_y(t) = (1/\omega_c)\dot{v}_x(t) = -v_\perp\sin(\omega_c t + \varphi_0)$. **Scope note** (per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Scope and pedagogical fit* and `rules/maintenance-scope.md`): stay strictly inside the existing two `:::{dropdown information}` derivation blocks. Do **not** rewrite the body's `important` boxes (clean per the audit — *Cyclotron frequency and radius*, *Classical Hall response*, and *Integer quantum Hall observation* all match their corresponding `_refs/` definitions and the body's narrative). Do **not** import Landau-level content, guiding-center-operator reconstruction $\boldsymbol{\xi} = \boldsymbol{x} - \hat{z}\times\boldsymbol{\pi}/(qB)$, kinetic-momentum operators, or any §4.3.2/§4.3.3 machinery — the cyclotron lesson must remain classical and pre-quantum. After the fix the Hall derivation grows by ~1 displayed line (the `qE_y - qv_xB = 0` intermediate step, plus the `\Rightarrow E_y = +v_xB`); the optional cyclotron companion adds ~1 line. Both consistent with `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Mandatory detail level* (depth changes are slicing existing steps finer, not adding topical scope). **Designer**: `phys130b-derivation-designer` produces concrete replacement MyST for the Hall block (and the optional cyclotron one-line insertion if accepted). **Implementation + post-edit validation**: `phys130b-notebook-writer` (Bash + Python `json.load`/`json.dump(indent=1, ensure_ascii=False)` per `rules/notebook-editing.md`; raw-string `r'...'` for LaTeX-bearing replacements per `notebook-editing.md` § *LaTeX in JSON*; post-save run both `python3 .claude/skills/notebook-writer/scripts/safe_edit.py validate notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-3-1-cyclotron-motion.ipynb` and `python3 .claude/scripts/validate_project.py --scope 4-3-1-cyclotron-motion` per `rules/validation.md`).

Preferred repair: a `\begin{split}` block with the implication arrow at the start of the second line (e.g. `qE_y + q v_x B = 0 \\ \Rightarrow\; E_y = -v_x B`); if `notebook-writer` judges that the implication-arrow spacing reads worse stacked than inline, leaving (f) as-is and noting the rationale in the session log is acceptable.
