- ~~**T-2026-05-11-DV-01**~~ — DONE 2026-05-11: see Session Log entry for `4-2-3-flux-ring` (Phase D, two sub-fixes applied to the *Symmetry Action on the Doublet* dropdown — Reflection limit-flip `\begin{split}` chain matching the Translation block, and a new motivation paragraph + `\begin{split}` block deriving $\hat{U}_{LG}$ from the standard §4.1.3 gauge transformation with $\alpha(\theta)=-\hbar\theta/q$ and flux shift $\Delta\Phi=-\Phi_0$). Original ticket text:  (Phase D, derivation quality, `notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-2-3-flux-ring.ipynb` cell 2 § *Half-Integer Flux: Anomalous Degeneracy*, `:::{admonition} Derivation: Symmetry Action on the Doublet` `:class: dropdown information` block at lines ~202–263). **Two scoped sub-fixes**, both per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Root causes* #1 (skipped intermediate steps) and #4 (missing motivation for "why this form"). **(a) Reflection limit-flip pedagogy.** The current Reflection step (line ~223) is a single inline equation: `\hat{R}|n\rangle = (1/\sqrt{2\pi})\int_0^{2\pi}\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}n\theta}|-\theta\rangle\mathrm{d}\theta = (1/\sqrt{2\pi})\int_0^{2\pi}\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}n\theta'}|\theta'\rangle\mathrm{d}\theta' = |-n\rangle`. The substitution $\theta' = -\theta$ silently flips the integration limits ($\int_0^{2\pi}\to\int_0^{-2\pi}$), and the $2\pi$-periodicity of $|\theta\rangle$ is what restores them to $[0,2\pi]$ — exactly the same pedagogical step the Translation block (lines ~210–216) makes explicit with the parenthetical *"(substituting $\theta'=\theta+\pi$ and using $2\pi$-periodicity to keep the integration limits)"*. The Reflection step deserves parallel treatment. **Proposed fix**: restructure the single line as a `\begin{split}` chain showing (i) substitution $\theta' = -\theta$, (ii) limit-flip and periodicity step that restores $\int_0^{2\pi}$, (iii) recognition as $|-n\rangle$ via the same `|n\rangle` definition used in the Translation step. Add a parenthetical clause matching the Translation block's wording. **(b) `\hat{U}_{LG}` gauge-function motivation gap.** The Large-gauge-transformation block (lines ~228–237) **defines** $\hat{U}_{LG}$ operationally as `\hat{U}_{LG}\psi(\theta) = \mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}\theta}\psi(\theta)` and *later* justifies calling it a *large* gauge transformation by noting the equivalent flux shift $\Phi\to\Phi-\Phi_0$. But the standard Ch 4 gauge transformation $\psi \to \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}q\alpha/\hbar}\psi$, $\boldsymbol{A}\to\boldsymbol{A}+\nabla\alpha$ (`rules/physics-conventions.md` § Chapter 4 EM Units; introduced in §4.1.3) is **never explicitly connected**: the gauge function $\alpha(\theta) = -\hbar\theta/q$ is never written, and $\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}q\alpha/\hbar} = \mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}\theta}$ is never recovered. This is `derivation-quality.md` anti-pattern *Missing motivation for "why this form"*. **Proposed fix**: insert one short prose-plus-split block (3–4 displayed lines) after the operational definition: identify $\alpha(\theta) = -\hbar\theta/q$ (multi-valued — winds by $-2\pi\hbar/q = -\Phi_0\,q/q$ per loop), recover the multiplier $\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}q\alpha/\hbar} = \mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}\theta}$, compute the flux shift $\Delta\Phi = \oint\nabla\alpha\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l} = -\Phi_0$, and cite `[4.1.3 Gauge Invariance](4-1-3-gauge-invariance)` for the underlying transformation rule. The word *large* then follows naturally — a continuous (small) $\alpha$ cannot wind, so this is precisely the gauge transformation small gauge cannot reach. **Scope note (per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Scope and pedagogical fit*)**: stay inside the existing *Derivation: Symmetry Action on the Doublet* dropdown; do **not** introduce covariant-derivative machinery or large-gauge Wu–Yang content from §4.4. **Designer**: `phys130b-derivation-designer` produces concrete replacement MyST; implementation + post-edit validation: `notebook-writer` per `rules/validation.md`.

- ~~**T-2026-05-11-DV-02**~~ — DONE 2026-05-11: see Session Log entry for `4-2-3-flux-ring` (Phase D, six sub-fixes applied to the *Josephson relations from coupled condensates* dropdown — tunneling-Hamiltonian motivation paragraph (Cooper-pair charge $2e$, symmetric $\pm eV$ shift, real-positive $K$ WLOG); explicit product+chain-rule `$$` block for $\mathrm{d}/\mathrm{d}t(\sqrt{n_L}\,\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}\theta_L})$; one-sentence real/imag justification noting reality of $n_{L,R}$, $\theta_{L,R}$; supercurrent $I_{\text{super}}=2e\,\dot{n}_L$ definition with explicit $I_c = 4eK\sqrt{n_L n_R}/\hbar$ replacing the prior $I_c \propto K\sqrt{n_L n_R}$ proportionality; bottom-row results displayed via $L\leftrightarrow R$, $V\to-V$, $\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}\delta}\to\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}\delta}$ symmetry with pair-conservation check $\dot{n}_R = -\dot{n}_L$; explicit separation of the *equal-density* ($n_L = n_R$) and *large-reservoir* ($\dot{n}_{L,R}\to 0$) assumptions as logically independent). Original ticket text: (Phase D, derivation quality, `notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-2-3-flux-ring.ipynb` cell 2 § *Application — The SQUID* / #### *Josephson relations*, `:::{admonition} Derivation: Josephson relations from coupled condensates` `:class: dropdown information` block at lines ~305–350). **Six scoped sub-fixes**, all per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Root causes* (#1 skipped steps, #4 missing motivation, #5 reverse-engineering) and § *Mandatory procedure* (steps 4 *show every evaluation*, 5 *state building-block results first*, 6 *explain where each term comes from*). The current chain compresses what should be ~14–18 displayed lines into 7 displayed equations and several "it follows that" prose jumps. **(a) Tunneling Hamiltonian motivation.** The $2\times 2$ matrix at line ~311 (`\mathrm{i}\hbar\,\mathrm{d}/\mathrm{d}t(\psi_L,\psi_R)^T = ((eV,K),(K,-eV))(\psi_L,\psi_R)^T`) is asserted from the one prose line *"Cooper-pair tunneling through the thin barrier couples them with amplitude $K$, while a voltage $V$ across the junction shifts the Cooper-pair energies by $\pm eV$"*. Three implicit choices are not flagged: (i) the Cooper-pair charge is $2e$, so the chemical-potential shift per Cooper pair across a voltage $V$ is $(2e)V$, split symmetrically as $\pm eV$ about the midpoint; (ii) the off-diagonal $K$ is taken **real** (any phase can be absorbed into the definition of $\theta_L, \theta_R$, so $K\in\mathbb{R}_{>0}$ WLOG); (iii) the sign convention for $V$ (which side is +, which is −) is implicit. **Proposed fix**: short setup paragraph (or `dropdown note` *Setting up the tunneling Hamiltonian* embedded inline) addressing all three. **(b) $\mathrm{d}/\mathrm{d}t(\sqrt{n_L}\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}\theta_L})$ product / chain rule.** The substitution at lines ~314–317 hides the chain rule: `\mathrm{i}\hbar\,\mathrm{d}/\mathrm{d}t(\sqrt{n_L}\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}\theta_L}) = \mathrm{i}\hbar[\dot{n}_L/(2\sqrt{n_L}) + \mathrm{i}\dot\theta_L\sqrt{n_L}]\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}\theta_L}` (product rule + $\mathrm{d}\sqrt{n_L}/\mathrm{d}t = \dot{n}_L/(2\sqrt{n_L})$). The current text jumps from "substitute and divide by $\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}\theta_L}$" straight to the post-cancellation expression. **Proposed fix**: insert one `\begin{split}` (2 lines) showing the product+chain rule explicitly before the division step — at minimum, add a parenthetical citation *(product rule, $\mathrm{d}\sqrt{n_L}/\mathrm{d}t = \dot{n}_L/(2\sqrt{n_L})$)*. **(c) Real/imag split justification.** The text at lines ~320–334 silently uses *"$n_L, n_R, \theta_{L,R}, \dot{n}_L, \dot\theta_L \in\mathbb{R}$"* to peel off real and imaginary parts of the equation. This physical assumption is invisible. **Proposed fix**: one prose sentence preceding the imaginary-part line stating *"Since $n_{L,R}$ and $\theta_{L,R}$ are real-valued physical densities and phases, the LHS imaginary part is $\hbar\dot{n}_L/(2\sqrt{n_L})$ and the real part is $-\hbar\dot\theta_L\sqrt{n_L}$; the RHS splits via $\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}\delta} = \cos\delta + \mathrm{i}\sin\delta$."* **(d) Supercurrent $\propto$ pair-transfer rate is never shown.** Line ~326 says *"The supercurrent is proportional to the rate of pair transfer, giving $I_{\text{super}} = I_c\sin\delta$ with $I_c \propto K\sqrt{n_L n_R}$."* The proportionality constant — Cooper-pair charge $2e$ times pair-transfer rate — is never displayed, and the sign convention (which way does positive current flow?) is omitted. Anti-pattern: *prefactor appears without a definition step*. **Proposed fix**: display the relation $I_{\text{super}} = -2e\dot{n}_L$ (with chosen sign convention stated), substitute the line above to get $I_c = 4eK\sqrt{n_L n_R}/\hbar$ explicitly (or, if the lecture prefers, retain $I_c \propto K\sqrt{n_L n_R}$ but add a single parenthetical absorbing $2e/\hbar$). **(e) "Same procedure on the bottom row".** Line ~336 reads *"The same procedure on the bottom row gives $\dot\theta_R = eV/\hbar - (K/\hbar)\sqrt{n_L/n_R}\cos\delta$."* This is the *"It can be shown that"* anti-pattern from `derivation-quality.md` § *Anti-patterns*. **Proposed fix**: replace with one sentence naming the discrete symmetry that yields the bottom row from the top row by inspection (e.g. *"swapping $L\leftrightarrow R$, $V\to-V$, and $\delta\to-\delta$ on the off-diagonal $\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}\delta}\to\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}\delta}$ maps the top-row equation onto the bottom row"*), **or** display the parallel bottom-row chain in two lines. The cancellation of $K\cos\delta$ then needs both equations visible. **(f) $n_L = n_R$ vs $\dot{n}_{L,R}\approx 0$ are independent assumptions.** Line ~342 reads *"using $n_L \approx n_R$ (bulk superconductors are large reservoirs, so the pair density is essentially unchanged by tunneling)"*. The parenthetical justifies $\dot{n}_{L,R}\to 0$ (small-rate limit), but the $K\cos\delta$ cancellation in $\dot\delta = \dot\theta_R - \dot\theta_L$ requires the **magnitudes** to satisfy $\sqrt{n_R/n_L} = \sqrt{n_L/n_R}$, i.e. $n_L = n_R$ — an *equal-superconductor* assumption that is logically independent of the *large-reservoir* assumption. **Proposed fix**: rewrite the sentence to make both assumptions explicit, e.g. *"For identical superconductors on the two sides ($n_L = n_R$), the $K\cos\delta$ terms in $\dot\theta_R - \dot\theta_L$ cancel exactly. The large-reservoir limit $\dot{n}_{L,R}\to 0$ is a separate consistency check showing the slow-tunneling regime preserves the equal-density starting point."* **Scope note**: stay inside the existing *Derivation: Josephson relations from coupled condensates* dropdown — no new sections, no fluxoid quantization (handled downstream in §*Two-junction interference*), no Cooper-pair wavefunction derivation from BCS (out of scope for §4.2.3 and Ch 4 generally). After the fix the derivation grows from ~7 to ~13–16 displayed lines — comfortably within `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Mandatory detail level* (4–7 lines per *single* commutator/identity, more for a multi-step physical derivation that integrates two equations of motion plus a current-density argument). **Designer**: `phys130b-derivation-designer` produces concrete replacement MyST; implementation + post-edit validation: `notebook-writer` per `rules/validation.md`.

- ~~**T-2026-05-11-DV-03**~~ — DONE 2026-05-11: see Session Log entry for `4-2-3-flux-ring` (Phase D, three scoped sub-fixes + the optional precondition reminder applied inside the existing *Derivation: RSJ time-averaged voltage* `:::{dropdown information}` block — (a) sign-convention sentence absorbing the sign of $\cos(\pi\Phi/\Phi_0)$ into the average phase $\delta$ so $I_{\max} = 2I_c|\cos(\pi\Phi/\Phi_0)| \geq 0$ WLOG; (b) single-$\dot\delta$ identification via time-independent fluxoid quantization $\delta_1 - \delta_2 = 2\pi\Phi/\Phi_0$ giving one ac-Josephson voltage drop; (c) rewrite of the elliptical $V/\dot\delta = V\cdot\hbar/(2eV) = \hbar/(2e)$ line into an explicit ac-Josephson citation; (d) running-regime precondition $I_{\text{bias}} > I_{\max} \geq 0$ added beside the Weierstrass integral identity). Original ticket text: (Phase D, derivation quality, `notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-2-3-flux-ring.ipynb` cell 2 § *Application — The SQUID* / #### *Measuring flux as voltage*, `:::{admonition} Derivation: RSJ time-averaged voltage` `:class: dropdown information` block at lines ~403–442). **Three scoped sub-fixes** plus one optional precondition reminder; chain is otherwise forward-motivated and uses a well-flagged building-block integral identity. **(a) Sign convention on $I_{\max}$ vs signed $\cos(\pi\Phi/\Phi_0)$.** The SQUID Supercurrent box (lines ~362–367) gives $I_{\text{super}} = 2I_c\cos(\pi\Phi/\Phi_0)\sin\delta$ (**signed** $\cos$), but the RSJ derivation at line ~406 writes $I_{\max} = 2I_c|\cos(\pi\Phi/\Phi_0)|$ and uses $I_{\text{bias}} = I_{\max}\sin\delta + V/R$ at line ~406. The two are equivalent only because the sign of $\cos$ can be absorbed into a phase redefinition $\delta\to\delta + \pi$ (or $\pi - \delta$). This convention switch is silent — a careful student will notice the discrepancy with the SQUID Supercurrent box and lose the thread. **Proposed fix**: one sentence at the top of the derivation (after the definition of $I_{\max}$) stating *"The sign of $\cos(\pi\Phi/\Phi_0)$ is absorbed into the average phase $\delta$ (a shift $\delta\to\delta+\pi$ flips the sign), so we may take $I_{\max} = 2I_c|\cos(\pi\Phi/\Phi_0)| \geq 0$ without loss of generality."* **(b) Single-$\dot\delta$ identification from fluxoid quantization.** The derivation slips from "two junctions in parallel" to a single $\dot\delta$ controlling the voltage across both. The bridge — that for fixed external $\Phi$ the constraint $\delta_1 - \delta_2 = 2\pi\Phi/\Phi_0$ (introduced at line ~358) is **time-independent**, so $\dot\delta_1 = \dot\delta_2$, and ac-Josephson $V = (\hbar/2e)\dot\delta_{1,2}$ gives one common voltage drop — is never stated. **Proposed fix**: one sentence at the top of the derivation (or in the preamble *Measuring flux as voltage* prose) noting *"At fixed external $\Phi$, $\delta_1 - \delta_2$ is time-independent, so $\dot\delta_1 = \dot\delta_2 = \dot\delta$ and a single ac-Josephson relation $V = (\hbar/2e)\dot\delta$ controls the voltage across the device."* **(c) `$V/\dot\delta = \hbar/(2e)$` is constant — cite ac-Josephson.** The cancellation at line ~424 (*"Since $V/\dot\delta = V\cdot\hbar/(2eV) = \hbar/(2e)$ is constant"*) is correct but elliptical — it uses ac-Josephson again. **Proposed fix**: add a single parenthetical *(again from ac-Josephson, $V = (\hbar/2e)\dot\delta$, so the ratio is constant — independent of $\delta$)*. **(d) Integral-identity precondition (optional).** Lines ~432–434 quote $\int_0^{2\pi}\mathrm{d}\delta/(a-b\sin\delta) = 2\pi/\sqrt{a^2-b^2}$ for $a > b > 0$. The condition $a > b > 0$ is exactly the *running regime* $I_{\text{bias}} > I_{\max}$ already stated at the top of the derivation (line ~406), but a one-sentence reminder *"valid because we are in the running regime $I_{\text{bias}} > I_{\max} \geq 0$"* would close the loop. **Optional** — do not file as a separate ticket; bundle into the same edit if convenient. **Scope note**: stay inside the existing *Derivation: RSJ time-averaged voltage* dropdown — no derivation of the Weierstrass-substitution integral identity itself (out of scope; correctly retained as a building-block citation per `derivation-quality.md` § *Minimum derivation depth* criterion (c)). After the fix the derivation grows from ~12 to ~14–15 displayed/prose lines — consistent with `derivation-quality.md` § *Mandatory detail level*. **Designer**: `phys130b-derivation-designer` produces concrete replacement MyST; implementation + post-edit validation: `notebook-writer` per `rules/validation.md`.

- ~~**T-2026-05-10-ST-01**~~ — DONE 2026-05-10: rewrote `### Summary` in cell 2 of `notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-2-3-flux-ring.ipynb` from 4 bullets to **6** bullets (within the rule's 5–7 typical range), each with a **bold lead-in + colon** per `skills/summary-designer/TEMPLATES.md` gold reference: (a) **Flux ring as a solvable AB system** (Hamiltonian + Peierls substitution + $\Phi/\Phi_0$ dimensionless flux); (b) **Spectrum and flux periodicity** ($E_n(\Phi)$ parabolic branches, period $\Phi_0$, half-integer crossings); (c) **Persistent current** (sawtooth $-\partial E_0/\partial\Phi$); (d) **Flux-modulated tunneling** ($\Gamma(\Phi) = \Gamma_0\cos^2(\pi\Phi/\Phi_0)$, half-integer destructive interference); (e) **Half-flux anomaly** (new bullet — names $\hat{T}_\pi$ as Pauli $\hat{Z}$, $\hat{S} = \hat{R}\hat{U}_{LG}$ as Pauli $\hat{X}$, projective phase $\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}\pi}=-1$ → $\{\hat{T}_\pi, \hat{S}\} = 0$ → 't Hooft anomaly → 2-dim irrep, the central conceptual insight previously absent); (f) **SQUID magnetometry** ($I_{\max}(\Phi) = 2I_c|\cos(\pi\Phi/\Phi_0)|$ + flux-tunable voltage). **Phantom claim fixed**: bullet 2 of the old Summary attributed the half-flux degeneracy to “time-reversal and translational symmetry”; time-reversal is not invoked anywhere in cell 2. The new bullet 5 correctly identifies the protecting algebra as $\hat{T}_\pi$ (translation-by-$\pi$) and $\hat{S}$ (reflection-with-large-gauge), both grounded in the *Anomaly* prose and the *Derivation: Symmetry Action on the Doublet* dropdown. Notation discipline: upright `\mathrm{i,e,d}`, $\hat{T}_\pi$ / $\hat{S} = \hat{R}\hat{U}_{LG}$, $\Phi_0 = h/q$; no `\quad`/`\qquad`; no display equations in Summary bullets (inline math only). See Also block left intact (still 4 bullets — handled separately by T-2026-05-10-ST-03). Filed as new ticket **T-2026-05-10-ST-04** below per the scheduled task's “new issues discovered during work become new tickets” discipline. Designer: `summary-designer` → `notebook-writer`. Original ticket text: (Phase C, style & structure, `notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-2-3-flux-ring.ipynb` cell 2, `### Summary`): rewrite the Summary to (a) **fix the phantom claim** in bullet 2 — currently “at **pi-flux** ($\Phi = \Phi_0/2$) the ground state becomes doubly degenerate due to **time-reversal and translational symmetry**”, but the body's § *Half-Integer Flux: Anomalous Degeneracy* actually protects the doublet by the **anticommutation** of translation $\hat{T}_\pi$ (acting as Pauli $\hat{Z}$ on $\{\vert 0\rangle, \vert 1\rangle\}$) and reflection-with-large-gauge $\hat{S} = \hat{R}\hat{U}_{LG}$ (acting as Pauli $\hat{X}$), realized as a 't Hooft anomaly; time-reversal is **not** invoked anywhere in cell 2. (b) **Expand the Summary from 4 to 5–7 bullets** per `rules/content-style.md` § Summary and See Also (“typically 5–7 bullets … allow 4 only when the lesson is intentionally short”) — cell 2 is the largest in §4.2 (21k chars across 7 `###` body sections), not intentionally short. (c) Add at least one bullet covering the **half-flux anomaly mechanism**: $\{\hat{T}_\pi, \hat{S}\} = 0$ forces a 2-dimensional irrep, so the doublet cannot be lifted by any perturbation that preserves both symmetries — the central conceptual insight of the lesson is currently absent from the recap. Notation discipline during rewrite: `\hat{T}_\pi`, `\hat{S} = \hat{R}\hat{U}_{LG}`, `\Phi_0`, upright `\mathrm{i,e,d}`, no `\quad`/`\qquad` side-by-side, no display equations in Summary bullets (per `content-style.md` § Summary and See Also default “no `{eq}` / equation-label spam” — at most one short anchor if truly needed). Per the rule's **Grounding** clause, each new bullet must be defensible from cell 2 prose. The Poll touches no SQUID-specific content — no Josephson $\sin\delta$ junction current, no $\cos(\pi\Phi/\Phi_0)$ envelope, no RSJ $\langle V\rangle$ formula. Per `rules/content-style.md` § Poll Design (“**Closely tied to lecture content** … must test understanding of material covered in the same lecture note”), choose one of: **(a) Move the Poll** to the end of § *Tunneling Across the Ring: Flux Dependence* — that section already derives the $\Phi_{\mathrm{path}} = \pi\Phi/\Phi_0$ half-loop phase and the $\Gamma(\Phi) = \Gamma_0\cos^2(\pi\Phi/\Phi_0)$ flux modulation, so the destructive-interference condition $\Phi = (n+1/2)\Phi_0$ is the natural conceptual check there. The Poll currently inserted between § *Application — The SQUID* body and § Summary should then be removed from that location. **(b) Rewrite the Poll stem** to test a SQUID-specific result — e.g. “For a dc SQUID biased below $2I_c$, at what flux $\Phi$ does the time-averaged voltage $\langle V\rangle$ become nonzero?” with answer $\Phi = \Phi_0/2$ (where $\cos(\pi\Phi/\Phi_0) = 0$ and the supercurrent channel shuts off, forcing the bias through the resistive channel) and three plausible distractors (e.g. “$\Phi = 0$” — the supercurrent maximum; “$\Phi = \Phi_0$” — same as $\Phi = 0$ by periodicity, common AB-quantization confusion; “any $\Phi > 0$” — ignoring the $\cos$ envelope). **(ii) Stem wording.** “Creating a quantized flux $\Phi$” is **misleading**: an external solenoid threads a continuous classical magnetic flux through the ring, and only the superconducting-ring response (§ Flux Quantization in `4-2-2`) makes flux quantized. Replace “creating a quantized flux $\Phi$” with “threading a magnetic flux $\Phi$” (option a) or restate in terms of the SQUID's applied flux (option b). Poll **title** also needs a rename — “Interference and flux quantization” misdescribes the question (the answer concerns *destructive interference*, not *flux quantization* in the superconducting sense): “Poll: Two-path destructive interference” (option a) or “Poll: SQUID flux-modulated voltage” (option b). All four `rules/content-style.md` § Poll Design hard constraints must remain after the rewrite (one definite correct answer, all distractors plausible-for-a-reason, <1 min to answer, `dropdown poll` so collapsed by default). Designer: `lecture-designer` (placement decision — prefer (a) for minimum content change; (b) only if a clean SQUID-specific stem can be written that meets all four Poll constraints) → `notebook-writer`.

(iii) Same derivation, ~line 237 — `$$\hat{T}_\pi\hat{S}\vert n\rangle = \hat{T}_\pi\vert 1-n\rangle = (-1)^{1-n}\vert 1-n\rangle, \qquad \hat{S}\hat{T}_\pi\vert n\rangle = \hat{S}\,(-1)^n\vert n\rangle = (-1)^n\vert 1-n\rangle.$$` → stack as a `\begin{split}` with the two compositions on aligned lines, then conclude `\hat{T}_\pi\hat{S} = -\hat{S}\hat{T}_\pi` separately (the prose around this evaluation already states the conclusion). (iv) *Derivation: RSJ time-averaged voltage* dropdown, ~line 322 — `$$\int_0^{2\pi}\frac{\mathrm{d}\delta}{a - b\sin\delta} = \frac{2\pi}{\sqrt{a^2 - b^2}}, \qquad a > b > 0.$$` → move the side condition `a > b > 0` to prose introducing or following the displayed identity (e.g. "for $a > b > 0$, the standard contour-integral identity gives") so the display block contains only the equation. **Notation discipline** during the rewrite: keep upright `\mathrm{i,e,d}`, `\vert n\rangle` (no glued letter/digit per `physics-conventions.md` § Ket/Bra), `\Phi`/`\Theta` symbol roles unchanged, no `align`/`aligned` (use `\begin{split}` only). **Symbol-collision sweep** required after edit: re-grep cell 2 for `\qquad`/`\quad` (target = 0 inside `$$` blocks; intra-line `\quad` for spacing inside one logical equation is fine if any survives) and verify `\Phi`/`\Theta`/`\alpha`/`\hbar` counts unchanged. The AB-phase `split` block currently shows two displayed lines ($\Delta\Phi_{\mathrm{AB}} = \Phi_{\mathrm{path,upper}} - \Phi_{\mathrm{path,lower}}$ and immediately $= (q/\hbar)\oint \boldsymbol{A}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l} = q\Phi/\hbar$), compressing the chain shown in `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` § *Aharonov-Bohm Effect* (lines ~1519–1556) which presents 5 explicit lines: (i) substitute the single-path phase formula on the upper and lower integrals; (ii) reverse the lower path orientation to combine into the closed loop $\mathcal{C} = \mathcal{C}_{\text{upper}} - \mathcal{C}_{\text{lower}}$; (iii) write the result as $(q/\hbar)\oint_\mathcal{C} \boldsymbol{A}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l}$; (iv) apply Stokes' theorem $\oint_\mathcal{C} \boldsymbol{A}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l} = \int_\mathcal{S} (\nabla\times\boldsymbol{A})\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{S} = \int_\mathcal{S} \boldsymbol{B}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{S}$; (v) identify the integral with the enclosed flux $\Phi$ to land on $q\Phi/\hbar$. (Homework Solutions Collection — professor directive (`feedback.md`, 2026-05-20) — run 39 — 2026-05-20: scheduled-task automated run (manager agent, no human present). This run COMPLETES the directive. Created `homework/5-2-3_solutions.md` — 8 homework problems from notebook `5-2-3-applications` copied verbatim from the Homework cell (8 bold problem lines; problems 2, 4, 7, 8 retain their (a)/(b)/(c) sub-part structure), each with a complete worked solution — and updated `homework/homework.md` with the 5.2.3 entry and its 8 problem titles. Solutions cover: P1 phase cancellation — expanding the two bare-propagator phases shows the exponent splits into a t1-independent prefactor e^{-iE_f t/hbar}e^{iE_i t0/hbar} (pure phase, modulus 1, drops out of |.|^2) plus the t1-dependent Bohr phase i omega_fi t1, so P^(1) depends only on V_fi(t1) e^{i omega_fi t1}; the cancellation is forced by global-phase invariance of states and by freedom in the zero of energy. P2 sinc-squared properties — (a) peak height (sin a t/a)^2 -> t^2 as a->0 so P^(1)|_0 = |V_fi|^2 t^2/hbar^2; (b) first zero at a t = pi, width ~ pi/t; (c) substitution u = a t gives integral = t * integral(sin u/u)^2 du = pi t; (peak)x(width) ~ t^2 * (1/t) = t matches the integrated weight, and dividing the continuum-summed area by t leaves a t-independent rate. P3 sinc-to-delta — acting on a test function g and substituting u = a t turns t^{-1}(sin a t/a)^2 into (sin u/u)^2 g(u/t); dominated convergence (dominator (sin u/u)^2 sup|g|) sends g(u/t)->g(0), leaving pi g(0), hence the limit is pi delta(a). P4 density of states — (a) counting k-points at density V/(2pi)^3 in a ball of radius k = sqrt(2mE)/hbar gives N(E) = V(2mE)^{3/2}/(6 pi^2 hbar^3) and rho = dN/dE = V(2m)^{3/2} sqrt(E)/(4 pi^2 hbar^3) = Vm sqrt(2mE)/(2 pi^2 hbar^3); (b) Fermi golden rule with this rho at E_f = E_i + hbar omega gives W_i = Vm|V_fi|^2 sqrt(2m(E_i+hbar omega))/(pi hbar^4), scaling as sqrt(E_i) for E_i >> hbar omega. P5 adiabatic ramp Lorentzian — the ramp integral int_{-infty}^0 e^{(i omega_fi + 1/tau)t1} dt1 = 1/(i omega_fi + 1/tau) (lower limit vanishes since tau>0); squaring gives P = |V_fi|^2/[(E_f-E_i)^2 + (hbar/tau)^2], a Lorentzian with FWHM 2 hbar/tau in Delta E and 2/tau in omega_fi. P6 adiabatic to static — the tau->infty limit at fixed Delta E != 0 gives |V_fi|^2/(E_f-E_i)^2, identical to |<f|i(V)>|^2 from non-degenerate perturbation theory (5.1.2); they must agree by the adiabatic theorem (infinitely slow switch-on deforms |i> into the exact eigenstate |i(V)> with no real transitions, so the transition probability is just the squared admixture). P7 three-level Raman — continuing HW 5.2.2.8 (units hbar=1, stated explicitly to match the target formula and the reference); expanding cos(omega t1)cos(omega t2) against the level-3 Bohr phase e^{-i Delta(t2-t1)} gives four nested-integral terms with Omega1 = Delta +/- omega, Omega2 = -Delta +/- omega; only the two mixed-sign terms have Omega1 + Omega2 = 0 and grow linearly in t (t/(i Omega1)); summing 1/(Delta+omega) + 1/(Delta-omega) = 2 Delta/(Delta^2-omega^2) yields <2|G(t,0)|1> ~ (i lambda_0^2 t/2) Delta/(Delta^2-omega^2); (b) P_{1->2} = (lambda_0^4 t^2/4) Delta^2/(Delta^2-omega^2)^2 ~ t^2, resonantly enhanced as omega/Delta->1; (c) the t^2 (not t) scaling is because |2> is a single discrete level (no continuum, no density of states, no delta-function rate) so the amplitude accumulates coherently — contrast with the incoherent linear-in-t golden-rule rate. P8 minimal Kubo — with H_0 = -(1/2)hbar omega_0 Z, E_1-E_0 = hbar omega_0; (a) the four matrix elements <0|X|1>=1, <1|X|0>=1, <0|Y|1>=-i, <1|Y|0>=+i; (b) numerator <0|jx|1><1|jy|0> - <0|jy|1><1|jx|0> = (1)(i)-(-i)(1) = 2i, so sigma_xy = i hbar (2i)/(hbar^2 omega_0^2) = -2/(hbar omega_0^2); (c) replacing jy->X makes the numerator an antisymmetric bilinear of identical operators, which vanishes — a Hall response requires non-commuting current operators ([X,Y] = 2iZ != 0 supplies the imaginary antisymmetric correlation). Cross-checked against the professor reference `_refs/PerturbationTheory.md` (Fermi golden rule, adiabatic process, and the worked three-level Raman sample problem, whose target amplitude i lambda_0^2 t Delta/(2(Delta^2-omega^2)) the solution reproduces). Solutions-file QA: all 8 problem statements reproduced verbatim against the source notebook Homework cell (8 bold problem lines, 8 `**Solution.**` blocks, 8 `---` separators), standalone `$$` delimiter lines all paired with blank-line spacing, `\begin{split}`/`\end{split}` pair balanced, inline `$` math balanced, no glued `\vert`, no stray control characters. No new tickets filed. Homework-solutions files done: all 39 in-scope subsection notebooks (all 9 Ch 1, all 6 Ch 2 in-scope, all 9 Ch 3 in-scope, all 9 Ch 4 in-scope, all 6 Ch 5) plus the `homework/homework.md` index — the directive is now COMPLETE and marked DONE in `feedback.md` (2026-05-20). Ch 4–6 phased maintenance resumes next run from the Phase Cursor: Round 1, Phase D, `6-1-2-entropy`.)

(Homework Solutions Collection — professor directive (`feedback.md`, 2026-05-20) — run 26 — 2026-05-20: scheduled-task automated run (manager agent, no human present). Ch 4–6 phased maintenance remains paused per the OPEN `feedback.md` directive; Phase Cursor unchanged (Round 1, Phase D, `6-1-2-entropy`). Created `homework/4-1-2_solutions.md` — 5 homework problems from notebook `4-1-2-electromagnetic-coupling` copied verbatim from the Homework cell, each with a complete worked solution — and updated `homework/homework.md` with the 4.1.2 entry and its 5 problem titles. Solutions cover: P1 velocity for the Pauli Hamiltonian — (a) [x_i,H_P] receives nothing from the spin-magnetic term −(q hbar/2m) sigma·B or the q phi term (both functions of x and sigma, which commute with x_i), so only the kinetic term survives and dx/dt = pi/m exactly as in the spinless case; (b) any term built from x and sigma alone commutes with x_i (position operators commute among themselves and act on a different tensor factor than the Pauli operators), so only p-containing terms contribute to the velocity operator, independent of the spin algebra; (c) spin enters the acceleration — [pi_i,H_spin] = i hbar (q hbar/2m) d_i(sigma·B) gives the Stern-Gerlach force (q hbar/2m) grad(sigma·B) = grad(mu·B) with mu = (q hbar/2m) sigma, so spin couples to motion only through the gradient of B (a uniform field exerts no translational force); P2 velocity uncertainty in B — (a) Robertson on the c-number commutator [v_x,v_y] = i hbar qB/m^2 gives Delta v_x Delta v_y >= hbar qB/(2m^2); (b) for a centered state <v_perp^2> = (Delta v_x)^2+(Delta v_y)^2 >= 2 Delta v_x Delta v_y >= hbar qB/m^2 by AM-GM, so the orbit area pi r_c^2 = pi m^2 v_perp^2/(qB)^2 >= pi hbar/(qB) = pi ell_B^2, saturated by the lowest Landau level; (c) v_x and v_y are conjugate (non-commuting) in a field, so the orbit cannot be squeezed below the minimum phase-space cell ~pi ell_B^2 — irreducible zero-point cyclotron motion; P3 cyclotron motion from Heisenberg — (a) [pi_x,H] = (i q hbar B/m) pi_y and [pi_y,H] = −(i q hbar B/m) pi_x give dpi_x/dt = omega_c pi_y, dpi_y/dt = −omega_c pi_x with omega_c = qB/m; (b) the complex combination pi_+ = pi_x + i pi_y obeys dpi_+/dt = −i omega_c pi_+, so (pi_x,pi_y) rotates rigidly at omega_c — classical cyclotron motion at the operator level; (c) pi^2 = 2m H commutes with H so it is conserved, and the magnetic force does no work, giving energy conservation when E = 0; P4 symmetric gauge verification — (a) curl of A = (B/2)(−y,x,0) is B z-hat; (b) [pi_x,pi_y] = i hbar qB explicitly, the two halves i hbar qB/2 coming symmetrically from the y-term of pi_x and the x-term of pi_y, matching the gauge-independent formula; (c) the nonzero commutator means v_x and v_y are incompatible observables — no joint eigenbasis, conjugate-pair behavior underlying Landau quantization; P5 field strength tensor — F_{0i} = d_0 A_i − d_i A_0 = −d_t A^i − d_i phi = E_i and F_{ij} = −(d_i A^j − d_j A^i) = −eps_ijk B_k, assembled into the antisymmetric 4x4 F_{mu nu} (electric field in the first row/column, magnetic field in the lower 3x3 block), with the raised-index F^{mu nu} flipping the electric block, and gauge invariance from d_mu d_nu alpha being symmetric. All key results verified symbolically with SymPy: symmetric-gauge curl A = [0,0,B], [pi_x,pi_y] = i hbar q B, F_xy = −B_z / F_xz = +B_y / F_yz = −B_x, the cyclotron ODE solution, and the minimum orbit area pi hbar/(qB). (Homework Solutions Collection - professor directive (`feedback.md`, 2026-05-20) - run 21 - 2026-05-20: scheduled-task automated run (manager agent, no human present). Ch 4-6 phased maintenance remains paused per the OPEN `feedback.md` directive; Phase Cursor unchanged (Round 1, Phase D, `6-1-2-entropy`). Created `homework/3-2-3_solutions.md` - 8 homework problems from notebook `3-2-3-free-particle-propagator` copied verbatim from the Homework cell, each with a complete worked solution - and updated `homework/homework.md` with the 3.2.3 entry and its 8 problem titles. Solutions cover: P1 dispersion relation - substituting the plane wave gives d_t psi = -i omega psi and nabla^2 psi = -k^2 psi, so hbar omega = hbar^2 k^2/(2m); since psi never vanishes the dispersion omega = hbar k^2/(2m) is necessary and sufficient, and de Broglie (p = hbar k, E = hbar omega) recovers E = p^2/(2m); P2 initial condition - (a) taking t -> 0 in the plane-wave integrand leaves the Fourier representation of delta^3(x-x'), (b) the closed form has imaginary-variance Gaussian width sigma ~ sqrt(hbar t/m) -> 0 while each Cartesian Fresnel factor integrates to 1, so K_free is a nascent delta; P3 direct Gaussian integration - completing the square in k gives -i beta (k - r/2beta)^2 + i r^2/(4 beta), the shift q = k - r/(2 beta) factorizes into three Cartesian Fresnel integrals each = sqrt(pi/i beta), and (2 pi)^-3 (pi/i beta)^3/2 = (1/4 pi i beta)^3/2 with beta = hbar t/(2m) reproduces the prefactor (m/2 pi i hbar t)^3/2 and exponent i m r^2/(2 hbar t); P4 slice agreement - the 1D closed form at t = dt is identical term-by-term to K_dt^free from 3.2.2, proving the slice formula was exact not approximate and the path integral is self-consistent; P5 action conjugate relations - grad S_cl = m r/t = p and -d_t S_cl = m r^2/(2 t^2) = p^2/(2m) = E (the Hamilton-Jacobi relations), and the first-order expansion S_cl(x+dx,t+dt) = S_cl + p.dx - E dt makes e^{i S_cl/hbar} locally a plane wave e^{i(p.x - E t)/hbar}; P6 wavepacket spreading - completing the square in x' keeps psi Gaussian, sigma(t)^2 = sigma_0^2 + (hbar t/(2 m sigma_0))^2 with spreading time tau = 2 m sigma_0^2/hbar; numerically tau ~ 1.7e-16 s for a 1 Angstrom electron and ~2.8e27 s for a 1 mm baseball (~10^10 ages of the universe); P7 magnitude and normalization - the exponential is a pure phase so |K_free|^2 = (m/2 pi hbar t)^3, position-independent; this is consistent with integral K_free d^3x = 1 because that integrates the complex amplitude as a Fresnel integral (convergent by phase cancellation, not spatial decay), and |K_free|^2 is not a probability density because it is non-normalizable and K is the response to a non-normalizable delta-function initial state; P8 macroscopic phase factors - theta = m r^2/(2 hbar t) is ~1.1e32 cycles for a baseball (1 m, 1 s) and ~6.8e-4 cycles for an electron (1 nm, 1 ps); the phase gradient d theta/d r = m r/(hbar t) sweeps 2 pi over ~4e-33 m for the baseball (unobservably fine fringes -> classical trajectory) versus ~0.7 micron for the electron (resolvable fringes -> routine interference). Quantitative claims verified programmatically with Python: electron tau = 1.73e-16 s, baseball tau = 2.86e27 s (ratio 1.65e43, ~7e9 ages of the universe); theta/2pi = 1.14e32 (baseball) and 6.82e-4 (electron); phase-gradient 2 pi spacings 4.4e-33 m and 7.3e-7 m. Created `homework/3-2-2_solutions.md` - 8 homework problems from notebook `3-2-2-schrodinger-equation` copied verbatim from the Homework cell, each with a complete worked solution - and updated `homework/homework.md` with the 3.2.2 entry and its 8 problem titles. Solutions cover: P1 zeroth Gaussian moment - the polar trick gives integral of exp(-r^2) over the plane = pi, factorizing yields integral exp(-x^2) dx = sqrt(pi), rescaling gives sqrt(pi/b) for Re b > 0, and the analytic continuation b -> -i alpha is justified by rotating the contour onto the pi/4 ray (where i alpha (dx)^2 becomes the real Gaussian -alpha u^2 and the connecting arcs vanish since Re[i alpha R^2 e^{2i phi}] <= 0 for 0 <= phi <= pi/4), giving M0 = e^{i pi/4} sqrt(pi/alpha) = sqrt(i pi/alpha); P2 second moment - the derivative trick d M0/d alpha = i M2 combined with d M0/d alpha = -M0/(2 alpha) gives M2 = i M0/(2 alpha) = (i hbar dt/m) M0, matching eq-moments-result; P3 slice normalization - integral of K_dt^free over x' equals sqrt(m/2 pi i hbar dt) * M0 = 1 for all x and dt, so a spatially constant wavefunction is left unchanged over one slice (consistent with d_t psi = 0 when d_x^2 psi = 0); P4 composition of two slices - completing the square in x' gives the cross term i alpha (x-x'')^2/2 and a residual Gaussian integral M0(2 alpha); the prefactor N^2 M0(2 alpha) = sqrt(alpha/2 i pi) and alpha/2 = m/(2 hbar (2 dt)), so K_{2 dt}^free has exactly the K_dt^free form with dt -> 2 dt, verifying the semigroup/composition law (cf HW 3.2.1.6) and that A = 1/M0 is the normalization that makes prefactors compose; P5 probability conservation - d_t|psi|^2 from the Schrodinger equation and its conjugate (real V cancels the potential terms) equals (i hbar/2m) d_x(psi* d_x psi - psi d_x psi*), so j = (hbar/m) Im(psi* d_x psi) and d_t rho + d_x j = 0; integrating with psi -> 0 at infinity gives d/dt integral|psi|^2 dx = 0; P6 higher moments - repeated derivative trick gives M_{2k} = c_k M0 alpha^-k with c_k = (i/2)^k (2k-1)!! dimensionless, and since alpha^-k ~ dt^k one has A M_{2k} = O(dt^k); dividing the slice difference by dt and taking dt -> 0 kills every k >= 2 term and keeps only k = 1 (M2), so the expansion truncates exactly at the second moment (odd moments vanish by parity); P7 wavepacket spreading - sigma_p ~ hbar/sigma gives velocity spread v ~ hbar/(m sigma), doubling time tau ~ m sigma^2/hbar; numerically tau ~ 8.6e-17 s for a 1 Angstrom electron and tau ~ 1.4e27 s for a 1 mm baseball (~3e9 times the age of the universe), so macroscopic objects behave classically; P8 potential placement freedom - evaluating V at the source point x-dx gives slice phase exp[-i V(x-dx) dt/hbar]; expanding V(x-dx) and integrating against the kinetic Gaussian, the linear term pairs with M1 = 0 then M2, and the quadratic term pairs with M2, so the difference from sampling V at the endpoint is O(dt^2) and the Schrodinger equation is independent of where V is sampled (matching the slice-action freedom of HW 3.2.1.4). Quantitative claims verified programmatically with Python: the electron tau ~ 8.64e-17 s and baseball tau ~ 1.37e27 s (~3.2e9 age-of-universe ratio), and c_1 = i/2, c_2 = -3/4. (Homework Solutions Collection - professor directive (`feedback.md`, 2026-05-20) - run 19 - 2026-05-20: scheduled-task automated run (manager agent, no human present). Ch 4-6 phased maintenance remains paused per the OPEN `feedback.md` directive; Phase Cursor unchanged (Round 1, Phase D, `6-1-2-entropy`). Created `homework/3-2-1_solutions.md` - 7 homework problems from notebook `3-2-1-path-integral-formulation` copied verbatim from the Homework cell, each with a complete worked solution - and updated `homework/homework.md` with the 3.2.1 entry and its 7 problem titles. Solutions cover: P1 propagating a Gaussian wavepacket through the free propagator - Gaussian integration over x' gives psi(x,t) = (pi sigma^2)^(-1/4) (1+i t/tau)^(-1/2) exp[-(x^2/2 sigma^2)/(1+i t/tau)] with tau = m sigma^2/hbar, |psi|^2 stays Gaussian of width sigma_t = sigma sqrt(1+(hbar t/m sigma^2)^2), and tau approx 8.6e-17 s (~86 attoseconds) for an electron localized to 1 Angstrom; P2 composition test - explicit square-completion shows the midpoint convolution of two t/2 propagators reproduces K(x,t;x',0), generalized to a composition lemma valid for any intermediate time (cross-term collapses to (x-x')^2/T, prefactor to sqrt(m/2 pi i hbar T)), then (b) two intermediate times and (c) induction over N uniform slices, noting the free-particle slicing is exact at every finite N because the Fresnel kernel is closed under convolution; P3 free-particle slice action - constant-velocity straight line gives S = m(x_{n+1}-x_n)^2/(2 dt) directly; P4 slice action with a potential - kinetic part as in P3, potential part by the midpoint rule with the linear Taylor term cancelling by symmetry about the slice midpoint, leaving a curvature correction (V''/24)(x_{n+1}-x_n)^2 dt that is O(dt^2) for the path-integral displacement scale; P5 phase difference between nearby slices - dS_slice = p_n Delta to first order with p_n = m(x_{n+1}-x_n)/dt (Hamilton-Jacobi relation), so the slice kernel is locally a plane wave in the endpoint with effective de Broglie wavelength lambda = h/p_n = h dt/(m(x_{n+1}-x_n)); P6 functional equation from composition - composition of two slices yields A(2 dt) F(.;2 dt) = A(dt)^2 conv(F,F), the convolution law that a slice kernel of duration 2 dt is the convolution of two duration-dt kernels; dimensional analysis ([K_dt] = L^-1) plus the Fresnel-width dt^(1/2) scaling forces A(dt) propto dt^(-1/2), anticipating the section 3.2.2 result A = sqrt(m/2 pi i hbar dt); P7 typical paths in the slicing - setting S_slice/hbar ~ 1 gives the coherent-contribution displacement scale |x_{n+1}-x_n| ~ sqrt(hbar dt/m), the slice velocity ~ dt^(-1/2) diverges as dt -> 0, so typical Feynman paths are continuous but nowhere differentiable (Brownian/fractal, not smooth classical orbits). Quantitative claims verified programmatically with NumPy: tau ~ 8.6e-17 s for the electron; the analytic psi(x,t) vs a direct grid convolution of the propagator agree to 1e-12; the |psi|^2 width matches the sigma_t formula and stays normalized; the P2(a) composition integral reproduces K(x,t;x',0) (finite-grid agreement ~3e-4 on an oscillatory Fresnel integrand, analytic proof exact); the P5 first-order dS/dDelta equals p_n. Created `homework/3-1-1_solutions.md` - 7 homework problems from notebook `3-1-1-geometric-optics` copied verbatim from the Homework cell, each with a complete worked solution - and updated `homework/homework.md` with a new "Chapter 3 - Path Integral" section and the 3.1.1 entry with its 7 problem titles. Solutions cover: P1 the law of reflection from Fermat's principle - minimizing ell(x) = sqrt(x^2+a^2) + sqrt((d-x)^2+b^2) yields sin(theta_i) = sin(theta_r), with the positive second derivative confirming a genuine minimum and the mirror-image (unfolding) construction as an alternative derivation; P2 total internal reflection glass-to-air - critical angle sin(theta_c) = n2/n1 = 2/3, theta_c = arcsin(2/3) approx 41.8 deg, and the physical argument that for theta > theta_c Snell's law would require sin(theta_2) > 1 so no real refracted ray exists; P3 a horizontal ray in n(z) = n0(1+alpha z) curves toward higher n by the Fermat trade-off (a path bowing toward lower n lowers the optical path length at first order in the excursion while paying only second order in extra geometric length), with a wavefront-tilt cross-check; P4 d^2 L/dx^2 = n1 h1^2/(x^2+h1^2)^(3/2) + n2 h2^2/((d-x)^2+h2^2)^(3/2) > 0, so the Snell stationary point is a minimum (L strictly convex); P5 the Foucault 1850 result (speed of light in water < c) supports the wave theory over Newton's corpuscle theory, and Fermat's principle in the form delta(integral n ds) = 0 depends only on the value of n, not the direction of the speed change (the least-time reading is what requires v = c/n); P6 curved-mirror focusing - (a) sagitta z_P = h^2/(2R) from expanding R - sqrt(R^2 - h^2), (b) the paraxial optical path length L(h) = (u+v) + (h^2/2)(1/u + 1/v - 2/R), (c) the mirror equation 1/u + 1/v = 2/R from requiring the h^2 coefficient to vanish so all paraxial rays share one path length; P7 the optics-mechanics dictionary - (a) a free particle under Hamilton's principle moves in a straight line at constant speed (analog of a straight ray in a uniform medium), (b) Maupertuis' principle delta(integral p ds) = 0 with momentum magnitude p = sqrt(2m(E-V)) playing the role of n, so a fixed-energy particle curves toward lower potential just as light curves toward higher index. Quantitative claims verified programmatically with NumPy: the critical angle 41.81 deg, the reflection minimum (sin theta_i = sin theta_r at the minimizing x) with positive ell'', the refraction L'' formula vs finite-difference, and the paraxial sagitta and source-to-P distance vs exact (agreement through O(h^4)). Solutions-file QA: all 7 problem statements reproduced verbatim against the source notebook, 64 display-math (`$$`) delimiter lines all paired and correctly spaced (blank line before/after, none inside), inline `$` math balanced, no malformed ket pipes, no stray control characters. No new tickets filed. One un-ticketed observation for a future maintenance pass (the phased program is paused per `feedback.md`): notebook `3-1-1` Homework Problem 3 asks to argue that a horizontal ray in n(z) = n0(1+alpha z), alpha > 0, "will curve downward"; the ray bends toward higher n, which for this index profile is the +z direction, so "downward" is correct only if z measures depth into the medium - the convention the solution adopts and states explicitly. Under the usual convention of z as altitude the ray would curve upward. The notebook's own Problem 7(b) states light "curves toward regions of higher n", consistent with the depth reading. Solutions cover: P1 the triangle rule for j1=1, j2=3/2 giving J in {1/2,3/2,5/2} with dimension check sum(2J+1)=2+4+6=12=3x4; P2 deriving the two-spin-1/2 triplet by lowering |1,1>=|up up> with J_- (|1,0>=(|up dn>+|dn up>)/sqrt2, |1,-1>=|dn dn>) and the singlet |0,0>=(|up dn>-|dn up>)/sqrt2 by orthogonality + normalization, with the J^2|0,0>=0 check; P3 why two identical fermions sharing one spatial orbital must be spin-singlet (symmetric spatial part forces antisymmetric spin part, so the symmetric triplet is Pauli-forbidden); P4 the identity L.S=(J^2-L^2-S^2)/2 (cross terms equal since [L_i,S_j]=0), <L.S>=(hbar^2/2)[j(j+1)-l(l+1)-3/4], and the hydrogen 2p fine-structure splitting Delta E=(3/2) lambda hbar^2 between j=3/2 and j=1/2; P5 spin-1 (x) spin-1/2 coupling under H=-S_A.S_B - (a) block-diagonalization by M, the M=+1/2 block [[-1/2,1/sqrt2],[1/sqrt2,0]] with eigenvalues +1/2 (J=3/2) and -1 (J=1/2) and all six coupled |J,M> states, (b) the change-of-basis matrix U block-diagonal in M with M=+1/2 CG block [[sqrt(1/3),sqrt(2/3)],[sqrt(2/3),-sqrt(1/3)]], (c) P_1/2+P_3/2=I and P_1/2 P_3/2=0 by completeness/orthogonality and the projectors as functions of S_A.S_B: P_3/2=(2/3)(S_A.S_B+I), P_1/2=-(2/3)(S_A.S_B-I/2). All Problem 4 and 5 key results - the S_A.S_B matrix, per-M-block eigenvalues/eigenvectors, the six coupled states' orthonormality, the projector identities, and the spin-orbit expectation values - verified with NumPy at machine precision; all 5 problem statements verified verbatim against the source notebook. Ch 4–6 phased maintenance remains paused per the OPEN `feedback.md` directive; Phase Cursor unchanged (Round 1, Phase D, `6-1-2-entropy`). Created `homework/2-1-3_solutions.md` — 10 homework problems from notebook `2-1-3-second-quantization` copied verbatim from the Homework cell, each with a complete worked solution — and updated `homework/homework.md` with the 2.1.3 entry and its 10 problem titles. Solutions cover: the bosonic number-operator spectrum n = 0,1,2,... from the ladder/norm argument; the [n,b-dagger] and [n,b] commutators; (c-dagger)^2 = 0 as Pauli exclusion in operator form; <0|b b-dagger|0> - <0|b-dagger b|0> = 1 as the vacuum commutator; the 3 bosonic vs 1 fermionic two-mode N=2 Fock states; <{n}|H|{n}> = sum eps_a n_a; [H,N] = 0 and particle-number conservation; the second-quantized kinetic operator T = sum_k eps_k n_k vs the first-quantized form; harmonic-oscillator equal partition (<x^2>, <p^2>, KE = PE = E_n/2, and the |0>+|1> superposition); and the Schwinger-boson su(2) construction (commutators, S^2 = (N/2)(N/2+1), the spin-1 N=2 matrices, and the s = N/2 map). P9 and P10 verified with NumPy at machine precision. No new tickets filed; one un-ticketed observation is recorded in the Phase Cursor run note (three mismatched homework titles in the 2-1-3 notebook — P1/P3 swapped, P7 mislabeled). Next run: solutions for `2-2-1-angular-momentum-algebra`. See the Phase Cursor section for the full run note.)

(Homework Solutions Collection — professor directive (`feedback.md`, 2026-05-20) — run 10 — 2026-05-20: scheduled-task automated run (manager agent, no human present). Solutions cover: tensor-product dimension (dimensions multiply); orthonormality and completeness of the two-qubit computational basis; the Kronecker matrix Z(x)I; factorization of <A(x)B> in product states; [A_i,B_j]=0 for i!=j from the mixed-product rule; the Heisenberg matrix with spectrum J{1,1,1,-3} and 3 Pauli strings; the Pauli-string decomposition of |00><00|; and the 2^(N+1)-2 vs 2N parameter counts with the difference identified as entanglement degrees of freedom. Matrix and counting results verified with NumPy; all 8 problem statements verified verbatim against the source. No new tickets filed. Next run: solutions for `2-1-2-symmetrization`. See the Phase Cursor section for the full run note.)

(Homework Solutions Collection — professor directive (`feedback.md`, 2026-05-20) — run 7 — 2026-05-20: scheduled-task automated run (manager agent, no human present). Ch 4–6 phased maintenance remains paused per the OPEN `feedback.md` directive; Phase Cursor unchanged (Round 1, Phase D, `6-1-2-entropy`). Created `homework/1-3-1_solutions.md` — 9 homework problems from notebook `1-3-1-unitary-evolution` copied verbatim from the Homework cell, each with a complete worked solution — and updated `homework/homework.md` with the 1.3.1 entry. Solutions cover: unitarity of e^{iGθ} from the Taylor series; the Pauli exponential e^{iσ^xθ/2}=cos(θ/2)I+i sin(θ/2)σ^x via spectral decomposition; generator extraction G=-i dU/dθ|_0=σ^z/2; the two-level evolution operator for H=E_0 I+Δσ^z; the derivation of the time-dependent Schrödinger equation; norm preservation from unitarity; superposition dynamics and the Bohr-frequency relative phase; the composition law U(t_1)U(t_2)=U(t_1+t_2) and reversibility; and e^{iAπ} for a non-Pauli observable via spectral decomposition. All numeric results verified with NumPy (e^{iσ^xθ/2} and e^{iσ^zθ/2} matched to cos/sin closed forms across a θ-sweep, finite-difference generator extraction, the two-level U(t), norm preservation under random states, the composition law, and e^{iAπ}=diag(1,-1) for eigenvalues 2,-3). No new tickets filed. Next run: solutions for `1-3-2-schrodinger-picture`. See the Phase Cursor section for the full run note.)

(Homework Solutions Collection — professor directive (`feedback.md`, 2026-05-20) — run 5 — 2026-05-20: scheduled-task automated run (manager agent, no human present). Ch 4–6 phased maintenance remains paused per the OPEN `feedback.md` directive; Phase Cursor unchanged (Round 1, Phase D, `6-1-2-entropy`). Created `homework/1-2-2_solutions.md` — 8 homework problems from notebook `1-2-2-uncertainty-and-incompatibility` copied from the Homework cell, each with a complete worked solution — and updated `homework/homework.md` with the 1.2.2 entry. Solutions cover: direct matrix proof that [X,Z] = -2iY; expectation values and uncertainties on the state |+> with the Robertson check 0 >= 0; the commutator product (Leibniz) rule via the inserted-term trick; matrix verification of all three Pauli commutators [X,Y]=2iZ, [Y,Z]=2iX, [Z,X]=2iY; why a Z-eigenstate carries dZ = 0 without violating Robertson (the bound is state-dependent and vanishes when <Y> = 0); the result [sigma^i, sigma^j] = 0 iff i = j and its consequence that no two distinct Pauli operators share an eigenbasis; dZ = |sin 2alpha| maximized at alpha = pi/4, 3pi/4 (the X-eigenstates); and min of dX*dZ = 0 achieved by any X- or Z-eigenstate. All numeric results verified with NumPy (Pauli commutators, expectation values and uncertainties, the product rule on random 2x2 matrices, an alpha-sweep confirming max dZ at pi/4, and a Bloch-sphere sweep confirming min dX*dZ = 0). One new ticket filed: T-2026-05-20-NB-62 (Homework Problem 7 line-break corruption in the 1-2-2 notebook — Ch 1, outside the Ch 4–6 phased scope, LOW priority). Next run: solutions for `1-2-3-measurement-operators`. See the Phase Cursor section for the full run note.)

The old P1 ("Thermal equilibrium entropy" — compute the thermal state and entropy $S(\beta)$ of a generic $E_0<E_1$ qubit and show $S\to\ln 2$ as $\beta\to0$, $S\to0$ as $\beta\to\infty$) duplicated Problem 9 ("Two-level entropy", $E_1=0,E_2=\Delta$), the better-constructed version (guided sub-parts + physical reasoning); per the NB-60 ticket P1 was the replacement candidate. New P1 — **"Entropy and measurement outcomes"** — is a near-lecture problem grounded in cell-2’s *Connection to Information Theory* section, which previously had **no** homework coverage: (a) show the measurement-outcome probabilities $p_i=\langle e_i\vert\hat\rho\vert e_i\rangle$ form a valid distribution; (b) for $\hat\rho=\hat I/2$ compute the Shannon entropy $H=-\sum_i p_i\ln p_i$ and verify $H=S(\hat\rho)$ in any basis; (c) for the pure state $\vert0\rangle\langle0\vert$ compute $H$ in the $\{\vert0\rangle,\vert1\rangle\}$ and $\{\vert+\rangle,\vert-\rangle\}$ bases and explain why $H$ is basis-dependent while $S=0$ is not. The replacement satisfies `rules/content-style.md` § Homework Design (one concept per problem; § Ongoing maintenance "do not duplicate") and homework-designer §2.5 Connection / not §3.1 lecture-duplication (it asks the student to *use* the result, not re-derive a lecture step); sentence-case ASCII bold title and per-line sub-parts conform to § Problem format. Homework count stays 9 (within 5–10); the easy-first difficulty gradient holds — the new P1 is easy/near-lecture. Cells 0/1/2 byte-identical; cell-3 length 3111 → 3576 chars. Two lines pack four operations: (i) binomial expansion + Pauli-identity substitution combined; (ii) trace + simplification combined. **Post-edit NEW form** (1352 chars): lead-in *'Start from $\hat{\rho} = \frac{1}{2}(\hat{I} + \boldsymbol{r}\cdot\hat{\boldsymbol{\sigma}})$. Two auxiliary identities enter the chain: the Pauli identity $(\boldsymbol{r}\cdot\hat{\boldsymbol{\sigma}})^2 = \vert\boldsymbol{r}\vert^2 \hat{I}$, and the traces $\operatorname{Tr}(\hat{I}) = 2$, $\operatorname{Tr}(\hat{\sigma}^i) = 0$ for $i \in \{X, Y, Z\}$.'* + 4-line `\begin{split}` chain with `&=` alignment per `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Display Math: (1) $\operatorname{Tr}(\hat\rho^2) = \tfrac14\operatorname{Tr}(\hat I + \boldsymbol r\cdot\hat{\boldsymbol\sigma})^2$, (2) $= \tfrac14\operatorname{Tr}(\hat I + 2\boldsymbol r\cdot\hat{\boldsymbol\sigma} + (\boldsymbol r\cdot\hat{\boldsymbol\sigma})^2)$, (3) $= \tfrac14\operatorname{Tr}(\hat I + 2\boldsymbol r\cdot\hat{\boldsymbol\sigma} + \vert\boldsymbol r\vert^2\hat I)$, (4) $= \tfrac14(2 + 0 + 2\vert\boldsymbol r\vert^2) = \tfrac12(1 + \vert\boldsymbol r\vert^2)$. Trailing prose annotates the four steps in plain English (square + factor + trace; binomial expansion without identity; apply Pauli identity; apply traces and simplify). Each step now performs exactly one operation per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Mandatory detail level* + § *Mandatory procedure* item 5 (*'State building-block results first. Round-trip read-back equals in-memory edited string. **Consistency check on the new `\begin{split}` layout**: `&=` alignment markers on lines 2–4 align the equals signs; line 1 leads with `\operatorname{Tr}(\hat{\rho}^2) &=`. `\\` line breaks between split rows; no `\\` after the final row per LaTeX convention. The `\begin{split}` block is nested inside the outer `$$...$$` per `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Display Math (*'For multiline equations, always use `\begin{split} ... \end{split}` inside `$$...$$`'* and *'Do NOT use `align` or `aligned` environments in notebooks'*). No `\quad` / `\qquad` introduced — the chain is single-column. Precedent: NB-47 closure on the same notebook 2026-05-19 also converted to `\begin{split}` and validated cleanly. (Ch 4–6 Round 1 — Phase D (Derivation quality) audit on `6-1-1-mixed-states` (first Ch 6 subsection, §6.1.1 — Mixed States: density matrix, purity, ensemble ambiguity, Bloch ball, expectation values, time evolution) 2026-05-19 **complete**: read-only structural diff of all three derivation dropdowns in cell 2 of `notes_src/ch6_quantum-foundations/6-1-1-mixed-states.ipynb` against the professor's reference per `CLAUDE.md` `_refs/` map and `rules/derivation-quality.md` § Mandatory detail level. Ch 6 derivation primary reference is `_refs/QuantumStatistics.nb` per the `CLAUDE.md` § Reference materials table, but the density-matrix / Bloch / Von Neumann material lives in `_refs/QubitsAndEntanglement.nb` § *Density Matrix* (line 19899), § *Dynamics of Density Matrix* / *von Neumann equation* (line 21057), § *Pure State and Mixed State* (line 23440), and § *Purity* + HW solution (lines 23965 / 24052 / 24108); `QuantumStatistics.nb` only references mixed-state ensembles in the maximum-entropy / canonical-ensemble context (line 5096+) and does not derive the §6.1.1 results. Audit therefore aligns against `_refs/QubitsAndEntanglement.nb` for the three derivations. **(D-1) Derivation: Purity from Bloch Vector — too compressed vs `_refs/`.** Lecture (post-NB-51 cell-2 pos ~6540, *dropdown information* between `### The Bloch Ball` and `### Expectation Values`) shows **2 displayed-math lines** ((i) $\hat{\rho}^2 = \tfrac14(\hat I + 2\boldsymbol r\cdot\hat{\boldsymbol\sigma} + |\boldsymbol r|^2\hat I)$ — combines binomial expansion of $(\hat I + \boldsymbol r\cdot\hat{\boldsymbol\sigma})^2$ with substitution of the Pauli identity $(\boldsymbol r\cdot\hat{\boldsymbol\sigma})^2 = |\boldsymbol r|^2\hat I$ in a single step; (ii) $\operatorname{Tr}(\hat{\rho}^2) = \tfrac14(2 + 2|\boldsymbol r|^2) = \tfrac12(1 + |\boldsymbol r|^2)$ — combines trace operation with simplification). `_refs/QubitsAndEntanglement.nb` § *Purity* HW solution (cell `hw: 834866580465521418` at line 24052; solution at line 24108) shows the same derivation in **4 displayed lines**: (1) $\operatorname{Tr}\rho^2 = \tfrac14\operatorname{Tr}(\mathbb{1} + \langle\boldsymbol\sigma\rangle\cdot\boldsymbol\sigma)^2$ (substitute $\rho$ form), (2) $= \tfrac14\operatorname{Tr}(\mathbb{1} + 2\langle\boldsymbol\sigma\rangle\cdot\boldsymbol\sigma + (\langle\boldsymbol\sigma\rangle\cdot\boldsymbol\sigma)(\langle\boldsymbol\sigma\rangle\cdot\boldsymbol\sigma))$ (binomial expansion only, identity not yet applied), (3) $= \tfrac14(1 + \langle\boldsymbol\sigma\rangle\cdot\langle\boldsymbol\sigma\rangle)\operatorname{Tr}\mathbb{1}$ (apply Pauli identity, drop $\operatorname{Tr}\sigma^i = 0$ term, factor $\operatorname{Tr}\mathbb{1}$), (4) $= \tfrac12(1 + \langle\boldsymbol\sigma\rangle^2)$ (substitute $\operatorname{Tr}\mathbb{1} = 2$). Per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § Mandatory detail level (*'Each commutator evaluation or algebraic derivation in a dropdown must contain at least as many displayed intermediate lines as the corresponding passage in `_refs/`. Typically this means 4–7 lines per commutator evaluation.'*) — lecture below the binding standard; strict rule violation. Filed as **T-2026-05-19-DV-13** (**HIGH** — strict `_refs/`-alignment violation, scope is single dropdown, fix expands 2 → 4 lines matching `_refs/` chain). **(D-2) Derivation: Von Neumann Equation — too compressed; `\dot{\hat U}^\dagger` sign-flip step unstated.** Lecture (post-NB-51 cell-2 pos ~9230, *dropdown information* between `### Time Evolution` and the *Purity is Conserved* `attention` box) shows **1 displayed-math line** packing four operations: (a) product-rule differentiation of $\hat\rho(t) = \hat U\hat\rho(0)\hat U^\dagger$, (b) substitution of $\dot{\hat U} = -(\mathrm{i}/\hbar)\hat H\hat U$, (c) substitution of the conjugate $\dot{\hat U}^\dagger = +(\mathrm{i}/\hbar)\hat U^\dagger\hat H$ (sign-flip critical; differentiating $\hat U^\dagger = \mathrm{e}^{+\mathrm{i}\hat H t/\hbar}$), and (d) recognition of $\hat U\hat\rho(0)\hat U^\dagger = \hat\rho(t)$ in the final step. `_refs/QubitsAndEntanglement.nb` § *Dynamics of Density Matrix* (line 21057) does not derive Von Neumann equation in the lecture text — the reference frames the derivation as a homework problem (cell `hw: 6327714880484912580` at line 21133: *'derive the von Neumann equation Eq. (...) from the Schrödinger equation Eq. (...)'*) — so the `_refs/` line count is undefined for this derivation. Apply the *typically 4-7 lines* default from `rules/derivation-quality.md` § Mandatory detail level. Lecture 1 line ≪ 4 default minimum; strict rule violation. Critical unstated step: the conjugate derivative sign flip ($\dot{\hat U}^\dagger = +(\mathrm{i}/\hbar)\hat U^\dagger\hat H$) — the `+` sign vs the `-` sign on $\dot{\hat U}$ is the source of the commutator structure; students taught to take $(\dot{\hat U})^\dagger$ without seeing the daggered form lose the connection between Hermitian conjugation and the sign. Filed as **T-2026-05-19-DV-14** (**HIGH** — strict default-minimum violation + critical missing sign-flip step; scope is single dropdown; fix expands 1 → ~5 lines: (1) starting form, (2) product rule, (3) substitute $\dot{\hat U}$ and $\dot{\hat U}^\dagger$ separately with explicit signs, (4) factor and recognize $\hat U\hat\rho(0)\hat U^\dagger = \hat\rho(t)$, (5) commutator form with explicit $\mathrm{i}\hbar$ on LHS). **(D-3) Derivation: Purity Bounds — Cauchy-Schwarz step skipped; mixed-state cross-term reasoning skipped.** Lecture (post-NB-51 cell-2 pos ~4555, *dropdown information* between `### Purity` and `### Ensemble Ambiguity`) shows **3 conceptual bullet-lines**: (i) pure-state idempotent $\hat\rho^2 = |\psi\rangle\langle\psi|\psi\rangle\langle\psi| = \hat\rho$, (ii) Cauchy-Schwarz lower bound $\sum\lambda_i^2 \geq (\sum\lambda_i)^2/d = 1/d$, (iii) mixed-state strict $\sum\lambda_i^2 < (\sum\lambda_i)^2 = 1$. `_refs/QubitsAndEntanglement.nb` § *Purity* (line 23965) states $\operatorname{Tr}\rho^2 = \sum p_i^2$ as a definitional line then asserts $\operatorname{Tr}\rho^2 \in [0,1]$ *'by construction'* without deriving the lower bound 1/d; the pure/mixed criterion (line 23987) is stated as a piecewise without a strict-inequality derivation. Lecture therefore **goes beyond `_refs/`** in deriving the 1/d lower bound, but in doing so skips two algebra steps a student needs: (a) the Cauchy-Schwarz invocation — the factor of $d$ enters because Cauchy-Schwarz with $a_i = \lambda_i$ and $b_i = 1$ gives $(\sum\lambda_i)^2 = (\sum a_i b_i)^2 \leq (\sum a_i^2)(\sum b_i^2) = d\sum\lambda_i^2$, i.e. $\sum\lambda_i^2 \geq (\sum\lambda_i)^2/d$; the $d$-factor's provenance is opaque without showing $b_i = 1$; (b) the mixed-state strict-inequality reasoning — $(\sum\lambda_i)^2 = \sum\lambda_i^2 + 2\sum_{i<j}\lambda_i\lambda_j$, so if at least two $\lambda_i > 0$ the cross-sum is strictly positive, giving $\sum\lambda_i^2 < (\sum\lambda_i)^2 = 1$. Not a strict `_refs/`-alignment violation (the reference doesn't derive these at all), but per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § Anti-patterns *'A prefactor appears without a definition step'* applies to the $1/d$ factor. Filed as **T-2026-05-19-DV-15** (**LOW** — judgment call: lecture is already richer than `_refs/`, fix is expand-by-2-lines to make Cauchy-Schwarz and cross-term steps explicit; can be deferred or executed at designer's discretion). **Passing checks (not filed):** (i) **Scope and pedagogical fit** — all three derivations stay within concepts introduced earlier in the cell: (a) Purity Bounds invokes only $\hat\rho = \sum p_i|\psi_i\rangle\langle\psi_i|$ + idempotency, both established in the preceding `### The Density Matrix` and `### Purity` body sections; (b) Purity from Bloch Vector invokes the Bloch form $\hat\rho = \tfrac12(\hat I + \boldsymbol r\cdot\hat{\boldsymbol\sigma})$ established in the immediately-preceding `### The Bloch Ball` `important` box and the Pauli identity $(\boldsymbol r\cdot\hat{\boldsymbol\sigma})^2 = |\boldsymbol r|^2\hat I$ (a standard Ch 1 / Ch 4 result, named in the dropdown lead-in); (c) Von Neumann Equation invokes only $\hat\rho(t) = \hat U(t)\hat\rho(0)\hat U^\dagger(t)$ and $\dot{\hat U} = -(\mathrm{i}/\hbar)\hat H\hat U$, both stated in the immediately-preceding `### Time Evolution` `important` box. No scope-creep into later sections per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § Scope and pedagogical fit. (ii) **One focus per derivation** — each of the three dropdowns isolates a single point per § Scope and pedagogical fit rule 1. (iii) **Notation** — all three derivations use the correct upright `\mathrm{i}`, `\mathrm{d}`; `\hat` on every operator; bold `\boldsymbol` on vectors; `\operatorname{Tr}` (per the NB-46 closure); `\vert` for ket pipes (per the NB-45 closure); `\hat{\sigma}^i` indexed component clarified in the Bloch box (per the NB-51 closure). No notation drift inside the derivations. (iv) **Reverse-engineering check** — none of the three derivations exhibit reverse-engineering from the answer; all proceed forward (premises → algebra → result). (v) **"It can be shown that" check** — no banned hand-waves in any derivation. Validator pre-check clean (`safe_edit.py validate` and `validate_project.py --scope 5-2-3-applications`). **Three derivations PASS Phase D:** (1) *Derivation: phase cancellation in the first-order amplitude* (cell 2 § Transition Probability) — three displayed lines (spectral form → collect phases → factor) match `_refs/` lines 2090–2107 structurally and exceed in pedagogical narration; (2) *Derivation: exponential to sinc-squared* (cell 2 § Fermi's Golden Rule) — single-line chain $(\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}2\alpha t}-1)/(2\mathrm{i}\alpha)=\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}\alpha t}\sin(\alpha t)/\alpha$ + $|\cdot|^2$ matches `_refs/` lines 2144–2161 (which collapses the same algebra into one line); (3) *Derivation: Lorentzian from the ramp integral* (cell 2 § Adiabatic Process) — two displayed lines (integral → modulus) **exceed** `_refs/` lines 2249–2256 in depth (the reference jumps directly to the final Lorentzian). **One derivation NEEDS WORK** — *Derivation: Kubo formula* (cell 2 § Kubo Formula): two chain-consistency issues identified, both filed as **new tickets** per `.claude/README.md` § 1.4 (one ticket per session, audit drops findings, do not fix in same run): **T-2026-05-19-DV-11** — EM-field / vector-potential sign inconsistency in the `\begin{split}` opener before the dropdown: the three stated relations $\boldsymbol{E}=\boldsymbol{E}\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}\omega t}$, $\delta\boldsymbol{A}=(\mathrm{i}\boldsymbol{E}/(\omega+\mathrm{i}0^+))\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}\omega t}$, and $\boldsymbol{E}=-\partial_t\delta\boldsymbol{A}$ are not simultaneously satisfiable — computing $-\partial_t\delta\boldsymbol{A}$ with the stated $\delta\boldsymbol{A}$ gives $-\boldsymbol{E}\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}\omega t}$, not $+\boldsymbol{E}\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}\omega t}$. **T-2026-05-19-DV-12** — Step 4 matrix-element shortcut $\hat{j}_a^{\mathcal{I}}(\tau)|\gamma\rangle=\mathrm{e}^{+\mathrm{i}\omega_{\gamma\alpha}\tau}\hat{j}_a|\gamma\rangle$ is (i) not a valid operator equation and (ii) has the wrong-sign exponential — correct shortcut is $\langle\alpha|\hat{j}_a^{\mathcal{I}}(\tau)|\gamma\rangle=\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}\omega_{\gamma\alpha}\tau}\,j^a_{\alpha\gamma}$ under the lecture's own definition $\omega_{\gamma\alpha}=(E_\gamma-E_\alpha)/\hbar$; the sign error propagates to the displayed commutator expansion (two exponential signs need flipping). **Audit note:** both errors **cancel out** in the boxed `Kubo formula (zero temperature)` result Eq. {eq}`eq-kubo-hall-523` — DV-11's wrong-sign $\delta\boldsymbol{A}$ flips $\delta\hat{H}$, which is then re-inserted with the same sign into Step 3 so $\sigma_{ab}$ comes out unchanged; DV-12's swapped exponentials get re-swapped by the dummy index $\gamma\leftrightarrow\alpha$ inside the antisymmetric Hall combination. These are **chain-consistency / pedagogical-clarity fixes**, not result fixes — the final boxed $\sigma_{xy}$ and the integer Hall coda $\sigma_{xy}=\nu e^2/h$ are independently correct. Per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Mandatory procedure / Student-confusion test*, the chain must still be repaired because a careful student verifying any displayed line will see the inconsistency. **Phase Cursor unchanged** — stays at **Round 1 / Phase D (Derivation quality) / `5-2-3-applications`** per `.claude/README.md` § 1.2 + § 1.3 (Phase D advances only when all Phase D tickets on this notebook close). Step (a) inserts the **Hermitian conjugate of the $\hat{U}_0$ Schrödinger equation** as its own displayed block — *"Taking the Hermitian conjugate of $\mathrm{i}\hbar\partial_t\hat{U}_0=\hat{H}_0\hat{U}_0$ (Eq. `eq-5-2-1-U0`) and using $\hat{H}_0^{\dagger}=\hat{H}_0$ gives the equation for $\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}$"* followed by $\mathrm{i}\hbar\partial_t\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}=-\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{H}_0$ — closing the audit's flagged implicit-dagger gap (the previous prose cited only the un-daggered $\hat{U}_0$ equation while the chain immediately used $-\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{H}_0\hat{U}$). Step (b) expands the previously-2-equality jump $=\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{V}\hat{U}=\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}\hat{U}_{\mathcal{I}}$ into a 5-line `\begin{split}...\end{split}` stack threading the explicit identity insertion $\hat{U}_0\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}=\hat{I}$: $\mathrm{i}\hbar\partial_t\hat{U}_{\mathcal{I}}=-\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{H}_0\hat{U}+\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}(\hat{H}_0+\hat{V})\hat{U}=\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{V}\hat{U}=\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{V}\,\hat{U}_0\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\,\hat{U}=(\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{V}\hat{U}_0)(\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{U})=\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}\hat{U}_{\mathcal{I}}$, with a closing prose line crediting the identity-insertion step and citing the existing definition labels `eq-5-2-1-def-VI` and `eq-5-2-1-def-UI`. VERIFY-FIRST: pre-edit OLD anchor count = 1 in cell 2, 0 in cells 0/1/3; post-edit OLD count = 0, NEW count = 1. (Ch 4–6 Round 1 — Phase D (Derivation quality) audit on `5-2-1-interaction-picture` 2026-05-17 **complete**: read-only structural diff of cell 2's three `dropdown information` derivations in `notes_src/ch5_perturbation-theory/5-2-1-interaction-picture.ipynb` against `_refs/PerturbationTheory.md` lines 1680-1797 (§ *Interaction Picture*) per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § Mandatory procedure (read `_refs/` first → outline chain → structural diff afterward) and § Mandatory detail level: match `_refs/` (designer role per `.claude/README.md` § 2.1 — no edits, no validators). **Pass summary**: all three derivations use correct admonition class (`dropdown information`) per `rules/content-style.md` § Admonition Usage; all operators carry `\hat{}` per `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Vectors and Operators; `\mathrm{i}` / `\mathrm{e}` / `\hbar` upright and consistent throughout; no `\quad` / `\qquad` side-by-side layouts (block-stacked via `\begin{split}` per § Display Math, NB-39 already closed); no `\vert[A-Za-z0-9]` glued-pipe sites; no banned phrases (no *\"It can be shown that\"*, *\"By the Heisenberg equation\"* without context, etc.); all cross-references (`{eq}`eq-5-2-1-def-psi-I``, `{eq}`eq-5-2-1-schrodinger-state``) resolve to equations defined upstream in the same cell. **Findings** (single-scope tickets filed below — to be closed before Phase D wraps on this stem and the cursor advances to the next ch4–6 target): (α) cell 2 *Derivation: state equation in the interaction picture* (`dropdown information`) is compressed below the reference detail level — lecture shows **4 displayed `$$...$$` blocks** but `_refs/PerturbationTheory.md` lines 1717-1732 shows the analogous derivation as **7 displayed `$$...$$` blocks**. The lecture's third step (\"Use $\mathrm{i}\hbar\partial_t\hat{U}_0=\hat{H}_0\hat{U}_0$ and Eq. {eq}`eq-5-2-1-schrodinger-state`; the $\hat{H}_0$ terms cancel, giving\") collapses three reference sub-steps into one prose sentence — (a) multiplying through by $\mathrm{i}\hbar$, (b) substituting both Schrödinger equations explicitly so two $\hat{H}_0\hat{U}_0\vert\psi\rangle_{\mathcal{I}}$ terms are visible, (c) cancelling those terms — and the final \"multiply by $\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}$\" step hides the implicit $\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{U}_0=\hat{I}$ identity. Per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § Mandatory detail level (*\"Each commutator evaluation or algebraic derivation in a dropdown must contain at least as many displayed intermediate lines as the corresponding passage in `_refs/`\"*) and § Root causes #1 (Skipped intermediate steps), the missing intermediate lines are required → ticket **T-2026-05-17-DD-01** (severity: highest of the three findings, 3-line expansion). (β) cell 2 *Derivation: operator equation for $\hat{U}_{\mathcal{I}}$* (`dropdown information`) skips two implicit identity-evaluation steps that `rules/derivation-quality.md` § Mandatory procedure step 4 (*\"Show every commutator / identity evaluation\"*) requires explicit: (i) the Hermitian conjugate $\mathrm{i}\hbar\partial_t\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}=-\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{H}_0$ obtained by daggering $\mathrm{i}\hbar\partial_t\hat{U}_0=\hat{H}_0\hat{U}_0$ (cited but never displayed; the substituted $-\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{H}_0\hat{U}$ term appears with no derivation); (ii) the identity-insertion $\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{V}\hat{U}=\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{V}\hat{U}_0\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{U}=\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}\hat{U}_{\mathcal{I}}$ that bridges the bare $\hat{V}$ chain to the interaction-picture quantities (the lecture writes \"$=\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}\hat{V}\hat{U}=\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}\hat{U}_{\mathcal{I}}$\" with no visible insertion step). `_refs/` line 1772-1783 does not derive this operator equation explicitly (defers to the state-equation chain), so there is no `_refs/` line-count anchor — but `rules/derivation-quality.md` § Anti-patterns (*\"A prefactor / definition step missing\"*) flags the implicit dagger and identity-insertion independently → ticket **T-2026-05-17-DD-02** (severity: middle, 2-line addition). (γ) cell 2 *Derivation: matrix elements and Bohr frequencies* (`dropdown information`) middle displayed block collapses the eigenstate-action step — lecture jumps from $\langle m\vert\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}(t)\hat{V}(t)\hat{U}_0(t)\vert n\rangle$ directly to $\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}(E_m-E_n)t/\hbar}V_{mn}(t)$ in one line, skipping the natural intermediate $\mathrm{e}^{+\mathrm{i}E_m t/\hbar}\langle m\vert\hat{V}(t)\vert n\rangle\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}E_n t/\hbar}$ that exhibits the eigenstate-action $\langle m\vert\hat{U}_0^{\dagger}=\langle m\vert\mathrm{e}^{+\mathrm{i}E_m t/\hbar}$ and $\hat{U}_0\vert n\rangle=\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}E_n t/\hbar}\vert n\rangle$. The eigenstate-action is the conceptual mechanism by which Bohr frequencies emerge — skipping it loses the pedagogical payoff of having stated the spectral expansions in the dropdown's opening block. `_refs/` lines 1700-1702 does not show this derivation at all, so the lecture is more detailed than `_refs/` globally — but within the lecture's own middle block the 2-line jump still violates `rules/derivation-quality.md` § Mandatory procedure step 4 → ticket **T-2026-05-17-DD-03** (severity: lowest, 1-line insertion). **Audit observations** (not filed): the dropdown opener \"Using\" + spectral-expansion split block at the top of D1 is well-presented; the prose connector \"the $\hat{H}_0$ terms cancel\" in D2 step 3 explains *why* the cancellation occurs (preserves physical intuition) — the issue is the missing displayed math, not the prose framing; D3's final $\hat{V}_{\mathcal{I}}\hat{U}_{\mathcal{I}}$ identification is correct but compressed. No derivation imports concepts from later sections (e.g., none of the three blocks names objects introduced only after §5.2.1 — Dyson series, Green's functions, etc. — per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § Scope and pedagogical fit). **Phase Cursor stays at Round 1 / Phase D (Derivation quality) / `5-2-1-interaction-picture`** per `.claude/README.md` § 1.4 — phase-audit pass advanced C → D 2026-05-17 with the close of HD-04; this audit fills the queue with three Phase D fix tickets which must close before the cursor advances to the next ch4–6 target (next depth-first in `notes_src/_toc.yml` after `5-2-1-interaction-picture` = `5-2-2-dyson-series` at Phase A). Precedent for cursor-stays-on-findings: 2026-05-17 Phase C audit on the same notebook filed 4 single-scope tickets (NB-40 / NB-41 / SD-06 / HD-04) and stayed at C until all 4 closed earlier today; 2026-05-17 Phase D audit on `5-1-3-degenerate-perturbation-theory` filed 4 single-scope tickets (DV-17 / DV-18 / DV-19 / DV-20) and stayed at D until all 4 closed (precedent for Phase D pass discipline). Reader: manager (orchestrator) — no `*-designer` invoked during the read-only audit per `.claude/README.md` § 2.1 (designers audit and propose; only `notebook-writer` edits); tickets routed at fix time: DD-01 / DD-02 / DD-03 → `phys130b-derivation-designer` → `phys130b-notebook-writer` per § 2.3 (designer drafts the expansion / insertion / addition; writer applies via single atomic `text.replace` and runs validators per `rules/validation.md`).)
