first-order energy matrix derivative is a trivial 2-line corollary (reference does not develop separately, acceptable as a decorative dropdown); second-order energy matrix derivative is *more* developed than reference (reference just states the result lines 1153–1155); first-order state derivative is *more* developed than reference (reference just states result lines 1156–1158); Example 1 problem setup and Step 1 first-order block check match reference Steps (i)/(ii) cleanly; Example 2 problem setup, operator matrices, and degenerate-manifold table match reference one-for-one. **FINDINGS** (single-scope tickets filed below — Phase D queue grows from 0 to 4 open on this notebook): (α) cell 2 *Derivation: degenerate Hellmann-Feynman identities* Step 3 ends with the m≠n identity `⟨m,α∣∂_λ n,β⟩=V_{mα,nβ}/(E_n−E_m)` but never derives or states the conjugate identity `⟨∂_λ m,α∣n,β⟩=V_{mα,nβ}/(E_m−E_n)` that the subsequent *Derivation: second-order energy matrix derivative* explicitly invokes (*"use the second Hellmann-Feynman identity ... and its conjugate relation"*). Reference (`_refs/PerturbationTheory.md` lines 1135–1143) gives both identities side-by-side in one display block. Per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § Root causes #1 (Skipped intermediate steps) and § Mandatory procedure step 4 (*"show every commutator/identity evaluation"*), the conjugate is a building block invoked downstream and must be either stated or derived alongside the primary identity → ticket **T-2026-05-17-DV-17**; (β) cell 2 *Example: Three-level bright/dark mechanism* Step 4 (*Exact zero-mode check*) collapses to two single-line claims `H_0|D⟩=0` (with brief justification *"no |3⟩ component"*) and `V|D⟩=0` (no further derivation), but the second claim requires the explicit `(λ_1^* λ_2^* − λ_2^* λ_1^*)=0` cancellation that the reference (`_refs/PerturbationTheory.md` lines 1289–1317) develops over ~5 displayed lines (write V as a sum of outer products → act on the |D⟩ form → first term vanishes because ⟨3|D⟩=0 → second term is (1/√(|λ_1|²+|λ_2|²))|3⟩(λ_1^*λ_2^*−λ_2^*λ_1^*) → bracket cancels). Per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § Mandatory detail level (4–7 displayed lines per algebraic evaluation matching `_refs/`) and § Anti-patterns, this is the canonical skipped-steps violation → ticket **T-2026-05-17-DV-18**; (γ) cell 2 *Example: Spin-1 splitting from a degenerate manifold* Step 1 (*degenerate PT: construct effective blocks*) jumps directly to the 2×2 matrix form of `H^eff_1` without showing how the entries `1±λ+λ²/2` and `λ²/2` are built from V matrix elements (`V_{+1,+1}=1`, `V_{−1,−1}=−1`, `V_{+1,0}=V_{0,+1}=V_{−1,0}=V_{0,−1}=1/√2`, etc.) plugged into the `important` *Energy matrix expansion up to second order* formula. Reference (`_refs/PerturbationTheory.md` lines 1400–1416) shows the full bra-ket-product chain `H^eff_1 = |+1⟩′(E_1 + V_{+1,+1}λ + V_{+1,0}V_{0,+1}/(E_1−E_0)λ²)⟨+1|′ + ...` for the 4 entries before collapsing to the matrix → ticket **T-2026-05-17-DV-19**; (δ) cell 2 same example "representative first-order state structure" `|±1⟩′ = |±1⟩ + (λ/√2)|0⟩` and `|0⟩′ = |0⟩ − (λ/√2)(|+1⟩+|−1⟩)` is stated without showing the substitution `|±1⟩′ = |±1⟩ + |0⟩ V_{0,±1}/(E_1−E_0)λ + ... = |±1⟩ + (λ/√2)|0⟩ + ...` from the *State expansion and first-order mixing* `important` box. Reference (`_refs/PerturbationTheory.md` lines 1386–1394) shows the explicit substitution → ticket **T-2026-05-17-DV-20**. **Borderline / observations NOT filed**: (ε) the *Derivation: degenerate Hellmann-Feynman identities* Step 2 collapses `Σ_α [...] δ_{mn}δ_{γα}` to a single non-summed term silently (the orthonormality reduction is one step the reference shows explicitly in lines 1107–1122); the lecture's combined form is mathematically transparent and a strict structural-diff would file this, but the depth is acceptable for a corollary step inside a longer derivation — borderline, not filed; (ζ) the *Derivation: second-order energy matrix derivative* opening differentiates `∂_λ E = ⟨n,α|V|n,β⟩` once more without explicitly noting `∂_λ V = 0` for the linear ansatz `H(λ) = H_0 + λV` — same exact pattern as the now-closed T-2026-05-16-DV-16 on §5.1.2 (route (i) was to extend the connector to read *"Differentiate once more; since V has no λ dependence in the linear ansatz ... only the bra and ket are differentiated:"*). Could be a parallel ticket on §5.1.3, but borderline given the §5.1.2 fix already establishes the pattern — left as an observation; (η) the m=n "in-manifold overlaps A_{γβ} ... gauge/basis-choice dependent ... can be set to zero by parallel-transport gauge after fixing the first-order in-manifold basis" justification appears in both *Derivation: second-order energy matrix derivative* and *Derivation: first-order state derivative* without the term *"parallel-transport gauge"* being defined anywhere in the body — borderline § Scope and pedagogical fit per `rules/derivation-quality.md`; the phrasing is explanatory rather than instrumental (the reader does not need to apply the gauge themselves), so this is left as an observation rather than a ticket. Executed via `phys130b-derivation-designer` → `phys130b-notebook-writer` handoff (`.claude/README.md` § 2.3); designer-preferred **route (a)** (show the algebra inline) with the **optional companion** also taken per the ticket precedent (T-2026-05-11-DV-04 on `4-3-1-cyclotron-motion` bundled its optional companion). **(a) Required: BAC-CAB inversion in *Derivation: kinematics of the spin axis*** — the previous closing text *"With this transverse choice, $\boldsymbol\Omega=\boldsymbol n\times\dot{\boldsymbol n}.$ (eq-Omega-from-n)"* (a 0-step assertion that violated `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Mandatory detail level* "a 2-line derivation that skips from the definition to the answer is a rule violation unless the result genuinely follows in one step of trivial algebra" — BAC-CAB is **not** trivial algebra, and HW Problem 1(b) explicitly asks students to do the same chain) was replaced with: introductory prose naming the BAC-CAB identity $\boldsymbol a\times(\boldsymbol b\times\boldsymbol c)=\boldsymbol b\,(\boldsymbol a\cdot\boldsymbol c)-\boldsymbol c\,(\boldsymbol a\cdot\boldsymbol b)$ and citing `{eq}`eq-axis-kinematics``; a 3-equality `\begin{split}` block showing (line 1) $\boldsymbol n\times\dot{\boldsymbol n} = \boldsymbol n\times(\boldsymbol\Omega\times\boldsymbol n)$ substitution from `eq-axis-kinematics`, (line 2) $= \boldsymbol\Omega\,(\boldsymbol n\cdot\boldsymbol n) - \boldsymbol n\,(\boldsymbol n\cdot\boldsymbol\Omega)$ BAC-CAB expansion, (line 3) $= \boldsymbol\Omega$ collapse using the scalar conditions; closing prose naming $\boldsymbol n\cdot\boldsymbol n=1$ and $\boldsymbol n\cdot\boldsymbol\Omega=0$; final boxed result $\boldsymbol\Omega=\boldsymbol n\times\dot{\boldsymbol n}$ with `(eq-Omega-from-n)` label preserved verbatim. Structurally diffs to `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` lines 3961–3972: `_refs/` presents the chain as a single inline equality with the scalar-condition rationale in trailing prose (3 equalities + prose); the lecture rendering uses a 4-line `\begin{split}` block (3 equalities visually stacked on separate lines with surrounding prose), which preserves `_refs/` step count while adopting the notebook's `\begin{split}` convention per `rules/physics-conventions.md` § Display Math. **(b) Optional companion taken: 3-line expansion of *Check: curl of the spin-sphere potential*** — the previous 2-line compression *"Using $A_\varphi=S(\cos\theta-1)/\sin\theta$, $(1/\sin\theta)\partial_\theta[S(\cos\theta-1)]=-S$"* was expanded into a 3-line `\begin{split}` block matching `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` lines 4666–4673: (line 1) substitution $A_\varphi\sin\theta = S(\cos\theta-1)$, (line 2) elementary derivative $\partial_\theta[S(\cos\theta-1)] = -S\sin\theta$, (line 3) division $(1/\sin\theta)\partial_\theta(A_\varphi\sin\theta) = -S$; closing prose $\nabla_\perp\times\boldsymbol A=-S\boldsymbol n$ unchanged. Both algebraic moves remain individually trivial, but the slicing makes each step visible at the same granularity as `_refs/`. **Scope discipline** per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Scope and pedagogical fit* and `rules/maintenance-scope.md`: edits stayed strictly inside the two existing `:::{dropdown information}` blocks — no scope creep into representation theory, Berry-phase quantization, or monopole-harmonic content; no touches to cell 1 Prompts, cell 3 Homework, the *Spin-monopole dictionary* `important` box, the *Spin quantization as monopole flux quantization* `important` box, the *Equation of Motion for the Spin Axis* boxed `eq-spin-axis-dynamics`, the Lorentz-analog box, the *Derivation: spin-axis equation* dropdown (passed Phase D audit verbatim, Finding 2), the Summary, or the See Also; the abstract spin-sphere $\boldsymbol B(\boldsymbol n) = (g/(4\pi))\boldsymbol n$ convention (intentional scope discipline per Phase B audit) not touched. **Implementation discipline** per `rules/notebook-editing.md`: Bash + Python `json.load` / `json.dump(indent=1, ensure_ascii=False)`; two atomic `text.replace` calls on raw-string `r'...'` blocks (required since the targets contain `\b` near `\boldsymbol`, `\d` near `\dot`, `\O` near `\Omega`, `\t` near `\theta`, `\v` near `\varphi` — all JSON control-escape hazards per `rules/notebook-editing.md` § *LaTeX in JSON*); `assert text.count(old) == 1` guard before each replacement; post-write reload verified the new content is present once via `text.count(r"BAC-CAB identity") == 1`, `text.count(r"= \boldsymbol\Omega\,(\boldsymbol n\cdot\boldsymbol n)") == 1`, and `text.count(r"A_\varphi\sin\theta &= S(\cos\theta-1)") == 1`. | 2026-05-12 | `4-3-3-quantum-hall-effect` | Phase D (Derivation quality) audit — read-only chain-quality sweep of the two derivation chains in cell 2 of `notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-3-3-quantum-hall-effect.ipynb` per `rules/derivation-quality.md` (§ *Root causes*, § *Mandatory procedure*, § *Minimum detail level*, § *Anti-patterns*) and per the `phys130b-derivation-designer` skill. `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` § *Quantum Hall Effect* (lines ~3030–3828) carries parallel material for both chains. **Finding 1 — *Argument 1: Laughlin's Charge Pumping* (body prose, Steps 1–4): PASS.** Forward chain, single focus, no skipped commutators, signs consistent throughout. Borderline: Step 1's $r_m^2(t)$ algebraic solution is a single-line jump (`_refs/` lines 3174–3180 shows 3 substitutions) — not separately ticket-worthy; bundled as optional companion. **Finding 2 — *Derivation: Kubo evaluation on Landau-level eigenstates* (`:::{dropdown information}` block): NEEDS WORK.** Step 5 first equation $(q/m)^2 q\hbar B/(\hbar\omega_c)^2 = q/\hbar$ is wrong; correct value is $q/(\hbar B)$ — missing a factor of $1/B$. With $\omega_c = qB/m$: $(\hbar\omega_c)^2 = \hbar^2 q^2 B^2/m^2$ and $(q/m)^2 q\hbar B = q^3\hbar B/m^2$, ratio $= q/(\hbar B)$. Downstream final result $|\sigma_{xy}| = (q^2/h)(N_{\mathrm{occ}}/N_\Phi)$ is nevertheless correct (verified by direct algebra: $\sigma_{xy} = -qN_{\mathrm{occ}}/(AB) = -q^2 N_{\mathrm{occ}}/(hN_\Phi)$ using $N_\Phi = qBA/h$), but the published Step 5 decomposition does not compose to it — composing the wrong $q/\hbar$ with the correct $1/A = qB/(hN_\Phi)$ leaves a stray factor of $B$. Classic *reverse-engineering from the answer* pattern per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Root causes* #5. The `$\mathrm{i}^2 = -1$ absorbs into the carrier-sign convention` sentence is a secondary paper-over (separate sign-discipline issue, out of scope). Filed as **T-2026-05-12-DV-06** in the queue. Designer recommendation: route (a) minimal typo fix `q/\hbar` → `q/(\hbar B)`, with route (b) cleaner Step 5 forward composition as alternative. Optional companion bundled: expand *Argument 1* Step 1 algebra to 3 lines matching `_refs/`. **Other Kubo-derivation items confirmed NOT separately ticket-worthy** (one ticket, one focus): (i) Step 4 *"blocked pairs cancel pairwise"* argument verified correct; (ii) Step 2 antisymmetric current product correct including *"swap = c.c."* via Hermiticity; (iii) Step 1 matrix elements match `_refs/` lines 3522–3534. **Scope-expansion candidates intentionally NOT bundled** per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Scope and pedagogical fit*: (a) *Kubo formula* `important` box (derivation lives in §5.2.3 per explicit `{eq}` ref); (b) Argument 1 single-valuedness condition (heuristic / semi-classical, consistent with body framing); (c) Argument 1 → Argument 2 transition is interpretive prose, not a chain. **Cursor stays at D / `4-3-3-quantum-hall-effect`** with 1 open D ticket (T-2026-05-12-DV-06) per `.claude/README.md` § 1.3 and § 1.4 — advances to the next Ch 4 subsection at Phase A only after T-DV-06 closes, per the precedent of `4-3-2-landau-quantization` Phase D 2026-05-12. **Next session**: execute T-DV-06 via `phys130b-derivation-designer` → `phys130b-notebook-writer` handoff per `.claude/README.md` § 2.3 (one-ticket-per-session per § 1.4). Reviewed the **two** `:::{dropdown information}` derivation blocks in cell 2 — *Derivation: Cyclotron Frequency and Radius* (in § *Classical baseline: cyclotron motion*) and *Derivation: from force balance to $\rho_{xy}^{\mathrm{cl}}(B)$* (in § *Classical Hall effect and its expectation*) — against `rules/derivation-quality.md` and against `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` § *Cyclotron Motion* (lines ~1595–1764) and § *Hall Effect* (lines ~1766–1893), which both carry parallel material; structural-diff procedure applied per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Mandatory procedure*. **Finding 1 — Cyclotron Frequency and Radius derivation: PASS.** Forward-motivated chain (Lorentz EOM → component form → SHO equation → quote general solution with $\varphi_0$ → integrate to circular trajectory → define $r_c$), depth roughly matches `_refs/` line counts, no skipped commutators, single focus, no scope creep into guiding-center-operator or Landau-level content. Minor borderline observation (not a separate ticket; bundled as an optional companion below): notebook quotes both $v_x(t)$ and $v_y(t)$ together as the SHO general solution, whereas `_refs/` derives $v_y$ from $v_x$ via the first-order relation $\dot{y} = (1/\omega_c)\,\mathrm{d}\dot{x}/\mathrm{d}t$ — the pedagogically tighter route makes clear $v_y$ is **forced** to be $-v_\perp\sin$, not an independent SHO mode. **Finding 2 — Hall force-balance derivation: NEEDS WORK.** Sign bug. Displayed equation `qE_y + qv_xB = 0` implies $E_y = -v_xB$, but the Lorentz cross-product $\boldsymbol{v}\times\boldsymbol{B}$ for $\boldsymbol{v} = v_x\hat{x}$, $\boldsymbol{B} = B\hat{z}$ is $-v_xB\hat{y}$ (since $\hat{x}\times\hat{z} = -\hat{y}$), so the correct force balance is `qE_y - qv_xB = 0` giving $E_y = +v_xB$. **The notebook's own *Cyclotron Frequency and Radius* derivation has the same cross-product correctly** (`m\dot{v}_y = -qBv_x`), so the Hall block is internally inconsistent with its own cyclotron block. The downstream paper-over $\rho_{xy}^{\mathrm{cl}} \equiv |E_y|/j_x = B/(nq)$ masks the sign error but adds a second issue: for electrons $q = -e < 0$, the signed $B/(nq) < 0$ while $|E_y|/j_x \geq 0$ — contradiction. The body's *Classical Hall response* `important` box $\rho_{xy}^{\mathrm{cl}}(B) = B/(nq)$ is signed (no absolute value). Cleanest fix is route (a): correct the sign in the force-balance display, drop the absolute value, write $\rho_{xy}^{\mathrm{cl}} = E_y/j_x = B/(nq)$ directly. Filed as **T-2026-05-11-DV-04** with route (a) preferred, route (b) declared not preferred, and the cyclotron-companion line listed as optional. Per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Root causes* #3 (*Wrong notation*) and #5 (*Reverse-engineering from the answer* — the `|·|` paper-over is a smoking gun) and § *Anti-patterns*. **Other potential Phase D items confirmed NOT ticket-worthy**: (i) body's *Why low-$T$, high-$B$ changes the game* `attention` block uses $\Delta E = \hbar\omega_c = \hbar qB/m$ with unsigned $q$ — regime argument prose, not a derivation chain; the unsigned-$q$ reading is consistent with the body's generic-$q$ convention. (ii) Integer-quantum-Hall plateau formula $\rho_{xy} = h/(\nu e^2)$ in § *Experimental fact: Hall plateaus* is **stated as an experimental fact**, not derived in this lesson (§4.3.3 is the actual derivation) — no chain to audit here. (iii) Homework derivations (HW1(a), HW3(a–c)) are exercises for students, outside Phase D scope (handled by `homework-designer` if any depth issue arises). (iv) The absence of the guiding-center-operator reconstruction $\boldsymbol{\xi} = \boldsymbol{x} - \hat{z}\times\boldsymbol{\pi}/(qB)$ that `_refs/` shows immediately after the cyclotron derivation is **correct scope discipline** per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Scope and pedagogical fit* — the reconstruction is a Landau-level tool (§4.3.2) and the cyclotron lesson must stay pre-quantum here. **Cursor stays at D / `4-3-1-cyclotron-motion`** (1 open D ticket: T-2026-05-11-DV-04); will advance to next notebook `4-3-2-landau-quantization` at Phase A only after T-DV-04 closes, per `.claude/README.md` § 1.3 and the precedent set by `4-2-3-flux-ring` Phase D 2026-05-11. **Active queue after this session: 1 open ticket** (T-2026-05-11-DV-04). Next session: execute T-DV-04 via `phys130b-derivation-designer` → `phys130b-notebook-writer` handoff per `.claude/README.md` § 2.3, one-ticket-one-session per `.claude/README.md` § 1.4. No notebook files modified this session. Designer: `phys130b-derivation-designer` (audit pass — no edits, no validator run). | D |

Rationale: (1) the existing HW3 sub-parts (a)–(c) do not actually exercise sign-flipping (they compute resistivity-tensor inversion and discuss $\rho_{xx}$ vs. $\tau$ dependence) — they would gain nothing from the explicit electron specialization; (2) HW1 in the same notebook already uses generic charge $q$ throughout its cyclotron-drift problem, so option (ii) keeps HW1 and HW3 conventionally aligned; (3) the body lecture's cyclotron-frequency `important` box ($\omega_c = qB/m$, $r_c = mv_\perp/(qB)$), Lorentz EOM ($m\dot{\boldsymbol{v}} = q\,\boldsymbol{v}\times\boldsymbol{B}$), and classical-Hall `important` box ($\rho_{xy}^{\mathrm{cl}}(B) = B/(nq)$) all use generic $q$ — option (ii) reproduces those expressions exactly in HW3, eliminating the silent body→HW switch the ticket flagged. **Three substitutions in HW3 preamble + (a) + (b)** (no change to titles, sub-part count, or (c) wording per ticket scope discipline): preamble $m\boldsymbol{v}/\tau = -e(\boldsymbol{E} + \boldsymbol{v}\times\boldsymbol{B}) \to m\boldsymbol{v}/\tau = q(\boldsymbol{E} + \boldsymbol{v}\times\boldsymbol{B})$ with new clause *for a carrier of charge $q$*; (a) $\boldsymbol{j} = -ne\boldsymbol{v} \to \boldsymbol{j} = nq\boldsymbol{v}$; (b) $\rho_{xy} = B/(ne) \to \rho_{xy} = B/(nq)$ (now matches the body's classical-Hall box verbatim). **Note on the body's forward sentence** *"sign conventions explored in homework"* (end of *Derivation: from force balance to $\rho_{xy}^{\mathrm{cl}}(B)$*): with this edit, HW3 no longer specializes to electrons, so that sentence becomes slightly orphaned — but the body sentence is not strictly inaccurate (HW1 implicitly explores sign via the charge-independent drift velocity $\boldsymbol{v}_d = \boldsymbol{E}\times\boldsymbol{B}/B^2$, and HW3's $\rho_{xy} = B/(nq)$ result does carry the sign information). Added a parenthetical clause matching the Translation block's wording (line 217 of the pre-edit cell). **(b) `\hat{U}_{LG}` gauge-function motivation** — replaced the bare operational definition with a four-paragraph block that (i) recalls the §4.1.3 gauge-transformation rule $\boldsymbol{A}\to\boldsymbol{A}+\nabla\alpha$, $\psi\to\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}q\alpha/\hbar}\psi$ via `[§4.1.3](4-1-3-gauge-invariance)`, (ii) introduces the *winding* gauge function $\alpha(\theta) = -\hbar\theta/q$, (iii) displays a `\begin{split}` recovering $\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}q\alpha/\hbar} = \mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}\theta}$ and computing $\oint\nabla\alpha\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l} = -2\pi\hbar/q = -\Phi_0$ (using $\Phi_0 = 2\pi\hbar/q$), (iv) explains the *large* vs *small* distinction in terms of multi-valued $\alpha$ vs single-valued multiplier, then introduces $\hat{U}_{LG}$ as a named rephasing operator and keeps the original operational $\hat{U}_{LG}\psi_n(\theta) = \psi_{n-1}(\theta)$ split block plus the Hamiltonian-shift argument. **Scope discipline** per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Scope and pedagogical fit*: both fixes stayed strictly inside the existing dropdown; no covariant-derivative machinery or large-gauge Wu–Yang content from §4.4 imported; the derivation's single focus on *symmetry algebra of the half-flux doublet* is unchanged. **Edit method** per `rules/notebook-editing.md` / `rules/tooling-security.md`: Bash + Python `json.load`/`json.dump(indent=1, ensure_ascii=False)` with `text.replace(old, new, 1)` on the two scoped blocks (each verified to occur exactly once with `assert old in text`); `text_to_source` helper for `\n`-terminated source-list reconstruction. Six sub-fixes applied per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Root causes* (#1 skipped intermediate steps, #4 missing motivation, #5 reverse-engineering from the answer) and § *Mandatory procedure* (steps 4 *show every evaluation*, 5 *state building-block results first*, 6 *explain where each term comes from*). **(a) Tunneling Hamiltonian motivation** — added one preamble paragraph before the $2\times 2$ Schrödinger matrix stating (i) Cooper-pair charge is $2e$ with $e>0$, (ii) voltage sign convention (L higher than R when $V>0$), (iii) the symmetric $\pm eV$ shift = half pair-charge times the potential drop on each side, (iv) the WLOG choice of $K\in\mathbb{R}_{>0}$ via absorption of $\arg K$ into $\theta_{L,R}$. **(b) Product+chain rule** — inserted a new `$$` block displaying $\mathrm{d}/\mathrm{d}t(\sqrt{n_L}\,\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}\theta_L}) = (\dot{n}_L/(2\sqrt{n_L}) + \mathrm{i}\dot\theta_L\sqrt{n_L})\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}\theta_L}$ between the substitution and the post-multiplication line, with prose citing the product rule and $\mathrm{d}\sqrt{n_L}/\mathrm{d}t = \dot{n}_L/(2\sqrt{n_L})$. **(c) Real/imag split justification** — added one sentence noting that $n_{L,R}$, $\theta_{L,R}$, and their time derivatives are all real, so LHS imaginary part is $\hbar\dot{n}_L/(2\sqrt{n_L})$ and real part is $-\hbar\dot\theta_L\sqrt{n_L}$; RHS split via $\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}\delta}=\cos\delta+\mathrm{i}\sin\delta$. **(d) Supercurrent ↔ pair-transfer rate** — replaced the elliptical prose "supercurrent is proportional to the rate of pair transfer, giving $I_c \propto K\sqrt{n_L n_R}$" with an explicit definition of $I_{\text{super}}$ as the rate of Cooper-pair charge accumulating on the L electrode (positive direction R$\to$L), the relation $I_{\text{super}} = 2e\,\dot{n}_L$ with chosen sign convention stated, and a `\begin{split}` block giving the explicit $I_c = 4eK\sqrt{n_L n_R}/\hbar$. **(e) Bottom row by symmetry** — replaced "the same procedure on the bottom row gives ..." (the *"It can be shown that"* anti-pattern per `derivation-quality.md` § *Anti-patterns*) with a sentence naming the $L\leftrightarrow R$, $V\to-V$, $\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}\delta}\to\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}\delta}$ symmetry on the matrix, then displayed a `\begin{split}` showing both $\dot{n}_R = -\dot{n}_L$ (pair conservation) and $\dot\theta_R$ — so both equations are visible for the $K\cos\delta$ cancellation in $\dot\delta = \dot\theta_R - \dot\theta_L$. **(f) $n_L=n_R$ vs $\dot{n}_{L,R}\approx 0$ as independent assumptions** — rewrote the "$n_L\approx n_R$ (bulk superconductors are large reservoirs ...)" sentence to first state the *equal-density* assumption that makes the $\sqrt{n_R/n_L}$ and $\sqrt{n_L/n_R}$ prefactors both unity (so $K\cos\delta$ cancels in the difference), then state the *large-reservoir* limit $\dot{n}_{L,R}\to 0$ separately as a consistency check that keeps the equal-density condition stable in time. **Scope discipline** per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Scope and pedagogical fit*: edit stayed strictly inside the existing `:::{dropdown information}` block; no new sections, no fluxoid quantization (handled downstream in *Two-junction interference*), no Cooper-pair wavefunction derivation from BCS imported. **Edit method** per `rules/notebook-editing.md` / `rules/tooling-security.md`: Bash + Python `json.load`/`json.dump(indent=1, ensure_ascii=False)` with `text.replace(old, new, 1)` on the single dropdown block (verified to occur exactly once; raw-string Python `r'''...'''` for LaTeX); `text_to_source` helper for `\n`-terminated source-list reconstruction. | 2026-05-09 | `4-2-2-aharonov-bohm-effect` | Applied ticket T-2026-05-09-DV-03 (Phase D, derivation quality, cell 2 § *Two-Path Interference* opening). Replaced the hand-wave "$\Phi_{\mathrm{path}}[\mathcal{C}] = (q/\hbar)\int_\mathcal{C} \boldsymbol{A}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l}$ from minimal coupling ($\hat{\boldsymbol{p}} \to \hat{\boldsymbol{p}} - q\boldsymbol{A}$)" — flagged as an "it can be shown that"-style jump per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Anti-patterns* — with a referenced opening sentence ("obtained by gauging $\boldsymbol{A}$ away locally with the gauge transformation of [§4.1.3 Gauge Invariance](4-1-3-gauge-invariance) (derivation below)") and a new `:::{admonition} Derivation: Single-path phase from local gauge transformation` `:class: dropdown information` block. **Designer (`derivation-designer`)** read `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` § *Aharonov-Bohm Effect* (lines ~1515–1556) first per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Mandatory procedure*; the reference treats the single-path phase as a known Berry-phase result ($\psi \overset{\mathcal{C}}{\rightarrow} \psi\,\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}\Theta_\mathcal{C}}$ with $\Theta_\mathcal{C} = (q/\hbar)\int_\mathcal{C}\boldsymbol{A}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{x}$) without itself deriving it. An exhaustive scan of `notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-2-1-berry-phase.ipynb` cell 2 found **no** `(eq-...)` labels on the Berry-phase line-integral form $\Phi_{\mathrm{Berry}}(\mathcal{C}) = \int_\mathcal{C}\boldsymbol{A}_n\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{R}$, so option (b) of the ticket (cite §4.2.1 via `{eq}`) was not available. Option (a) chosen: a self-contained dropdown deriving the path phase from the §4.1.3 local gauge transformation in 4 displayed equations / ~6 displayed lines (matches `_refs/derivation-quality.md` § *Mandatory detail level* lower bound for short standalone derivations). Chain: (i) define gauge function $\alpha(\boldsymbol{x}) \equiv -\int_{\boldsymbol{x}_0,\mathcal{C}}^{\boldsymbol{x}} \boldsymbol{A}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l}'$ with $\nabla\alpha = -\boldsymbol{A}$ (curl-free + path-independent in any simply connected $\boldsymbol{B}=0$ sub-region); (ii) `\begin{split}` block applying $\boldsymbol{A}\to\boldsymbol{A}+\nabla\alpha = 0$, $\psi\to\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}q\alpha/\hbar}\psi$ from §4.1.3 and noting $\tilde\psi$ obeys the $\boldsymbol{A}=0$-gauge Schrödinger equation; (iii) one-line inversion $\psi(\boldsymbol{x}) = \mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}q\alpha/\hbar}\tilde\psi = \tilde\psi\,\exp((\mathrm{i}q/\hbar)\int_\mathcal{C}\boldsymbol{A}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l}')$; (iv) read off $\Phi_{\mathrm{path}}[\mathcal{C}] = (q/\hbar)\int_\mathcal{C}\boldsymbol{A}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l}$. Closing **Connection to Berry phase** paragraph identifies the EM connection $q\boldsymbol{A}/\hbar$ with the parameter-space Berry connection $\boldsymbol{A}_n$ of §4.2.1 and forward-points to the AB-loop derivation that follows ($\boldsymbol{B}=0$ region multiply connected ⇒ no global $\boldsymbol{A}=0$ gauge ⇒ inter-path phase mismatch is the AB phase). Notation respects `rules/physics-conventions.md` (Ch 4 EM SI, $\alpha$ for gauge function, vertical-stacked `\begin{split}` for paired relations — no `\quad`/`\qquad`). Pure markdown insertion via Bash + Python `json.load`/`json.dump` (`rules/notebook-editing.md`); cell-2 source-text length 15697 → 18343 (delta +2646). Validators clean (`safe_edit.py validate` → *OK: 1 notebook(s) validated, no issues found*; `validate_project.py --scope 4-2-2-aharonov-bohm-effect` → *All validations passed*). Designer: `derivation-designer` → `notebook-writer`. | D |
| 2026-05-09 | `4-2-2-aharonov-bohm-effect` | Applied ticket T-2026-05-09-DV-02 (Phase D, derivation quality, cell 2 § *Flux Quantization*): (a) inserted an explicit displayed `\begin{split}` chain showing $\boldsymbol{j}_s = (q\vert\Psi\vert^2/m)(\hbar\nabla\Theta - q\boldsymbol{A}) = 0 \Rightarrow \hbar\nabla\Theta - q\boldsymbol{A} = 0 \Rightarrow \nabla\Theta = (q/\hbar)\boldsymbol{A}$ (replacing the prior single-line $\nabla\Theta=(q/\hbar)\boldsymbol{A}$ result, which was asserted without the intermediate algebraic substitution required by `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Mandatory procedure* step 6). (b) added a `:::{admonition} Derivation: Supercurrent for a Cooper-pair condensate` `:class: dropdown information` block deriving the supercurrent from the gauge-coupled probability current. **Designer (`derivation-designer`)** read `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` first per `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Mandatory procedure*; the reference (lines 1452–1572 + 2625–2640) carries only a Berry-phase argument with $q=-e$ / $\phi_0=h/e$ and contains **no** rigid-condensate / Cooper-pair / supercurrent derivation, so the new dropdown is a self-contained polar-substitution chain following the standard $\boldsymbol{j} = (1/m)\mathrm{Re}[\Psi^*\hat{\boldsymbol{\pi}}\Psi]$ recipe. Provenance for the probability-current formula itself: an exhaustive scan of `notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-1-2-electromagnetic-coupling.ipynb` and `notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-1-3-gauge-invariance.ipynb` found **zero** `(eq-...)` labels and no labeled $$ ...$$ display of $\boldsymbol{j}$ — the expression appears only as a single row of a gauge-invariant-quantities table in §4.1.3 cell 2. Without a citable equation upstream, ticket option (b) was chosen: a forward-ready inline derivation block that names §4.1.2 by file-link form `[§4.1.2 Electromagnetic Coupling](4-1-2-electromagnetic-coupling)` as the source of $\hat{\boldsymbol{\pi}} = \hat{\boldsymbol{p}} - q\boldsymbol{A}$, then derives $\boldsymbol{j}$ in 6 displayed lines: (i) state $\boldsymbol{j} = (1/m)\mathrm{Re}[\Psi^*\hat{\boldsymbol{\pi}}\Psi]$ with $\hat{\boldsymbol{\pi}} = -\mathrm{i}\hbar\nabla - q\boldsymbol{A}$ and polar form $\Psi = \vert\Psi\vert\,\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}\Theta}$; (ii) two-line `split` evaluating $\hat{\boldsymbol{\pi}}\Psi = (-\mathrm{i}\hbar\nabla\vert\Psi\vert + \hbar\vert\Psi\vert\nabla\Theta - q\boldsymbol{A}\vert\Psi\vert)\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}\Theta}$ via product rule; (iii) one-line $\Psi^*\hat{\boldsymbol{\pi}}\Psi = -\mathrm{i}\hbar\vert\Psi\vert\nabla\vert\Psi\vert + \vert\Psi\vert^2(\hbar\nabla\Theta - q\boldsymbol{A})$; (iv) prose noting first term imaginary, second real; (v) one-line probability current $\boldsymbol{j} = (\vert\Psi\vert^2/m)(\hbar\nabla\Theta - q\boldsymbol{A})$; (vi) closing prose mapping probability $\boldsymbol{j}$ to charge $\boldsymbol{j}_s = q\boldsymbol{j}$. Total displayed lines: 6, comfortably above the `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Mandatory detail level* 4–7-line bar. **Notation discipline** (`rules/physics-conventions.md`): vectors `\boldsymbol{...}`, operators `\hat{...}`, upright `\mathrm{i,e,d}`, uppercase `\Theta` for macroscopic phase (matches existing § *Flux Quantization* introduction), `\vert\Psi\vert` (no glued letter — `\vert` followed by `\` per `physics-conventions.md` § Ket/Bra), `\Phi` for flux unchanged, no `\chi` introduced (Ch 4 gauge-function reservation respected), `\bigl(...\bigr)` for clarity, no `\quad/\qquad` for side-by-side (the `\Longrightarrow\quad` is intra-line spacing inside one logical equation, not separating equations), no `align`/`aligned`, only `\begin{split}`. **Symbol-collision sweep**: post-edit cell-2 counts — `\Theta` 9 → 17 (new uses inside the dropdown's polar form), `\alpha` 4 → 4 (gauge-function role in § *Gauge Invariance* preserved), `\chi` 0 → 0 (`physics-conventions.md` § Chapter 4 EM Units rule); `\Phi` continues to denote magnetic flux throughout the cell (no scalar-potential collision). **Implementation** (`rules/notebook-editing.md`): single coordinated `json.load` / `json.dump` save via Bash + Python with one unique multi-line anchor (full prose+display block from "Deep inside the superconductor" through the old `$$\nabla\Theta = (q/\hbar)\,\boldsymbol{A}$$` block plus the lead-in "Substituting this into the single-valuedness condition"); anchor verified `count == 1` before substitution; raw strings used so `\nabla`, `\boldsymbol`, `\mathrm`, `\hbar`, `\Phi`, `\Theta`, `\Psi`, `\hat`, `\Longrightarrow`, `\bigl/\bigr`, `\neq` survive Python escapes (`rules/notebook-editing.md` § *LaTeX in JSON*). (b) prose framing the orientation reversal that joins the two segments into the closed loop $\mathcal{C}$; (c) one-line equation for the closed-loop form; (d) prose framing Stokes' theorem and identification of $\boldsymbol{B}=\nabla\times\boldsymbol{A}$; (e) three-line `split` showing $\oint=\int(\nabla\times\boldsymbol{A})\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{S}=\int\boldsymbol{B}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{S}=\Phi$; (f) prose noting that any cap $\mathcal{S}$ must cut the solenoid (so $\Phi=\pi R^2 B$ regardless of cap); (g) one-line final identification $\Delta\Phi_{\mathrm{AB}}=q\Phi/\hbar$; closing prose on fringe shift retained. Total displayed-line count went from 2 → 7, comfortably above the `_refs/` 5-line bar (`rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Mandatory detail level*). **Reorder per anti-pattern fix** (`rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Anti-patterns*: "Reverse-engineering from the answer"): removed the `:::{admonition} Aharonov-Bohm Phase Shift` `:class: important` block from the **end of § *The Aharonov-Bohm Setup*** (where it stated $\Delta\Phi_{\mathrm{AB}}=q\Phi/\hbar$ **before** any derivation), and reinserted it (lightly rewritten, identical formula) **after** the derivation chain in § *Two-Path Interference*. Order is now: derivation → result `important` → `Topological Invariance` `important` → Discussion → Poll → § *Gauge Invariance*. **Topological Invariance** box trimmed to drop its now-redundant Stokes restatement ("By Stokes' theorem, the circulation equals the enclosed flux $\int\boldsymbol{B}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{S}$") since the chain immediately above already establishes that identity; box now focuses purely on shape/winding-number invariance. **Notation discipline** (`rules/physics-conventions.md`): vectors `\boldsymbol{A,l,S,B}`, upright `\mathrm{d}` differentials, uppercase `\Phi` for magnetic flux, `\mathcal{C,S}` for loop and surface, no `\mathbf`, no `\quad`/`\qquad` side-by-side layout (multi-equality kept inside one `\begin{split}` per `physics-conventions.md` § Display Math), no `align`/`aligned` env. | 2026-05-08 | `4-2-2-aharonov-bohm-effect` | T-2026-05-08-NB-04 DONE: cells 2 & 3 — repaired the 5 real-newline corruptions inside inline `$...$` math flagged by the B-audit. Byte-level inspection (`json.load` → `''.join(source)` → char-by-char walk over `$...$` spans) showed two corruption shapes, not one: (i) **`<NL>eq` for `\neq`** (the `\` of `\neq` was *replaced* by the LF, not preceded by it) at cell 2 char-offsets 2223 (`$\boldsymbol{A} \neq 0$` in *AB Setup*) and 10245 (`$\boldsymbol{B} \neq 0$` in the Poll choice (C)); (ii) **bare `<NL>` before intact `\nabla`** at cell 2 offset 4561 (`$\boldsymbol{A} \to \boldsymbol{A} + \nabla\alpha$` in *Gauge Invariance*), cell 3 offset 1582 (HW 3 stem, same gauge-transformation expression), and cell 3 offset 4575 (HW 7(b) Berry-connection definition `$\boldsymbol{A}_\text{Berry} = \mathrm{i}\langle\psi(\boldsymbol{r})\vert\nabla_{\boldsymbol{r}}\vert\psi(\boldsymbol{r})\rangle$`). Shape (i) matches the `rules/notebook-editing.md` § *LaTeX in JSON — escape character hazards* table row `\n → newline (0x0A) → corrupts \nabla, \nu, \neq, …` exactly — Python wrote `"\neq"` without raw-string discipline and the LF survived into the JSON. Shape (ii) is a softer cousin: the `\nabla` backslash survived, but a stray LF remained between the preceding token and the command (likely from a multi-line Python string literal during an earlier edit). Fix: shape (i) → `<NL>eq` → `\neq` (one substitution per occurrence, restoring the backslash); shape (ii) → drop the LF entirely (a space already separates the previous token, so no extra space is needed). Single coordinated `json.load` / `json.dump` save touching cells 2 and 3 only; `text_to_source` applied per `rules/notebook-editing.md`; cell 2 source-text length 11343 → 11344 (+1: net `\neq`+`\neq` adds 2, `+ \nabla` strip removes 1 = +1), cell 3 source-text length 5034 → 5032 (−2: two LFs removed). Post-edit byte sweep `remaining_inline_newlines(text) == 0` for both cells. Render check on the 5 fixed spots confirms intended LaTeX (`yet $\boldsymbol{A} \neq 0$`, `$\boldsymbol{A} \to \boldsymbol{A} + \nabla\alpha$`, `$\boldsymbol{B} \neq 0$`, HW 3 same as cell 2 *Gauge Invariance*, HW 7(b) `$\boldsymbol{A}_\text{Berry} = \mathrm{i}\langle\psi(\boldsymbol{r})\vert\nabla_{\boldsymbol{r}}\vert\psi(\boldsymbol{r})\rangle$` — `\vert\nabla` permitted because `\vert` is followed by `\` not a letter/digit per `physics-conventions.md` § Ket/Bra). **Validation**: `safe_edit.py validate notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-2-2-aharonov-bohm-effect.ipynb` → *OK: 1 notebook(s) validated, no issues found*; `validate_project.py --scope 4-2-2-aharonov-bohm-effect` → *Notebooks checked: 1 / All validations passed*. **Validator-coverage observation** (per ticket request to "note in the fix run whether to extend the check"): `validate_project.py`'s existing `\n`-at-source-array-boundary heuristic (catalogued in `rules/notebook-editing.md` § *LaTeX in JSON*) does **not** catch real LFs that survive `''.join(source)` — i.e. embedded inside a single inline `$...$` span — because the heuristic looks at the source-array split pattern, not at within-text inline-math newlines. A small extension would scan each markdown cell's joined text for `\$[^$]*\n[^$]*\$` (single-`$` regions containing LF) and flag them. Filing as a separate maintenance candidate rather than expanding scope of this fix run; not a new ticket yet (low recurrence rate; cleaner to bundle with any future ch4 audit). | 2026-05-08 | Ch 4 (general + rules) | Professor in-session feedback DONE: gauge / rephasing function symbol must be `\alpha` (per `physics-conventions.md` § Chapter 4 EM Units); `\chi` was being used in five places across Ch 4 as a stand-in. **Rule update (`physics-conventions.md` § Chapter 4 EM Units):** added a new bullet codifying `\alpha` as the universal Ch 4 gauge / rephasing function symbol — EM gauge transformation $\psi\to\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}q\alpha/\hbar}\psi$, $\boldsymbol A\to\boldsymbol A+\nabla\alpha$; Berry parameter-space rephasing $\vert n(\boldsymbol R)\rangle\to\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}\alpha(\boldsymbol R)}\vert n(\boldsymbol R)\rangle$ with $\boldsymbol A_n\to\boldsymbol A_n-\nabla_{\boldsymbol R}\alpha$; Berry-in-time rephasing $\vert n(t)\rangle\to\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}\alpha(t)}\vert n(t)\rangle$ with $\phi_n\to\phi_n-\dot\alpha$; Wu-Yang / Dirac-monopole transition function $\boldsymbol A^{(N)}-\boldsymbol A^{(S)}=\nabla\alpha$ with overlap rephasing $\psi^{(N)}=\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}q\alpha/\hbar}\psi^{(S)}$. `\chi` is reserved (e.g. generic ket name in §1.3.2) and must not appear in Ch 4. **Validator update (`.claude/scripts/validate_project.py` `_check_ch4_si_units`):** added a Ch 4-only pattern `\\chi(?![A-Za-z])` that flags any `\chi` macro with the message *"uses \chi as a gauge / rephasing function symbol; Chapter 4 uses \alpha …"*. **Substitutions across 4 notebooks (Bash + Python `re.sub(r"\\chi(?![a-zA-Z])", r"\\alpha", …)`):** `4-1-gauge-field.ipynb` cell 1 (1 × $\chi$ → $\alpha$ in $\psi(\boldsymbol x)\to\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}q\alpha(\boldsymbol x)/\hbar}\psi$); `4-2-1-berry-phase.ipynb` cell 2 (8 × $\chi$ → $\alpha$: cell-2 rephasing intro $\vert n(t)\rangle\to\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}\alpha(t)}\vert n(t)\rangle$, $\phi_n\to\phi_n-\dot\alpha$, boundary terms $\alpha(0)-\alpha(t)$, parameter-space rephasing $\vert n(\boldsymbol R)\rangle\to\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}\alpha(\boldsymbol R)}\vert n\rangle$, EM analog $\psi(\boldsymbol r)\to\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}\alpha(\boldsymbol r)}\psi$, $\boldsymbol A_n\to\boldsymbol A_n-\nabla_{\boldsymbol R}\alpha$, boundary terms $\alpha(\boldsymbol R_{\text{end}})-\alpha(\boldsymbol R_{\text{start}})$, Discussion-dropdown rephasing question); cell 3 HW Problem 3(a) (2 × $\chi$ → $\alpha$). **Cone-angle collision pre-rename in 4-2-1 cell 3:** HW Problems 1 and 4 already used `\alpha` as the cone half-angle (5 occurrences across HW1(a)(b)(c) and HW4(a)(b)). Renamed cone half-angle `\alpha` → `\theta_0` (5 substitutions) before applying `\chi` → `\alpha`, so the gauge function and the cone half-angle never collide in the same problem; `\theta_0` reads naturally as "polar angle of the cone" (the path traces $\theta = \theta_0$ at fixed half-angle). No collisions in cell 2 (existing `\alpha` already meant gauge function). `4-2-2-aharonov-bohm-effect.ipynb` cell 2 (4 × $\chi$ → $\alpha$: gauge-transformation block $\boldsymbol A\to\boldsymbol A+\nabla\alpha$, $\psi\to\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}q\alpha/\hbar}\psi$, single-path phase boundary terms $(q/\hbar)[\alpha(\text{end})-\alpha(\text{start})]$); cell 3 HW (3 × $\chi$ → $\alpha$). `4-4-2-dirac-monopole.ipynb` cell 2 (17 × $\chi$ → $\alpha$: Wu-Yang transition function $\boldsymbol A^{(N)}-\boldsymbol A^{(S)}=\nabla\alpha$, overlap rephasing $\psi^{(N)}=\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}q\alpha/\hbar}\psi^{(S)}$, winding equation $(q/\hbar)[\alpha(2\pi)-\alpha(0)]=2\pi n$, alternative $\alpha(\varphi)=g\varphi/(2\pi)$ derivation); cell 3 HW Problem 2 (2 × $\chi$ → $\alpha$). **Surveyed but left unchanged:** `1-3-2-schrodinger-picture.ipynb` cell 3 uses `\vert\chi(t)\rangle` as a generic comparison ket — this is unrelated to gauge functions, lives outside Ch 4, and the rule explicitly preserves the `\chi` ket-name use case; left intact. Validators clean (`validate_project.py --scope ch4` → 16 notebooks, *All validations passed*; `safe_edit.py validate` on each of the 4 touched files → *no issues found*). Pre-existing ch5/ch6 issues unrelated to this edit are not touched. | B |

| 2026-05-08 | 4-2-1-berry-phase | Notation update (professor chat 2026-05-08): introduced dedicated symbol $\phi_n(t) = \mathrm{i}\langle n(t)\vert\partial_t n(t)\rangle$ for the Berry connection in time, in direct analogy with the EM scalar potential $\phi$ (subscripted $_n$ to avoid clash with `physics-conventions.md`'s reservation of bare $\phi$ for scalar electric potential, and to mirror $\boldsymbol A_n$ for the spatial component). Cell-2 edits: (a) *Adiabatic Theorem* `important` box now defines $\phi_n(t)$ explicitly, gives $\Phi_{\mathrm{Berry}}(t)=\int_0^t \phi_n(t')\,\mathrm{d}t'$, and shows the gauge transformation $\phi_n \to \phi_n - \dot\chi$ (mirroring the EM $\phi \to \phi - \partial_t\alpha$ from §4.1.3); the older descriptive phrase "Berry connection along the time axis" is replaced everywhere by the named **Berry connection in time**. (b) *Derivation: Adiabatic Following and the Berry Phase* `dropdown information` ODE now reads $\dot\psi_n = -(\mathrm{i}/\hbar)E_n\psi_n + \mathrm{i}\,\phi_n\psi_n$ with the Berry-connection identification $\langle n\vert\partial_t n\rangle = -\mathrm{i}\phi_n$ named explicitly. (c) *Berry Phase and Berry Connection* parameter-space refinement section's chain-rule display equation now reads $\phi_n(t)=\mathrm{i}\langle n\vert\partial_t n\rangle = \dot{\boldsymbol R}\cdot\boldsymbol A_n(\boldsymbol R)$ (showing the time-component-as-directional-derivative-of-spatial-component identity), with the parameter-space connection now explicitly named **Berry connection in parameter space** (analog of EM $\boldsymbol A$); duplicate naming sentence further down was removed. Berry phase identity $\Phi_{\mathrm{Berry}}(\mathcal C)=\int_0^t\phi_n\,\mathrm{d}t'=\int_{\mathcal C}\boldsymbol A_n\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol R$ now displays both forms side by side. Cleaned a dangling "with **dynamical phase** and **Berry phase**" stub left over from the box-splice (replaced with one-sentence prose linking the two phases to their physical origins). **Counts**: cell-2 source-array length 306 → 318; bare $\phi$ count = 0 (subscripted $\phi_n$ used throughout); $\boldsymbol A_n$ count unchanged. **Validation:** `safe_edit.py validate` and `validate_project.py --scope 4-2-1-berry-phase` clean (no long-line warnings). **Convention note:** $\phi_n$ subscript $_n$ (with $n$ matching $\boldsymbol A_n$ and $\boldsymbol\Omega_n$) avoids the bare-$\phi$ reservation in `physics-conventions.md` § Phase Symbols. The pair $(\phi_n,\boldsymbol A_n)$ is the natural "4-potential" of the Berry connection on (time × parameter space), with gauge transformations $(\phi_n,\boldsymbol A_n)\to(\phi_n-\dot\chi,\,\boldsymbol A_n-\nabla_{\boldsymbol R}\chi)$ mirroring the EM $(\phi,\boldsymbol A)\to(\phi-\partial_t\alpha,\,\boldsymbol A+\nabla\alpha)$ structure (the spatial-component sign difference is a convention reflecting that the Berry $\boldsymbol A_n$ is defined with $+\mathrm{i}$ rather than the EM $-\mathrm{i}q/\hbar$ in the covariant derivative). **Scope:** symbol-naming refinement only; no derivation logic changed; no homework changes. Cursor stays at D / `4-2-1-berry-phase` per `.claude/README.md` § 1.4. Designer: `derivation-designer` (notation-naming consistency check) → `notebook-writer`. | D |

| 2026-05-08 | 4-2-1-berry-phase | T-2026-05-08-01 DONE: cell 2 § *Berry Phase and Berry Connection*, inserted new `:::{admonition} Derivation: Berry Phase from the Adiabatic Ansatz` `:class: dropdown information` block immediately under the line-integral display equation $\Phi_{\mathrm{Berry}}=\mathrm{i}\oint_{\mathcal{C}}\langle n\vert\nabla_{\boldsymbol{R}}\vert n\rangle\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{R}$ and just before the *Berry Connection and Berry Curvature* `important` box. Forward chain (5 displayed equations, plus a one-line reality aside) per `derivation-quality.md` § Mandatory detail level: (i) adiabatic ansatz $\vert\psi\rangle=c_n(t)\,\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}\Phi_{\mathrm{dyn}}}\vert n(\boldsymbol{R}(t))\rangle$ with $\Phi_{\mathrm{dyn}}=-\int_0^t E_n\,\mathrm{d}t'/\hbar$; (ii) substitute into $\mathrm{i}\hbar\,\partial_t\vert\psi\rangle=\hat{H}\vert\psi\rangle$, expand via product rule into a `\begin{split}` two-line block, identify the $-\hbar\,\dot\Phi_{\mathrm{dyn}}\,c_n\vert n\rangle$ vs $E_n\,c_n\,\vert n\rangle$ cancellation; (iii) project onto $\langle n(\boldsymbol{R}(t))\vert$, drop interband $\langle m\vert\partial_t n\rangle$ ($m\ne n$) by adiabaticity, get $\dot c_n/c_n=-\langle n\vert\partial_t n\rangle$; (iv) chain rule $\partial_t\vert n\rangle=\dot{\boldsymbol{R}}\cdot\nabla_{\boldsymbol{R}}\vert n\rangle$, integrate over $t'\in[0,t]$ to get $c_n(t)=c_n(0)\,\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}\Phi_{\mathrm{Berry}}(t)}$ with $\Phi_{\mathrm{Berry}}(t)=\mathrm{i}\int_{\boldsymbol{R}(0)}^{\boldsymbol{R}(t)}\langle n\vert\nabla_{\boldsymbol{R}}\vert n\rangle\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{R}$; (v) close to a loop on $\boldsymbol{R}(T)=\boldsymbol{R}(0)$ to recover the body equation. Reality of $\Phi_{\mathrm{Berry}}$ shown via differentiating $\langle n\vert n\rangle=1$ in $\boldsymbol{R}$, giving $\langle n\vert\nabla_{\boldsymbol{R}}\vert n\rangle$ purely imaginary. Notation matches existing cell 2 conventions: `\mathrm{i,e,d}` upright, `\hat{H}`, `\boldsymbol{R}`, `\Phi_{\mathrm{Berry}}/\Phi_{\mathrm{dyn}}` subscripts in `\mathrm{...}`, `\vert n(\boldsymbol{R})\rangle` with explicit space (`physics-conventions.md`). Scope rule (`derivation-quality.md` § Scope and pedagogical fit): single focus = the Berry-phase identity; uses only objects already on the page (TDSE, instantaneous eigenstate, adiabatic ansatz); does **not** preempt later sections (no covariant derivative, no curvature, no spin-1/2 specifics). `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` § Berry Phase derives the Phase=Action form instead — `derivation-quality.md` § Scope of `_refs/` alignment explicitly permits the new lecture to choose its own route, but the chosen route's math content is shown line by line. | 2026-05-08 | 4-2-1-berry-phase | Professor feedback DONE (delivered in chat 2026-05-08). Cell 2 § *Example: Spin-1/2 in a Rotating Magnetic Field*: the Hamiltonian was written as $\hat{H}(t) = -\hbar\omega_B\,\hat{\boldsymbol{\sigma}}\cdot\boldsymbol{n}(t)$ with the symbol $\omega_B$ never defined — the surrounding text introduces $\boldsymbol{B}(t)=B\,\boldsymbol{n}(t)$ and the field strength $B$, so $\omega_B$ was the only undefined quantity in the example. Per professor instruction, dropped $\omega_B$ and rewrote the Hamiltonian using $B$ directly: $\hat{H}(t) = -\mu B\,\hat{\boldsymbol{\sigma}}\cdot\boldsymbol{n}(t)$, adding the inline definition *"with $\mu$ the magnetic-moment magnitude associated with $\hat{\boldsymbol{\sigma}}$."* This is the standard $\hat H = -\hat{\boldsymbol{\mu}}\cdot\boldsymbol{B}$ form with $\hat{\boldsymbol{\mu}} = \mu\,\hat{\boldsymbol{\sigma}}$ (so $\mu = g\mu_{\mathrm B}/2$ in standard atomic-units conventions). The Berry-phase computation that follows is unaffected because it depends only on the eigenstate, which is determined by the *direction* $\hat{\boldsymbol{n}}$ (not on $\mu B$). Cross-checked: cell 2 has zero remaining $\omega_B$ instances; HW#5(a) in cell 3 still uses $\omega_L = \gamma B$ for the Larmor frequency (separate context, untouched). Single uniquely-anchored string replace; cell 2 source-array length unchanged (242). `safe_edit.py validate notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-2-1-berry-phase.ipynb` and `validate_project.py --scope 4-2-1-berry-phase` both clean. Phase D ticket queue for `4-2-1-berry-phase` still has T-2026-05-08-01 (in-place derivation of $\Phi_{\mathrm{Berry}}$ formula) and T-2026-05-08-03 (solid-angle integration step). The dropdown previously leapt from the coordinate-component curl $\Omega_{\theta\varphi}=\partial_\theta A_\varphi-\partial_\varphi A_\theta=-\tfrac12\sin\theta$ directly to the vector form $\boldsymbol{\Omega}=-\tfrac12\hat{\boldsymbol{n}}$ with only the parenthetical *"on the unit sphere parameter space ($R=1$), this is equivalently"* — the surface-flux / area-element conversion was implicit. Replaced the leap with a 4-line explanation in vector-calculus language consistent with the body's $\nabla\times\boldsymbol{A}$ and $\boldsymbol{\Omega}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{S}$ tone (rather than differential-forms language flagged in the ticket): (1) note $\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{S}=\hat{\boldsymbol{n}}\sin\theta\,\mathrm{d}\theta\,\mathrm{d}\varphi$ is the unit-sphere vector area element; (2) match $\int_S\boldsymbol{\Omega}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{S}=\int_S(\boldsymbol{\Omega}\cdot\hat{\boldsymbol{n}})\sin\theta\,\mathrm{d}\theta\,\mathrm{d}\varphi=\int_S\Omega_{\theta\varphi}\,\mathrm{d}\theta\,\mathrm{d}\varphi$; (3) read off $\boldsymbol{\Omega}\cdot\hat{\boldsymbol{n}}=\Omega_{\theta\varphi}/\sin\theta=-\tfrac12$; (4) since the eigenstate depends only on the field *direction* $\hat{\boldsymbol{n}}$ (not magnitude $\vert\boldsymbol{B}\vert$), the curvature is purely radial → $\boldsymbol{\Omega}=-\tfrac12\hat{\boldsymbol{n}}$. Also tweaked the lead-in *"Now take curl on the sphere"* → *"Now take the curl on the sphere"* and added the missing article. Single uniquely-anchored string replace; cell 2 source-array length 236 → 242 (six new lines: lead sentence + blank + `$$` + flux-matching display + `$$` + blank + bridging sentence — net of removing the *"on the unit sphere parameter space ($R=1$), this is equivalently"* paragraph). All other content untouched (eigenstate setup, $A_\theta=A_\varphi$ component derivation, monopole-interpretation closing line, key result `important` box, worked example, See Also). Notation per `physics-conventions.md`: $\mathrm{d}$ upright, $\boldsymbol{\Omega}$ / $\boldsymbol{S}$ / $\hat{\boldsymbol{n}}$ bold-with-hat, no `\vec`, no `\quad`, no `align`/`aligned`. `safe_edit.py validate notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-2-1-berry-phase.ipynb` and `validate_project.py --scope 4-2-1-berry-phase` both clean. Phase D ticket queue for `4-2-1-berry-phase` reduced to T-2026-05-08-01 (Berry-phase formula derivation) and T-2026-05-08-03 (solid-angle integration step). Cursor stays at D / `4-2-1-berry-phase` per `.claude/README.md` § 1.4. (c) the `Derivation: Berry Connection for Spin-1/2` `dropdown information` block — physics is correct (computed $A_\theta=0$, $A_\varphi=-(1-\cos\theta)/2$ matches the Wu-Yang northern-hemisphere monopole potential at $S=\hbar/2$ in `_refs/` § Quantization of Spin lines 4760–4790, accounting for the orthonormal-vs-coordinate basis factor $\sin\theta$); spinor and bra defined upfront, $\partial_\theta\vert\uparrow\rangle$ and $\partial_\varphi\vert\uparrow\rangle$ shown explicitly, inner products evaluated line-by-line, both $A_\theta=0$ conclusion and $A_\varphi=-\sin^2(\theta/2)=-(1-\cos\theta)/2$ identity present; curl $\Omega_{\theta\varphi}=-\tfrac12\sin\theta$ correct ($\partial_\theta A_\varphi=-\tfrac12\sin\theta$, $\partial_\varphi A_\theta=0$). **Two leaps flagged:** (c.i) the conversion $\Omega_{\theta\varphi}=-\tfrac12\sin\theta\Rightarrow\boldsymbol\Omega=-\tfrac12\hat{\boldsymbol n}$ uses the $S^2$ area-form / Hodge correspondence implicitly → **T-2026-05-08-02**; (c.ii) the `Spin-1/2 Berry Phase` `important` box leaps from $\boldsymbol\Omega=-\tfrac12\hat{\boldsymbol n}$ to $\Phi_{\mathrm{Berry}}=-\Omega_{\text{solid}}/2$ without showing the surface integral $\int_S\boldsymbol\Omega\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol S=-\tfrac12\Omega_{\text{solid}}$ and the unit-sphere identification $\hat{\boldsymbol n}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol S=\mathrm{d}\Omega_{\text{solid}}$ → **T-2026-05-08-03** (one-line repair, may share a `notebook-writer` session with T-02). **Items checked and OK** (no ticket needed): physics correctness end-to-end against `_refs/`; sign of the half-solid-angle result ($-\Omega_{\text{solid}}/2$, consistent with $\boldsymbol\Omega=-\tfrac12\hat{\boldsymbol n}$, HW#2/HW#4/HW#7(a) post T-2026-05-07-09 fix, and `_refs/` Wu-Yang $A_\varphi=S(\cos\theta-1)/\sin\theta$ at $S=\hbar/2$); $-\pi$ result of the precessing-equator example ($\Omega_{\text{solid}}=2\pi$, half = $\pi$, sign flip); upright $\mathrm{i,d,e}$, $\hat H$ hatted, $\boldsymbol R$ bold (`physics-conventions.md`); $\Phi_{\mathrm{Berry}}$/$\Phi_{\mathrm{dyn}}$ subscripted (post T-2026-05-07-10); intermediate-step depth of the spin-1/2 derivation (~12 displayed lines, comparable to or richer than the closest `_refs/` analog `_refs/` § Quantum Spin / Hamiltonian's Wu-Yang potential definition); single-focus and in-context (no preempting §4.2.2 Aharonov-Bohm or §4.4.2/4.4.3 monopole — only forward parenthetical "cf. §4.4.3" used). **Items considered out of scope** (not Phase D): the body's $\boldsymbol\Omega_n=\nabla_{\boldsymbol R}\times\boldsymbol A_n$ implicitly assumes 3D parameter space (in 2D $S^2$ a 2-form curvature, in higher-dim the Berry curvature 2-form $F_{ij}$) — this is a `physics-conventions.md` / pedagogical-framing question, properly Phase B/C; not ticketed here. | 2026-05-06 | 4-1-2-electromagnetic-coupling | T-2026-05-06-09 DONE: cell 3 HW Problem 1, replaced lecture-duplication problem ("Velocity from kinetic momentum" — verbatim of the body's `Derivation: Velocity from the Hamiltonian` dropdown) with a Transfer-style problem on the Pauli Hamiltonian per `homework-designer/SKILL.md` § 2.1 Transfer + § 3.1 Lecture-duplication. New title: `**1. Velocity for the Pauli Hamiltonian.**` (4 ASCII words, capitalized) with three guided sub-parts: (a) compute $[\hat{x}_i, \hat{H}_{\mathrm{P}}]$ for $\hat{H}_{\mathrm{P}} = \hat{\boldsymbol{\pi}}^2/(2m) - (q\hbar/2m)\,\hat{\boldsymbol{\sigma}}\cdot\boldsymbol{B}(\hat{\boldsymbol{x}}) + q\phi$ and show the spin-magnetic term drops, recovering $\mathrm{d}\hat{\boldsymbol{x}}/\mathrm{d}t = \hat{\boldsymbol{\pi}}/m$ (near-lecture, easy — extends the body method to one new term); (b) generalize — any term built from $\hat{\boldsymbol{x}}$ and $\hat{\boldsymbol{\sigma}}$ alone cannot enter the velocity operator (medium, structural argument); (c) where the spin influences motion — compute the spin-magnetic contribution to $m\,\mathrm{d}\hat{\boldsymbol{v}}/\mathrm{d}t$ and recover the Stern-Gerlach force $(q\hbar/2m)\,\nabla(\hat{\boldsymbol{\sigma}}\cdot\boldsymbol{B})$ (medium, parallel calculation — the spin couples to motion only through the *gradient* of $\boldsymbol{B}$). Chose option (i) over (ii)/(iii) per ticket sketch — closest to the lecture method, satisfies near-lecture P1 difficulty (`homework-designer/SKILL.md` § 1.3 Difficulty distribution), Pauli operators already in the students' vocabulary from Ch 1, and the structural-zero result is a sharper conceptual takeaway than picture-comparison or self-consistency inversion. Notation: SI throughout (no $c$ factors), `\mathrm{i,e,d}` upright, `\hat{\boldsymbol{\sigma}}` per `physics-conventions.md` Vectorized Pauli, `\hat{H}_{\mathrm{P}}` upright subscript, `\boldsymbol{\sigma}\cdot\boldsymbol{B}` (vector-vector dot product allowed). | 2026-05-08 | 4-1-3-gauge-invariance | Cell 2 two coordinated edits per professor chat 2026-05-08. **(i)** *Total = Kinetic + Potential* table: added covariant-derivative equal signs so gauge invariance is manifest from row structure. **Momentum** kinetic entry now $\hat{\boldsymbol{\pi}} = -\mathrm{i}\hbar\boldsymbol{D} = m\hat{\boldsymbol{v}}$; **Energy** kinetic entry now $\hat{\boldsymbol{\pi}}^2/2m = \mathrm{i}\hbar D_t$. Parallels canonical entries ($-\mathrm{i}\hbar\nabla$, $\mathrm{i}\hbar\partial_t$): ordinary derivative (canonical, gauge-dependent) vs covariant derivative (kinetic, gauge-invariant via homogeneous transformation of $\boldsymbol{D}\psi$, $D_t\psi$ from §4.1.1). **(ii)** Removed the misleading *Caution: Total Hamiltonian vs Energy Eigenvalues* admonition (it claimed $E_n$ is gauge-invariant under arbitrary gauge transformations — false: under $\phi \to \phi - \partial_t\alpha$, $\hat H$ shifts by $-q\partial_t\alpha$ and $E_n$ shifts with it; $E_n$ is only invariant under purely time-independent gauge transformations, which is too subtle to introduce here). Also removed the *Energy eigenvalues $E_n$* row from the *Gauge-invariant (physical)* classification table — per professor instruction we avoid the time-vs-space gauge subtlety entirely rather than qualify it. **Validation:** `safe_edit.py validate` and `validate_project.py --scope 4-1-3-gauge-invariance` clean. Scope: cell 2 only (table inside *Total = Kinetic + Potential*, plus removal of one `caution` admonition and one row from *Classification of Physical Quantities* gauge-invariant table); no homework changes; no other notebooks touched. | D |

| 2026-05-13 | `4-4-2-dirac-monopole` | Phase D (Derivation quality) audit — read-only chain-quality sweep of cell 2 per `rules/derivation-quality.md` (§ *Root causes*, § *Mandatory procedure*, § *Minimum derivation depth*, § *Anti-patterns*) and `phys130b-derivation-designer` skill. **Two derivation chains audited**: (1) body-prose chain in § *Aharonov-Bohm Phase and Quantization*; (2) `:::{admonition} Derivation: same condition from the single-patch loop integral :class: dropdown information`. **`_refs/` consultation**: `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` § *Magnetic Monopole* (lines 4070–4793) carries parallel material in a **different framing** (abstract spin-axis on unit sphere with $S = \hbar s$), so structural-diff is partial; other rule criteria fully applied. **Finding 1 — body derivation: PARTIAL** — step (v) *winding = total flux* asserts $\alpha(2\pi)-\alpha(0) = \oint\boldsymbol{B}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{S} = g$ **without derivation**; the actual bridge (Stokes on upper hemisphere via $\boldsymbol{A}^{(N)}$ gives $g/2$; Stokes on lower via $\boldsymbol{A}^{(S)}$ with opposite orientation gives $-g/2$; subtraction using $\boldsymbol{A}^{(N)}-\boldsymbol{A}^{(S)} = \nabla\alpha$ yields $\alpha(2\pi)-\alpha(0) = g$) is compressed into one equality, violating `rules/derivation-quality.md` § *Root causes* #1 (*Skipped intermediate steps*) and § *Mandatory procedure* item 4. Filed as **T-2026-05-13-DV-08** with designer routes (a) inline bridge or (b) small dropdown. **Finding 2 — single-patch dropdown: PASS** (forward chain, correct algebra: $(1-\cos(\pi/2))/\sin(\pi/2) \cdot \sin(\pi/2) = 1$ at equator, $\int_0^{2\pi}(g/4\pi)\mathrm{d}\varphi = g/2$, signs consistent, no reverse-engineering, single-focus). One borderline algebraic compression — the $\sin(\pi/2)$ factor from $\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l} = r\sin\theta\,\mathrm{d}\varphi\,\hat{\boldsymbol{\varphi}}$ and the $1/\sin(\pi/2)$ factor from $\boldsymbol{A}^{(N)}$ cancel without explicit attribution — **bundled as optional companion** into T-DV-08 per T-DV-04 / DV-07 precedents. **Not ticket-worthy**: (i) dropdown's *''Both phases are valid in their respective patches; their difference on the overlap is what must be a multiple of $2\pi$''* — restates body's single-valuedness; (ii) HW2(a) curl verification and HW5 winding number / HW7 angular momentum — homework exercises; (iii) *Topological Origin of Quantization* `important` box — result statement, no derivation; (iv) *Charge Quantization from Monopoles* algebra — trivial division. **Scope discipline** per `rules/maintenance-scope.md`: proposed bridge stays in body's existing scope; optional companion stays in existing dropdown; no Wu-Yang / monopole-harmonics / fiber-bundle scope creep. **Cursor stays at D / `4-4-2-dirac-monopole`** until T-DV-08 closes per `.claude/README.md` § 1.4 (audit-vs-fix split); on close, advance to `4-4-3-monopole-harmonics` at Phase A. ## Phase Cursor narrative (Round 2 — superseded 2026-05-24)

notebook. **Phase A (Validate)** ran clean 2026-05-23 (no tickets).

ticket — **NB-210** (uppercase `\Phi` used for the scalar electric
potential where `physics-conventions.md` reserves lowercase `\phi`),
closed 2026-05-23. Cursor advanced B → C. The **Phase D (Derivation quality)** step is the next phase-audit step (no longer gated); Phase D will be **skipped** — a §4.1 section parent has no derivation / `_refs`-aligned content — completing the §4.1 parent A–D pass for Round 2 and advancing the cursor to `4-1-1-gauge-principle` at Phase A. **Phase D for `4-1-gauge-field` was skipped 2026-05-23** — a §4.1 section parent has no derivation / `_refs`-aligned content — completing its Round 2 A–D pass; the cursor advanced to `4-1-1-gauge-principle` at Phase A. `4-1-1-gauge-principle` has since completed its **Phase A (Validate)** audit (2026-05-23 — both static validators (`safe_edit.py validate`, `validate_project.py --scope 4-1-1-gauge-principle`) pass clean; the 4-cell subsection layout is correct — cell 0 title `# 4.1.1 Gauge Principle`, cell 1 Prompts admonition `:class: tip` (5 bullets), cell 2 `## Lecture Notes` → `### Overview` → five `###` body sections → `### Summary` + `See Also`, cell 3 `## Homework` with 7 numbered `**N. Title.**` problems — with no `---` / nested `:::` / collapsed-line / missing-`\n` / control-char / banned-pattern / student-facing-scope issues; one LOW structure-signal ticket **NB-212** filed for a stray double-blank-line run in cell 2). The cursor advanced A → B. The **Phase B (Notation) audit** ran 2026-05-23 and filed one LOW notation ticket — **NB-213** (cell-3 Homework Problem 6(a) writes the non-covariant remainder as `\psi^2 \cdot \mathrm{e}^{2\mathrm{i}q\alpha/\hbar}`, using `\cdot` as a scalar product between two scalar factors, which `physics-conventions.md` § Dot Product reserves for vector dot products only); all other notation — SI EM units, upright `\mathrm{i}/\mathrm{e}/\mathrm{d}`, `\boldsymbol`/`\hat`, plain `\nabla`, `\phi` scalar potential / `\Phi` flux / `\varphi` azimuth, `\alpha`-only gauge function, `\begin{split}` display math — is convention-clean. Verified correct (NumPy): the open-path shift $(q/\hbar)\int_{\mathcal{C}}\nabla\alpha\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l}=(q/\hbar)[\alpha(\boldsymbol{r}_{\text{end}})-\alpha(\boldsymbol{r}_{\text{start}})]$, the closed-loop-holonomy gauge-invariance claim, and homework P1/P2/P3/P5/P6 (symmetric-vs-Landau canonical-momentum split, the Coulomb-gauge residual-freedom $\nabla^2\alpha=0$ condition, the P6 classification). Two single-scope MEDIUM tickets were filed — **NB-227** (the `Open Path vs Closed Loop` note declares the single-endpoint object $\mathrm{e}^{+\mathrm{i}q\int_{\mathcal{C}}\boldsymbol{A}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l}/\hbar}\psi(\boldsymbol{r}_{\text{end}})$ gauge-invariant — it is not: wrong sign and missing the $\psi^*(\boldsymbol{r}_{\text{start}})$ factor, it acquires $\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}q[2\alpha(\boldsymbol{r}_{\text{end}})-\alpha(\boldsymbol{r}_{\text{start}})]/\hbar}$) and **NB-228** (homework P4(a) asks to "show" $\hat{H}'\psi_n'=E_n\psi_n'$, valid only for a time-independent gauge function — for $\partial_t\alpha\neq0$, $\hat{H}'\psi_n'=(E_n-q\,\partial_t\alpha)\psi_n'$). This **completes the Round 2 A–D pass** for `4-1-3-gauge-invariance` and the Round 2 §4.1 block (the `4-1` parent plus `4-1-1`/`4-1-2`/`4-1-3`); the cursor advanced to `4-2-berry-phase` (§4.2 section parent) at Phase A. The **Phase D (Derivation quality)** audit on `4-2-2-aharonov-bohm-effect` is now gated only on the remaining `4-2-2` Phase C ticket **NB-241**. **NB-241 was closed 2026-05-23** — cell 3's two homework cross-reference links carrying a redundant `../ch4_phase-and-gauge/` directory prefix (Problem 4(c) `[4.4.2 Dirac Monopole]`, Problem 5(a) `[4.2.1 Berry Phase]`) were converted to the documented bare-stem `[Topic Name](stem)` form (`](4-4-2-dirac-monopole)`, `](4-2-1-berry-phase)`), matching the notebook's own cell-2 / `See Also` links and `myst-references.md` § Cross-reference conventions; both static validators pass clean. With NB-241 closed, **Phase C is complete** for `4-2-2-aharonov-bohm-effect`; the **Phase D (Derivation quality)** audit is no longer gated and is the next phase-audit step — the notebook has one `dropdown information` derivation block (`Derivation: Single-Path Phase from Local Gauge Transformation`) to structurally diff against `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md`. The **Phase D (Derivation quality)** audit then ran 2026-05-23: the notebook's one `dropdown information` block, `Derivation: Single-Path Phase from Local Gauge Transformation`, was structurally diffed against `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` § Aharonov-Bohm Effect / § Gauge Connection. The gauge-away-and-invert chain is correct, complete, and every step is shown — $\boldsymbol{A}=\nabla\alpha$ curl-free with $\alpha=-\int_\mathcal{C}\boldsymbol{A}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l}'$ so $\nabla\alpha=-\boldsymbol{A}$; the §4.1.3 gauge transformation sends $\boldsymbol{A}\to0$ and $\psi\to\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}q\alpha/\hbar}\psi$; inversion gives the single-path phase $\Phi_{\mathrm{path}}[\mathcal{C}]=(q/\hbar)\int_\mathcal{C}\boldsymbol{A}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l}$ — reproducing the reference single-path Berry phase $\Theta_\mathcal{C}=(q/\hbar)\int_\mathcal{C}\boldsymbol{A}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{x}$ (the lecture's gauge-transformation route is its own pedagogical choice and matches the reference physics). The two-path / Stokes assembly to $\Delta\Phi_{\mathrm{AB}}=q\Phi/\hbar$ and the proof-style homework P2 / P3 / P4 (winding-number phase difference, gauge dependence of the single-path phase, surface independence via the divergence theorem) were verified correct (NumPy). One single-scope MEDIUM ticket was filed — **NB-242** (Homework Problem 5 *AB phase as Berry phase*: part (b) asks to *show* the Berry connection $\boldsymbol{A}_\text{Berry}=\mathrm{i}\langle\psi\vert\nabla_{\boldsymbol{r}}\vert\psi\rangle$ reduces to $+(q/\hbar)\boldsymbol{A}$, but with the ansatz $\psi=\mathrm{e}^{+\mathrm{i}q\int\boldsymbol{A}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l}'/\hbar}\tilde\psi$ stated in the same sub-part the honest computation gives $-(q/\hbar)\boldsymbol{A}$ — exactly §4.2.1's own derived rule $\tilde{\boldsymbol{A}}_n=\boldsymbol{A}_n-\nabla\alpha$ for a rephased state, with the $\boldsymbol{A}_n=+\mathrm{i}\langle n\vert\nabla n\rangle$ convention; NumPy-confirmed ratio $-1$ on the standard solenoid potential — so part (c)'s $\oint\boldsymbol{A}_\text{Berry}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{r}=+q\Phi/\hbar$ inherits the wrong sign, the honest value being $-q\Phi/\hbar$). The cell-2 derivation dropdown and the AB-phase box are correct and untouched by this finding. This **completes the Round 2 A–D pass** for `4-2-2-aharonov-bohm-effect` and so completes §4.2.2; the cursor advanced to `4-2-3-flux-ring` at Phase A.

Title.**` problems — with intact source-array newlines, no collapsed cells (no source entry exceeds 1000 chars), balanced admonition / figure fences (cell 2: 23 `:::{` opens / 23 `:::` closes — two `:::{margin}` each wrapping a nested `:::{figure}` margin-figure pair, one standalone `:::{figure}`, the remaining 18 `:::{admonition}` blocks, maximum nesting depth 1, no nested admonitions; cell 1: 1 / 1), even `$$` counts (cell 1: 0, cell 2: 96 → 48 display blocks, cell 3: 8 → 4 blocks), all three referenced figure assets (`notes_src/assets/figs/4-2-3-flux_ring_energy.png`, `4-2-3-flux_ring_current.png`, `4-2-3-squid.png`) present on disk, and no `---` / control-char / missing-`\n` / banned-pattern (`plt.show()`, bare `### Application:` colon heading) / student-facing-scope (`.claude/`, `_refs/`, `progress.md`, `feedback.md`, `CLAUDE.md`) issues. **Phase A ran clean — no tickets filed.** One item is noted for the upcoming Phase C audit (outside Phase A scope): the `### Application — The SQUID` body section nests three `####` subsection headings (`Josephson relations`, `Two-junction interference`, `Measuring flux as voltage`) — a deeper heading level than the §4.1–4.2 sibling notebooks use; the Phase C style audit will adjudicate whether the `####` depth conforms to `notebook-architecture.md`. The cursor advanced A → B; the **Phase B (Notation)** audit on `4-2-3-flux-ring` is the next phase-audit step. The **Phase B (Notation)** audit then ran 2026-05-24 and filed three LOW notation tickets — **NB-243** (cell 2 writes all 75 ket/bra delimiters — 74 kets + 1 bra — with a bare `|` rather than `\vert`, against `physics-conventions.md` § Ket/Bra Notation, while cell 3 and the §4.2 sibling `4-2-2-aharonov-bohm-effect` already use `\vert`; the 44 `\vert` in cell 2 are all absolute-value bars), **NB-244** (the `Derivation: Josephson relations from coupled condensates` `dropdown information` block has two `$$` blocks laying two equations side-by-side joined by `\quad\Longrightarrow\quad`, against § Display Math — the closed NB-222 / NB-238 precedent), and **NB-245** (homework Problems 1–2 use `\varphi` for the dimensionless flux ratio `\Phi/\Phi_0`, off-label against the § Phase Symbols `\varphi`=azimuthal-angle role used by the §4.2.1 sibling). All other notation is convention-clean — upright `\mathrm{i}/\mathrm{e}/\mathrm{d}`, `\boldsymbol` vectors / `\hat` operators, plain `\nabla`, SI EM units (minimal coupling `\hat{\boldsymbol{\pi}}=\hat{\boldsymbol{p}}-q\boldsymbol{A}`, AB phase `\Delta\Phi_{\mathrm{AB}}=q\Phi/\hbar` and gauge phase `\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}q\alpha/\hbar}` with no `1/c`, flux quantum `\Phi_0=h/q`, supercurrent `\boldsymbol{j}=(\vert\Psi\vert^{2}/m)(\hbar\nabla\Theta-q\boldsymbol{A})` in SI form; no Gaussian markers), `\Phi` magnetic flux / `\Phi_0` flux quantum / `\Phi_{\mathrm{path}}` path-accumulated phase / `\Delta\Phi_{\mathrm{AB}}` AB phase, `\alpha`-only gauge function (no `\chi`/`\Lambda`), `\cdot` only for genuine vector dot products (all six are `\oint\ldots\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l}`), a consistent QI Pauli system (`\hat{X}`, `\hat{Z}` with `|0\rangle`/`|1\rangle` kets), and `\begin{split}` for every multi-line display block (no `align`/`aligned`). Items considered and not filed: the bare-italic multi-letter subscript `\hat{U}_{LG}` and the `I_{\max}` (cell 2) vs `I_\text{max}` (cell 3) source spelling — both render correctly and neither is a `physics-conventions.md` convention-table item; the condensate / order-parameter phase `\Theta` and the Josephson junction phases `\theta_L,\theta_R,\delta` — standard notation, and tagging them onto `\Phi` would collide with the magnetic flux; and the two `\qquad`-appended domain conditions (`n\in\mathbb{Z}`, `a>b>0`), the milder equation-plus-side-condition pattern (see NB-244). The physics was NumPy-verified. Four single-scope tickets were filed — **NB-136** (MEDIUM — the `Construction: Unambiguous State Discrimination POVM` dropdown normalizes $\hat M_1,\hat M_2$ by $\tfrac{1}{1+\vert c\vert^2}$, which makes $\hat M_3=\hat I-\hat M_1-\hat M_2$ non-positive — so the construction is not a valid POVM as written; the correct Ivanovic–Dieks–Peres normalization is $\tfrac{1}{1+\vert c\vert}$, the "Key property" probabilities $1-\vert c\vert^2$/$\vert c\vert^2$ should be $1-\vert c\vert$/$\vert c\vert$, and the result is asserted with no intermediate steps), **NB-137** (MEDIUM — the `Naimark's Theorem` box equation `eq-povm-neumark` $\hat M_i=\operatorname{Tr}_\text{aux}(\hat P_i(\hat\rho\otimes\vert0\rangle\langle0\vert_\text{aux}))$ equates the state-independent $\hat M_i$ to an $\hat\rho$-dependent RHS; the correct form replaces $\hat\rho$ with $\hat I_{\mathcal H}$), **NB-138** (LOW — the Heterodyne example writes the complex coherent-state overlap $\langle\alpha\vert\beta\rangle$ equal to a real exponential; should be the modulus), and **NB-139** (LOW — homework/body symbol drift: overlap $s$ vs $c$, ancilla space $\mathcal K$ vs $\mathcal H_\text{aux}$). The octahedral IC-POVM completeness check ($\sum_{k=1}^{6}\tfrac13\vert\phi_k\rangle\langle\phi_k\vert=\hat I$), the POVM normalization check, and proof-style homework P1/P2/P5/P6/P7 were verified correct and not ticketed. Phase D completes the A–D pass for `6-3-2-povm`, and the cursor advanced to `6-3-3-quantum-channels` at Phase A.

With NB-150 closed, the open Phase C tickets for `6-3-3-quantum-channels` are now NB-151, NB-153, NB-154. **NB-151** was closed 2026-05-22 — cell-3 homework Problem 6's phase-flip channel symbol $\mathcal{E}_\text{phase}$, which collided with the cell-2 body's phase-damping ($T_2$ dephasing) channel of the same name, was renamed $\mathcal{E}_Z$ to match homework Problem 7 (one `assert count==4` joined-text replacement on cell 3). The **Phase D (Derivation quality)** audit ran 2026-05-22 — the notebook's three derivation-bearing blocks were structurally checked against `derivation-quality.md` and `_refs/`. `_refs/HOMEWORK.md` § 6.3.3 carries the §6.3.3 homework set with worked solutions, but its Solution 2 (depolarizing channel) gives Kraus operators $\hat K_0=\sqrt{1-p}\,\hat I$, $\hat K_j=\sqrt{p/3}\,\hat\sigma^j$ that do **not** reproduce the channel $(1-p)\hat\rho+\tfrac p2\hat I$ — NumPy: wrong in 500/500 random cases — and the reference itself flags the slip mid-solution (\"Wait, let me recalculate\"); the notebook's mathematically correct $p/4$ convention ($\hat K_0=\sqrt{1-3p/4}\,\hat I$, $\hat K_{1,2,3}=\sqrt{p/4}\,\hat X/\hat Y/\hat Z$, NumPy-verified to reproduce the channel and satisfy completeness) must **not** be aligned to the buggy reference, so `derivation-quality.md`'s intrinsic depth standard applies (the NB-110 / NB-136 / NB-150 precedent). All notebook physics was NumPy-verified correct (the Pauli identity $\hat X\hat\rho\hat X+\hat Y\hat\rho\hat Y+\hat Z\hat\rho\hat Z=2\hat I-\hat\rho$ over 2000 random trace-1 qubit states; depolarizing Kraus completeness and mixing-form reproduction over 500 random $p$; $\mathcal{E}_\text{amp}$ and $\mathcal{E}_Z$ commute — context for homework P6(c)). Three single-scope derivation-depth tickets were filed — **NB-155** (MEDIUM — the depolarizing-channel **Check** asserts the Pauli identity with zero supporting steps and compresses the Kraus→mixing-form substitution to one clause), **NB-156** (LOW — the `Proof Sketch` trace-preserving equality chain is a single long one-line `$$`, the NB-141-deferred `\begin{split}` layout call), and **NB-157** (LOW — the `Proof Sketch` completely-positive part omits the one-line reason for $\hat K_k\hat\rho\hat K_k^\dagger\ge0$ and does not name the extended-map Kraus operators $\hat K_k\otimes\hat I_B$). Phase D completes the A–D pass for `6-4-1-decoherence`, and the cursor advanced to `6-4-2-lindblad-master-equation` at Phase A.
