| NB-228 | 2026-05-23 | `4-1-3-gauge-invariance` | Phase D fix (the one actionable queue ticket — NB-66 and NB-81 remain deferred on unattended runs pending professor / rule sign-off): cell 3 of `notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-1-3-gauge-invariance.ipynb`, Homework Problem 4 (*Energy under gauge transformation*) part (a) asked the student to "show explicitly" that $\hat{H}'\psi_n'=E_n\psi_n'$ — rigorously true only for a time-independent gauge function. For a general $\alpha(\boldsymbol{r},t)$ the gauge transformation includes $\phi\to\phi-\partial_t\alpha$, giving $\hat{H}'=U\hat{H}U^\dagger-q\,\partial_t\alpha$ and $\hat{H}'\psi_n'=(E_n-q\,\partial_t\alpha)\psi_n'$ — not an eigenvalue equation. homework-designer + derivation-designer specified → notebook-writer applied the qualifier "For a time-independent gauge function $\alpha(\boldsymbol{r})$," to the start of part (a) (lower-casing the following "Refute" → "refute") via Bash + Python json I/O — one substring replace inside cell-3 source entry 26, replacement count asserted = 1, no LaTeX retyped. The qualifier was placed on **part (a) only**, not the Problem-4 title/setup line: parts (b) and (c) deliberately invoke the time-dependent / additive-constant scalar-potential freedom — (b)’s premise that $q\langle\phi\rangle$ is gauge-dependent is false under a strictly static gauge transformation ($\phi$ unchanged) — so a whole-problem static restriction would have invalidated (b). Scoping the restriction to (a) makes (a)’s "show explicitly" statement rigorously true while leaving the (b)/(c) arc (energy-zero ambiguity, spectroscopic differences) intact and correct; no new ticket needed. Single-scope: part (a) wording only; no equation label, derivation, prompt, or other homework content changed. Both static validators (`safe_edit.py validate`, `validate_project.py --scope 4-1-3-gauge-invariance`) pass clean. Phase cursor unchanged (Round 2 / Phase A / `4-2-berry-phase`) — an ad hoc ticket fix does not advance it (`.claude/README.md` § 1.4). Open queue 3 → 2 (NB-66, NB-81 remain — both deferred; no actionable tickets remain). |
| NB-227 | 2026-05-23 | `4-1-3-gauge-invariance` | Phase D fix (one of the two actionable 2026-05-23 Phase D findings on this notebook; NB-66 and NB-81 remain deferred on unattended runs pending professor / rule sign-off): cell 2 of `notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-1-3-gauge-invariance.ipynb`, the `Open Path vs Closed Loop` `:::{admonition}` (`:class: note`) box asserted that the single-endpoint object $\mathrm{e}^{+\mathrm{i}q\int_{\mathcal{C}}\boldsymbol{A}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l}/\hbar}\,\psi(\boldsymbol{r}_{\text{end}})$ is gauge-invariant — it is not (wrong sign; under $\boldsymbol{A}\to\boldsymbol{A}+\nabla\alpha$ it acquires $\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}q[2\alpha(\boldsymbol{r}_{\text{end}})-\alpha(\boldsymbol{r}_{\text{start}})]/\hbar}$, NumPy-verified). Fix option (a) from the ticket applied: the object was replaced with the gauge-invariant bilinear $\psi^*(\boldsymbol{r}_{\text{start}})\,\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}q\int_{\mathcal{C}}\boldsymbol{A}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l}/\hbar}\,\psi(\boldsymbol{r}_{\text{end}})$ — the Wilson-line exponent sign flipped $+\to-$ (matching the §4.1.1 covariant derivative $\boldsymbol{D}=\nabla-(\mathrm{i}q/\hbar)\boldsymbol{A}$ and the `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` § Gauge Connection compensating factor $\mathrm{e}^{-(\mathrm{i}/\hbar)A_\nu\delta x^\nu}$) and a $\psi^*(\boldsymbol{r}_{\text{start}})$ overlap factor prepended, so the combination genuinely \"compares the wavefunction at the two endpoints\" as the box's surrounding prose states. Option (a) chosen over (b) (keep the single transported object, relabel it gauge-*covariant*) because the bilinear is a genuine gauge-invariant *number*, fits the section's gauge-invariant-vs-gauge-dependent thread, and needs no prose rewording. Gauge invariance of the bilinear — and gauge-covariance of the single transported object (it transforms like $\psi(\boldsymbol{r}_{\text{start}})$) — NumPy-verified. derivation-designer audited → notebook-writer applied via Bash + Python json I/O: one substring replace inside cell-2 source entry 103, replacement count asserted = 1, no LaTeX retyped elsewhere. Cell-3 P5 wrote the condition as $\langle\psi\vert\hat{E}_i^\dagger\hat{E}_j\vert\phi\rangle=\delta_{ij}f(i,j)$ — two defects: (a) inconsistent with the cell-2 body `eq-qec-knill-laflamme` form $\hat{\Pi}\hat{E}_a^\dagger\hat{E}_b\hat{\Pi}=C_{ab}\hat{\Pi}$; the $\delta_{ij}$ also wrongly forced the Hermitian error-correlation matrix $C_{ij}$ diagonal. derivation-designer confirmed the finding against `derivation-quality.md` (within-notebook consistency) and `_refs/` — `_refs/HOMEWORK.md` § 6.4.3 carries only the 3-qubit bit-flip code / syndrome / recovery and no Knill-Laflamme material, so P5 is the notebook’s own design and must be internally consistent with the body box. notebook-writer restated the P5 condition via Bash + Python json I/O — one assert-count-guarded (count == 1) substring replacement on the joined cell-3 source, re-split with `text_to_source`: the displayed equation became $\langle\psi\vert\hat{E}_i^\dagger\hat{E}_j\vert\phi\rangle=C_{ij}\,\langle\psi\vert\phi\rangle$, the explanatory sentence now names $C_{ij}$ “a Hermitian matrix of constants independent of $\vert\psi\rangle$ and $\vert\phi\rangle$” (was “$f(i,j)$ ... independent of ...”), and a closing sentence ties it to the body: “Equivalently, in terms of the code-space projector $\hat{\Pi}$, this is the projector form $\hat{\Pi}\hat{E}_i^\dagger\hat{E}_j\hat{\Pi}=C_{ij}\hat{\Pi}$ of {eq}`eq-qec-knill-laflamme`.” P5(a)-(c) sub-parts left unchanged — P5(a) (verify for $\{\hat{I},\hat{X}_1,\hat{X}_2,\hat{X}_3\}$) still holds since the bit-flip code is non-degenerate ($C_{ij}=\delta_{ij}$), and (b)/(c) are open-ended and answered from the corrected statement. Cell count 4, the 4-cell subsection structure intact, no other cell or problem changed. Both static validators (`safe_edit.py validate`, `validate_project.py --scope 6-4-3-error-correction`) pass clean. | NB-205 | 2026-05-23 | `6-4-3-error-correction` | Phase D fix (queued from the 2026-05-23 Phase D audit — the first-resolved of the four `6-4-3` Phase D tickets NB-205–NB-208; NB-66 and NB-81 remain deferred on unattended runs): the cell-2 `:::{admonition} Derivation: Threshold Scaling Law` (`:class: dropdown information`) Step 4 hand-waved the threshold's origin — it wrote "Absorbing $N_{\min}$ into a normalized form gives the standard scaling law" — but $N_{\min}$, defined in Step 3 as the number of minimum-weight uncorrectable error chains, is *not* a $d$-independent constant: it grows exponentially with the code distance, so absorbing it into the $d$-independent $p_\text{th}$ asserts the threshold rather than deriving it (`derivation-quality.md` § Root causes "reverse-engineering from the answer" / "missing motivation for why this form"). derivation-designer confirmed the finding against `derivation-quality.md` and `_refs/` — `_refs/HOMEWORK.md` § 6.4.3 carries no threshold derivation (only the 3-qubit bit-flip code / syndrome / recovery), so the block is the notebook's own design and must stand on its own (`derivation-quality.md` § Scope). notebook-writer rewrote Step 4 (retitled "Normalize to the threshold" → "The threshold as a crossover") via Bash + Python json I/O — one assert-count-guarded (count == 1) substring replacement on the joined cell-2 source, re-split with `text_to_source`: the new Step 4 states $N_{\min}\sim\mu^d$ for a surface-code lattice with path-counting constant $\mu$, substitutes into the Step 3 estimate to $p_\text{logical}\sim\mu^d p^{(d+1)/2}=(\mu^2 p)^{(d+1)/2}/\mu$ (the regrouping $\mu^d=\mu^{d+1}/\mu=(\mu^2)^{(d+1)/2}/\mu$ shown explicitly), identifies $p_\text{th}\sim1/\mu^2$ as the crossover where combinatorial growth balances error suppression, and closes by explaining that the threshold is $d$-independent because both $\mu^d$ and $p^{(d+1)/2}$ scale with $d$ in the exponent (for $p<p_\text{th}$ the base $\mu^2 p<1$ so larger $d$ drives $p_\text{logical}\to0$; for $p>p_\text{th}$ larger $d$ makes the qubit worse). The reproduced scaling-law equation was kept unlabelled (the `eq-qec-threshold-scaling` label stays on the `important`-box equation — no duplicate); the Step 1 / Step 3 ceiling-notation inconsistency is the separate open ticket NB-207 and was left untouched. | NB-193 | 2026-05-23 | `6-4-2-lindblad-master-equation` | Phase D fix (queued from the 2026-05-23 Phase D audit — the third and last of the three `6-4-2` Phase D tickets NB-191–NB-193; NB-66 and NB-81 remain deferred on unattended runs): the cell-2 `### Common Lindblad Operators` Depolarizing item (item 3) `**Effect**` line stated the channel drives any state toward the maximally mixed state $\hat{I}/2$ "on timescale $1/\Gamma_d$", inconsistent with the rate the notebook's own homework P6(b) derives — the Bloch vector decays as $\dot{\boldsymbol r}=-(4\Gamma_d/3)\boldsymbol r$, an e-folding time $3/(4\Gamma_d)$, not $1/\Gamma_d$ (off by a factor $4/3$; the `derivation-quality.md` within-notebook consistency standard). Physics independently re-verified before the edit: the depolarizing Lindblad superoperator equals $(4\Gamma_d/3)(\hat{I}/2-\hat\rho)$ to machine precision ($\sim10^{-17}$) over 2000 random qubit states, and a direct integration from $\vert 0\rangle\langle 0\vert$ shows the deviation $\hat\rho-\hat{I}/2$ decays by exactly $1/e$ at $t=3/(4\Gamma_d)$ (the body's $1/\Gamma_d$ gives factor 0.264, not $1/e$). lecture-designer → notebook-writer rewrote the `**Effect**` line to the exact, homework-consistent form: \"Drives any state toward the maximally mixed state $\hat{I}/2$, with the deviation $\hat{\rho} - \hat{I}/2$ decaying as $\mathrm{e}^{-4\Gamma_d t/3}$ (e-folding time $3/(4\Gamma_d)$)\" — the deviation-from-maximally-mixed form introduces no new notation (no Bloch vector) yet is exactly equivalent to the homework P6(b) result $\dot{\boldsymbol r}=-(4\Gamma_d/3)\boldsymbol r$, and the explicit decay law mirrors the Phase Damping `**Effect**` bullet's style. Applied via Bash + Python json I/O — one assert-count-guarded single-line replacement on the joined cell-2 source, re-split with `text_to_source`; `\mathrm{e}` upright per `physics-conventions.md`, inline math only (no `$$` block touched). | NB-192 | 2026-05-23 | `6-4-2-lindblad-master-equation` | Phase D fix (queued from the 2026-05-23 Phase D audit — the second-resolved of the three `6-4-2` Phase D tickets NB-191–NB-193; NB-66 and NB-81 remain deferred on unattended runs): the cell-2 `:::{admonition} Example: Thermal Steady State` (`:class: dropdown example`) collapsed its solution to one clause — \"Balancing gain and loss: $\rho_{11}^\infty/\rho_{00}^\infty=\bar{n}_\text{th}/(\bar{n}_\text{th}+1)=\mathrm{e}^{-\hbar\omega/k_BT}$ (Boltzmann factor)\" — with zero intermediate steps (the NB-98 worked-example-collapse precedent, `derivation-quality.md` § minimum derivation depth). derivation-designer → notebook-writer expanded the `**Solution.**` paragraph into a four-step chain: (1) name the two opposing population flows and their rates — absorption $\Gamma_\uparrow=\Gamma_1\bar{n}_\text{th}$ via $\hat{L}_2$, decay $\Gamma_\downarrow=\Gamma_1\bar{n}_\text{th}+\Gamma_1=\Gamma_1(\bar{n}_\text{th}+1)$ via $\hat{L}_1$; (2) the excited-state population equation $\dot{\rho}_{11}=\Gamma_\uparrow\rho_{00}-\Gamma_\downarrow\rho_{11}$ (a displayed `$$` block) read off from the amplitude-damping gain/loss pattern of the body `### Common Lindblad Operators`; (3) a `\begin{split}` block setting $\dot{\rho}_{11}=0$ → population balance $\Gamma_\uparrow\rho_{00}^\infty=\Gamma_\downarrow\rho_{11}^\infty$ → ratio $\rho_{11}^\infty/\rho_{00}^\infty=\Gamma_\uparrow/\Gamma_\downarrow=\bar{n}_\text{th}/(\bar{n}_\text{th}+1)$; (4) inserting the Bose-Einstein occupation $\bar{n}_\text{th}=1/(\mathrm{e}^{\hbar\omega/k_BT}-1)$ to collapse the ratio to the Boltzmann factor $\mathrm{e}^{-\hbar\omega/k_BT}$ (a final displayed `$$`), then identifying it with the thermal state $\hat{\rho}_\infty=\mathrm{e}^{-\beta\hat{H}}/Z$, $\hat{H}=\hbar\omega\,\vert 1\rangle\langle 1\vert$ (§6.1.2). Physics NumPy-verified before the edit: $\dot{\rho}_{11}=\Gamma_\uparrow\rho_{00}-\Gamma_\downarrow\rho_{11}$ exact over 2000 random qubit states, the thermal diagonal state is an exact steady state of $\hat{L}_1,\hat{L}_2$ ($\Vert\mathcal{L}(\hat{\rho}_\infty)\Vert\sim10^{-16}$), and $\bar{n}_\text{th}/(\bar{n}_\text{th}+1)=\mathrm{e}^{-\hbar\omega/k_BT}$. Applied via Bash + Python json I/O — one assert-count-guarded single-line replacement of the `**Solution.**` line on the joined cell-2 source, re-split with `text_to_source`; the new `$$` blocks carry blank lines above/below and no labels, the kets use `\vert`, the multi-relation step uses `\begin{split}` (no `\quad`), per `physics-conventions.md` § Display Math. Cell count 4, the 4-cell subsection structure intact, no other cell or block touched. The amplitude-damping ladder operators `\hat{\sigma}_\pm` are system-agnostic and were deliberately left untouched (confirmed still present); a post-edit scan confirms zero `\hat{\sigma}^\mu` superscript-Pauli forms remain. QI `\hat{X}`/`\hat{Y}`/`\hat{Z}` are the same matrices as the spin Paulis, so the physics is identical (the P6(b) identity rests on $\sum_a\hat\sigma^a\hat\sigma^b\hat\sigma^a=-\hat\sigma^b$). Cell count 4, the 4-cell subsection structure intact, no physics or other content changed. The `### Summary` "Decoherence-free subspaces" bullet — the direct recap of the edited body section, which carried the identical singlet-only understatement — was aligned in the same edit so the notebook stays internally consistent. The convention-dependent prefactor was pinned down: the exponent now carries the dimensionally-consistent factor $4/\hbar^2$ — $4=2^2$ from the relative coupling $2g_k$ squared (the old form had bare coefficient 1) — with $J(\omega)=\sum_k g_k^2\delta(\omega-\omega_k)$ stated as the bath spectral density and $g_k$ the per-mode coupling energy; the ohmic high-$T$ limit $\Gamma(t)\approx\gamma t$ with $\gamma\propto k_BT$ reproduces `eq-decoherence-exponential`. The body lead-in changed "The microscopic derivation:" → "The microscopic origin of this decay:". Applied via Bash + Python json I/O — two assert-count-guarded substring replacements on the joined cell-2 source, re-split with `text_to_source`; cell count 4 and the 4-cell subsection structure intact, no equation label removed (the old dropdown `$$` block was unlabelled), no other cell touched. | NB-156 | 2026-05-22 | `6-3-3-quantum-channels` | Phase D derivation-depth fix (queued from the 2026-05-22 Phase D audit): the cell-2 (id `ccc68861`) `:::{admonition} Proof Sketch` (`:class: dropdown information`) **Trace-preserving** equality was a single long one-line `$$…$$` chaining four distinct trace manipulations — $\operatorname{Tr}(\sum_k \hat{K}_k\hat\rho\hat{K}_k^\dagger)=\sum_k\operatorname{Tr}(\hat\rho\hat{K}_k^\dagger\hat{K}_k)=\operatorname{Tr}(\hat\rho\sum_k\hat{K}_k^\dagger\hat{K}_k)=\operatorname{Tr}(\hat\rho\hat{I})=\operatorname{Tr}(\hat\rho)$ — against `physics-conventions.md` § Display Math (multiline equations use `\begin{split}`); the NB-141 closure note had explicitly deferred the `\begin{split}` layout call to a Phase D / `derivation-quality.md` decision. notebook-writer converted it to a six-line `&=`-aligned `\begin{split}` block, one relation per line: the linearity step (pull the sum outside) is now shown explicitly as its own line $\sum_k\operatorname{Tr}(\hat{K}_k\hat\rho\hat{K}_k^\dagger)$ before the cyclicity step, and the completeness substitution and $\operatorname{Tr}(\hat\rho\hat{I})=\operatorname{Tr}(\hat\rho)$ are separate lines, so the chain reads as a stepwise derivation. A short prose lead-in names the manipulations in order (trace linearity, cyclic invariance, the Kraus completeness relation $\sum_k\hat{K}_k^\dagger\hat{K}_k=\hat{I}$), matching the gold-reference derivation style of `6-1-1-mixed-states` (Von Neumann Equation), which justifies steps in a prose lead-in rather than with inline `\text{}` annotations (no corpus `\begin{split}` block uses per-line `\text{}` justifications). No physics change — the chain was already correct and complete. Applied via Bash + Python json I/O — one `assert count==1` substring replacement on the joined cell-2 source, re-split with `text_to_source`; cell-2 source 250 → 257 entries, max line 616 chars (well under the 1000-char source-line validator limit), cell count 4, the 4-cell structure intact. | NB-150 | 2026-05-22 | `6-3-3-quantum-channels` | Phase C / Phase-D-overlap entropy-monotonicity fix (queued from the 2026-05-22 Phase C audit): the cell-2 body section `### Reversibility and Irreversibility` listed, under "Irreversible (Non-Unitary) Channel", the bullet "Entropy generally increases: $S(\mathcal{E}(\hat\rho)) > S(\hat\rho)$ (output more mixed)" — false for a general CPTP map. The contradictory `### Summary` $S(\mathcal{E}(\hat\rho))\le S(\hat\rho)$ claim flagged at audit time had already been removed by the NB-149 Summary restructure, so the body bullet was the sole remaining offender. derivation-designer verified against general QM and NumPy: unitary channels conserve $S$ ($\Delta S=0$); the unital channels depolarizing and phase damping ($\mathcal{E}(\hat I)=\hat I$, both confirmed unital) never decrease $S$ (min $\Delta S\ge0$ over 1000 random states each); non-unital amplitude damping is non-monotone (1234/2000 random states $S$ up, 766 down) and at $\gamma=1$ purifies any input to $\vert0\rangle\langle0\vert$; the two audit examples reproduced exactly (depolarizing $p=0.5$ on $\vert0\rangle$: $S\,0\to0.56$; amplitude damping $\gamma=1$ on $\mathrm{diag}(0.7,0.3)$: $S\,0.61\to0$). `_refs/QuantumStatistics.nb` carries no quantum-channel / Kraus / CPTP material, so `derivation-quality.md`'s intrinsic standard applies (the NB-110 / NB-136 precedent). notebook-writer replaced the single cell-2 source-array entry (idx 187, in-place single-entry replacement, no re-split) with: "Entropy is not monotonic in general: a **unital** channel — one with $\mathcal{E}(\hat{I}) = \hat{I}$, such as depolarizing or phase damping — cannot decrease entropy, $S(\mathcal{E}(\hat{\rho})) \geq S(\hat{\rho})$, whereas a non-unital channel such as amplitude damping can raise *or* lower it (damping toward the pure ground state can even purify a mixed input)." The Reversible-list $S(\mathcal{E}(\hat\rho))=S(\hat\rho)$ unitary bullet was confirmed correct and left unchanged; the irreversibility framing (no inverse, information lost) is untouched, so the section now cleanly separates irreversibility from entropy monotonicity. | NB-138 | 2026-05-21 | `6-3-2-povm` | Phase D heterodyne-overlap notation fix (queued from the Phase D audit): cell 2 `:::{admonition} Example: Heterodyne Detection (Optical POVM)` (`:class: dropdown example`) wrote the coherent-state overlap as the real equality $\langle\alpha\vert\beta\rangle=\mathrm{e}^{-\vert\alpha-\beta\vert^2/2}$, but the LHS is a complex inner product carrying a phase ($\langle\alpha\vert\beta\rangle=\mathrm{e}^{-\vert\alpha\vert^2/2-\vert\beta\vert^2/2+\alpha^{*}\beta}$) while the RHS is positive real — only the modulus is real. notebook-writer applied the ticket fix via Bash + Python json I/O — one `assert count==1` substring replacement on the joined cell-2 text changing the overlap to the modulus form $\vert\langle\alpha\vert\beta\rangle\vert=\mathrm{e}^{-\vert\alpha-\beta\vert^2/2}$, re-split with `text_to_source`. The physics was confirmed before the edit: $\vert\langle\alpha\vert\beta\rangle\vert^2=\mathrm{e}^{-\vert\alpha-\beta\vert^2}$ follows from $\vert\alpha-\beta\vert^2=\vert\alpha\vert^2+\vert\beta\vert^2-2\,\mathrm{Re}(\alpha^{*}\beta)$. The bullet pedagogical point — coherent states are non-orthogonal, $\vert\langle\alpha\vert\beta\rangle\vert\neq0$ for $\alpha\neq\beta$ — is unchanged; the existing `\vert \alpha-\beta\vert` spacing was kept so the glued-`\vert` validator check does not trip. Cell count 4 and the 4-cell structure unchanged. Both static validators (`safe_edit.py validate`, `validate_project.py --scope 6-3-2-povm`) clean |
| NB-137 | 2026-05-21 | `6-3-2-povm` | Phase D Naimark-equation fix (queued from the Phase D audit): cell 2 `:::{admonition} Naimark's Theorem` (`:class: important`) stated the element-recovery identity `eq-povm-neumark` as $\hat M_i=\operatorname{Tr}_\text{aux}(\hat P_i(\hat\rho\otimes\vert0\rangle\langle0\vert_\text{aux}))$ — logically inconsistent, equating the fixed, state-independent POVM element $\hat M_i$ to an $\hat\rho$-dependent RHS. derivation-designer confirmed the fix; the physics was independently NumPy-verified across four textbook Naimark dilations (n=2,3,4 outcomes, d=2,3 dimensions; trivial embedding $\hat I_{\mathcal H}\otimes\vert0\rangle_\text{aux}$, orthogonal projectors $\hat P_i$ on the extended space): the corrected form $\hat M_i=\operatorname{Tr}_\text{aux}(\hat P_i(\hat I_{\mathcal H}\otimes\vert0\rangle\langle0\vert_\text{aux}))$ recovers $\hat M_i$ exactly (err ~1e-16), the $\hat\rho$ form does not (err 0.2–0.9), and the full trace $\operatorname{Tr}[\hat P_i(\hat\rho\otimes\vert0\rangle\langle0\vert_\text{aux})]$ reproduces $p_i=\operatorname{Tr}(\hat M_i\hat\rho)$ — confirming the box's "$\hat M_i=$" LHS selects the element-recovery form. notebook-writer applied the recommended minimal fix via Bash + Python json I/O — one `assert`-guarded cell-2 source-array entry replaced, $\hat\rho\to\hat I_{\mathcal H}$ (written `\hat{I}_{\mathcal{H}}`); the box's "Physical picture" prose and all other math unchanged. `safe_edit.py validate` + `validate_project.py --scope 6-3-2-povm` pass. |

Downstream homework-solutions re-check filed as NB-140. |

| NB-132 | 2026-05-21 | `6-3-2-povm` | Phase C body / Summary contradiction fix (queued from the Phase C audit): cell 2’s `### Measurement with a POVM` presented the square-root (Lüders) form `eq-povm-collapse` $\hat\rho'_i=\sqrt{\hat M_i}\hat\rho\sqrt{\hat M_i}/\operatorname{Tr}(\hat M_i\hat\rho)$ as *the* post-measurement state, contradicting `### Summary` bullets 2–3, which say the post-measurement state is **not** determined by the POVM alone (the Summary claim was also undeveloped in the body — a phantom-summary issue). The ticket’s recommended **conservative option** was applied — autonomous-safe because it adds only a factual caveat from standard measurement theory and makes no pedagogical judgement call. lecture-designer specified a one-paragraph caveat; the physics was confirmed against standard QM — a POVM element factors as $\hat M_i=\hat A_i^\dagger\hat A_i$ in many ways (any $\hat A_i=\hat U_i\sqrt{\hat M_i}$ with $\hat U_i$ unitary reproduces $p_i=\operatorname{Tr}(\hat M_i\hat\rho)$ but updates the state differently), so the post-measurement state is genuinely realization-dependent and the square-root form is only the canonical Lüders choice. notebook-writer applied via Bash + Python json I/O — one `assert count==1` substring replacement on the joined cell-2 text, re-split with `text_to_source`. The new paragraph, inserted between the $\sqrt{\hat M_i}$ unique-square-root sentence and the **Normalization check** line, states `eq-povm-collapse` is the **Lüders update**, the canonical state-update rule; that — unlike the outcome probability — the post-measurement state is **not** fixed by the POVM alone, since the same element can be implemented by different physical measurements that share outcome statistics yet leave the system in different states; and that the Lüders form is the default when no apparatus detail is specified. The body’s true statement that $\sqrt{\hat M_i}$ is the unique *positive* square root was left intact. The `### Summary` needed no edit — bullets 2–3 were already the physically correct statement, so the contradiction was one-sided (the body overclaimed) and is resolved by the body caveat alone, which also develops the previously phantom realization-dependence claim. | NB-124 | 2026-05-21 | `6-3-1-projective-measurement` | Phase D notation-attribution fix (last open Phase D ticket, queued from the Phase D audit): cell 2 — the **Normalization Check** below the `Measurement Postulate: Probability and Collapse` box justified $\operatorname{Tr}(\hat\rho')=\operatorname{Tr}(\hat P_i\hat\rho\hat P_i)/p_i=1$ with "since $\hat P_i^2=\hat P_i$ and $\hat P_i$ is Hermitian", but the identity $\operatorname{Tr}(\hat P_i\hat\rho\hat P_i)=p_i$ uses the **cyclic invariance of the trace** plus idempotence $\hat P_i^2=\hat P_i$ — Hermiticity is not used (`derivation-quality.md` failure mode "misleading notation choices": citing Hermiticity misleads the reader into thinking it is the relevant property). derivation-designer confirmed the claim with a NumPy check — $\operatorname{Tr}(P\rho P)=\operatorname{Tr}(P\rho)$ holds even for an oblique (non-Hermitian) idempotent $P$ ($P^2=P$, $P\ne P^\dagger$), so Hermiticity is genuinely irrelevant to the identity. notebook-writer applied via Bash + Python json I/O — two `assert count==1` cell-2 substring replacements: (i) the Normalization-Check justification was corrected to "since $\hat P_i^2=\hat P_i$ and the trace is cyclic"; (ii) the `### Repeated Measurements` proof — whose display chain ends $\operatorname{Tr}(\hat P_i\hat\rho\hat P_i)/p_i=p_i/p_i=1$ with the final $=p_i$ step unjustified — was given an explicit shared justification line ("Here $\hat P_i\hat P_i=\hat P_i$ by idempotence, and the final equality uses the cyclic invariance of the trace: $\operatorname{Tr}(\hat P_i\hat\rho\hat P_i)=\operatorname{Tr}(\hat P_i\hat P_i\hat\rho)=\operatorname{Tr}(\hat P_i\hat\rho)=p_i$"). Only the two cell-2 prose lines changed; no physics, derivation chain, or display equation altered; longest cell-2 source entry 671 chars. With NB-124 closed, all Phase D tickets for `6-3-1-projective-measurement` are resolved. Both static validators (`safe_edit.py validate`, `validate_project.py --scope 6-3-1-projective-measurement`) clean |

The Zeno effect has no `_refs/` counterpart, so the intrinsic depth standard applies (NB-110 precedent). derivation-designer drafted the corrected chain; the physics was NumPy-verified before the edit — the exact single-interval survival probability matches $1-c(\Delta t)^2$ with $c=(\Delta H)^2/\hbar^2$ to $O((\Delta t)^3)$ across 4 random Hermitian $\hat H$, the first-order-only expansion was confirmed to exceed 1, and the homework P9 case $\hat H=\tfrac{\hbar\omega}{2}\hat Y$ gives $c=\omega^2/4$ (matching the exact $\cos^2(\omega\Delta t/2)$ survival). notebook-writer applied via Bash + Python json I/O — an index-anchored replacement of the whole admonition block (between the `Derivation` and `Discussion` admonition openers, `assert count==1` plus pre/post checks that the buggy 'first order' text is gone and `### Degenerate Eigenvalues` is untouched), re-split with `text_to_source`. The rebuilt dropdown is an 11-displayed-line chain: second-order expansion of the evolution operator, the survival amplitude $\langle\psi_0\vert\psi(\Delta t)\rangle$, a four-line `\begin{split}` modulus-squared computation exhibiting the linear-term cancellation and the quadratic fall-off, the coefficient identified as the energy variance $c=(\Delta H)^2/\hbar^2$, the $n=T/\Delta t$ independent-interval product, and the $\ln P=(T/\Delta t)\ln(1-c(\Delta t)^2)\approx-cT\Delta t\to 0$ limit step. Longest cell-2 source entry 671 chars (under the 1000-char limit). Both static validators (`safe_edit.py validate`, `validate_project.py --scope 6-3-1-projective-measurement`) clean |

| NB-111 | 2026-05-21 | `6-2-3-bell-inequality` | Phase D derivation-motivation fix: the cell-2 `Derivation: Quantum CHSH Violation` (`dropdown information`) introduced its four optimal measurement directions with only the bare line "To maximize CHSH, we choose:" — no rationale for *why* the configuration is optimal (`derivation-quality.md` failure mode 4, missing motivation for "why this form"). derivation-designer drafted a motivation paragraph, inserted as its own paragraph before the direction-vector display blocks: the CHSH combination $E_{11}+E_{12}+E_{21}-E_{22}$ is largest in magnitude when all four terms add with the same sign, and since the singlet correlator $E=-\cos\theta$ depends only on the inter-direction angle, the optimal configuration places all four directions in the $x$-$z$ plane with Alice's two settings mutually orthogonal and Bob's two at $\pm45^\circ$ from $\hat{\boldsymbol a}_1$ — three of the four Alice-Bob pairs at $45^\circ$, the pair $(\hat{\boldsymbol a}_2,\hat{\boldsymbol b}_2)$ at $135^\circ$. Part (b) was rewritten as a wavepacket centred at the parameter point $\boldsymbol{R}$ with the Peierls phase referenced from $\boldsymbol{R}$ (lower limit), giving $\boldsymbol{A}_\text{Berry}(\boldsymbol{R})=+(q/\hbar)\boldsymbol{A}(\boldsymbol{R})$ — NumPy-verified. Part (c) keeps target $q\Phi/\hbar$ (was correct), with $\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{r}\to\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{R}$. Single-scope: P5(b)/(c) only; both static validators clean. Phase Cursor unchanged — this was an ad hoc queue-ticket fix, not a phase-audit pass (`.claude/README.md` § 1.4). Ticket Queue now has 2 OPEN (NB-66, NB-81, both deferred). - **2026-05-23** — **Phase D audit — `4-2-2-aharonov-bohm-effect`** (§4.2.2 subsection — Round 2; audit pass, one ticket NB-242 filed). `feedback.md` had no open professor items (the homework-solutions directive is DONE 2026-05-20). The `progress.md` Ticket Queue § Open held only the two deferred tickets NB-66 (professor sign-off on the entropy convention) and NB-81 (corpus-wide trace-macro rule decision), neither actionable on unattended runs — so with no actionable queue ticket this run advanced the phase program (scheduled-task orchestration step 3): the **Phase D (Derivation quality)** audit on `4-2-2-aharonov-bohm-effect`, the next phase-audit step. The notebook's one `dropdown information` block, `Derivation: Single-Path Phase from Local Gauge Transformation`, was structurally diffed against `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` § Aharonov-Bohm Effect / § Gauge Connection: the gauge-away-and-invert chain ($\boldsymbol{A}=\nabla\alpha$ curl-free, $\alpha=-\int_\mathcal{C}\boldsymbol{A}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l}'$, §4.1.3 gauge transformation to $\boldsymbol{A}=0$, inversion to $\Phi_{\mathrm{path}}[\mathcal{C}]=(q/\hbar)\int_\mathcal{C}\boldsymbol{A}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l}$) is correct, complete, every step shown, and reproduces the reference single-path Berry phase $\Theta_\mathcal{C}=(q/\hbar)\int_\mathcal{C}\boldsymbol{A}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{x}$; the two-path / Stokes assembly to $\Delta\Phi_{\mathrm{AB}}=q\Phi/\hbar$ and proof-style homework P2 / P3 / P4 were verified correct (NumPy). One single-scope MEDIUM ticket **NB-242** was filed — Homework Problem 5 (*AB phase as Berry phase*) part (b) asks to show the Berry connection $\boldsymbol{A}_\text{Berry}=\mathrm{i}\langle\psi\vert\nabla_{\boldsymbol{r}}\psi\rangle$ reduces to $+(q/\hbar)\boldsymbol{A}$, but with the ansatz $\psi=\mathrm{e}^{+\mathrm{i}q\int\boldsymbol{A}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l}'/\hbar}\tilde\psi$ stated in the same sub-part the honest computation gives $-(q/\hbar)\boldsymbol{A}$ (exactly §4.2.1's derived rule $\tilde{\boldsymbol{A}}_n=\boldsymbol{A}_n-\nabla\alpha$; NumPy-confirmed ratio $-1$), so part (c)'s $\oint\boldsymbol{A}_\text{Berry}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{r}=+q\Phi/\hbar$ inherits the wrong sign ($\oint=-q\Phi/\hbar$). This is an audit pass — the finding is recorded as a ticket, not fixed this run (`.claude/README.md` § 1.4: audits record tickets). The Phase D audit **completes the Round 2 A–D pass** for `4-2-2-aharonov-bohm-effect`; the phase cursor advanced to `4-2-3-flux-ring` at Phase A. Open queue 2 → 3 (NB-66, NB-81 deferred; NB-242 actionable). *(Scheduled-task run, no human present.)*

- **2026-05-23** — **NB-228 fix** on `4-1-3-gauge-invariance` (§4.1.3 third subsection — Round 2). `feedback.md` had no open professor items (the homework-solutions directive is DONE 2026-05-20); the `progress.md` Ticket Queue held three OPEN tickets — NB-66 (deferred, professor sign-off on the entropy convention) and NB-81 (deferred, corpus-wide trace-macro rule decision) stay deferred on unattended runs, leaving NB-228 as the one actionable ticket. Fix: cell 3 of `notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-1-3-gauge-invariance.ipynb`, Homework Problem 4 (*Energy under gauge transformation*) part (a) asked the student to "show explicitly" that $\hat{H}'\psi_n'=E_n\psi_n'$ — rigorously true only for a time-independent gauge function $\alpha(\boldsymbol{r})$. For a general $\alpha(\boldsymbol{r},t)$ the gauge transformation includes $\phi\to\phi-\partial_t\alpha$, so $\hat{H}'=U\hat{H}U^\dagger-q\,\partial_t\alpha$ and $\hat{H}'\psi_n'=(E_n-q\,\partial_t\alpha)\psi_n'$ — not an eigenvalue equation. The ticket-recommended qualifier "for a time-independent gauge function $\alpha(\boldsymbol{r})$" was added, but to **part (a) only**, not the Problem-4 title/setup line. Reason verified during the fix: parts (b) and (c) deliberately rely on the time-dependent / additive-constant scalar-potential freedom — (b) premises that $q\langle\phi\rangle$ is gauge-dependent, which is false under a strictly static gauge transformation ($\phi$ unchanged) — so restricting the whole problem to a static $\alpha$ would have invalidated (b). Scoping the restriction to (a) makes (a)’s "show explicitly" statement true and leaves the (b)/(c) arc (energy-zero ambiguity, spectroscopic differences) intact and correct; no new ticket needed. notebook-writer applied the fix via Bash + Python json I/O — one substring replace inside cell-3 source entry 26 ("(a) Refute this by showing explicitly that" → "(a) For a time-independent gauge function $\alpha(\boldsymbol{r})$, refute this by showing explicitly that"), replacement count asserted = 1, no LaTeX retyped. Single-scope: part (a) wording only; no equation label, derivation, prompt, or other homework content changed. Both static validators (`safe_edit.py validate`, `validate_project.py --scope 4-1-3-gauge-invariance`) pass clean. NB-228 closed and moved to Recently closed. Phase Cursor unchanged (Round 2 / Phase A / `4-2-berry-phase`) — an ad hoc ticket fix does not advance the cursor (`.claude/README.md` § 1.4). Open queue 3 → 2 (NB-66, NB-81 both deferred; no actionable tickets remain). *(Scheduled-task run, no human present.)*

- **2026-05-23** — **NB-227 fix** on `4-1-3-gauge-invariance` (§4.1.3 third subsection — Round 2). `feedback.md` had no open professor items (the homework-solutions directive is DONE 2026-05-20); the `progress.md` Ticket Queue held four OPEN tickets — NB-66 (deferred, professor sign-off on the entropy convention) and NB-81 (deferred, corpus-wide trace-macro rule decision) stay deferred on unattended runs, leaving NB-227 and NB-228 (the two 2026-05-23 Phase D audit findings on this notebook) actionable; NB-227 is the next pending ticket. Fix: cell 2 of `notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-1-3-gauge-invariance.ipynb`, the `Open Path vs Closed Loop` `:::{admonition}` (`:class: note`) box stated that the single-endpoint object $\mathrm{e}^{+\mathrm{i}q\int_{\mathcal{C}}\boldsymbol{A}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l}/\hbar}\,\psi(\boldsymbol{r}_{\text{end}})$ \"compares the wavefunction at the two endpoints ... is gauge-invariant\" — but that object is not gauge-invariant (wrong sign; under $\boldsymbol{A}\to\boldsymbol{A}+\nabla\alpha$ it acquires $\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}q[2\alpha(\boldsymbol{r}_{\text{end}})-\alpha(\boldsymbol{r}_{\text{start}})]/\hbar}$). Fix option (a) from the ticket applied: the non-gauge-invariant object was replaced with the gauge-invariant bilinear $\psi^*(\boldsymbol{r}_{\text{start}})\,\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}q\int_{\mathcal{C}}\boldsymbol{A}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{l}/\hbar}\,\psi(\boldsymbol{r}_{\text{end}})$ — the Wilson-line exponent sign flipped $+\to-$ and a $\psi^*(\boldsymbol{r}_{\text{start}})$ overlap factor prepended. Option (a) was chosen over option (b) (keep the single transported object and relabel it gauge-*covariant*) because the bilinear is a genuine gauge-invariant *number*, it matches the box's existing \"compares the wavefunction at the two endpoints\" prose with no further rewording, and it keeps the box on the section's gauge-invariant-vs-gauge-dependent thread without introducing the separate covariance concept. The minus sign matches the §4.1.1 covariant derivative $\boldsymbol{D}=\nabla-(\mathrm{i}q/\hbar)\boldsymbol{A}$ and `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` § Gauge Connection ($\mathrm{e}^{-(\mathrm{i}/\hbar)A_\nu\delta x^\nu}$); gauge invariance of the bilinear was NumPy-verified, and the single transported object was confirmed gauge-covariant (it transforms like $\psi(\boldsymbol{r}_{\text{start}})$). notebook-writer applied the fix via Bash + Python json I/O — one substring replace inside cell-2 source entry 103, replacement count asserted = 1, no LaTeX retyped elsewhere. Single-scope: one `note`-box sentence; no equation label, derivation, prompt, or homework content changed. Both static validators (`safe_edit.py validate`, `validate_project.py --scope 4-1-3-gauge-invariance`) pass clean. NB-227 closed and moved to Recently closed. NB-228 (the homework P4(a) static-gauge restriction) remains the one actionable open ticket. Phase Cursor unchanged (Round 2 / Phase A / `4-2-berry-phase`) — an ad hoc ticket fix does not advance the cursor (`.claude/README.md` § 1.4). Open queue 4 → 3 (NB-66, NB-81 deferred; NB-228 actionable). *(Scheduled-task run, no human present.)*

Single-scope: one dropdown, one prose clause; no displayed equation block, equation label, other dropdown, or homework changed. Purely mechanical, one prose token; no equation, label, derivation, or homework content changed (the dropdown’s already-correct `A^{\mu}` / `A_{\mu}` statements untouched). Both static validators (`safe_edit.py validate`, `validate_project.py --scope 4-1-2-electromagnetic-coupling`) pass clean. NB-217 closed and moved to Recently closed. The notebook's math was compared clause by clause against `rules/physics-conventions.md`. **Convention-clean:** upright `\mathrm{i}/\mathrm{e}/\mathrm{d}`; `\boldsymbol` vectors and `\hat` operators; plain `\nabla`; SI electromagnetic units (minimal coupling $\hat{\boldsymbol\pi}=\hat{\boldsymbol p}-q\boldsymbol A$, $\boldsymbol E=-\nabla\phi-\partial_t\boldsymbol A$, $\boldsymbol B=\nabla\times\boldsymbol A$); `\phi` reserved for the scalar electric potential (no `\Phi` misuse); `\cdot` used only for genuine vector dot products (the two occurrences are $\boldsymbol a\cdot\boldsymbol b$ and $\hat{\boldsymbol\sigma}\cdot\boldsymbol B$); `\begin{split}` for all multiline display math (no `align`/`aligned`, no side-by-side `\quad`); no bare-`|` or glued-`\vert`; consistent spin-system Pauli notation ($\hat{\boldsymbol\sigma}$, $\hat{\sigma}^{x,y,z}$) in the homework; and the $c=1$ natural-units convention explicitly declared in each tensor block (the `Field Strength Tensor` `important` box, the `Spacetime Notation` dropdown, and homework Problem 5). **One MEDIUM ticket filed — NB-217:** the cell-2 prose line introducing spacetime notation writes the 4-potential with a lowered index, $A_\mu = (\phi, \boldsymbol{A})$, assigning $+\boldsymbol{A}$ to the covariant spatial part; under the metric $g_{\mu\nu}=\mathrm{diag}(+1,-1,-1,-1)$ used in the same cell's `Spacetime Notation` dropdown the covariant 4-potential is $A_\mu=(\phi,-\boldsymbol{A})$ and it is the contravariant $A^\mu=(\phi,+\boldsymbol{A})$ — the prose contradicts the dropdown's own correct statement and `physics-conventions.md`, and is the index-error failure mode named in `derivation-quality.md`. The fix is a single-token prose change `$A_\mu$` → `$A^\mu$` (a notebook-wide scan confirmed exactly one offending occurrence). One item was **considered and not filed:** the covariant Maxwell equations and the covariant Lorentz-force equation sit in cell-2 main prose just after the `Field Strength Tensor` `important` box rather than inside an admonition/derivation/homework block — but the $c=1$ declaration in that box immediately precedes them and the passage reads as one contiguous tensor-notation block, so the convention is adequately stated. Per the audit-not-fix rule (`.claude/README.md` § 1.2) the finding was filed, not fixed. Fix: in cell 2 of `notes_src/ch4_phase-and-gauge/4-1-1-gauge-principle.ipynb`, the dropdown `Derivation: covariance of $\boldsymbol{D}$ and $D_t$` proved only that the single covariant derivative transforms homogeneously, while the lesson's keystone (gauge-invariance of the squared-operator `eq-cov-schrod`) was asserted with no connecting step. derivation-designer read `_refs/PhaseAndGauge.md` § Gauge Transformation first (the reference handles the squared operator by operator conjugation $\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{i}\chi}\nabla^2\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}\chi}=(\nabla+\mathrm{i}\nabla\chi)^2$ — the same iteration logic) and drafted a closing paragraph: the covariance proof used only $\psi'=\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}q\alpha/\hbar}\psi$, so $D_i\psi$ and $D_t\psi$ themselves transform homogeneously; the covariant derivative therefore iterates ($D'_j(D'_i\psi')=\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}q\alpha/\hbar}D_j(D_i\psi)$, shown as a `\begin{split}` display), so $\boldsymbol{D}^2\psi$ and $(-\mathrm{i}\hbar\boldsymbol{D})^2\psi$ transform homogeneously; the paragraph closes with a forward-pointer to the gauge-invariant Schrödinger equation, deliberately not naming `eq-cov-schrod` or minimal coupling inside the dropdown (`derivation-quality.md` § Scope forbids importing later-section objects; a forward-pointer is the endorsed pattern). The $D^2$-covariance identity was re-verified with sympy ($D'(D'\psi')-\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{i}q\alpha/\hbar}D(D\psi)=0$). notebook-writer applied the addition via Bash + Python json I/O (`json.dump`, `indent=1`, `ensure_ascii=False`) — one assert-count-guarded (count == 1) substring insertion before the dropdown's closing `:::`, re-split with `text_to_source`. Single-scope, within the one dropdown; no equation label, homework, or other cell touched. Both static validators (`safe_edit.py validate`, `validate_project.py --scope 4-1-1-gauge-principle`) pass clean. NB-214 closed and moved to Recently closed. Phase Cursor unchanged (Round 2 / Phase A / `4-1-2-electromagnetic-coupling`): NB-214 is an ad hoc Phase-D fix ticket, not a phase audit, so the cursor does not advance (`.claude/README.md` § 1.4). The fix stays entirely inside the dropdown; no ripple to the body labelled equation. Cell count 4, the 4-cell subsection structure intact, no other cell changed. Cell-3 homework Problem 5 (“Knill-Laflamme Conditions”) stated the Knill-Laflamme condition as $\langle\psi\vert\hat{E}_i^\dagger\hat{E}_j\vert\phi\rangle=\delta_{ij}f(i,j)$ — inconsistent with the cell-2 body `eq-qec-knill-laflamme` $\hat{\Pi}\hat{E}_a^\dagger\hat{E}_b\hat{\Pi}=C_{ab}\hat{\Pi}$, and incorrect as written: the body form sandwiched between codewords is $\langle\psi\vert\hat{E}_i^\dagger\hat{E}_j\vert\phi\rangle=C_{ij}\langle\psi\vert\phi\rangle$, which vanishes for orthogonal codewords, whereas P5’s $\delta_{ij}f(i,j)$ equals $f(i,i)\neq0$ for $i=j$ — so the stated “iff” was unsatisfiable for any code with two or more orthogonal codewords, and the $\delta_{ij}$ wrongly forced the Hermitian error-correlation matrix diagonal. derivation-designer confirmed the finding against `derivation-quality.md` and `_refs/` (`_refs/HOMEWORK.md` § 6.4.3 has no Knill-Laflamme material — P5 is the notebook’s own design and must match the body box). notebook-writer restated the condition via Bash + Python json I/O — one assert-count-guarded (count == 1) substring replacement on the joined cell-3 source, re-split with `text_to_source`: the equation became $\langle\psi\vert\hat{E}_i^\dagger\hat{E}_j\vert\phi\rangle=C_{ij}\,\langle\psi\vert\phi\rangle$, $C_{ij}$ is now named a Hermitian matrix of constants, and a closing sentence ties it to the body’s projector form {eq}`eq-qec-knill-laflamme`. P5(a)-(c) left unchanged (P5(a) still holds — the bit-flip code is non-degenerate). Cell count 4, structure intact, no other cell changed. Both static validators (`safe_edit.py validate`, `validate_project.py --scope 6-4-3-error-correction`) pass clean. Open queue 5 → 5 (NB-206 closed, NB-209 filed; NB-66, NB-81 deferred on unattended runs; NB-207-NB-208 remain). *(Scheduled-task run, no human present.)*

- **2026-05-23** — **NB-205 fix** (Phase D ticket) on `6-4-3-error-correction`. `feedback.md` had no open professor items (the homework-solutions directive is DONE 2026-05-20); the run took the next actionable ticket from the `progress.md` queue — **NB-205** (MEDIUM, Phase D), the next open actionable ticket after NB-66 and NB-81 (both deferred on unattended runs pending professor / rule sign-off) and the first of the four `6-4-3` Phase D tickets NB-205–NB-208. The cell-2 `:::{admonition} Derivation: Threshold Scaling Law` Step 4 hand-waved the threshold's origin: it wrote "Absorbing $N_{\min}$ into a normalized form gives the standard scaling law", but $N_{\min}$ (the count of minimum-weight uncorrectable error chains, from Step 3) is not a constant — it grows $\sim\mu^d$ with the code distance — so absorbing a $d$-dependent quantity into the $d$-independent $p_\text{th}$ asserts rather than derives the threshold (`derivation-quality.md` "reverse-engineering from the answer" anti-pattern). derivation-designer re-verified against `derivation-quality.md` and `_refs/`: `_refs/HOMEWORK.md` § 6.4.3 carries no threshold derivation, so the block is the notebook's own design and stands on its own (`derivation-quality.md` § Scope). notebook-writer rewrote Step 4 (retitled "Normalize to the threshold" → "The threshold as a crossover") via Bash + Python json I/O — one assert-count-guarded (count == 1) substring replacement on the joined cell-2 source, re-split with `text_to_source`: $N_{\min}\sim\mu^d$ for a lattice with path-counting constant $\mu$, the substitution $p_\text{logical}\sim\mu^d p^{(d+1)/2}=(\mu^2 p)^{(d+1)/2}/\mu$ with the regrouping $\mu^d=\mu^{d+1}/\mu=(\mu^2)^{(d+1)/2}/\mu$ shown, $p_\text{th}\sim1/\mu^2$ identified as the crossover, and a closing paragraph on the $d$-independence of the threshold. The reproduced scaling-law equation stays unlabelled (no `eq-qec-threshold-scaling` duplicate); the Step 1 / Step 3 ceiling inconsistency is the separate ticket NB-207, left untouched. Phase D is a read-only designer audit — no notebook edit, no validator run; the four tickets enter the queue for later `notebook-writer` fix runs. The cell-2 `### Common Lindblad Operators` Depolarizing item `**Effect**` line said the channel drives any state toward $\hat{I}/2$ "on timescale $1/\Gamma_d$", inconsistent with the homework-P6(b)-derived Bloch decay $\dot{\boldsymbol r}=-(4\Gamma_d/3)\boldsymbol r$ (e-folding time $3/(4\Gamma_d)$, off by a factor $4/3$). Physics independently re-verified: the depolarizing superoperator equals $(4\Gamma_d/3)(\hat{I}/2-\hat\rho)$ to $\sim10^{-17}$ over 2000 random qubit states, and integration from $\vert 0\rangle\langle 0\vert$ shows $\hat\rho-\hat{I}/2$ decays by exactly $1/e$ at $t=3/(4\Gamma_d)$. lecture-designer → notebook-writer rewrote the `**Effect**` line to the exact homework-consistent form — "Drives any state toward the maximally mixed state $\hat{I}/2$, with the deviation $\hat{\rho} - \hat{I}/2$ decaying as $\mathrm{e}^{-4\Gamma_d t/3}$ (e-folding time $3/(4\Gamma_d)$)" — the deviation-from-maximally-mixed form adds no new notation and mirrors the Phase Damping `**Effect**` bullet's explicit-decay-law style. Applied via Bash + Python json I/O (one assert-count-guarded single-line replacement on cell 2, re-split with `text_to_source`); inline math only, `\mathrm{e}` upright per `physics-conventions.md`. The new `note` gives the in-context physical reasoning the lesson supports — $\hat H_\text{int}$ commutes with $\hat Z$ so the qubit stays in its pointer basis, and the two branches drive the bath with equal-and-opposite force (relative coupling $2g_k$) — then states the independent-boson-model overlap. The convention-dependent prefactor (NB-178 secondary item) was pinned down: the exponent now carries the dimensionally-consistent $4/\hbar^2$ (the $4=2^2$ from the relative coupling $2g_k$ squared; the old form had bare coefficient 1), with $J(\omega)=\sum_k g_k^2\delta(\omega-\omega_k)$ named as the bath spectral density; the ohmic high-$T$ limit $\Gamma(t)\approx\gamma t$, $\gamma\propto k_BT$ recovers `eq-decoherence-exponential`. Body lead-in "The microscopic derivation:" → "The microscopic origin of this decay:". Applied via Bash + Python json I/O (two assert-guarded substring replacements, `text_to_source` re-split); cell count 4, 4-cell structure intact. `safe_edit.py validate` and `validate_project.py --scope 6-4-1-decoherence` both clean. Cell 2 § Reduced Density Matrix introduced `eq-decoherence-reduced-explicit` after the words "Computing explicitly:" with zero intermediate partial-trace lines, and the two off-diagonal coefficients carried a conjugation error — $\alpha^*\beta\langle E_1\vert E_0\rangle$ / $\beta^*\alpha\langle E_0\vert E_1\rangle$ where the partial trace of the entangled state $\alpha\vert0\rangle\vert E_0\rangle+\beta\vert1\rangle\vert E_1\rangle$ gives $\alpha\beta^*\langle E_1\vert E_0\rangle$ / $\beta\alpha^*\langle E_0\vert E_1\rangle$. Fix (derivation-designer → notebook-writer): the lead-in now cites `eq-decoherence-entangled-state`, the result equation carries the corrected $\alpha\beta^*$ / $\beta\alpha^*$ coefficients, and a new `dropdown information` block "Derivation: Reduced Density Matrix from the Partial Trace" states the partial-trace overlap rule $\operatorname{Tr}_E(\vert s\rangle\langle s'\vert\otimes\vert E_i\rangle\langle E_j\vert)=\langle E_j\vert E_i\rangle\,\vert s\rangle\langle s'\vert$, expands the joint projector into its four tensor-product terms, and applies $\operatorname{Tr}_E$ term by term — matching the depth of `_refs/HOMEWORK.md` § 6.4.1 Solution 1(a). Homework P1(c), which inherited the slip ($\rho_{01}=\lambda/2$), now targets $\rho_{01}=\lambda^*/2$ with a note that it reduces to $\lambda/2$ for a real overlap (consistent with P2/P4, where the overlap is real). Conjugation verified with NumPy — over random complex amplitudes and non-orthogonal environment states the computed $\rho_{01}=\alpha\beta^*\langle E_1\vert E_0\rangle$. `safe_edit.py validate` and `validate_project.py --scope 6-4-1-decoherence` both clean. - **2026-05-22** — **Phase D fix, NB-156** on `6-3-3-quantum-channels` (queued from the 2026-05-22 Phase D audit) — a LOW display-math layout fix, the second of the three `6-3-3` Phase D tickets. The cell-2 (id `ccc68861`) `:::{admonition} Proof Sketch` (`:class: dropdown information`) **Trace-preserving** equality was a single long one-line `$$…$$` — $\operatorname{Tr}(\sum_k\hat{K}_k\hat\rho\hat{K}_k^\dagger)=\sum_k\operatorname{Tr}(\hat\rho\hat{K}_k^\dagger\hat{K}_k)=\operatorname{Tr}(\hat\rho\sum_k\hat{K}_k^\dagger\hat{K}_k)=\operatorname{Tr}(\hat\rho\hat{I})=\operatorname{Tr}(\hat\rho)$ — chaining four distinct trace manipulations (linearity to pull the sum out, cyclic invariance term by term, linearity to pull the sum back in, the completeness relation $\sum_k\hat{K}_k^\dagger\hat{K}_k=\hat{I}$, then $\operatorname{Tr}(\hat\rho\hat{I})=\operatorname{Tr}(\hat\rho)$) on one line, against `physics-conventions.md` § Display Math (multiline equations use `\begin{split}`); the NB-141 closure note had explicitly deferred the `\begin{split}` layout call to Phase D. notebook-writer converted the block to a six-line `&=`-aligned `\begin{split}` block, one relation per line — the previously fused linearity step is now its own line ($\sum_k\operatorname{Tr}(\hat{K}_k\hat\rho\hat{K}_k^\dagger)$) before the cyclicity step, and the completeness substitution and $\operatorname{Tr}(\hat\rho\hat{I})=\operatorname{Tr}(\hat\rho)$ are separate lines, so the chain reads as a stepwise derivation. A short prose lead-in names the manipulations in order (trace linearity, cyclic invariance, the Kraus completeness relation) — the gold-reference derivation style of `6-1-1-mixed-states` (Von Neumann Equation), which justifies the steps of a `\begin{split}` chain in a prose lead-in rather than with inline `\text{}` annotations (a corpus-wide grep confirmed no `\begin{split}` block uses per-line `\text{}` justifications). No physics change — the chain was already correct and complete; the NB-157 **Completely positive** paragraph in the same `Proof Sketch` was left untouched as a separate open ticket. - **2026-05-22** — **Phase D fix, NB-155** on `6-3-3-quantum-channels` (queued from the 2026-05-22 Phase D audit) — a MEDIUM derivation-depth fix, the first of the three open `6-3-3` Phase D tickets. The cell-2 § *Common Quantum Channels* → *2. Depolarizing Channel* inline **Check** line asserted the Pauli identity $\hat{X}\hat\rho\hat{X}+\hat{Y}\hat\rho\hat{Y}+\hat{Z}\hat\rho\hat{Z}=2\hat{I}-\hat\rho$ bare ("using the Pauli identity …") and compressed the Kraus→mixing-form substitution to the single clause "the Kraus form reproduces" — the identity does the entire work of converting the Kraus form to the mixing form yet was neither proved in-place, cited to an earlier notebook by label, nor flagged as a standard textbook result, against `derivation-quality.md` § Minimum derivation depth. derivation-designer drafted and notebook-writer applied a `:::{admonition} Derivation: Depolarizing Kraus Form Reproduces the Mixing Form` (`:class: dropdown information`) block inserted immediately after the **Check** line, structured per `derivation-quality.md` § 5 as building block → assembly: (i) the **Building block — the Pauli identity** derives $\hat{X}\hat\rho\hat{X}+\hat{Y}\hat\rho\hat{Y}+\hat{Z}\hat\rho\hat{Z}=2\hat{I}-\hat\rho$ in ~5 displayed lines from the Bloch form $\hat\rho=\tfrac12(\hat{I}+\boldsymbol{r}\cdot\hat{\boldsymbol\sigma})$ and the Pauli conjugation rules (distinct Paulis anticommute and square to $\hat{I}$, so e.g. $\hat{X}\hat{Y}\hat{X}=-\hat{Y}$) — conjugating term by term in a 3-line `\begin{split}`, then summing in a 2-line `\begin{split}` and substituting $\boldsymbol{r}\cdot\hat{\boldsymbol\sigma}=2\hat\rho-\hat{I}$; (ii) the **Assembly — the Kraus sum** evaluates $\sum_k\hat{K}_k\hat\rho\hat{K}_k^\dagger$ from {eq}`eq-channel-depol-kraus` in a 4-line `\begin{split}` block, substituting the Pauli identity to reach $(1-p)\hat\rho+\tfrac{p}{2}\hat{I}$ — the mixing form {eq}`eq-channel-depolarizing` at $d=2$. The visible inline **Check** line was left unchanged as the result statement (`content-style.md`: key results visible, extended derivations in `dropdown`). The physics was NumPy-verified before the edit — the Pauli identity and the Kraus→mixing-form reproduction (with the completeness relation) both hold to ~1e-16 over 20000 random trace-1 qubit states and random $p\in[0,1]$. `_refs/HOMEWORK.md` § 6.3.3 Solution 2 carries the same identity but in a $p/3$ Kraus convention it abandons mid-solution ("Wait, let me recalculate"), and writes the identity itself wrong as $2\operatorname{Tr}(\rho)\mathbf{1}-3\rho$ before self-correcting to $2\mathbf{1}-\rho$ — so per the ticket the notebook's mathematically correct $p/4$ convention was not aligned to the buggy reference, and `derivation-quality.md`'s intrinsic depth standard applied (the NB-110 / NB-150 precedent). Applied via Bash + Python json I/O — one `assert count==1` substring replacement on the cell-2 (id `ccc68861`) joined source, re-split with `text_to_source`; cell-2 source 212 → 250 entries, max line 616 chars (well under the 1000-char source-line validator limit), cell count 4, the 4-cell structure intact. - **2026-05-22** — **Phase D (Derivation quality) audit** of `6-3-3-quantum-channels` — the §6.3 third and final subsection; this audit completes the Round 1 A–D pass for the notebook. The three derivation-bearing blocks — the `Proof Sketch` (`dropdown information`) for the Kraus representation theorem, the inline depolarizing-channel **Check**, and the proof-style homework (P1 necessity/sufficiency, P3/P4 verifications, P6/P7 composition and channel-distinguishing) — were structurally checked against `derivation-quality.md` and `_refs/`. `_refs/HOMEWORK.md` § 6.3.3 is the only `_refs/` file with quantum-channel content (`QuantumStatistics.nb` and the other reference notes carry none); it gives the §6.3.3 homework with worked solutions, but its Solution 2 presents depolarizing Kraus operators $\hat K_0=\sqrt{1-p}\,\hat I$, $\hat K_j=\sqrt{p/3}\,\hat\sigma^j$ that do **not** reproduce the channel $(1-p)\hat\rho+\tfrac p2\hat I$ (NumPy: wrong in 500/500 random cases) — the reference self-flags the slip mid-solution (\"Wait, let me recalculate\") — so the notebook's mathematically correct $p/4$ convention must **not** be aligned to it, and `derivation-quality.md`'s intrinsic depth standard applies (the NB-110 / NB-136 / NB-150 precedent). All notebook physics was NumPy-verified correct: the Pauli identity $\hat X\hat\rho\hat X+\hat Y\hat\rho\hat Y+\hat Z\hat\rho\hat Z=2\hat I-\hat\rho$ (2000 random trace-1 states), the depolarizing Kraus completeness and mixing-form reproduction (500 random $p$), and $\mathcal{E}_\text{amp}\circ\mathcal{E}_Z=\mathcal{E}_Z\circ\mathcal{E}_\text{amp}$ (relevant to homework P6(c), whose \"is the composition commutative?\" answer is *yes* for these two channels — both act on the coherences by a scalar and amplitude damping's population transfer is independent of the coherences). Three single-scope derivation-depth tickets were filed: **NB-155** (MEDIUM — the depolarizing-channel **Check** asserts the Pauli identity $\hat X\hat\rho\hat X+\hat Y\hat\rho\hat Y+\hat Z\hat\rho\hat Z=2\hat I-\hat\rho$ with zero supporting steps and compresses the Kraus→mixing-form substitution to one clause, against `derivation-quality.md` § Minimum derivation depth; fix = add a short `dropdown information` deriving the identity and showing the substitution), **NB-156** (LOW — the `Proof Sketch` trace-preserving equality is a single long one-line `$$` chaining four trace manipulations; the NB-141 closure note explicitly deferred the `\begin{split}` layout call to Phase D), and **NB-157** (LOW — the `Proof Sketch` completely-positive paragraph has two one-line it-follows-that gaps: no reason given for $\hat K_k\hat\rho\hat K_k^\dagger\ge0$, and the extended-map Kraus operators $\hat K_k\otimes\hat I_B$ are not named). The `Proof Sketch` trace-preserving chain content and the amplitude/phase-damping Kraus definitions were otherwise judged complete and correct. Phase D completes A–D for `6-3-3-quantum-channels`; the phase cursor advanced to the `6-4-open-quantum-systems` §6.4 section parent at Phase A. Open ticket queue now 5 — NB-66 and NB-81 (both deferred on unattended runs pending professor / rule sign-off) plus the three new `6-3-3` Phase D tickets NB-155–NB-157; NB-155 is the next actionable ticket. *(Scheduled-task run, no human present.)*

- **2026-05-22** — **Phase C fix, NB-153** on `6-3-3-quantum-channels` (queued from the 2026-05-22 Phase C audit) — a LOW Pauli notation-system drift fix. The cell-2 body `### Common Quantum Channels` depolarizing-channel block presented its Kraus operators with spin-system Pauli operators $\hat\sigma^i$ ($i=x,y,z$) — one indexed line in the `eq-channel-depol-kraus` `\begin{split}` block ($\hat K_i=\sqrt{p/4}\,\hat\sigma^i$) and three $\hat\sigma^i$ in the completeness **Check** sentence’s identity $\sum_i\hat\sigma^i\hat\rho\hat\sigma^i=2\hat I-\hat\rho$ — while the cell-3 homework (P2/P4/P6/P7) and the notebook’s uniformly QI-system kets ($\vert0\rangle,\vert1\rangle$ throughout) use QI-system $\hat X,\hat Y,\hat Z$ for the same depolarizing construction, against `physics-conventions.md` § Notation Consistency: Spin vs QI (QI kets must pair with $\hat X,\hat Y,\hat Z$, not $\hat\sigma^i$). lecture-designer → notebook-writer standardized the body to QI notation via Bash + Python json I/O — two source-array edits on cell 2: (i) the `eq-channel-depol-kraus` split block’s single indexed line was expanded to three explicit lines $\hat K_1=\sqrt{p/4}\,\hat X$, $\hat K_2=\sqrt{p/4}\,\hat Y$, $\hat K_3=\sqrt{p/4}\,\hat Z$ (one source entry → three, the split block growing from 2 to 4 relation lines, matching the explicit form of the amplitude- and phase-damping Kraus blocks); (ii) the **Check** sentence’s spin-Pauli identity was rewritten in QI form, $\hat X\hat\rho\hat X+\hat Y\hat\rho\hat Y+\hat Z\hat\rho\hat Z=2\hat I-\hat\rho$, the awkward “$\hat\sigma^i\hat\rho\hat\sigma^i$-summation” phrasing replaced by “the Pauli identity”. The homework P2(c) Bloch-vector parametrization $\hat\rho=\tfrac12(\hat I+\boldsymbol r\cdot\hat{\boldsymbol\sigma})$ uses the vectorized Pauli $\hat{\boldsymbol\sigma}$, which `physics-conventions.md` § Vectors and Operators lists as valid in *both* systems (“Both”), so it is not a drift and was left unchanged. A post-edit scan confirms zero `\hat{\sigma}^` in the notebook; the QI-form Kraus operators were NumPy-verified over random $p$ and random qubit density matrices — the completeness relation $\sum_k\hat K_k^\dagger\hat K_k=\hat I$, the channel reproducing $(1-p)\hat\rho+\tfrac p2\hat I$, and the Pauli identity all hold to machine precision. Cell count 4, the 4-cell structure intact. Both static validators (`safe_edit.py validate`, `validate_project.py --scope 6-3-3-quantum-channels`) pass clean. - **2026-05-22** — **Phase C / Phase-D-overlap fix, NB-150** on `6-3-3-quantum-channels` (queued from the 2026-05-22 Phase C audit) — a MEDIUM entropy-monotonicity correction. The cell-2 body section `### Reversibility and Irreversibility` listed, under "Irreversible (Non-Unitary) Channel", the bullet "Entropy generally increases: $S(\mathcal{E}(\hat\rho)) > S(\hat\rho)$ (output more mixed)" — a claim false for a general CPTP map. The contradictory `### Summary` $S(\mathcal{E}(\hat\rho))\le S(\hat\rho)$ claim the Phase C audit also flagged had already been dropped by the NB-149 Summary restructure, so the body bullet was the sole remaining offender. derivation-designer verified the physics (NumPy): unitary channels conserve $S$ ($\Delta S=0$); the unital channels depolarizing and phase damping ($\mathcal{E}(\hat I)=\hat I$, both confirmed unital) never decrease $S$ (min $\Delta S\ge0$ over 1000 random states each); non-unital amplitude damping is non-monotone (1234/2000 random states $S$ up, 766 down) and at $\gamma=1$ purifies any input to $\vert0\rangle\langle0\vert$; the two ticket examples reproduced exactly (depolarizing $p=0.5$ on $\vert0\rangle$: $S\,0\to0.56$; amplitude damping $\gamma=1$ on $\mathrm{diag}(0.7,0.3)$: $S\,0.61\to0$). `_refs/QuantumStatistics.nb` carries no quantum-channel / Kraus / CPTP material, so `derivation-quality.md`'s intrinsic standard applies (the NB-110 / NB-136 precedent). notebook-writer applied via Bash + Python json I/O — one `assert count==1` in-place single-entry replacement of the cell-2 source-array entry (idx 187), no joined-text re-split: the bad bullet became "Entropy is not monotonic in general: a **unital** channel — one with $\mathcal{E}(\hat{I}) = \hat{I}$, such as depolarizing or phase damping — cannot decrease entropy, $S(\mathcal{E}(\hat{\rho})) \geq S(\hat{\rho})$, whereas a non-unital channel such as amplitude damping can raise *or* lower it (damping toward the pure ground state can even purify a mixed input)." The Reversible-list $S(\mathcal{E}(\hat\rho))=S(\hat\rho)$ unitary bullet was confirmed correct and left untouched, and the irreversibility framing (no inverse, information lost) is unchanged, so the section now cleanly separates irreversibility from entropy monotonicity. Cell-2 source entry count unchanged (210); cell count 4, the 4-cell structure intact. Both static validators (`safe_edit.py validate`, `validate_project.py --scope 6-3-3-quantum-channels`) pass clean. Phase cursor unchanged (Round 1, Phase D, `6-3-3-quantum-channels`) — NB-150 is a queued Phase C fix ticket from a completed audit, not a phase-audit step (`.claude/README.md` § 1.4); the Phase D (Derivation quality) audit stays gated on the remaining Phase C tickets NB-151, NB-153, NB-154 closing. - **2026-05-22** — **Phase B fix, NB-145** on `6-3-3-quantum-channels` (queued from the 2026-05-22 Phase B audit) — a LOW `physics-conventions.md` § Dot Product cleanup. Cell 2 had two `\cdot` misuses: (i) the `Proof Sketch` (`dropdown information`) block’s trace-preservation chain wrote `\operatorname{Tr}(\hat{\rho} \cdot \hat{I})` — `\cdot` between the two operators `\hat{\rho}` and `\hat{I}` (an operator product); (ii) the depolarizing-channel **Check** line wrote `3\cdot\tfrac{p}{4}` — `\cdot` between the number 3 and the scalar `\tfrac{p}{4}`. `\cdot` is reserved for dot products between vectors, never for operator products or scalar multiplication. notebook-writer applied via Bash + Python json I/O on cell 2 — two `assert count==1` substring replacements on the joined cell text, re-split with `text_to_source`: `\hat{\rho} \cdot \hat{I}` → the juxtaposition `\hat{\rho}\hat{I}`, and `3\cdot\tfrac{p}{4}` → `3\,\tfrac{p}{4}` (thin space — the explicit `3 × p/4` structure showing the three equal Pauli-Kraus contributions to the completeness sum was kept). A post-edit scan confirmed zero residual `\cdot` in cell 2; the cell-3 homework P2(c) `\boldsymbol{r}\cdot\hat{\boldsymbol{\sigma}}` is a legitimate vector dot product (Bloch-vector contraction) and was deliberately left untouched. The within-line edits left the cell-2 source entry count unchanged (198 entries); cell count 4 and the 4-cell structure unchanged. Both static validators (`safe_edit.py validate`, `validate_project.py --scope 6-3-3-quantum-channels`) pass clean. - **2026-05-21** — Ticket **NB-138** (LOW, Phase D) closed for `6-3-2-povm`, the §6.3 second subsection — a heterodyne-overlap notation fix queued from this notebook Phase D audit. Cell 2 `:::{admonition} Example: Heterodyne Detection (Optical POVM)` (`:class: dropdown example`) wrote the coherent-state overlap as $\langle\alpha\vert\beta\rangle=\mathrm{e}^{-\vert\alpha-\beta\vert^2/2}$ — a real equality for what is a complex inner product. The exact overlap $\langle\alpha\vert\beta\rangle=\mathrm{e}^{-\vert\alpha\vert^2/2-\vert\beta\vert^2/2+\alpha^{*}\beta}$ carries a phase; only its modulus is real. The fix is the one specified in the ticket and is a mechanical one-symbol notation correction, so it was applied directly (derivation-designer→notebook-writer roles collapsed for a LOW mechanical fix). notebook-writer applied via Bash + Python json I/O — one `assert count==1` substring replacement on the joined cell-2 text, re-split with `text_to_source`, changing the overlap to the modulus form $\vert\langle\alpha\vert\beta\rangle\vert=\mathrm{e}^{-\vert\alpha-\beta\vert^2/2}$. The physics was confirmed before the edit — $\vert\langle\alpha\vert\beta\rangle\vert^2=\mathrm{e}^{-\vert\alpha-\beta\vert^2}$ follows from $\vert\alpha-\beta\vert^2=\vert\alpha\vert^2+\vert\beta\vert^2-2\,\mathrm{Re}(\alpha^{*}\beta)$. The bullet pedagogical point — coherent states are non-orthogonal — is unchanged, the trailing $\neq0$ for $\alpha\neq\beta$ kept; the existing `\vert \alpha-\beta\vert` spacing was preserved so the validator glued-`\vert` check does not trip. Cell count 4 and the 4-cell structure unchanged. Both static validators (`safe_edit.py validate`, `validate_project.py --scope 6-3-2-povm`) pass clean. Phase cursor unchanged (Round 1, Phase A, `6-3-3-quantum-channels`) — NB-138 was a queued Phase-D fix ticket from a completed audit, not a phase-audit step (`.claude/README.md` § 1.4). Open tickets are now NB-66 and NB-81 (both deferred on unattended runs pending professor / rule sign-off) plus NB-139 and NB-140. *(Scheduled-task run, no human present.)*

- **2026-05-21** — Ticket **NB-137** (MEDIUM, Phase D) closed for `6-3-2-povm`, the §6.3 second subsection — a Naimark's-theorem equation fix queued from this notebook's Phase D audit. Cell 2's `:::{admonition} Naimark's Theorem` (`:class: important`) box stated the element-recovery identity `eq-povm-neumark` as $\hat M_i=\operatorname{Tr}_\text{aux}(\hat P_i(\hat\rho\otimes\vert0\rangle\langle0\vert_\text{aux}))$, which is logically inconsistent: the LHS $\hat M_i$ is a fixed, state-independent POVM element while the RHS depends on the system state $\hat\rho$ — varying $\hat\rho$ changes the RHS while $\hat M_i$ is fixed. derivation-designer confirmed the fix, and the physics was independently NumPy-verified across four random POVMs (n=2,3,4 outcomes, d=2,3 dimensions), each built with an explicit textbook Naimark dilation (trivial embedding $\hat I_{\mathcal H}\otimes\vert0\rangle_\text{aux}$, orthogonal projectors $\hat P_i$ on the extended space): the corrected form $\hat M_i=\operatorname{Tr}_\text{aux}(\hat P_i(\hat I_{\mathcal H}\otimes\vert0\rangle\langle0\vert_\text{aux}))$ recovers $\hat M_i$ exactly (error ~1e-16), the current $\hat\rho$ form does not (errors 0.2–0.9), and the full trace $\operatorname{Tr}[\hat P_i(\hat\rho\otimes\vert0\rangle\langle0\vert_\text{aux})]$ reproduces the outcome probability $p_i=\operatorname{Tr}(\hat M_i\hat\rho)$ exactly — confirming the two correct forms and that the box's "$\hat M_i=$" LHS selects the element-recovery form (the alternative probability form $p_i=\operatorname{Tr}[\dots]$ keeps $\hat\rho$). notebook-writer applied the ticket's recommended minimal fix via Bash + Python json I/O — a single cell-2 source-array entry replacement (`assert`-guarded against the exact pre-edit string; post-edit checks confirmed zero residual buggy substring and the corrected substring unique), swapping $\hat\rho$ for $\hat I_{\mathcal H}$ (written `\hat{I}_{\mathcal{H}}`, consistent with the notebook's hatted-identity and `\mathcal{H}` conventions). No other math, the box's "Physical picture" prose, or cell structure changed; the box's "there exist … orthogonal projectors $\{\hat P_i\}$ … such that [eq]" reads correctly with the corrected identity. Both static validators (`safe_edit.py validate`, `validate_project.py --scope 6-3-2-povm`) pass clean. Phase cursor unchanged (Round 1, Phase A, `6-3-3-quantum-channels`) — NB-137 was a queued Phase-D fix ticket from a completed audit, not a phase-audit step (`.claude/README.md` § 1.4). Open tickets are now NB-66 and NB-81 (both deferred on unattended runs pending professor / rule sign-off) plus NB-138, NB-139, and NB-140. *(Scheduled-task run, no human present.)*

- **2026-05-21** — Ticket **NB-136** (MEDIUM, Phase D) closed for `6-3-2-povm`, the §6.3 second subsection. The cell-2 *Construction: Unambiguous State Discrimination POVM* dropdown used the normalization $1/(1+\vert c\vert^2)$, under which $\hat M_3=\hat I-\hat M_1-\hat M_2$ is not positive semidefinite (NumPy-verified negative eigenvalue at every overlap $0<\vert c\vert<1$) — so the construction was not a valid POVM. Rewrote the dropdown with the correct Ivanovic–Dieks–Peres normalization $1/(1+\vert c\vert)$, derived from an explicit $\hat M_3\ge0$ spectrum argument, fixed the success/inconclusive probabilities to $1-\vert c\vert$ / $\vert c\vert$ (were $1-\vert c\vert^2$ / $\vert c\vert^2$), and added the three previously skipped steps (the 2D completeness identity, the normalization-fixing eigenvalue computation, and the $\langle\psi_1\vert\hat M_1\vert\psi_1\rangle$ evaluation) per `derivation-quality.md`. `safe_edit.py validate` and `validate_project.py --scope 6-3-2-povm` pass. Downstream `homework/6-3-2_solutions.md` P4 re-check filed as NB-140. Phase cursor unchanged (ad hoc queue ticket, not a phase-audit advance).

- **2026-05-21** — Phase D (Derivation quality) audit of `6-3-2-povm`, the §6.3 second subsection and the current notebook in the Round-1 phase cursor — the last phase for this notebook. The physics was independently NumPy-verified. Four single-scope tickets were filed. **NB-136** (MEDIUM, derivation-designer → notebook-writer) — the cell-2 `Construction: Unambiguous State Discrimination POVM` dropdown normalizes $\hat M_1,\hat M_2$ by $\tfrac{1}{1+\vert c\vert^2}$; with that factor $\hat M_3=\hat I-\hat M_1-\hat M_2$ is not positive semidefinite (NumPy: $\hat M_3$ has a negative eigenvalue for every overlap $0<\vert c\vert<1$), so the construction is not a valid POVM as written — the correct Ivanovic–Dieks–Peres normalization is $\tfrac{1}{1+\vert c\vert}$, the "Key property" probabilities $1-\vert c\vert^2$/$\vert c\vert^2$ should be $1-\vert c\vert$/$\vert c\vert$, and the result is asserted with no intermediate steps. **NB-137** (MEDIUM, derivation-designer → notebook-writer) — the `Naimark's Theorem` `important`-box equation `eq-povm-neumark` $\hat M_i=\operatorname{Tr}_\text{aux}(\hat P_i(\hat\rho\otimes\vert0\rangle\langle0\vert_\text{aux}))$ equates the $\hat\rho$-independent POVM element to an $\hat\rho$-dependent RHS (NumPy-verified: the RHS does not equal $\hat M_i$ and does not reproduce $p_i$); the correct element-recovery form replaces $\hat\rho$ with $\hat I_{\mathcal H}$. **NB-138** (LOW, derivation-designer → notebook-writer) — the Heterodyne example writes the complex coherent-state overlap $\langle\alpha\vert\beta\rangle$ equal to a real exponential; only its modulus is real. **NB-139** (LOW, notebook-writer) — homework/body symbol drift: the two-state overlap is $c$ in the body but $s$ in homework P3, and the Naimark ancilla space is $\mathcal H_\text{aux}$ in the body but $\mathcal K$ in homework P5. Phase D completes the A–D pass for `6-3-2-povm`, and the cursor advanced to `6-3-3-quantum-channels` at Phase A. No notebook file was modified this run (audit only), so no validators were run. - **2026-05-21** — Ticket **NB-132** (MEDIUM, Phase C) closed for `6-3-2-povm`, the §6.3 second subsection and the current notebook in the Round-1 phase cursor — a body / Summary contradiction fix queued from this notebook’s Phase C audit. Cell 2’s body section `### Measurement with a POVM` presented the square-root (Lüders) form `eq-povm-collapse` $\hat\rho'_i=\sqrt{\hat M_i}\hat\rho\sqrt{\hat M_i}/\operatorname{Tr}(\hat M_i\hat\rho)$ as *the* post-measurement state, while `### Summary` bullets 2–3 say the post-measurement state is **not** determined by the POVM alone — an internal contradiction, with the Summary’s realization-dependence claim also undeveloped in the body (a phantom-summary issue). The ticket’s recommended **conservative option** was taken — it is autonomous-safe because it adds only a factual caveat drawn from standard measurement theory and makes no pedagogical judgement call (the non-conservative path, fully developing realization-dependence in the body, is what the ticket flagged as possibly warranting professor sign-off). lecture-designer specified a one-paragraph caveat; the physics was confirmed against standard QM — a POVM element factors as $\hat M_i=\hat A_i^\dagger\hat A_i$ in many ways (any measurement operator $\hat A_i=\hat U_i\sqrt{\hat M_i}$ with $\hat U_i$ unitary reproduces the same outcome probability $p_i=\operatorname{Tr}(\hat M_i\hat\rho)$ but updates the state differently), so the post-measurement state is genuinely realization-dependent and the square-root form is only the canonical Lüders choice. notebook-writer applied via Bash + Python json I/O — one `assert count==1` substring replacement on the joined cell-2 text, re-split with `text_to_source`. The caveat was inserted between the $\sqrt{\hat M_i}$ unique-square-root sentence and the **Normalization check** line: it states `eq-povm-collapse` is the **Lüders update**, the canonical state-update rule; that — unlike the outcome probability — the post-measurement state is **not** fixed by the POVM alone, since the same element can be implemented by different physical measurements that share outcome statistics yet leave the system in different states; and that the Lüders form is the default taken when no apparatus detail is specified. The body’s true statement that $\sqrt{\hat M_i}$ is the unique *positive* square root was left intact (uniqueness of the positive root is correct; other, non-positive measurement operators still exist). The `### Summary` was reviewed and needed no edit — bullets 2–3 were already the physically correct statement, so the contradiction was one-sided (the body overclaimed) and is resolved by the body caveat alone, which also develops the previously phantom realization-dependence claim. Cell count 4 and the 4-cell structure unchanged; cell-2 source 208 → 210 entries, longest entry 482 chars (well under the 1000-char limit). Both static validators (`safe_edit.py validate`, `validate_project.py --scope 6-3-2-povm`) pass clean. Phase cursor unchanged (Round 1, Phase D, `6-3-2-povm`) — NB-132 was a queued Phase-C fix ticket, not a phase-audit step (`.claude/README.md` § 1.4); the Phase D (Derivation quality) audit of `6-3-2-povm` remains the next phase-audit step. - **2026-05-21** — Ticket **NB-124** (LOW, Phase D) closed for `6-3-1-projective-measurement`, the §6.3 first subsection — the last open Phase D ticket for this notebook, queued from its Phase D audit. Cell 2's **Normalization Check** (just below the `Measurement Postulate: Probability and Collapse` box) justified the unit trace $\operatorname{Tr}(\hat\rho')=\operatorname{Tr}(\hat P_i\hat\rho\hat P_i)/p_i=1$ with "since $\hat P_i^2=\hat P_i$ and $\hat P_i$ is Hermitian", but the identity actually used, $\operatorname{Tr}(\hat P_i\hat\rho\hat P_i)=\operatorname{Tr}(\hat P_i\hat P_i\hat\rho)=\operatorname{Tr}(\hat P_i\hat\rho)=p_i$, needs the **cyclic invariance of the trace** plus idempotence $\hat P_i^2=\hat P_i$ — Hermiticity of $\hat P_i$ is not used in that step (`derivation-quality.md`, failure mode "misleading notation choices"). derivation-designer confirmed the claim — a NumPy check verified $\operatorname{Tr}(P\rho P)=\operatorname{Tr}(P\rho)$ holds even for an oblique (non-Hermitian) idempotent $P$, so Hermiticity is genuinely irrelevant to the identity. notebook-writer applied via Bash + Python json I/O — two `assert count==1` substring replacements on cell 2. The Normalization-Check justification was corrected to "since $\hat P_i^2=\hat P_i$ and the trace is cyclic". The `### Repeated Measurements` proof, whose display chain $p_i^{(2)}=\dots=\operatorname{Tr}(\hat P_i\hat\rho\hat P_i)/p_i=p_i/p_i=1$ left the final $=p_i$ step unjustified, was given an explicit shared justification line: "Here $\hat P_i\hat P_i=\hat P_i$ by idempotence, and the final equality uses the cyclic invariance of the trace: $\operatorname{Tr}(\hat P_i\hat\rho\hat P_i)=\operatorname{Tr}(\hat P_i\hat P_i\hat\rho)=\operatorname{Tr}(\hat P_i\hat\rho)=p_i$." Both the Normalization Check and the Repeated-Measurements proof now cite the correct property. Only the two cell-2 prose lines changed; no physics, derivation chain, or display equation altered; longest cell-2 source entry 671 chars (under the 1000-char limit).
